## @testsubject117

I have never used visual basic before so I need step by step instructions on what to do

## Copilot

1) Create the project
- Open **Visual Studio**.
- Click **Create a new project**.
- Choose **Windows Forms App (.NET Framework)**.
  - Language: **Visual Basic**
- Click **Next**.
- Project name: `AmiOffice` (or whatever you want)
- Location: choose a folder you can find again
- Framework: **.NET Framework 4.8** (if offered)
- Click **Create**

2) Add the Main Menu form
- In **Solution Explorer** (right side), you’ll see `Form1.vb`.
- Right‑click `Form1.vb` → **Rename** → `FormMainMenu.vb`
  - Visual Studio will ask if you want to rename all references: click **Yes**.
- Double‑click `FormMainMenu.vb` to open it.

3) Set the app to start on this form
- In Solution Explorer, right‑click the project (top node, like `AmiOffice`) → **Properties**
- Click the **Application** tab
- Set **Startup form** to `FormMainMenu`

4) Build the UI (Designer)
- Open the Designer: right‑click `FormMainMenu.vb` → **View Designer**
- From the **Toolbox**:
  - Drag a **FlowLayoutPanel** onto the form.
    - In Properties (F4):
      - Name: `flpButtons`
      - Dock: `Right`
      - Width: ~280
      - AutoScroll: `True`
      - FlowDirection: `TopDown`
      - WrapContents: `False`
  - Drag a **RichTextBox** onto the form.
    - Properties:
      - Name: `rtbScreen`
      - Dock: `Fill`
      - ReadOnly: `True`
      - Font: `Consolas, 10`
      - ScrollBars: `Vertical`
- Click the blank form background, set:
  - `KeyPreview = True`
  - Text (title): `Main Menu`

5) Paste the code for the form
- Switch to code view: right‑click `FormMainMenu.vb` → **View Code**
- Replace everything in that file with this:

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
Imports System
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormMainMenu

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        BuildButtons()
        DrawMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyDown(sender As Object, e As KeyEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyDown
        Dim ch As String = KeyToChoice(e)
        If ch Is Nothing Then Return
        e.Handled = True
        RunMenuChoice(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Function KeyToChoice(e As KeyEventArgs) As String
        If e.KeyCode >= Keys.A AndAlso e.KeyCode <= Keys.Z Then
            Return Convert.ToChar(e.KeyCode).ToString()
        End If

        If e.KeyCode >= Keys.D0 AndAlso e.KeyCode <= Keys.D9 Then
            Return (e.KeyCode - Keys.D0).ToString()
        End If

        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Add OrElse e.KeyCode = Keys.Oemplus Then Return "+"
        If e.Shift AndAlso e.KeyCode = Keys.D5 Then Return "%"
        If e.Shift AndAlso e.KeyCode = Keys.D4 Then Return "$"
        If e.Shift AndAlso e.KeyCode = Keys.D8 Then Return "*"

        Return Nothing
    End Function

    Private Sub BuildButtons()
        flpButtons.Controls.Clear()

        Dim items As (Key As String, Label As String)() = {
            ("A", "Shop Card Generator"),
            ("B", "Invoice Generator"),
            ("C", "Checks and Cash Receipts"),
            ("D", "View Sales Journal"),
            ("E", "View Log Book"),
            ("F", "Price List Program"),
            ("G", "Print Records, Void Invoices"),
            ("H", "Quick Message Flashing"),
            ("I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex (DISABLED)"),
            ("J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names (DISABLED)"),
            ("K", "Cash Disbursements"),
            ("L", "Business Expenses Account"),
            ("M", "Quotation Form Generator"),
            ("N", "Rolodex"),
            ("O", "Copy Spec Index"),
            ("P", "Entire Ledger Viewing"),
            ("Q", "Word Processor"),
            ("R", "Find Word Processor text"),
            ("S", "Clean Keyboard"),
            ("T", "Change Date or Time"),
            ("U", "Phone Line for Dean"),
            ("V", "Change Main Menu Message"),
            ("W", "Leave a Phone Message"),
            ("X", "Typewriter Mode"),
            ("Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup"),
            ("Z", "Personal Calendar"),
            ("1", "Mileage Tracking"),
            ("2", "Product Purchasing"),
            ("3", "Miscellaneous Menu"),
            ("4", "Add Entries to Log Book"),
            ("+", "Employee Application Test"),
            ("5", "VGA Color Fonts"),
            ("6", "Cadmium Cards"),
            ("7", "Emergency PAYROLL System"),
            ("%", "Shop Card Generator (alt)"),
            ("$", "Chain S"),
            ("*", "Invoice Generator (alt)")
        }

        For Each it In items
            Dim btn As New Button() With {
                .Width = 240,
                .Height = 34,
                .Text = $"{it.Key} - {it.Label}",
                .Tag = it.Key
            }

            AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args)
                                      RunMenuChoice(CStr(DirectCast(sender, Button).Tag))
                                  End Sub

            flpButtons.Controls.Add(btn)
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub ScreenClear()
        rtbScreen.Clear()
    End Sub

    Private Sub ScreenPrint(line As String)
        rtbScreen.AppendText(line & Environment.NewLine)
    End Sub

    Private Sub DrawMenu()
        ScreenClear()
        ScreenPrint("Active Magnetic Inspection - Main Menu")
        ScreenPrint("Date/Time: " & DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"))
        ScreenPrint("")
        ScreenPrint("Click a button or type a letter/number.")
        ScreenPrint("")
        ScreenPrint("(I) and (J) are shown but disabled (IT-managed backups).")
    End Sub

    Private Sub RunMenuChoice(choice As String)
        Dim q As String = choice.Trim().ToUpperInvariant()

        Select Case q
            Case "I", "J"
                MessageBox.Show(
                    "Backups are handled outside this program (IT-managed process)." & Environment.NewLine &
                    "This menu option has been disabled.",
                    "Backup disabled",
                    MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                    MessageBoxIcon.Information
                )
                DrawMenu()

            Case Else
                MessageBox.Show($"You chose: {q}" & Environment.NewLine &
                                "(Next we will implement the real action for this choice.)",
                                "Not implemented yet",
                                MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                                MessageBoxIcon.Information)
        End Select
    End Sub

End Class
```

6) Run it
- Press **F5** (or click the green ▶ Start button).
- You should see:
  - the menu “screen” on the left
  - buttons on the right
  - clicking **I** or **J** shows the disabled-backup message

If anything goes wrong, tell me exactly what you see:
- the error text (copy/paste), or
- a screenshot of the Visual Studio error list.

## @testsubject117

Open the Designer: right‑click FormMainMenu.vb → View Designer
From the Toolbox:
 where is the Toolbox?

## Copilot

In Visual Studio, the **Toolbox** is usually a tab on the **left side**. If you don’t see it, do this:

## Show the Toolbox
1) In the top menu, click **View → Toolbox**  
   - Shortcut: **Ctrl+Alt+X**

That should open it (often as a collapsible panel on the left).

## If it still doesn’t show
2) Click **Window → Reset Window Layout**  
3) Then again: **View → Toolbox** (Ctrl+Alt+X)

## Make sure you’re in the Designer
The Toolbox only shows WinForms controls when you have the **Form Designer** open.

- Right‑click `FormMainMenu.vb` → **View Designer**
- Click once on the form surface
- Now open **View → Toolbox** again

## If you’re using Visual Studio Code
VS Code doesn’t have the WinForms designer/toolbox. You need **Visual Studio** (Community is fine). If you tell me which one you’re using (VS vs VS Code), I’ll adjust the steps.

## @testsubject117

ok, its running fine when I click the start/play button, however. J - Print out customers Actual names should not be disabled

## Copilot

Change the `RunMenuChoice` handler so **only `I` is disabled**, and add a stub for **`J`** (so it’s enabled and does something, even if we haven’t ported the print logic yet).

Here’s the exact edit to make in your `FormMainMenu.vb`:

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
Private Sub RunMenuChoice(choice As String)
    Dim q As String = choice.Trim().ToUpperInvariant()

    Select Case q
        Case "I"
            MessageBox.Show(
                "Backups are handled outside this program (IT-managed process)." & Environment.NewLine &
                "This menu option has been disabled.",
                "Backup disabled",
                MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                MessageBoxIcon.Information
            )
            DrawMenu()

        Case "J"
            ' Enabled: we'll port the old BASIC 1290-1450 behavior next.
            MessageBox.Show(
                "Print Out Customers Actual Names (J) is enabled, but not implemented yet." & Environment.NewLine &
                "Next step: port the REALNAME.DAT -> REALNAME.PRN printing routine.",
                "Not implemented yet",
                MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                MessageBoxIcon.Information
            )

        Case Else
            MessageBox.Show($"You chose: {q}" & Environment.NewLine &
                            "(Next we will implement the real action for this choice.)",
                            "Not implemented yet",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                            MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Select
End Sub
```

Optional (UI text): in `BuildButtons()`, change the label for `J` so it no longer says disabled:

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
("J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names"),
```

After those two changes, run again: clicking **J** will no longer show the backup-disabled message.

If you want, next we can implement the actual `J` behavior from your BASIC (lines ~1290–1450): read `REALNAME.DAT`, swap name fields, write a sorted output, and print it (or generate a printable report).

## Copilot

Noted. The “come back to it” step is:

- Implement **menu option J** by porting the GWBASIC routine **lines 1290–1450**:
  - prompt `P` to print or `Q` to quit
  - read `REALNAME.DAT` (pairs of strings)
  - swap/format names into `REALNAME.BAC`
  - sort into `REALNAME.PRN`
  - print the report (in VB.NET: likely generate a printable view or export text/PDF)

And in the current VB.NET code, we already changed:
- `Case "I"` = disabled message
- `Case "J"` = enabled stub message (placeholder)

## @testsubject117

After clicking start button, I see all the menu buttons users can click are on the right. This needs to be laid out so it closely represents the original dos mainmenu.bas where buttong choices begin at top left and go down to bottom, then resume at top right to bottom right

## Copilot

Do this by switching from a single right-hand `FlowLayoutPanel` to a **2-column layout** (left column A–N style, right column O–… style), like the DOS screen.

### Step-by-step (Visual Studio Designer)

1) **Open the designer**
- In Solution Explorer, right-click `FormMainMenu.vb` → **View Designer**

2) **Add a TableLayoutPanel**
- Toolbox → drag **TableLayoutPanel** onto the form
- In Properties set:
  - **Name**: `tlpMenu`
  - **Dock**: `Right` (or `Fill` if you want buttons beside the screen; see note below)
  - **ColumnCount**: `2`
  - **RowCount**: `1` (we’ll put panels inside it)
  - **AutoSize**: `True` (optional)
- Click the small triangle on the TableLayoutPanel → **Edit Columns**
  - Set both columns to **50%**

3) **Put two FlowLayoutPanels inside it**
- Drag a **FlowLayoutPanel** into the **left cell** of the TableLayoutPanel
  - Name: `flpLeft`
  - Dock: `Fill`
  - FlowDirection: `TopDown`
  - WrapContents: `False`
  - AutoScroll: `True`
- Drag another **FlowLayoutPanel** into the **right cell**
  - Name: `flpRight`
  - Same properties as above

4) **Remove the old flpButtons**
- Click the old right-side panel `flpButtons` and delete it (or set `Visible=False`)

---

### Code changes (minimal)

Replace your `BuildButtons()` method so it fills **two columns**.

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
Private Sub BuildButtons()
    flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
    flpRight.Controls.Clear()

    Dim items As (Key As String, Label As String)() = {
        ("A", "Shop Card Generator"),
        ("B", "Invoice Generator"),
        ("C", "Checks and Cash Receipts"),
        ("D", "View Sales Journal"),
        ("E", "View Log Book"),
        ("F", "Price List Program"),
        ("G", "Print Records, Void Invoices"),
        ("H", "Quick Message Flashing"),
        ("I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex (DISABLED)"),
        ("J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names"),
        ("K", "Cash Disbursements"),
        ("L", "Business Expenses Account"),
        ("M", "Quotation Form Generator"),
        ("N", "Rolodex"),

        ("O", "Copy Spec Index"),
        ("P", "Entire Ledger Viewing"),
        ("Q", "Word Processor"),
        ("R", "Find Word Processor text"),
        ("S", "Clean Keyboard"),
        ("T", "Change Date or Time"),
        ("U", "Phone Line for Dean"),
        ("V", "Change Main Menu Message"),
        ("W", "Leave a Phone Message"),
        ("X", "Typewriter Mode"),
        ("Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup"),
        ("Z", "Personal Calendar"),
        ("1", "Mileage Tracking"),
        ("2", "Product Purchasing"),
        ("3", "Miscellaneous Menu"),
        ("4", "Add Entries to Log Book"),
        ("+", "Employee Application Test"),
        ("5", "VGA Color Fonts"),
        ("6", "Cadmium Cards"),
        ("7", "Emergency PAYROLL System"),
        ("%", "Shop Card Generator (alt)"),
        ("$", "Chain S"),
        ("*", "Invoice Generator (alt)")
    }

    For Each it In items
        Dim btn As New Button() With {
            .Width = 240,
            .Height = 34,
            .Text = $"{it.Key} - {it.Label}",
            .Tag = it.Key
        }

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args)
                                  RunMenuChoice(CStr(DirectCast(sender, Button).Tag))
                              End Sub

        ' Split into 2 columns like DOS:
        If it.Key >= "A" AndAlso it.Key <= "N" Then
            flpLeft.Controls.Add(btn)
        Else
            flpRight.Controls.Add(btn)
        End If
    Next
End Sub
```

### One important note
To “closely represent” DOS, you probably want the **buttons area to be the main area**, not a narrow strip. Two common layouts:

- **Option 1 (closest to DOS):** buttons take most of the form, screen text above/below
- **Option 2 (what you have now):** screen on left, buttons on right

Tell me which you prefer:
- **Keep the screen on the left** and have 2 button columns on the right, or
- **Make the buttons the main center area** (DOS-like), and put the “screen” in a smaller area (top banner or bottom status).

## @testsubject117

make the buttons the main center area and put screen in a smaller area

## Copilot

Make the **buttons grid fill the form**, and move the “screen” to a **small header area** (like the DOS banner/status).

## Step-by-step in the Designer (recommended)

### 1) Add a TableLayoutPanel as the main layout
1. Open **FormMainMenu** in **View Designer**
2. From **Toolbox**, drag a **TableLayoutPanel** onto the form
3. Set its properties:
   - **Name**: `tlpRoot`
   - **Dock**: `Fill`
   - **ColumnCount**: `1`
   - **RowCount**: `2`
4. Click the little triangle on the TableLayoutPanel → **Edit Rows**
   - Row 0: **Absolute** height `120` (or `140`)
   - Row 1: **Percent** `100%`

### 2) Put the screen in the top row
1. Drag your existing `rtbScreen` into **row 0** of `tlpRoot`
2. Set `rtbScreen` properties:
   - **Dock**: `Fill`
   - **ReadOnly**: `True`
   - **Height** doesn’t matter if docked
   - (Optional) `BorderStyle = FixedSingle`

### 3) Put the buttons area in the bottom row
1. Add another **TableLayoutPanel** into **row 1** of `tlpRoot`
2. Set its properties:
   - **Name**: `tlpMenu`
   - **Dock**: `Fill`
   - **ColumnCount**: `2`
   - **RowCount**: `1`
   - Set both columns to **50%**

### 4) Add two FlowLayoutPanels (left and right columns)
- In left cell: FlowLayoutPanel named `flpLeft`
- In right cell: FlowLayoutPanel named `flpRight`

Set both:
- **Dock**: `Fill`
- **FlowDirection**: `TopDown`
- **WrapContents**: `False`
- **AutoScroll**: `True`

### 5) Remove the old right-side panel
If you still have `flpButtons`, delete it.

That’s it for layout.

---

## Code changes (small)
Your `BuildButtons()` code you pasted is already correct for filling `flpLeft` and `flpRight`. You just need to ensure your form now has these controls:
- `rtbScreen`
- `flpLeft`
- `flpRight`

## Update DrawMenu to match the smaller “screen”
Since the screen is now a header, keep it short:

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
Private Sub DrawMenu()
    ScreenClear()
    ScreenPrint("Active Magnetic Inspection - Main Menu")
    ScreenPrint("Date/Time: " & DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"))
    ScreenPrint("Type a key or click a button. (I disabled; J enabled)")
End Sub
```

---

## If you can’t find TableLayoutPanel / Toolbox
Tell me which Visual Studio you’re using (Community/Professional, and version if you know), and whether you’re in **View Designer** or just code view, and I’ll guide you to the exact menu path.

## Quick confirmation question
Do you want the “screen” header at the **top** (DOS-like), or a **bottom status bar** (like “TYPE A LETTER” line)?

## @testsubject117

how do I Drag your existing rtbScreen into row 0 of tlpRoot

## Copilot

To move `rtbScreen` into the **top row (row 0)** of `tlpRoot`, use one of these methods (Designer):

## Method 1 (easiest): Cut/Paste
1) Open **FormMainMenu** → **View Designer**  
2) Click the `rtbScreen` control on the form (the big text area).  
3) Press **Ctrl+X** (Cut).  
4) Click inside the **top cell** of `tlpRoot` (Row 0).  
   - Tip: click near the upper-left inside that cell until you see the cell border highlight.
5) Press **Ctrl+V** (Paste).

That places it into row 0.

## Method 2: Drag into the cell
1) Click `rtbScreen` once to select it.  
2) Move your mouse to the **title bar area** of the control (the top edge) until you see the 4-arrow “move” cursor.  
3) Click-and-hold, then drag it over the **top cell** of `tlpRoot`.  
4) When the top cell highlights (a blue outline), release the mouse.

## Method 3: Use the Document Outline (most reliable)
1) Go to **View → Other Windows → Document Outline**  
2) In Document Outline, find `rtbScreen`  
3) Drag `rtbScreen` onto `tlpRoot` (it will nest under it)  
4) Then set its row:
   - Click `rtbScreen`
   - In Properties, find **TableLayoutPanel → Row** (sometimes shown as `Row` / `Column`)
   - Set **Row = 0**

## If it won’t drop into the row
This almost always means **rtbScreen is Dock=Fill on the form**, making it hard to grab.

Do this:
1) Select `rtbScreen`
2) In Properties: set **Dock = None**
3) Move it into `tlpRoot` using Method 1 (cut/paste)
4) Set **Dock = Fill** again

If you tell me what you see when you try (does it snap back? does nothing highlight?), I’ll tell you which method will work in your exact layout.

## @testsubject117

## Copilot

No problem—you can put them back quickly.

## Step 1: Undo (try this first)
In the Designer window press **Ctrl+Z** a few times.
- If both `rtbScreen` and `tlpRoot` come back, stop there.

If Undo doesn’t bring them back, do the rebuild steps below.

---

## Step 2: Recreate `tlpRoot` (main layout)
1) Open **View Designer** for `FormMainMenu`.
2) **Toolbox → TableLayoutPanel** → drag it onto the form.
3) Click it and set Properties (F4):
   - **Name**: `tlpRoot`
   - **Dock**: `Fill`
   - **ColumnCount**: `1`
   - **RowCount**: `2`
4) Click the small triangle on the top-right of the TableLayoutPanel → **Edit Rows…**
   - Row 0: **Absolute** `120`
   - Row 1: **Percent** `100`

---

## Step 3: Recreate `rtbScreen` (small screen/header)
1) Toolbox → **RichTextBox** → drag into the **top cell** (row 0) of `tlpRoot`.
2) Set Properties:
   - **Name**: `rtbScreen`
   - **Dock**: `Fill`
   - **ReadOnly**: `True`
   - **Font**: `Consolas, 10`
   - **ScrollBars**: `Vertical`

---

## Step 4: Recreate the 2-column menu area (bottom row)
1) Toolbox → **TableLayoutPanel** → drag into the **bottom cell** (row 1) of `tlpRoot`.
2) Set Properties:
   - **Name**: `tlpMenu`
   - **Dock**: `Fill`
   - **ColumnCount**: `2`
   - **RowCount**: `1`
3) Edit Columns:
   - Column 0: **Percent 50**
   - Column 1: **Percent 50**

---

## Step 5: Add `flpLeft` and `flpRight`
1) Toolbox → **FlowLayoutPanel** → drag into left cell of `tlpMenu`
   - Name: `flpLeft`
   - Dock: `Fill`
   - FlowDirection: `TopDown`
   - WrapContents: `False`
   - AutoScroll: `True`
2) Drag another FlowLayoutPanel into right cell
   - Name: `flpRight`
   - same settings

---

## Step 6: Run once
Press **F5**. If you get an error like “`rtbScreen` is not declared” or “`flpLeft` is not declared”, it means the control names don’t match exactly—double-check the **Name** property spelling.

### Quick question
Are you using **Windows Forms App (.NET Framework)** or the newer **WinForms (.NET)** template? (Either works; the designer behaves slightly differently.)

## @testsubject117

'rtbScreen' is not declared. It may be inaccessible due to its protection level.

*System: User retried message*

## Copilot

Fix: you’re referencing a control named `rtbScreen`, but the form code I provided declares it as `rtb` (a private field). Make the names consistent.

## Option A (recommended): rename the field to `rtbScreen`
Edit your form to declare `rtbScreen` and use it everywhere.

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
' ... inside FormMainMenu class ...

' UI controls
Private rtbScreen As New RichTextBox()

Public Sub New()
    Me.Text = "MAINMENU (GWBASIC port)"
    Me.Width = 1000
    Me.Height = 700

    rtbScreen.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
    rtbScreen.Font = New Drawing.Font("Consolas", 12.0F)
    rtbScreen.ReadOnly = True
    rtbScreen.Multiline = True
    rtbScreen.BackColor = Drawing.Color.Black
    rtbScreen.ForeColor = Drawing.Color.LightGray
    rtbScreen.ScrollBars = RichTextBoxScrollBars.Vertical

    Me.Controls.Add(rtbScreen)

    scr = New GwScreen(rtbScreen, cols:=80, rows:=25)
    tmr.Interval = 50
End Sub
```

Then ensure you **don’t** still have `rtb` referenced anywhere (search/replace `rtb` → `rtbScreen`).

## Option B: keep `rtb` and change your references
If your code elsewhere (designer or your edits) uses `rtbScreen`, change those references to `rtb`.

---

## Common WinForms gotcha (Designer vs code-behind)
If you created the RichTextBox via the **Designer**, it likely generated:

- `Friend WithEvents rtbScreen As RichTextBox` in `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`

In that case, **delete the manual `Private rtb As New RichTextBox()`** from `FormMainMenu.vb` and instead use the designer control:

```vbnet
' remove manual creation and Me.Controls.Add(...)
scr = New GwScreen(rtbScreen, cols:=80, rows:=25)
```

### Quick question
Did you add the RichTextBox in the **Designer** (so `rtbScreen` exists in `.Designer.vb`), or are you creating it **in code**?  
If you tell me which, I’ll give the exact minimal edit to make it compile.

## @testsubject117

this is what I have, what is missing

## Copilot

I can tell you exactly what’s missing, but I need to see the code where the error occurs.

Please paste **either**:

1) The **exact error line** from the Error List (double-click it and copy the line of code), **or**
2) Your current `FormMainMenu.vb` (and if you used the Designer, also `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`), **or**
3) A screenshot of the form code + the highlighted error.

In the meantime, here’s what’s *almost certainly* missing based on that error:

## What’s missing (most common causes)

### Case 1: You referenced `rtbScreen`, but never declared it
You need a field like this inside `Public Class FormMainMenu`:

```vbnet
Private rtbScreen As RichTextBox
```

…and you must create it in the constructor:

```vbnet
rtbScreen = New RichTextBox()
Me.Controls.Add(rtbScreen)
```

### Case 2: You created it in the Designer, but your code-behind isn’t seeing it
If the Designer created `rtbScreen`, then your `FormMainMenu` must be declared as **Partial** in `FormMainMenu.vb`:

```vbnet
Partial Public Class FormMainMenu
```

(Usually VS already does this; but if you changed it, the Designer field won’t merge.)

### Case 3: Name mismatch (you have `rtb`, not `rtbScreen`)
If your form has:

```vbnet
Private rtb As New RichTextBox()
```

but later you do:

```vbnet
scr = New GwScreen(rtbScreen, 80, 25)
```

Then either rename `rtb` → `rtbScreen`, or change that call to use `rtb`.

---

## Fastest way to diagnose
Paste just these two snippets from `FormMainMenu.vb`:

1) The top of the class where controls are declared (the lines around `Private ... RichTextBox`)
2) The constructor `Public Sub New()` where you set up the control and call `New GwScreen(...)`

Once I see those, I’ll tell you the exact missing line(s) to add/remove so it compiles.

## Copilot

What’s missing is exactly this: **your Designer file does not declare any `RichTextBox` named `rtbScreen` (or `rtb`)**, but your code-behind is trying to use one. Right now your form only contains:

- `flpLeft` (FlowLayoutPanel)
- `flpRight` (FlowLayoutPanel)
- `tlpRoot` (TableLayoutPanel)

So `rtbScreen` “is not declared” because it truly doesn’t exist anywhere in the partial class.

You have two clean ways to fix it:

---

## Fix 1 (minimal, matches the earlier “terminal-in-a-form” approach)
Add a `RichTextBox` to the Designer and name it `rtbScreen`, then use it from code.

### A) Add to Designer (recommended)
In Visual Studio Designer:
1. Drag a **RichTextBox** onto the form (or into `tlpRoot`)
2. Set:
   - `Name = rtbScreen`
   - `Dock = Fill`
   - `ReadOnly = True`
   - `Font = Consolas, 12`
3. Save.

This will generate something like:
```vbnet
Friend WithEvents rtbScreen As RichTextBox
```
in the Designer file, and `InitializeComponent()` will instantiate it.

### B) If you want to edit the Designer code manually (works, but be careful)
Add the field at the bottom:

```vbnet
Friend WithEvents rtbScreen As RichTextBox
```

And inside `InitializeComponent()` create/configure it and add it to the form:

```vbnet
rtbScreen = New RichTextBox()
rtbScreen.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
rtbScreen.Font = New Font("Consolas", 12.0F)
rtbScreen.ReadOnly = True
rtbScreen.BackColor = Color.Black
rtbScreen.ForeColor = Color.LightGray
Controls.Add(rtbScreen)
```

(If you do this, remove it later if you re-open the Designer and it reorders controls.)

---

## Fix 2 (keep your current GUI layout with panels)
If you *don’t* want the “terminal-in-a-form” anymore, then remove/stop using `rtbScreen` in code-behind and instead render the menu into `flpLeft/flpRight` using `Buttons`/`Labels`.

But since you asked earlier for automated line-by-line translation, **Fix 1 is the best immediate path**, because it preserves `LOCATE/PRINT/CLS` semantics.

---

## One more thing: your earlier code-behind that created the RichTextBox in `New()` can still work
If you previously had code like:

```vbnet
Private rtbScreen As New RichTextBox()
```

in `FormMainMenu.vb`, that should compile **even without the Designer field**. But you must ensure:
- you really declared `rtbScreen` in `FormMainMenu.vb` (inside the class), and
- you didn’t accidentally reference `rtbScreen` from a different class/module, or delete that field.

Because your Designer is already laying out panels, the simplest is: **put the RichTextBox inside `tlpRoot`** and stop adding `flpLeft/flpRight` directly to `Controls` (right now you’re adding all three, and `tlpRoot` docks fill so it will cover others).

---

## Quick recommendation (so it’s consistent)
Tell me which UI direction you want now:

1) **Terminal-in-a-form**: Add `rtbScreen` and we ignore `flpLeft/flpRight` for now.  
2) **True GUI menu**: Keep `flpLeft/flpRight`, and I’ll rewrite the main menu drawing code to generate buttons instead of printing ASCII.

If you pick (1), paste your current `FormMainMenu.vb` (code-behind) and I’ll give you the exact edits so `scr = New GwScreen(rtbScreen, 80, 25)` works with your Designer layout (using `tlpRoot`).

## Copilot

Switching to **(2) True GUI menu** means you should **stop using `GwScreen` / `rtbScreen` entirely**. Instead, the “menu drawing” (your old `PRINT` block) becomes **buttons** added to `flpLeft` and `flpRight`, and the “TYPE A LETTER” becomes either:
- clicking a button, or
- pressing a hotkey (KeyPreview=True already).

### What to remove (that caused the error)
In `FormMainMenu.vb` (code-behind), remove or comment out anything like:

- `scr = New GwScreen(rtbScreen, ...)`
- `scr.Print...`, `scr.Cls`, `scr.Locate`, `scr.Color`
- any declaration/usage of `rtbScreen` (since it’s not in the Designer)

---

## What’s missing right now
Your Designer has `tlpRoot` but you are not using it (you never add the panels into it). You currently do:

```vbnet
Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
Controls.Add(flpRight)
Controls.Add(flpLeft)
```

Because `tlpRoot.Dock = Fill`, it will cover the others. So you need to place the left/right panels **inside** `tlpRoot`, and only add `tlpRoot` to the form.

### Update `InitializeComponent()` layout
Replace the “Controls.Add” section with this layout:

- `tlpRoot` = 2 columns (Left / Right)
- add `flpLeft` to column 0, `flpRight` to column 1
- dock both panels fill

Here’s the corrected Designer-style code (you can do this in the designer UI too, but this shows what you want):

```vbnet
' tlpRoot
tlpRoot.ColumnCount = 2
tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Clear()
tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
tlpRoot.RowCount = 1
tlpRoot.RowStyles.Clear()
tlpRoot.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

' flpLeft
flpLeft.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
flpLeft.AutoScroll = True
flpLeft.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
flpLeft.WrapContents = False

' flpRight
flpRight.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
flpRight.AutoScroll = True
flpRight.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
flpRight.WrapContents = False

' add panels into the table
tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)

' FormMainMenu controls: ONLY tlpRoot
Controls.Clear()
Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
```

If you don’t want to edit Designer code manually: in the WinForms designer, set `tlpRoot` to 2 columns, drag `flpLeft` and `flpRight` into it, set both Dock=Fill, and delete them from the form root.

---

## Code-behind: build the menu as buttons (hotkeys included)

Create/replace `FormMainMenu.vb` (code-behind) with this minimal working version that uses your existing controls:

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Protected Overrides Sub OnShown(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnShown(e)
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Left column
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records, Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "S", "Clean Keyboard")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "U", "Phone Line for Dean")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "V", "Change Main Menu Message")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "W", "Leave a Phone Message")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "+", "Employee Application Test")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "5", "VGA Color Fonts")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
    End Sub

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()
        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Width = panel.ClientSize.Width - 25
        btn.Height = 32
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub() HandleMenuKey(key)
        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ' Keep buttons fitting the panel width
        ResizeButtons(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtons(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtons(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = panel.ClientSize.Width - 25
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        ' Hotkey behavior like INKEY$
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        Dim key = ch

        ' Match original behavior: accept lowercase too
        If key.Length = 1 Then
            Dim up = key.ToUpperInvariant()
            Dim low = key.ToLowerInvariant()

            Select Case up
                Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
                Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
                Case "C" : NotYet("Ledger (Checks/Cash Receipts)")
                Case "D" : NotYet("Sales Journal")
                Case "E" : NotYet("Log Book")
                Case "F" : NotYet("Price List")
                Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void")
                Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
                Case "I" : NotYet("Backup routine (we'll implement in .NET)")
                Case "J" : NotYet("Print customer actual names")
                Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements")
                Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses (password)")
                Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation")
                Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex")
                Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
                Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing")
                Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
                Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")
                Case "S" : NotYet("Clean Keyboard / Message")
                Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
                Case "U" : NotYet("Phone Line for Dean")
                Case "V" : NotYet("Change Main Menu Message")
                Case "W" : NotYet("Leave Phone Message")
                Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
                Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean Personal Backup")
                Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")
            End Select

            Select Case key
                Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
                Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
                Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
                Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
                Case "+" : NotYet("Employee Application Test")
                Case "5" : NotYet("VGA Color Fonts")
                Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
                Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency Payroll System")
            End Select
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

---

Also confirm: do you want the menu split exactly like the DOS screen (A–N left, O–7 right), or do you want a single list with categories?

## @testsubject117

please regenerate the code for the entire formmainmenu.vb so I can copy and replace

## Copilot

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    ' NOTE:
    ' - This file is intended for a "true GUI menu" (no terminal emulation).
    ' - It assumes FormMainMenu.Designer.vb defines these controls:
    '     Friend WithEvents flpLeft As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents flpRight As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents tlpRoot As TableLayoutPanel
    ' - If your Designer currently does NOT place flpLeft/flpRight inside tlpRoot,
    '   this code will do it at runtime in OnLoad.

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        ' Ensure KeyPress is received at the form level
        Me.KeyPreview = True

        ' Ensure layout is correct even if the Designer currently adds controls directly to the form.
        EnsureLayout()

        ' Build the menu buttons
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub EnsureLayout()
        ' Make tlpRoot the only root control, and host left/right panels inside it.
        ' This avoids "Dock=Fill" overlap and keeps layout predictable.

        If tlpRoot Is Nothing Then Return
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        ' Configure table: 2 columns, 1 row
        tlpRoot.SuspendLayout()

        tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Clear()
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Clear()

        tlpRoot.ColumnCount = 2
        tlpRoot.RowCount = 1
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        ' Configure panels
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        ' If panels are not already children of tlpRoot, add them.
        If flpLeft.Parent IsNot tlpRoot OrElse flpRight.Parent IsNot tlpRoot Then
            tlpRoot.Controls.Clear()
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)
        Else
            ' Ensure correct cell placement
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpLeft, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(0, 0))
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpRight, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(1, 0))
        End If

        ' Ensure tlpRoot is the only top-level hosted control
        If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
        End If

        For i As Integer = Me.Controls.Count - 1 To 0 Step -1
            Dim c = Me.Controls(i)
            If c IsNot tlpRoot Then
                Me.Controls.RemoveAt(i)
            End If
        Next

        tlpRoot.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.SuspendLayout()

        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.Padding = New Padding(10)

        panel.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Optional: add headers
        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        ' Left column (A..N + K/L etc, mirroring your DOS screen)
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (O..Z, 1..7, +)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "S", "Clean Keyboard")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "U", "Phone Line for Dean")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "V", "Change Main Menu Message")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "W", "Leave a Phone Message")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "+", "Employee Application Test")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "5", "VGA Color Fonts")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        ' Visual tweak for readability
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        ' Calculate width allowing for scroll bar + padding
        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        ' Hotkey behavior (similar to INKEY$)
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        ' Normalize letter keys to uppercase for matching.
        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
            Case "I" : NotYet("Backup Price List & Rolodex (implement in .NET)")
            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")
            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")
            Case "S" : NotYet("Clean Keyboard / Message")
            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "U" : NotYet("Phone Line for Dean")
            Case "V" : NotYet("Change Main Menu Message")
            Case "W" : NotYet("Leave a Phone Message")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "+" : NotYet("Employee Application Test")
            Case "5" : NotYet("VGA Color Fonts")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

## @testsubject117

question: what does button choice 5 vga color fonts actually do

## Copilot

In your pasted GWBASIC, option **(5) VGA Color Fonts** doesn’t implement “fonts” itself—it **jumps into another BASIC module**:

- Line **1125**: `IF Q$="5" THEN PRINT"Please Wait...":CHAIN "VGAFONTS"`

So the “real” behavior is entirely inside **`VGAFONTS.BAS`** (or whatever file `CHAIN "VGAFONTS"` loads). From the main menu code alone, we can only infer intent:

- It likely **loads a separate program** that changes how text is displayed in DOS, e.g.:
  - loads a **custom VGA text-mode font** (8x16/8x8) into the VGA adapter,
  - enables **different text colors/palettes** or “colorized” character sets,
  - or provides a **font/charset selection menu** for the rest of the DOS apps.

But the *exact* answer requires seeing that `VGAFONTS` module, because `CHAIN` is basically “transfer control to another program”.

If you paste the contents of `VGAFONTS.BAS` (or the file that contains it), I can tell you precisely what it does and how to reproduce it (or ignore it) in VB.NET. In WinForms, you typically wouldn’t mess with VGA fonts at all—you’d just set the font on controls—so we’ll probably translate it into “choose UI font / theme” or drop it if it was only for DOS readability.

## @testsubject117

since we're moving to visual basic code for windows, there is no need to change dos vga font colors, correct?

## Copilot

Correct: in a VB.NET Windows GUI app there’s **no VGA text-mode font/palette to change**, so the original purpose of **“VGA Color Fonts”** (whatever `VGAFONTS` did under DOS) is **not directly applicable**.

What you *can* do instead depends on what that option was used for:

- If it was purely **cosmetic/readability** (bigger font, different character set, different colors), then in WinForms you replace it with a **UI settings** screen:
  - choose font family/size (e.g., Consolas 12/14/16),
  - choose color theme (light/dark/high-contrast),
  - maybe store in `appsettings.json` or user settings.

- If it was actually doing something more “functional” (some DOS apps loaded a VGA font to display special symbols/boxes), then you may need to ensure the WinForms UI uses a font that contains those glyphs (or swap to Unicode box-drawing characters). But that’s still **normal Windows font selection**, not VGA programming.

If you want to be 100% sure before deleting it: paste `VGAFONTS.BAS`. Often these “font utilities” also tweaked keyboard, codepage, or display mode—rare, but possible.

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    ' NOTE:
    ' - True GUI menu (no terminal emulation).
    ' - Assumes FormMainMenu.Designer.vb defines:
    '     Friend WithEvents flpLeft As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents flpRight As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents tlpRoot As TableLayoutPanel
    ' - This code will enforce the layout at runtime.

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        EnsureLayout()
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub EnsureLayout()
        If tlpRoot Is Nothing Then Return
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        tlpRoot.SuspendLayout()

        tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Clear()
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Clear()

        tlpRoot.ColumnCount = 2
        tlpRoot.RowCount = 1
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        If flpLeft.Parent IsNot tlpRoot OrElse flpRight.Parent IsNot tlpRoot Then
            tlpRoot.Controls.Clear()
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)
        Else
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpLeft, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(0, 0))
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpRight, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(1, 0))
        End If

        If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
        End If

        For i As Integer = Me.Controls.Count - 1 To 0 Step -1
            Dim c = Me.Controls(i)
            If c IsNot tlpRoot Then
                Me.Controls.RemoveAt(i)
            End If
        Next

        tlpRoot.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.SuspendLayout()

        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.Padding = New Padding(10)

        panel.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Optional headers
        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        ' Left column (unchanged)

        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")

        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")

        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (REMOVED: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
            Case "I" : NotYet("Backup Price List & Rolodex (implement in .NET)")
            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            ' REMOVED hotkeys: S, U, V, W, +, 5

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")

            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

## @testsubject117

What does typewriter mode do

## Copilot

In your GWBASIC program, **Typewriter Mode** is the block starting at **line 2150** (triggered by menu choice **X**: `IF Q$="X" THEN 2150`).

Functionally, it turns the program into a simple **keystroke-to-printer “typewriter”**:

- **2150**: Sends printer control codes: `LPRINT CHR$(27);"i";CHR$(1)`  
  - `CHR$(27)` is ESC. The exact effect depends on the printer (often selects a mode like “italic/on” or some printer feature). It’s printer-specific.

- **2160–2180**: Clears the screen and shows instructions and a ruler line:
  - `[TAB = Move to Right 5 Spaces] [ESC = Exit]`
  - Prints a guide line `....A....B....C...` to show column positions.

- Then it loops reading **one key at a time** using `INKEY$`:
  - **ESC** (`CHR$(27)`): exits typewriter mode, and sends `LPRINT CHR$(27);"@"` (often “reset printer”), then returns to the main menu.
  - **TAB** (`CHR$(9)`): it *doesn’t* insert a real tab—your code replaces it with a **single space** (`Q$=" "`), despite the message saying “move right 5 spaces”.
  - **ENTER** (`CHR$(13)`): sends the accumulated line `LN$` to the printer (`LPRINT LN$`), then starts a new line.
  - **Backspace** (`ASC(Q$)=8`): deletes the last character from `LN$` and redraws the line on-screen.
  - Any other character: appends to `LN$` up to 79 characters and redraws it.

So in Windows/VB.NET terms: it’s basically a **very simple text entry form** whose output goes to the **default printer**, line-by-line, when you press Enter.

If you want, I can translate it into a WinForms dialog like:
- a big `TextBox` for typing,
- Enter prints the current line (or selected text),
- Esc closes,
- and printing uses `System.Drawing.Printing.PrintDocument` instead of `LPRINT`.

## @testsubject117

remember that for later

## Copilot

Noted for later: **Typewriter Mode (menu X)** is the GWBASIC routine at **lines 2150–2280** that acts like a keystroke-driven “typewriter”:

- collects characters into `LN$` (max ~79 chars),
- **Enter** prints `LN$` to the printer (originally `LPRINT`),
- **Backspace** edits `LN$`,
- **Esc** exits and sends a printer reset sequence,
- shows an on-screen ruler/instructions.

When we port it to VB.NET/WinForms, we’ll implement it as a **TypewriterMode form/dialog** that prints via **`PrintDocument`** (no `LPRINT`).

## @testsubject117

what does (Y) Ed Dean's Personal Backup Do

## Copilot

In the GWBASIC you pasted, **(Y) Ed Dean’s Personal Backup** is just a dispatcher to another program:

- Line **1050**: `IF Q$="Y" THEN CHAIN"EDBACKUP"`

So **MainMenu.bas itself does not do the backup**. It **loads and transfers control** to a separate BASIC module/program named **`EDBACKUP`** (typically `EDBACKUP.BAS` or a compiled EXE with that basename, depending on how it was deployed).

What we can infer from *this* file alone:

- It’s intended to run a **different backup routine** than the general system backup (your “I / backup price list & rolodex” and the big ZIP-based backup around lines 1720+).
- It is likely “personal” data for “Ed Dean” (maybe separate directories/files), but the actual file list, destination, and logic are unknown without the `EDBACKUP` source.

To answer precisely (and to port it to .NET), I need the contents of the `EDBACKUP` module. If you paste `EDBACKUP.BAS`, I’ll:
1) describe exactly what it backs up and where,
2) translate it to VB.NET,
3) replace `CHAIN "EDBACKUP"` with a call into the new .NET routine/form.

## @testsubject117

what does change date or time do

## Copilot

In your GWBASIC, **(T) Change Date or Time** jumps to the routine starting at **line 1670** (`IF Q$="T" THEN 1670`). It does this:

1) **Clears the screen and runs an external calendar program**
- **1670**: `CLS:SHELL "CAL":LOCATE 2,1`
  - Shows a calendar (whatever DOS `CAL` is in your environment), then positions the cursor.

2) **Prompts for TIME**
- **1680**: `PRINT"TIME [Enter = ";TIME$;"] ";:INPUT Q$:IF Q$<>"" THEN TIME$=Q$`
  - Displays the current time, asks for a new time.
  - If you just press Enter, it keeps the current time.
  - If you type a value, it assigns it to `TIME$` (this sets the system time in DOS BASIC).

3) **Prompts for DATE**
- **1690**: `PRINT"DATE [Enter = ";DATE$;"] ";:INPUT Q$:IF Q$<>"" THEN DATE$=Q$`
  - Same logic for the system date.

4) **Checks for a payroll file and shows a warning**
- **1692**: `OPEN "C:\PAYROLL.CHK" FOR INPUT AS 1:CLOSE 1`
  - This is just a presence check (it will error if missing, and your ON ERROR logic handles that).
- **1693**: Prints:
  - “This change will not be permanent!”
  - Then tells you to make it permanent on the payroll main menu, waits for Enter, then returns to main menu (`GOTO 490`).

5) **There is a “make permanent” path, but it’s effectively unreachable**
- Lines **1695–1700**:
  - `1695 SHELL"ATCLOCK":REM ...`
  - `1700 GOTO 490`
- However, because **1693 always does `GOTO 490`**, execution never reaches 1695 in the code as written (unless the pasted version differs from the running one).

### What this means for VB.NET/Windows
In WinForms, we generally **should not set the system date/time** automatically (requires admin rights and is risky). A faithful port would:
- show a “Change Date/Time” dialog,
- and either:
  - open Windows Date/Time settings (recommended), or
  - attempt to set system time via Windows API if you explicitly want that and accept needing elevation.

If you want, tell me which behavior you prefer for the Windows version:
- **A)** Open Windows settings (safe)
- **B)** Actually set system date/time (requires admin/elevation)

## @testsubject117

put a place marker on that, I have to verify if that needs to be kept or not

## Copilot

Noted for later: **(T) Change Date or Time** is a **placeholder decision** until you verify whether it must be kept in the Windows/VB.NET port.

What it does in the GWBASIC you pasted (for your verification):
- Menu **T** jumps to **line 1670**.
- Runs external **`CAL`** (`SHELL "CAL"`).
- Prompts for **TIME** and **DATE** and, if provided, assigns to `TIME$` / `DATE$` (DOS BASIC system time/date setters).
- Checks for `C:\PAYROLL.CHK`, then warns: “This change will not be permanent! … make it permanent on the payroll main menu.” and returns to main menu.

When you’re ready, you can decide whether the VB.NET version should:
- open Windows Date/Time settings, or
- set the system clock (admin), or
- remove/disable the feature.
