Right now every run replaces `\\invoice\MainMenu\Backups\WORD.zip`. If you want history, change it to include date/time, e.g. `WORD_2026-04-13_1530.zip`. Tell me if you want **overwrite** or **timestamped**.

## 2) Make migration one-time (recommended)
Right now `MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()` runs every time you press **I**. Even though it “skips if exists”, it still scans `\\invoice\` each time.

Common pattern: create a marker file after first run:
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\.migrated`

Then skip migration if it exists.

If you want, paste your current `MigrationService.vb` and I’ll return a drop-in version that:

- logs what it moved to a text file in `Data`,
- and shows a clearer error if `\\invoice` is unreachable.

## @testsubject117

Time Stamped, and yes make migration 1 time

## Copilot

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO
Imports System.IO.Compression
Imports System.Linq

Public Module BackupService

    Public Sub RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' 1) Word docs => WORD.zip
        Dim wordZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, "WORD.zip")
        CreateZipFromPattern(wordZip, AppPaths.WordDocsDir, "*.doc")

        ' 2) Rotating data backup => BACKUP##.zip
        Dim packNumPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "PACK.NUM")
        Dim n As Integer = GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath)
        Dim dataZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"BACKUP{n}.zip")

        CreateDataZip(dataZip, AppPaths.DataDir)
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateZipFromPattern(zipPath As String, folder As String, pattern As String)
        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                If Directory.Exists(folder) Then
                    For Each f In Directory.GetFiles(folder, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
                        archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, Path.GetFileName(f), CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                    Next
                End If
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateDataZip(zipPath As String, dataDir As String)
        Dim includePatterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT", "*.CUR", "PRC*.PRC", "PHONE*.LST", "BILLS.*", "PACK.NUM"
        }

        Dim files As New System.Collections.Generic.List(Of String)

        For Each pat In includePatterns
            If Directory.Exists(dataDir) Then
                Try
                    files.AddRange(Directory.GetFiles(dataDir, pat, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
                Catch
                End Try
            End If
        Next

        files = files.Distinct(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList()

        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                For Each f In files
                    archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, Path.GetFileName(f), CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                Next
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath As String) As Integer
        Dim n As Integer = 1

        Try
            If File.Exists(packNumPath) Then
                Integer.TryParse(File.ReadAllText(packNumPath).Trim(), n)
            End If
        Catch
            n = 1
        End Try

        If n < 1 OrElse n > 99 Then n = 1

        Dim nextN As Integer = n + 1
        If nextN > 99 Then nextN = 1

        Try
            File.WriteAllText(packNumPath, nextN.ToString())
        Catch
        End Try

        Return n
    End Function

End Module
```

Here are the exact drop-in changes to get **timestamped Word backups** and **one-time migration**.

---

## 1) Update `BackupService.vb` to timestamp `WORD_yyyy-MM-dd_HHmmss.zip`

Replace just the Word zip part inside `RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()`:

```vbnet
' 1) Word docs => WORD_yyyy-MM-dd_HHmmss.zip (timestamped)
Dim stamp As String = DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd_HHmmss")
Dim wordZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"WORD_{stamp}.zip")
CreateZipFromPattern(wordZip, AppPaths.WordDocsDir, "*.doc")
```

Everything else in `BackupService.vb` can stay the same.

If you want `.docx` too, tell me and I’ll adjust it to include both.

---

## 2) Make migration run only once (marker file)

In `MigrationService.vb`, add a marker file in `DataDir`, and skip migration if it exists.

### Drop-in `MigrationService.vb` (recommended)
Replace your whole `MigrationService.vb` with this:

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO

Public Module MigrationService

    Private ReadOnly LegacyRoot As String = "\\invoice"
    Private ReadOnly MarkerPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, ".migrated")

    Public Sub EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.AppRoot)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' If we've already migrated on this share, do nothing.
        If File.Exists(MarkerPath) Then Return

        Dim patterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT", "*.CUR", "*.LST", "*.PRC",
            "PACK.NUM", "BILLS.*", "*.CHK", "*.TXT"
        }

        Dim movedAny As Boolean = False

        For Each pattern In patterns
            Dim files As String()

            Try
                files = Directory.GetFiles(LegacyRoot, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
            Catch
                Continue For
            End Try

            For Each src In files
                Dim dest As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, Path.GetFileName(src))

                ' Skip if already migrated
                If File.Exists(dest) Then Continue For

                Try
                    File.Move(src, dest)
                    movedAny = True
                Catch
                    ' ignore (in use / permissions / etc.)
                End Try
            Next
        Next

        ' Create marker even if nothing moved, so we don't keep scanning \\invoice\ forever.
        Try
            File.WriteAllText(MarkerPath, $"migrated={DateTime.Now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}")
        Catch
            ' If we can't write marker, migration will try again next time.
        End Try
    End Sub

End Module
```

---

## 3) Optional (but helpful): show the timestamped file name
Right now FormMainMenu shows just the backup directory. If you want it to show the exact `WORD_...zip` created, we can have `RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()` return the filenames.

If you want that, tell me and I’ll adjust the function signature cleanly.

## @testsubject117

## Copilot

Not really. The backup and one-time migration will work fine even if the UI message only says “Backup complete” and shows the folder.

It only matters if you want any of these:

- **Proof / audit:** users can tell exactly which ZIP was created (useful when there are many timestamped `WORD_*.zip` files).
- **Troubleshooting:** if Word ZIP succeeds but data ZIP fails, returning filenames makes it clearer what completed.
- **“Open the zip” convenience:** you can automatically open Explorer to the exact file.

If you don’t need any of that, keep it simple: leave `RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()` as `Sub` and just show the backup folder like you’re doing now.

## @testsubject117

Prevent two PCs from writing the same BACKUP#.zip at the same time

## Copilot

Add a **network lock file** around the PACK.NUM read/advance + ZIP creation, so only one PC can run the backup at a time.

### Change: `BackupService.vb`
Add this helper method anywhere inside `BackupService` (near the bottom is fine):

```vbnet
Private Function AcquireBackupLock(lockPath As String) As FileStream
    ' Creating with FileShare.None makes it a cross-machine mutex on a UNC share.
    ' If another PC holds it, this throws an IOException.
    Return New FileStream(lockPath, FileMode.OpenOrCreate, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
End Function
```

Then update `RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()` to wrap the rotating BACKUP##.zip part with the lock:

```vbnet
Public Sub RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
    Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
    Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

    ' 1) Word docs (timestamped) - can run without lock
    Dim stamp As String = DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd_HHmmss")
    Dim wordZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"WORD_{stamp}.zip")
    CreateZipFromPattern(wordZip, AppPaths.WordDocsDir, "*.doc")

    ' 2) Rotating data backup => BACKUP##.zip (LOCKED)
    Dim lockPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "backup.lock")

    Using lockStream As FileStream = AcquireBackupLock(lockPath)

        Dim packNumPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "PACK.NUM")
        Dim n As Integer = GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath)
        Dim dataZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"BACKUP{n}.zip")

        CreateDataZip(dataZip, AppPaths.DataDir)

    End Using
End Sub
```

### What users will see if two PCs press **I** at the same time
The **second** one will likely fail with an error like “The process cannot access the file … backup.lock … because it is being used by another process.”

If you want a nicer message, we can catch `IOException` in `FormMainMenu` around the backup call and show “Backup already running on another PC—try again in a minute.”

If you paste your current `Case "I"` block, I’ll show the exact minimal `Catch` to add for that.

## @testsubject117

Every time I run this I get a windows smart screen dialogue, how can we stop that

## Copilot

You can’t reliably “code your way out” of SmartScreen. SmartScreen shows that dialog when the EXE is **unknown/untrusted** (no reputation) and/or is **marked as coming from the Internet**. To stop it, you typically need one (or both) of these fixes:

## 1) Remove the “Mark of the Web” (MOTW) from the files you copied
If you copy the published folder via browser download, email, some zip tools, etc., Windows tags the files and SmartScreen warns.

### Fix (manual, per workstation, easiest test)
1) On the workstation, right‑click **AMiOffice.exe** → **Properties**
2) If you see **Unblock** at the bottom, check it → **OK**

If you copied a whole folder of DLLs, they can be blocked too. Fastest approach:
- Put the published output into a **.zip** on your dev PC
- On the workstation: right‑click the zip → **Properties → Unblock** → OK
- Then extract the zip (everything inside will be unblocked)

## 2) Code-sign the EXE (best long-term fix)
For multiple PCs, the real “no prompts” solution is to **digitally sign** your EXE with a trusted certificate:
- Ideally an **EV code-signing certificate** (builds SmartScreen reputation much faster)
- Standard code-sign cert also helps, but SmartScreen reputation can still take time

Once signed, SmartScreen warnings usually go away (assuming the cert is trusted).

## 3) Deploy via an internal trusted method (domain/IT approach)
If these PCs are managed (domain/Intune), IT can reduce prompts by:
- AppLocker / Windows Defender Application Control allow rules
- Group Policy to trust your publisher certificate
- Distributing via Software Center/Intune, etc.

## Quick questions (so I can tell you the best path)
1) Are these workstations on a **Windows domain** (managed by an IT admin), or just standalone PCs?
2) How are you copying the app over right now: **USB**, **network share**, or **downloaded zip**?

## @testsubject117

stand alone and copying via network share

## Copilot

On standalone PCs copying via a network share, SmartScreen usually appears for one of two reasons: **the files have a “Mark of the Web”** (downloaded-at-some-point) or Windows simply has **no reputation** for the unsigned EXE. Here’s what you can do.

## 1) First check: is the EXE “blocked” (MOTW)?
On a workstation:
1) Right‑click `AMiOffice.exe` → **Properties**
2) If you see an **Unblock** checkbox/button at the bottom, click it → **OK**
3) Run again.

If you see “Unblock”, that’s the cleanest fix.

### Best practice when distributing updates
If you ever move the app around as a `.zip`:
- **Unblock the zip** *before* extracting:
  - Right‑click the `.zip` → Properties → **Unblock**
  - Then extract
This prevents every extracted file from being blocked.

## 2) If there is NO “Unblock”, SmartScreen is about reputation
If the EXE is **unsigned**, SmartScreen can still warn even when copied from a LAN share. The only durable way to stop that on unmanaged standalone PCs is:

### Code-sign the EXE
- Buy a code signing certificate (EV is best for SmartScreen reputation)
- Sign your published EXE as part of the build/publish process

Without signing, you may be stuck with “Run anyway” prompts on some machines.

## 3) Also avoid running directly from the network share
Even if you copy “via” the share, make sure users run it from a local folder like:
- `C:\Apps\AMiOffice\AMiOffice.exe`
Running *from* the share can increase security prompts and file-lock issues.

## Tell me what you see and I’ll give the exact next step
On one workstation, in `AMiOffice.exe` → **Properties**:
- Do you see an **Unblock** option (yes/no)?
- On the **Digital Signatures** tab, is it empty? (it will be empty if not signed)

With those two answers, I’ll tell you the quickest path to zero prompts for your exact case.

## @testsubject117

no unblock and no digital signatures tab

## Copilot

That means the EXE isn’t “MOTW-blocked”, and it’s also **unsigned**. In that situation, SmartScreen is warning because the app has **no publisher trust/reputation**. On standalone PCs, the practical ways to stop the dialog are:

## Best fix (recommended): code-sign the EXE
1) Buy a **code signing certificate** (EV is most effective at eliminating SmartScreen warnings quickly).
2) Sign the published `AMiOffice.exe` (and optionally your `.dll`s).
3) Redistribute the signed build — SmartScreen prompts should stop once Windows trusts the signature (EV usually helps immediately; standard cert can still require some reputation buildup).

If you want, tell me whether you can purchase a cert, and I’ll give you the exact `signtool.exe` command and where to hook it into your publish.

## If you can’t buy a cert: local allow-list per workstation
This doesn’t require a cert, but it’s manual on every PC:

### A) Windows Security “Reputation-based protection”
Some PCs allow turning off the specific SmartScreen check:
- Windows Security → App & browser control → Reputation-based protection settings
- turn off “Check apps and files” (or similar)

Downside: reduces security and must be done on every workstation (and users may not have admin rights).

### B) “Run anyway” once per new build
Even with no cert, SmartScreen often stops warning for the *same exact* binary after a user approves it—but it can reappear when you rebuild/publish (hash changes).

## Also: keep the EXE stable between builds
SmartScreen reputation is per binary hash/publisher. If you rebuild constantly, it never builds reputation. So:
- Publish less frequently, or
- Sign it (best).

## Two questions so I can recommend the path
1) Do you have an admin password for these standalone PCs (can you change Windows Security settings)?
2) Are you willing/able to purchase a code-signing certificate (roughly yearly cost), or do you need a free/manual workaround?

## @testsubject117

I have admin access. How much is a code-signing certificate per year

## Copilot

Typical cost is **hundreds of dollars per year**:

- **Standard code-signing certificate:** commonly about **$200–$500/year**.
- **EV (Extended Validation) code-signing certificate:** commonly about **$500–$1,000+/year**.

The exact price depends on vendor (DigiCert, Sectigo, GlobalSign, SSL.com, etc.), term length, and whether you buy multi‑year (often discounted per year).

Practical guidance for your situation (standalone PCs, small internal deployment):

- If you want SmartScreen warnings to go away **reliably and professionally**, especially on machines you don’t control forever, **EV code signing** is the most effective.
- If this app is only for your own PCs and you control them as admin, you can avoid the cost by using local policy/settings changes—but that reduces security and is per‑PC.

If you want, I can outline the “admin-only, no-certificate” approach that minimizes impact (e.g., allowing just this app path) and compare it to code signing.

## @testsubject117

that's way too expensive. let's implement the changes in windows security settings

## Copilot

You can do it, but the only built-in way that consistently stops the SmartScreen “Windows protected your PC” prompt for **unsigned** apps is to **turn off SmartScreen app checking** (system-wide) on each workstation. There isn’t a “trust just this one EXE” switch in Windows Security for SmartScreen.

## Change Windows Security settings (per workstation)

1) Open **Windows Security**
   - Start → type **Windows Security** → open it
2) Go to **App & browser control**
3) Click **Reputation-based protection settings**
4) Turn off (names vary slightly by Windows version):
   - **Check apps and files** → **Off**  *(this is the SmartScreen prompt you’re seeing)*

Optional (only if you’re still getting prompts in other contexts):
- **SmartScreen for Microsoft Edge** (not relevant if you’re just running an EXE)
- **Potentially unwanted app blocking** (doesn’t usually cause “protected your PC”)

After changing it, run `AMiOffice.exe` again.

## Important notes
- This reduces protection against unknown/untrusted apps **for everything on that PC**, not just AMiOffice.
- You’ll likely need to do this on **each workstation** (unless you later manage them centrally).

## Alternative (safer) admin workaround if you don’t want to disable SmartScreen
If the prompt is only happening because you run from a shared folder, a safer approach is:
- copy the app to `C:\Apps\AMiOffice\` and run locally (you already do this)
- keep the app in a fixed folder and restrict who can write to it

But if you’re seeing the *SmartScreen protected your PC* prompt even running locally, disabling “Check apps and files” is the lever.

## Tell me your Windows version
Which are these machines running: **Windows 10** or **Windows 11**?  
(The menu labels are a bit different; I can give the exact clicks for your version.)

## @testsubject117

ok, that fixed it, what next?

## Copilot

Next, lock in a **repeatable release + rollout process** so updates are consistent across all workstations.

## 1) Make a standard “Release package” folder each time
On your dev PC:
- Publish **Release / self-contained / win-x64** to a versioned folder, e.g.
  - `C:\Publish\AMiOffice\2026-04-13_01\`
- Zip that folder to a single file:
  - `AMiOffice_2026-04-13_01.zip`

This gives you a single artifact you can copy/store.

## 2) Rollout steps on each workstation (consistent)
1) Close AMiOffice (make sure it’s not running in Task Manager).
2) Rename the old folder (easy rollback):
   - `C:\Apps\AMiOffice` → `C:\Apps\AMiOffice_old`
3) Create fresh:
   - `C:\Apps\AMiOffice`
4) Copy in the new published files (or unzip).
5) Run `C:\Apps\AMiOffice\AMiOffice.exe`
6) Quick test:
   - press **I**
   - confirm new `WORD_*.zip` + next `BACKUP#.zip`

Rollback = rename folders back.

## 3) Optional but highly recommended next steps
Pick any you want:

A) **Add an “About / Version” screen** (shows build date/time + machine name).  
This prevents “am I on the latest build?” confusion.

B) **Centralize updates**: a simple `Update.bat` on each workstation that copies from `\\invoice\MainMenu\Deploy\Latest\` to `C:\Apps\AMiOffice\`.

C) **Logging**: write a small log file in `\\invoice\MainMenu\Logs\AMiOffice.log` when backups run (timestamp, machine, success/fail).

Which one do you want next: **A (version display)**, **B (update script)**, or **C (logging)**?

## @testsubject117

A) yes add an about / version screen

## Copilot

Add an **About** dialog that shows: app name, version, build date (from the assembly), and machine/user. Then hook it to a key (commonly **A** or **F1**), or add a visible “About” menu item if you have one.

### 1) Create a new form: `FormAbout.vb`
Add a WinForms form named `FormAbout` with:
- a `Label` named `lblInfo` (set `AutoSize = False`, `Dock = Fill`, `Font = Consolas 10` if you like)
- an `OK` button (DialogResult = OK)

Then paste this code:

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Reflection

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim ver = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        Dim buildDate As DateTime? = Nothing
        Try
            buildDate = System.IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location)
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            $"App: {Application.ProductName}",
            $"Version: {ver}",
            $"Build (file time): {If(buildDate.HasValue, buildDate.Value.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss"), "unknown")}",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {System.Runtime.InteropServices.RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}"
        }

        lblInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)
    End Sub

End Class
```

### 2) Hook it into your main menu (example: key “A”)
In `FormMainMenu.vb` inside your key handler `Select Case up`, add:

```vbnet
Case "A"
    Using f As New FormAbout()
        f.ShowDialog(Me)
    End Using
```

If “A” is already used, tell me which keys are free and I’ll suggest one.

### 3) (Optional) Make the “Version” more meaningful
Right now it uses the assembly version. If you want it to show `1.0.0` → `1.0.1`, set it in:
- Project → Properties → **Application** → **Assembly Information…**

---

If you paste your current `Select Case up` block (the list of keys), I’ll pick the safest unused key and show the exact insertion point. If you want the About screen to also show your `\\invoice\...` paths and whether the share is reachable, say so and I’ll add those lines too.

## @testsubject117

how do I create a new winform

## Copilot

In Visual Studio you add a WinForms form like any other item.

## Create a new WinForm (VB)
1) In **Solution Explorer**, right‑click your project **AMiOffice**.
2) Click **Add** → **New Item…**
3) In the list, choose **Windows Form** (sometimes shown as **Windows Form (Visual Basic)**).
4) Name it: `FormAbout.vb`
5) Click **Add**

Visual Studio will create the form designer file and open the designer.

## Add the controls
1) Open the designer for `FormAbout`.
2) From **Toolbox**, drag:
   - a **Label** onto the form
   - a **Button** onto the form
3) Set properties (click the control, press **F4**):
   - Label:
     - `Name` = `lblInfo`
     - `Dock` = `Fill` (or size it manually)
     - `AutoSize` = `False`
   - Button:
     - `Text` = `OK`
     - `DialogResult` = `OK`
     - (optional) `Anchor` = Bottom, Right
4) For the form itself:
   - `Text` = `About AMiOffice`
   - `StartPosition` = `CenterParent`
   - `AcceptButton` = your OK button

## Wire the OK button (if needed)
If you set `DialogResult = OK`, you usually don’t need any code—clicking it will close the dialog automatically.

If you don’t see “Windows Form” as an option in **Add → New Item**, tell me what templates you *do* see and whether this is a VB WinForms project targeting `net10.0-windows`.

Case "A"

    End Using
```

Run again and press **A**—if the breakpoint doesn’t hit, that key is being handled elsewhere or the form doesn’t have focus.

- In `FormMainMenu_Load` (temporarily), add:

f.ShowDialog(Me)
```

Run—if the About dialog appears at startup, the form is fine and the issue is just the hotkey wiring. Remove those two lines afterward.

If you paste your `Select Case up` section (or tell me which key you used), I can tell you exactly where to put the breakpoint and what to check.

## Copilot

Yes—very likely. In your earlier menu layout you already had **A) Add New Customer** (or similar), so `Case "A"` is probably taken.

Use a key that isn’t part of the lettered menu options. Two good choices:

- **F1** for About/Help (common convention)
- **?** (Shift+/) for About/Help

### Recommended: use **F1**
In your key handler, add:

```vbnet
Case "F1"
    Using f As New FormAbout()
        f.ShowDialog(Me)
    End Using
```

Then run the app and press **F1**.

### If your handler only normalizes A–Z
If you currently do something like `up = key.ToUpper()` and only switch on letters, you may need to handle function keys separately. If you paste the start of your key handler (the part that reads the key), I’ll show the exact way to detect `Keys.F1` in VB WinForms.

## @testsubject117

I included the ? on the screen so users know it exists. I want to include version and build number as well as shared paths, Author, date, year and email address/contact info

## Copilot

Update `FormAbout_Load` to build a richer text block: **version + build number**, **author/contact**, **year/date**, and your **shared paths**. Here’s a drop-in version.

```vbnet name=FormAbout.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    ' TODO: customize these
    Private Const AuthorName As String = "YOUR NAME"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "you@example.com"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        ' "Build number" options:
        ' - File version (good if you set it in Assembly Information)
        ' - Informational version (good if you set it manually)
        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        ' Build date: last write time of the EXE on disk (simple + accurate for deployed build)
        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            $"Build Date (file time): {buildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author: {AuthorName}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        lblInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)
    End Sub

End Class
```

### Notes / what you need to fill in
- Replace:
  - `AuthorName`
  - `ContactEmail`
  - (optional) `CopyrightYear`

### About “version” vs “build number”
- `Version:` comes from the assembly version.
- `File Version:` and `Build:` come from attributes you set in **Project → Properties → Application → Assembly Information…**
  - If you want a single “build number” that you manually bump (e.g. `2026.04.13.1`), set **File version** or **Informational version** and display that.

If you tell me what exact format you want (example: `1.2.3` + `Build 2026-04-13.01`), I’ll tailor the labels so it looks clean and consistent.

## @testsubject117

I want the private const AppTitle to be in larger bold letters

## Copilot

To make just the title larger/bold, switch from a single `Label` (`lblInfo`) to a simple two-control layout:

- **Label** at top for the title (big/bold)
- **TextBox** (multiline, read-only) below for the details

### 1) Designer changes (FormAbout)
Add/adjust these controls:

- Add a new label: `lblTitle`
  - `Dock = Top`
  - `AutoSize = False`
  - `Height = 40` (adjust to taste)
  - `TextAlign = MiddleCenter`
  - `Font = Microsoft Sans Serif, 14pt, **Bold** (or similar)

- Change your existing `lblInfo` to a **TextBox** (recommended) OR keep label:
  - Recommended: replace with `txtInfo` TextBox
    - `Multiline = True`
    - `ReadOnly = True`
    - `ScrollBars = Vertical`
    - `Dock = Fill`
    - `Font = Consolas, 10pt`

(You can also keep `lblInfo` as a label, but large blocks of text behave better in a multiline TextBox.)

### 2) Code changes (`FormAbout.vb`)
Use `lblTitle` for the big title, and put the rest in `txtInfo`.

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Const AppTitle As String = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"

    ' TODO: customize these
    Private Const AuthorName As String = "YOUR NAME"
    Private Const DeveloperCompany As String = "YOUR COMPANY NAME"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "you@example.com"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        lblTitle.Text = AppTitle

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            $"Build Date (file time): {buildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author: {AuthorName}",
            $"Developer: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        txtInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)
    End Sub

End Class
```

### If you don’t want to add new controls
You *can’t* make only part of a standard WinForms `Label` bold/large unless you use a `RichTextBox` (or custom drawing). The two-control approach above is the simplest.

If you tell me whether you want `lblInfo` to stay a Label or you’re OK switching to `TextBox`/`RichTextBox`, I’ll tailor the exact steps.

## @testsubject117

switch to richtextbox

## Copilot

Do this with **two controls**: a big bold `lblTitle` at the top, and a `RichTextBox` (`rtbInfo`) filling the rest.

## 1) Designer changes (FormAbout)
1) Open `FormAbout` in the designer.
2) Add a **Label**:
   - `Name`: `lblTitle`
   - `Dock`: `Top`
   - `Height`: `44` (adjust)
   - `TextAlign`: `MiddleCenter`
   - `Font`: set to something like **Segoe UI, 14pt, Bold**
3) Add a **RichTextBox**:
   - `Name`: `rtbInfo`
   - `Dock`: `Fill`
   - `ReadOnly`: `True`
   - `BorderStyle`: `FixedSingle` (optional)
   - `Font`: `Consolas, 10pt` (optional)
4) If you still have `lblInfo`, delete it (or you can keep it, but you won’t use it).

## 2) Update `FormAbout.vb`
Replace your current `FormAbout_Load` code with this (keeps your constants and adds the company line):

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Const AppTitle As String = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"

    ' TODO: customize these
    Private Const AuthorName As String = "YOUR NAME"
    Private Const DeveloperCompany As String = "YOUR COMPANY NAME"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "you@example.com"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        lblTitle.Text = AppTitle

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            $"Build Date (file time): {buildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author: {AuthorName}",
            $"Developer: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)
        rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
    End Sub

End Class
```

## 3) Test
Run the app → press `?` → you should see:
- big bold title at top
- details in the RichTextBox with scrolling

If you want the **title inside the RichTextBox** (with mixed fonts in one control) instead of using a label, tell me and I’ll give you the RichTextBox formatting code (bold/size for first line, normal for the rest).

## @testsubject117

does this replace the label already on FormAbout.vb

## Copilot

No—this **adds a new label** (`lblTitle`) and **replaces the old details label** (`lblInfo`) with a **RichTextBox** (`rtbInfo`).

So the final setup is:

- **Keep/add:** `lblTitle` (new, big/bold title at the top)
- **Replace/remove:** `lblInfo` (the old big text label you were using for everything)
- **Add:** `rtbInfo` (RichTextBox for the details)

If you already have a label on the form now:
- If it’s your existing `lblInfo`, **delete it** (or rename it to `lblTitle` and change its properties to be the title label).
- The details should go into `rtbInfo`, not a label.

Quick easiest path if you don’t want extra controls:
- **Rename your existing label** to `lblTitle`
- Add the RichTextBox `rtbInfo` and set it to Dock=Fill

Then the code I gave will work unchanged.

## @testsubject117

this works but there is no ok button

## Copilot

Add an OK button by docking it at the bottom (or putting it in a bottom panel) so it doesn’t get covered by the `Dock=Fill` RichTextBox.

## Easiest designer fix (recommended)
1) In `FormAbout` designer, add a **Panel**:
   - `Name` = `pnlButtons`
   - `Dock` = `Bottom`
   - `Height` = `45`

2) Add a **Button** inside that panel:
   - `Name` = `btnOK`
   - `Text` = `OK`
   - `DialogResult` = `OK`
   - `Anchor` = `Bottom, Right`
   - Place it near the bottom-right of the panel

3) Set form properties:
   - `AcceptButton` = `btnOK`
   - (optional) `CancelButton` = `btnOK`

Now clicking OK (or pressing Enter/Esc if you set CancelButton) will close the form automatically—no code needed.

## If you want a code-only close handler (optional)
Double-click the OK button in the designer and add:

```vbnet
Private Sub btnOK_Click(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles btnOK.Click
    Me.Close()
End Sub
```

If you tell me what your current control docking is (lblTitle top, rtbInfo fill), I can suggest the exact dock order too (WinForms dock order matters).

## @testsubject117

ok, fixed it. now I want all of the about info to be centered not left justified

## Copilot

Set the RichTextBox alignment to center **after** you set its text.

Add these lines at the end of your `FormAbout_Load` (right after `rtbInfo.Text = ...`):

```vbnet
' Center all text in the RichTextBox
rtbInfo.SelectAll()
rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
rtbInfo.DeselectAll()
```

So the tail of your load should look like:

```vbnet
rtbInfo.Clear()
rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

rtbInfo.SelectAll()
rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
```

Notes:
- This centers *all* lines. If you later want only certain sections centered, we can align specific ranges instead.
- Your title label is already centered via `TextAlign = MiddleCenter`, so this completes the centering for the details area.

## @testsubject117

The main title is covering the rest of the info

## Copilot

That’s a **WinForms docking order** issue: a `Dock=Fill` control (your RichTextBox) is being laid out before the `Dock=Top` title label, so it occupies the whole client area and the label ends up overlapping it.

Fix it by ensuring the controls are added in this order (top/bottom first, fill last), or by reordering in the designer.

## Fix in the Designer (fastest)
1) Open `FormAbout` in **Designer**.
2) Go to **View → Other Windows → Document Outline**.
3) In the outline, reorder the controls so they are like:

- `lblTitle` (Dock = Top)
- `pnlButtons` (Dock = Bottom)  *(if you added it)*
- `rtbInfo` (Dock = Fill)

In WinForms, the **Fill control must be last** (lowest in the outline / added last), otherwise it can cover others.

### How to reorder
In Document Outline, you can drag items up/down. If it won’t drag, use:
- Right‑click a control → **Bring to Front / Send to Back** until:
  - `rtbInfo` is **Sent to Back** (or `lblTitle` is brought to front)

## Quick property check
- `lblTitle.Dock = Top`
- `pnlButtons.Dock = Bottom`
- `rtbInfo.Dock = Fill`

After reordering, run again—the title should sit above the RichTextBox without covering it.

If you want, paste your `FormAbout.Designer.vb` section that adds these controls (the `Controls.Add(...)` lines) and I’ll tell you the exact order it should be in.

## @testsubject117

it's still overlapping. how can we add a blank line so Private Const Authorname As String moves down?

## Copilot

Add vertical space by inserting an empty line **at the top of the RichTextBox content** (and/or padding the RichTextBox). The overlap won’t be fixed by text, but this *will* push the “Author:” line down visually.

### Option A (simplest): add a blank line at the start of `lines`
In your `lines` list, insert `""` as the first entry (or two of them):

```vbnet
Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
    "",   ' blank line
    "",   ' optional second blank line
    $"{product}",
    $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
    ...
}
```

### Option B: add top padding inside the RichTextBox
This adds space above the first line without changing the text:

```vbnet
rtbInfo.Select(0, 0)
rtbInfo.SelectionIndent = 0
rtbInfo.SelectionRightIndent = 0
rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
rtbInfo.SelectionFont = rtbInfo.Font

' Add space by increasing the paragraph spacing
rtbInfo.SelectionCharOffset = 0
' (RichTextBox doesn't have true padding; the reliable way is blank lines or adjusting margins)
```

WinForms `RichTextBox` doesn’t have a real `Padding` property, so **blank lines are the most reliable**.

### But: overlapping controls should still be fixed
If `lblTitle` is truly overlapping the RichTextBox, the correct fix is still docking/order or layout. One more thing to check in the designer:

- Make sure `lblTitle.AutoSize = False` and it has a fixed `Height` (like 44)
- Make sure `rtbInfo.Location.Y` is *below* the label at runtime (it should be automatic when docking is correct)

If you paste the `Controls.Add(...)` section from `FormAbout.Designer.vb`, I can point out exactly why it’s overlapping and what to change (it’s usually just the add order).

## @testsubject117

I want to add a picture next to developer name

## Copilot

To put a picture next to the developer name, don’t try to embed it in the RichTextBox. Add a **PictureBox** beside your “Author/Developer / Company / Phone / Email” section.

### 1) Add the image to the project resources
1) **Project → Properties → Resources**
2) If prompted, click **Create** resources file.
3) **Add Resource → Add Existing File…**
4) Select your image (PNG/JPG). Name it something like `DeveloperPhoto`.

### 2) Add a PictureBox on the About form
In the `FormAbout` designer:
1) Drag a **Panel** onto the form, name it `pnlHeader`
   - `Dock = Top`
   - `Height = 120` (adjust)
2) Move your existing `lblTitle` above it (keep `Dock=Top`), then place `pnlHeader` under it (also `Dock=Top`).
3) Inside `pnlHeader`, add:
   - **PictureBox** named `picDeveloper`
     - `SizeMode = Zoom`
     - `Dock = Left`
     - `Width = 96` (adjust)
   - **Label** named `lblDev`
     - `Dock = Fill`
     - `TextAlign = MiddleLeft`
     - `Font = Segoe UI, 10pt` (or whatever)

Keep `rtbInfo.Dock = Fill` below those top panels.

### 3) Set the picture + developer text in code
In `FormAbout_Load`, add:

```vbnet
picDeveloper.Image = My.Resources.DeveloperPhoto
lblDev.Text =
    $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}{Environment.NewLine}" &
    $"Company: {CompanyName}{Environment.NewLine}" &
    $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}{Environment.NewLine}" &
    $"Email: {ContactEmail}"
```

Then **remove** those four lines from the RichTextBox `lines` list (so you don’t show them twice). Leave the rest (version/build info, machine info, shared paths) in `rtbInfo`.

## @testsubject117

that isn't working. resources was moved in vb 2026 and doesn't have add resource

## Copilot

You don’t need the Resources designer at all. The most reliable approach in current Visual Studio is: **add the image file to your project** and load it at runtime.

## Option 1 (recommended): ship the image as “Content” and load from disk
### 1) Add the image to the project
1) In **Solution Explorer**: right‑click the project → **Add** → **Existing Item…**
2) Pick your file (e.g. `developer.png`)
3) Click the image in Solution Explorer → press **F4** (Properties):
   - **Build Action** = `Content`
   - **Copy to Output Directory** = `Copy if newer`

This will copy `developer.png` next to your EXE when you publish/copy files.

### 2) Add a PictureBox in the designer
Add a `PictureBox` named `picDeveloper` where you want it and set:
- `SizeMode = Zoom`

### 3) Load it in `FormAbout_Load`
Add:

```vbnet
Dim imgPath = Path.Combine(AppDomain.CurrentDomain.BaseDirectory, "developer.png")
If File.Exists(imgPath) Then
    picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromFile(imgPath)
Else
    picDeveloper.Visible = False
End If
```

## Option 2: embed the image as an Embedded Resource (no external file)
If you prefer it compiled into the exe:

1) Add the image to the project (**Add → Existing Item…**)
2) Select the image → Properties:
   - **Build Action** = `Embedded Resource`
3) Load it like this (update the resource name to match your project/default namespace + folders + filename):

```vbnet
Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
Dim resName = "AMiOffice.developer.png" ' adjust to match your project namespace + filename

Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
    If s IsNot Nothing Then
        picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(s)
    Else
        picDeveloper.Visible = False
    End If
End Using
```

To find the exact `resName`, I can give you a small snippet that lists all resource names at runtime and you just copy/paste the correct one.

---

Two quick questions so I can tailor the exact steps:
1) Do you want the image to be a **separate file next to the EXE** (Option 1) or **embedded into the EXE** (Option 2)?
2) What’s your project’s name/default namespace (shown in Project Properties)?

## @testsubject117

embedded in exe

## Copilot

Embed it as an **Embedded Resource** and load it into a `PictureBox` via `GetManifestResourceStream`.

## 1) Add the image and mark it Embedded Resource
1) **Solution Explorer → right‑click project → Add → Existing Item…** (pick `developer.png`)
2) Click `developer.png` in Solution Explorer → **Properties**:
   - **Build Action** = `Embedded Resource`
   - **Copy to Output Directory** = `Do not copy`

## 2) Add a PictureBox to the About form
In the designer add a `PictureBox` named `picDeveloper` and set:
- `SizeMode = Zoom`

## 3) Load the embedded image (robust way: auto-find the resource name)
Put this into `FormAbout.vb` (inside the class). It finds the resource by filename so you don’t have to guess the namespace:

```vbnet
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
' (you already import these)

Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
    Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
    Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

    ' Find any embedded resource that ends with "\developer.png" equivalent (resource names use dots)
    Dim resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".developer.png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
               OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".developer.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))

    If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(resName) Then
        picDeveloper.Visible = False
        Return
    End If

    Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
        If s Is Nothing Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        ' Copy to memory so the stream can be disposed safely
        Using ms As New MemoryStream()
            s.CopyTo(ms)
            ms.Position = 0
            picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
        End Using
    End Using
End Sub
```

Then call it at the top of `FormAbout_Load`:

```vbnet
LoadDeveloperImage()
```

## 4) If it still doesn’t show
Temporarily debug-print the embedded resource names:

```vbnet
Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, asm.GetManifestResourceNames())
```

If you paste the output lines, I’ll tell you exactly what the correct resource name is (and we can hardcode it if you prefer).

## @testsubject117

where do I place Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()

## Copilot

Place `Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()` **inside the `Public Class FormAbout` block**, but **outside** of `FormAbout_Load` (i.e., as a separate method at class level).

Example layout:

```vbnet
Public Class FormAbout

    ' constants go here...

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        LoadDeveloperImage()
        ' the rest of your About loading code...
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        ' image loading code here...
    End Sub

End Class
```

So: **below** your constants is fine, and either **above or below** `FormAbout_Load` is fine—just not nested inside another Sub.

Imports System.Reflection

        Catch

        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch

        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            "",   ' blank line
            "",   ' optional second blank line
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            "",
            $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}",
            $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}",

            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

        rtbInfo.SelectAll()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
        rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
    End Sub
    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

        ' Find any embedded resource that ends with "\developer.png" equivalent (resource names use dots)
        Dim resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
                   OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))

        If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(resName) Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
            If s Is Nothing Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            ' Copy to memory so the stream can be disposed safely

                s.CopyTo(ms)

## Copilot

The image isn’t loading for two reasons:

1) **`LoadDeveloperImage()` is never called** in `FormAbout_Load`.
2) Embedded resource names **don’t contain spaces** the way you’re checking. In .NET, the resource name will look like `YourDefaultNamespace.ESC_Logo.jpg` or `YourDefaultNamespace.Images.ESC_Logo.jpg` (spaces typically become `_`, and folders become extra `.parts`).

Here’s the smallest set of changes to make it work reliably.

## 1) Call `LoadDeveloperImage()` from `FormAbout_Load`
Put this near the top of `FormAbout_Load`:

```vbnet
LoadDeveloperImage()
```

## 2) Fix `LoadDeveloperImage()` to search by filename without assuming spaces
Replace your current `LoadDeveloperImage()` with this version:

```vbnet
Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
    Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
    Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

    ' Match by filename, ignoring namespace/folders. Resource names use dots, and spaces are usually '_' in VB projects.
    Dim resName =
        names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
        OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESCLogo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
        OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC.Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
        OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.ToLowerInvariant().Contains("esc") AndAlso n.ToLowerInvariant().EndsWith(".jpg"))

    If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(resName) Then
        picDeveloper.Visible = False
        Return
    End If

    Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
        If s Is Nothing Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        Using ms As New MemoryStream()
            s.CopyTo(ms)
            ms.Position = 0
            picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
        End Using
    End Using
End Sub
```

## 3) One-time check: verify the actual embedded resource name
If it still doesn’t show, temporarily add this at the end of `FormAbout_Load` (just for troubleshooting):

```vbnet
rtbInfo.AppendText(Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine & "Embedded resources:" & Environment.NewLine &
                   String.Join(Environment.NewLine, Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetManifestResourceNames()))
```

Run the app, open About, and you’ll see the exact resource name. Paste that line here and I’ll give you the clean “hardcoded” `resName` that will always work.

### Also confirm the file’s Build Action
In Solution Explorer click `ESC Logo.jpg` → Properties:
- **Build Action** must be **Embedded Resource**.

Once the picture loads, set `picDeveloper.SizeMode = Zoom` so it scales nicely.

## @testsubject117

where do I find FormAbout_Load

## Copilot

`FormAbout_Load` is the **Load event handler** for the About form. You can find it in any of these ways:

### Option 1 (fastest): use the dropdowns above the code editor
1) Open **FormAbout.vb** (the code view, not the designer).
2) At the top of the editor there are two dropdowns:
   - Left dropdown: pick **FormAbout**
   - Right dropdown: pick **Load**
3) Visual Studio will jump you to `Private Sub FormAbout_Load(...) Handles MyBase.Load`.

### Option 2: search in the file
In **FormAbout.vb**, press **Ctrl+F** and search for:
- `FormAbout_Load`
or
- `Handles MyBase.Load`

In the code you pasted earlier, it’s already there:

```vbnet
Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    ' ...
End Sub
```

### Option 3: from the designer (events)
1) Open **FormAbout** in the designer.
2) Click the blank form background (so the form itself is selected).
3) In the **Properties** window, click the **lightning bolt** (Events).
4) Find **Load** and double‑click it to generate/jump to `FormAbout_Load`.

If you don’t see any `Handles MyBase.Load` in your file, tell me what you *do* see at the top of the class (any Subs with `Handles ...`) and I’ll point you to the right place.

## @testsubject117

repasting FormAbout.vb what do I need to fix to get the developer picture to work

## Copilot

Fix these 3 things in your `FormAbout.vb`:

1) **Call** `LoadDeveloperImage()` inside `FormAbout_Load` (right after you set the title is fine).
2) **Search for the resource name correctly** (embedded resource names won’t contain spaces like `"ESC Logo.jpg"`).
3) Add `Imports System.Linq` **or** avoid `FirstOrDefault` (because it’s a LINQ extension method).

Below is your code with the minimal edits (keep everything else as-is).

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices
Imports System.Linq   ' <-- ADD THIS so FirstOrDefault works

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Const AppTitle As String = "About Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"

    ' TODO: customize these
    Private Const AuthorName As String = "Kirk Saffell"
    Private Const DeveloperCompany As String = "Enterprise Services & Consulting"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "capnkirk@capnkirk.com"
    Private Const AuthorPhone As String = "(661) 478-0990"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        lblTitle.Text = AppTitle

        LoadDeveloperImage() ' <-- ADD THIS (you weren't calling it)

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            "",   ' blank line
            "",   ' optional second blank line
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            "",
            $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}",
            $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

        rtbInfo.SelectAll()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
        rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

        rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

        ' Embedded resource names are like: DefaultNamespace.(Folders).ESC_Logo.jpg
        ' Spaces typically become underscores.
        Dim resName =
            names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
            OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESCLogo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
            OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.ToLowerInvariant().Contains("esc") AndAlso n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))

        If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(resName) Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
            If s Is Nothing Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                s.CopyTo(ms)
                ms.Position = 0
                picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
            End Using
        End Using

        picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
        picDeveloper.Visible = True
    End Sub

End Class
```

### One required project setting (outside this file)
In Solution Explorer, click **ESC Logo.jpg** → Properties:
- **Build Action** = **Embedded Resource**

### If it still doesn’t show
Add this temporarily right after `LoadDeveloperImage()` to print the actual embedded resource names:

```vbnet
rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetManifestResourceNames())
```

Open About and paste the resource name(s) you see—I’ll tell you exactly what to match (then you can remove the debug line).

## @testsubject117

how do I search for embedded resource

## Copilot

You can “search” for an embedded resource in two practical ways: **(A) in Visual Studio (file + properties)** and **(B) at runtime (list the resource names)**. The runtime method is the most reliable because it shows the *exact* name your EXE uses.

## A) In Visual Studio (confirm it’s embedded)
1) In **Solution Explorer**, find `ESC Logo.jpg`.
2) Click it and press **F4** (Properties).
3) Verify:
   - **Build Action** = `Embedded Resource`

That confirms it will be embedded, but it still doesn’t tell you the *manifest resource name*.

## B) At runtime (list all embedded resource names)
Temporarily add this code anywhere (best: at the end of `FormAbout_Load`) and run the app:

```vbnet
Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

rtbInfo.Text = "Embedded resources:" & Environment.NewLine &
               String.Join(Environment.NewLine, names)
```

Open your About box and you’ll see a list like:

- `YourProjectNamespace.ESC_Logo.jpg`
- or `YourProjectNamespace.Images.ESC_Logo.jpg`

That exact line is what you should use with `GetManifestResourceStream(...)`.

### Alternative: show it in a MessageBox
If you don’t want it in the RichTextBox:

```vbnet
MessageBox.Show(String.Join(Environment.NewLine, Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetManifestResourceNames()))
```

## What to do next
Paste the exact resource name line that corresponds to your logo (the one ending in `.jpg`), and I’ll give you the precise `resName = "..."` code to load it (no guessing, no underscores/spaces issues).

## @testsubject117

it won't open the about box now

## Copilot

That usually happens because the new resource-debug or image-load code is throwing an exception during `FormAbout_Load` (so the form fails while loading). The quickest fix is to make `LoadDeveloperImage()` **exception-safe** and (for now) avoid writing to `rtbInfo` before it’s ready.

Make these changes:

1) **Wrap `LoadDeveloperImage()` in `Try/Catch`** so it can’t crash the form.
2) Ensure `LoadDeveloperImage()` is called **after** `InitializeComponent()` (it is) and that `picDeveloper` exists on the form.

Replace your `LoadDeveloperImage()` with this safe version:

```vbnet
Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
    Try
        If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

        ' Look for your logo by filename (spaces usually become underscores)
        Dim resName =
            names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
            OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC.Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
            OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.ToLowerInvariant().Contains("esc") AndAlso n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))

        If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(resName) Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
            If s Is Nothing Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                s.CopyTo(ms)
                ms.Position = 0
                picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
            End Using
        End Using

        picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
        picDeveloper.Visible = True

    Catch ex As Exception
        ' Don't block the About box from opening
        If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        ' Optional: uncomment to see the real error while debugging
        ' MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")
    End Try
End Sub
```

Also, make sure you have this import at the top (or fully-qualify `FirstOrDefault` logic another way):

```vbnet
Imports System.Linq
```

### Now: find the real error
If it still won’t open, temporarily enable the MessageBox in the catch:

```vbnet
MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")
```

Run again and tell me what the error message says (copy/paste). That will pinpoint whether it’s:
- `picDeveloper` doesn’t exist (name mismatch),
- `FirstOrDefault` / LINQ not available,
- bad image stream,
- or something else in `FormAbout_Load`.

If you can, also confirm: in the Designer, is the PictureBox **exactly** named `picDeveloper`? (Name property, not the text.)

## @testsubject117

where do I insert MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")

## Copilot

Put it **inside the `Catch ex As Exception` block** in your `LoadDeveloperImage()` method.

Here’s the exact spot (copy/paste this whole method so you don’t have to guess):

```vbnet
Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
    Try
        If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

        Dim resName =
            names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
            OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC.Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) _
            OrElse names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.ToLowerInvariant().Contains("esc") AndAlso n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))

        If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(resName) Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
            If s Is Nothing Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                s.CopyTo(ms)
                ms.Position = 0
                picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
            End Using
        End Using

        picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
        picDeveloper.Visible = True

    Catch ex As Exception
        MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")  ' <-- INSERT/KEEP THIS LINE HERE
        If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
    End Try
End Sub
```

Run the app and open the About box—if something is failing, that message box will show the exact exception text. Paste the exception text here and I’ll tell you the precise fix.

## @testsubject117

can't copy the error text

## Copilot

Make the error easy to capture by showing it in a selectable textbox (or copying it to the clipboard) instead of a MessageBox.

## Option A (best): copy error to clipboard + show a short message
Replace your `Catch` block with this:

```vbnet
Catch ex As Exception
    Try
        Clipboard.SetText(ex.ToString())
    Catch
    End Try

    MessageBox.Show("LoadDeveloperImage failed. The full error was copied to your clipboard. Paste it here.",
                    "LoadDeveloperImage error")
    If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
End Try
```

Now run again, open About, then **Ctrl+V** here (or into Notepad) to paste the full error.

## Option B: show the error inside your RichTextBox (so you can select/copy)
Use this `Catch` instead:

```vbnet
Catch ex As Exception
    If rtbInfo IsNot Nothing Then
        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = ex.ToString()
    End If
    If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
End Try
```

Then you can click inside `rtbInfo`, **Ctrl+A**, **Ctrl+C**.

---

Also: if the About box “won’t open”, it may be throwing before `rtbInfo` is usable—so **Option A (Clipboard)** is the most reliable. After you paste the exception text, I’ll pinpoint the exact line and fix.

## @testsubject117

here is the full error

## Copilot

Paste the error text itself (it looks like it didn’t come through).  

If you can’t paste it, use this approach so you can capture it as a file:

## Write the exception to a text file on the Desktop
Replace your `Catch` block with:

```vbnet
Catch ex As Exception
    Try
        Dim p = Path.Combine(Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.DesktopDirectory),
                             "AboutBoxError.txt")
        File.WriteAllText(p, ex.ToString())
        MessageBox.Show("LoadDeveloperImage failed. Error written to Desktop\AboutBoxError.txt",
                        "LoadDeveloperImage error")
    Catch
        MessageBox.Show("LoadDeveloperImage failed, and writing AboutBoxError.txt also failed.",
                        "LoadDeveloperImage error")
    End Try

    If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
End Try
```

Run the app → open About → then open **Desktop\AboutBoxError.txt** and paste its contents here.

### While you’re at it (common cause)
Also confirm these two things (they’re the #1 causes):
1) In the designer, is the PictureBox **Name** exactly `picDeveloper`?
2) In Solution Explorer, click `ESC Logo.jpg` → Properties → is **Build Action = Embedded Resource**?

## @testsubject117

System.InvalidCastException: Conversion from string "" to type 'Boolean' is not valid.
 ---> System.FormatException: The input string '' was not in a correct format.
   at Microsoft.VisualBasic.CompilerServices.Conversions.ParseDouble(String Value, NumberFormatInfo NumberFormat)
   at Microsoft.VisualBasic.CompilerServices.Conversions.ToBoolean(String Value)
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at Microsoft.VisualBasic.CompilerServices.Conversions.ToBoolean(String Value)
   at AMiOffice.FormAbout.LoadDeveloperImage() in C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\FormAbout.vb:line 102

## Copilot

The crash is coming from **VB implicitly converting a String to Boolean** inside `LoadDeveloperImage()` (because `Option Strict Off` allows it). This almost always happens when code like this is used:

```vbnet
Dim resName = a OrElse b
```

where `a`/`b` are **strings**. `OrElse` is a Boolean operator, so VB tries to convert the string to Boolean → `""` can’t convert → `InvalidCastException`.

### Fix: don’t use `OrElse` to combine strings
Use an `If` chain (or `If(resName Is Nothing, ...)`) to pick the first match.

Also, **call `LoadDeveloperImage()`** from `FormAbout_Load` if you haven’t already.

Here’s a corrected `LoadDeveloperImage()` (no `OrElse` on strings):

```vbnet
Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
    Try
        If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

        Dim resName As String = Nothing

        resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC.Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                     n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If

        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
            picDeveloper.Visible = False
            Return
        End If

        Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
            If s Is Nothing Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                s.CopyTo(ms)
                ms.Position = 0
                picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
            End Using
        End Using

        picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
        picDeveloper.Visible = True

    Catch
        If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
    End Try
End Sub
```

And make sure you have:

```vbnet
Imports System.Linq
```

### One more key point: your file has a space
Embedded resource names usually turn spaces into `_`, so the likely resource is `...ESC_Logo.jpg`. Your image file is **`ESC Logo.jpg`**, so that’s why we check for `.ESC_Logo.jpg`.

If this still doesn’t show the image, add this temporarily (once) to see the exact resource name:

```vbnet
MessageBox.Show(String.Join(Environment.NewLine, Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetManifestResourceNames()))
```

Then we can hardcode the exact `resName`.

If you want, paste lines ~90–110 of your file (around line 102) and I’ll point to the exact expression that triggered the bad string→boolean conversion.

## @testsubject117

I want the picture to be between Build: and Author/Developer:

## Copilot

To place the picture **between** the “Build:” line and the “Author/Developer:” line, you should **stop using a single RichTextBox for everything** and instead split the About box into two sections:

- **Top RichTextBox** (or Label) with: Product / Version / File Version / Build
- **Middle panel** with: PictureBox + Author/Developer info
- **Bottom RichTextBox** with: machine info + shared paths (or keep it all in one bottom RTB)

A `RichTextBox` can’t reliably “insert an image between two lines of plain text” unless you start doing RTF/clipboard hacks; layout controls is the correct WinForms way.

### Recommended layout (simple)
1) Keep `lblTitle` Dock=Top
2) Add `rtbTop` Dock=Top (height ~90)
3) Add `pnlDev` Dock=Top (height ~110) containing:
   - `picDeveloper` Dock=Left (width ~96)
   - `lblDevInfo` Dock=Fill (TextAlign=MiddleLeft)
4) Keep your existing `rtbInfo` Dock=Fill for the rest (machine/shared paths)

#### Designer steps
- Add a new **RichTextBox**: `rtbTop`  
  - `Dock = Top`, `ReadOnly=True`, `BorderStyle=None` (optional), `Height=90`
- Add a new **Panel**: `pnlDev`  
  - `Dock = Top`, `Height=110`
- Inside `pnlDev` add:
  - `PictureBox` `picDeveloper` (`Dock=Left`, `Width=96`, `SizeMode=Zoom`)
  - `Label` `lblDevInfo` (`Dock=Fill`, `TextAlign=MiddleLeft`)
- Ensure dock order in Document Outline is:
  1. `lblTitle` (Top)
  2. `rtbTop` (Top)
  3. `pnlDev` (Top)
  4. `pnlButtons` (Bottom, if you have it)
  5. `rtbInfo` (Fill)

### Code changes (what to put where)
In `FormAbout_Load` build the “top” lines separately:

```vbnet
Dim topLines As New List(Of String) From {
    product,
    $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
    $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
    $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}"
}

rtbTop.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, topLines)
rtbTop.SelectAll()
rtbTop.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
rtbTop.DeselectAll()
```

Then load the image + developer text in the middle:

```vbnet
LoadDeveloperImage()

lblDevInfo.Text =
    $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}{Environment.NewLine}" &
    $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}{Environment.NewLine}" &
    $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}{Environment.NewLine}" &
    $"Contact: {ContactEmail}"

' optional: center the label text too
lblDevInfo.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
```

And keep the rest in your existing `rtbInfo` (remove the author/company/phone/contact/year lines from it since they’re now in the middle section).

---

If you prefer **not** adding new controls, tell me that explicitly and I can show the (more fragile) RTF approach to embed an image into a RichTextBox at a specific point—but the control-based layout above is the correct WinForms solution.

If anything changes later (e.g., you want it positioned between lines again), tell me and we can do it with an added panel without touching the rest of your About text.

Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Reflection

Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Const AppTitle As String = "About Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"

    ' TODO: customize these
    Private Const AuthorName As String = "Kirk Saffell"
    Private Const DeveloperCompany As String = "Enterprise Services & Consulting"
