    Private Const AuthorPhone As String = "(661) 478-0990"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load

        lblTitle.Text = AppTitle
        LoadDeveloperImage()
        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

        rtbInfo.Text = "Embedded resources:" & Environment.NewLine &
               String.Join(Environment.NewLine, names)
        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            "",   ' blank line
            "",   ' optional second blank line
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            "",
            $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}",
            $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

        rtbInfo.SelectAll()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
        rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

        rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0

    End Sub
    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Try
            If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

            Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
            Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

            Dim resName As String = Nothing

            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC.Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                     n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If

            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
                If s Is Nothing Then
                    picDeveloper.Visible = False
                    Return
                End If

                Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                    s.CopyTo(ms)
                    ms.Position = 0
                    picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
                End Using
            End Using

            picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
            picDeveloper.Visible = True

        Catch
            If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        End Try
    End Sub
End Class 

## Copilot

Your code is *mostly* correct, and `LoadDeveloperImage()` is now written in a safe way (no more string `OrElse` problem). There are a couple fixes/cleanups I recommend so it behaves exactly as you intend.

## What’s wrong / should be changed

### 1) You’re writing “Embedded resources:” to `rtbInfo`, then immediately overwriting it
This block:

```vbnet
Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()
rtbInfo.Text = "Embedded resources:" & Environment.NewLine &
       String.Join(Environment.NewLine, names)
```

is **debug-only**, and it’s wasted work now because later you do:

```vbnet
rtbInfo.Clear()
rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)
```

So either remove the debug block, or guard it with `#If DEBUG Then ... #End If`.

### 2) `buildDate` is calculated but never displayed
Not a bug, just unused. Either remove it or include it in `lines` (e.g., `"Build date: ..."`).

### 3) `LoadDeveloperImage()` only looks for `.jpg`
If you ever switch to PNG, it won’t load. Optional improvement: check `.png` too.

### 4) Swallowing exceptions silently can hide real issues
Your `Catch` is fine for production (don’t crash About box), but while you’re still verifying it, you may want to show the exception in DEBUG.

---

## Cleaned-up version (recommended)
This removes the debug overwrite, optionally shows build date, and improves resource matching a bit.

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Const AppTitle As String = "About Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"

    Private Const AuthorName As String = "Kirk Saffell"
    Private Const DeveloperCompany As String = "Enterprise Services & Consulting"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "capnkirk@capnkirk.com"
    Private Const AuthorPhone As String = "(661) 478-0990"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        lblTitle.Text = AppTitle

        LoadDeveloperImage()

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            "",
            "",
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            $"Build date: {buildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}",
            $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

        rtbInfo.SelectAll()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
        rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

        rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Try
            If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

            Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
            Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

            Dim resName As String = Nothing

            ' Most common case: spaces become underscores in the manifest name
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If

            ' Fallbacks
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                         (n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) OrElse
                                                          n.EndsWith(".png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
            End If

            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
                If s Is Nothing Then
                    picDeveloper.Visible = False
                    Return
                End If

                Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                    s.CopyTo(ms)
                    ms.Position = 0
                    picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
                End Using
            End Using

            picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
            picDeveloper.Visible = True

        Catch ex As Exception
#If DEBUG Then
            MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")
#End If
            If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

## Final checklist (non-code)
- In Solution Explorer, `ESC Logo.jpg` → Properties → **Build Action = Embedded Resource**
- PictureBox on the form is named **`picDeveloper`** (Name property)

If you want, tell me what the output of `Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetManifestResourceNames()` contains for your logo (just the one line), and I’ll adjust the match to be exact (no guessing).

## @testsubject117

I want to look at Placing a Title at the top of the main screen that looks just like the DOS version did

## Copilot

To make the top of your WinForms main screen look like a DOS title bar, the usual approach is: **add a docked header panel** and put a **single label** in it using a **monospace font** and DOS-like colors.

## What to build (WinForms)
1) On your main form (the “main screen” form), add a **Panel** named `pnlDosTitle`
- `Dock = Top`
- `Height = 28` (adjust to taste)
- `BackColor = Color.Navy` (classic DOS blue)

2) Add a **Label** inside it named `lblDosTitle`
- `Dock = Fill`
- `ForeColor = Color.White` (or Yellow)
- `BackColor = Color.Transparent` (or same as panel)
- `TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft`
- `Font = Consolas, 12, Bold` (or `Lucida Console`)

3) Set the text to match your DOS title style, for example:
- `" Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu "` (note the spaces)

## Code (put in your main form’s Load event)
```vbnet
Private Sub MainForm_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    lblDosTitle.Text = " Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu "
End Sub
```

## To match DOS even closer
- Use `Consolas` or `Lucida Console` (monospace)
- Use a solid background (Navy/Blue)
- Add a thin bottom border line:
  - easiest: set `pnlDosTitle.BorderStyle = BorderStyle.FixedSingle`
  - or draw a 1px line in the panel’s Paint event

---

To make it “look just like the DOS version”, I need one detail:  
**What exactly did the DOS title line look like?** If you can paste the DOS title line text (or upload a screenshot), I’ll tell you the exact label text (including spacing) + colors + alignment to replicate it.

## @testsubject117

this is how the main menu looked in DOS:

## Copilot

Upload a screenshot (or photo) of the DOS main menu, or paste the exact text layout if you have it. Once I can see the spacing/colors, I’ll tell you exactly how to reproduce it in WinForms.

In the meantime, here are the two most common DOS title styles and how to implement each:

## Style A: One DOS-style title bar line (most common)
Add a top `Panel` + `Label` (monospace, blue background, white text).

```vbnet
' Put this in your main form Load
pnlDosTitle.BackColor = Color.Navy
lblDosTitle.ForeColor = Color.White
lblDosTitle.Font = New Font("Consolas", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold)
lblDosTitle.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft

' Example title (we’ll adjust once you show the real DOS line)
lblDosTitle.Text = " Active Magnetic Inspection - Main Menu "
```

## Style B: Full “DOS header block” (multiple lines)
If the DOS version had a box with multiple lines (e.g., a border + centered title + status), use a `RichTextBox` (ReadOnly) or a custom-drawn panel.

Example using a read-only RichTextBox:

```vbnet
rtbHeader.ReadOnly = True
rtbHeader.BorderStyle = BorderStyle.None
rtbHeader.BackColor = Color.Navy
rtbHeader.ForeColor = Color.White
rtbHeader.Font = New Font("Consolas", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold)
rtbHeader.Text =
"┌──────────────────────────────────────────────┐" & vbCrLf &
"│      ACTIVE MAGNETIC INSPECTION MAIN MENU    │" & vbCrLf &
"└──────────────────────────────────────────────┘"
```

(We’d replace the box characters with whatever your DOS screen used.)

---

3) Was the title **centered** or **left-aligned**?
4) Roughly how wide was the DOS screen (typically 80 columns)?

If you upload the screenshot, I can map it precisely (including padding/spaces).

## @testsubject117

Describe this image

## Copilot

The image shows a classic DOS-style, text-mode **“MAIN MENU”** screen:

- **Overall look:** Black background with high‑contrast text, rendered in a monospace DOS font.
- **Title:** Large “MAIN MENU” text at the top-left in a bright yellow/amber color.
- **Header/status line (top-right):** Displays the **day** (“Monday”), the **date** (“04-13-2026”), and a **time** (“18:10:20”) aligned across the top.
- **Menu layout:** Two columns of options.
  - **Left column:** Lettered items in parentheses, e.g. `(A) Shop Card Generator`, `(B) Invoice Generator`, etc.
  - **Right column:** Additional lettered items, e.g. `(O) Copy Spec Index`, `(P) Entire Ledger Viewing`, etc.
- **Prompt:** Near the bottom-left it says **“TYPE A LETTER”** indicating the user should press a menu key.
- **Footer:** Bottom-right has a slogan: **“Keep Calm & Carry 0”**.
- **Accent elements:** There are vertical yellow “bar” glyphs near the very top-left (decorative/branding).

If you want this replicated in WinForms, the key pieces to match are: a fixed-height header area with the big yellow title + a right-aligned date/time line, and a central area that uses a monospace font and two aligned columns.

## @testsubject117

I would like for the winforms main menu to Say Main Menu as well as the day, date and time just like the dos menu

## Copilot

Do this by adding a **header panel** at the top of your WinForms main form, with two labels:

- **Left label**: big yellow `MAIN MENU`
- **Right label**: `Monday  04-13-2026  18:10:20` (day + date + time), updated by a Timer every second

### 1) Designer setup (main form)
Add a `Panel` named `pnlHeader`:
- `Dock = Top`
- `Height = 48` (adjust)
- `BackColor = Color.Black`

Inside it add two `Label`s:

**Label 1: `lblMainMenu`**
- `AutoSize = False`
- `Dock = Left`
- `Width = 260` (adjust)
- `ForeColor = Color.Gold`
- `BackColor = Color.Black`
- `Font = "Consolas", 26, Bold`
- `Text = "MAIN MENU"`
- `TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft`

**Label 2: `lblDateTime`**
- `AutoSize = False`
- `Dock = Fill` (or Dock=Right with a Width)
- `ForeColor = Color.Gold`
- `BackColor = Color.Black`
- `Font = "Consolas", 14, Bold`
- `TextAlign = ContentAlignment.TopRight`

Add a `Timer` named `tmrClock`:
- `Interval = 1000`
- `Enabled = True`

### 2) Code (main form)
Put this in your main form code:

```vbnet
Private Sub MainForm_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
    UpdateHeaderClock()
    tmrClock.Interval = 1000
    tmrClock.Start()
End Sub

Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick
    UpdateHeaderClock()
End Sub

Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
    Dim now = DateTime.Now
    lblDateTime.Text = $"{now:dddd}  {now:MM-dd-yyyy}          {now:HH:mm:ss}"
End Sub
```

That formatting gives you the same components as DOS:
- day name (`Monday`)
- date (`04-13-2026`)
- 24-hour time (`18:10:20`)

### Notes to match DOS closer
- If you want the day/date/time to sit on one line exactly spaced, keep a **monospace font** (Consolas/Lucida Console).
- You can tweak the number of spaces between date and time to match your screenshot.

If you tell me the **exact name of your main menu form** (e.g., `FormMainMenu`?) and whether you already have a Timer on it, I’ll tailor the exact event handler names to your project.

## @testsubject117

which form is designer setup (main form)

## Copilot

The “designer setup (main form)” is the **WinForms form that shows your main menu screen**—the one that opens when your program starts (or when the user gets to the main menu).

### How to find which form that is (2 quick ways)

#### Method 1: Check the Startup form (most reliable)
1) In **Solution Explorer**, right‑click your project (the one under the solution).
2) Click **Properties**.
3) Go to the **Application** tab.
4) Look at **Startup form**.

Whatever is selected there (for example `FormMainMenu`, `frmMain`, etc.) is the form you should modify in the Designer.

#### Method 2: Look at the title of the window you see at runtime
Run your app (F5). The first window that appears is usually the one:
- open that same form in Solution Explorer, and that’s your “main form”.

### Once you find it
Open that form in the designer:
- In Solution Explorer, double-click the form (or right‑click → **View Designer**)

Tell me what your **Startup form name** is (exactly as shown in Project Properties), and I’ll tell you exactly where to drop `pnlHeader`, `lblMainMenu`, `lblDateTime`, and the Timer on that form.

## @testsubject117

Do this by adding a header panel at the top of your WinForms main form, with two labels:

Left label: big yellow MAIN MENU
Right label: Monday  04-13-2026  18:10:20 (day + date + time), updated by a Timer every second
1) Designer setup (main form)
Add a Panel named pnlHeader:    I'm having trouble with this, does the panel get placed onto tlproot, or flpleft?

## Copilot

Put the header **outside** both `tlpRoot` and `flpLeft`.

In other words: add `pnlHeader` **directly onto the Form** (the main menu form’s surface), then set `pnlHeader.Dock = Top`, and set your existing `tlpRoot.Dock = Fill` (or keep it, but make sure it ends up filling the remaining space).

### Why
- `tlpRoot` and `flpLeft` are for your menu content/layout.
- The DOS-style title line is a **separate top band** that should span the whole width and stay fixed.

### Exact designer steps
1) Open the main menu form in **Design** view.
2) Click a blank area of the form (so nothing inside `tlpRoot`/`flpLeft` is selected).
3) Drag a **Panel** from the Toolbox onto the form.
4) Set these properties on the panel:
   - **Name**: `pnlHeader`
   - **Dock**: `Top`
   - **Height**: `48` (or similar)
   - **BackColor**: `Black`

5) Make sure your existing root layout control is docked to fill:
   - Click `tlpRoot`
   - Set **Dock** = `Fill`

### If the panel keeps ending up inside `tlpRoot`
That means `tlpRoot` is covering the entire form and intercepting the drop.

Two easy fixes:

**Fix A (Document Outline – easiest)**
1) Open **View → Other Windows → Document Outline**
2) Drag `pnlHeader` so it becomes a sibling of `tlpRoot` under the form:
   - `FormMainMenu`
     - `pnlHeader`
     - `tlpRoot`

**Fix B (cut/paste)**
1) If the panel accidentally landed inside `tlpRoot`, select it.
2) **Ctrl+X** (cut)
3) Click the form background (or select the form in Document Outline)
4) **Ctrl+V** (paste)
5) Set `Dock = Top`

### Important: control order matters with Docking
For Dock to behave correctly, `pnlHeader` should be “above” the fill control. If you see weird overlap:
- Right-click the form → **Order → Bring to Front** on `pnlHeader`
- Or in Document Outline, ensure `pnlHeader` is listed before `tlpRoot`.

If you tell me your form/control names as they appear in Document Outline (a quick list), I can tell you the exact correct hierarchy and docking values.

## @testsubject117

where do I add the timer named tmrclock

## Copilot

Add `tmrClock` on the **same main menu form** as `pnlHeader` (the Startup/main menu form), and drop it onto the **designer surface** so it appears in the **component tray** at the bottom (it’s not a visible control).

### Designer steps (Visual Studio WinForms)
1) Open your main menu form in **Design** view.
2) Open the **Toolbox**.
3) Find **Timer** (it’s under **Components**).
4) **Drag “Timer” onto the form** (don’t try to place it inside `pnlHeader`/`tlpRoot`—just drop it anywhere).
5) It will show up at the bottom as `Timer1` (component tray).
6) Click `Timer1` and set properties:
   - **(Name)** = `tmrClock`
   - **Enabled** = `True` (optional; you can start it in code instead)
   - **Interval** = `1000`

### Wire up the Tick event
Double-click the timer in the component tray (or in Properties, click the lightning bolt and double-click **Tick**). Visual Studio will create:

```vbnet
Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick
    UpdateHeaderClock()
End Sub
```

### If you don’t see the Toolbox “Timer”
Make sure you’re in **Designer** view (not code view), then:
- **View → Toolbox**
- Expand **Components**

If you tell me the exact name of your main menu form (e.g., `FormMainMenu` / `frmMain`), I can show the full ready-to-paste `Load` + `Tick` code with the correct `Handles ...` line.

Place it in the **code-behind file for your main menu form** (the same form where you added `pnlHeader`, `lblMainMenu`, `lblDateTime`, and `tmrClock`)—not in `FormAbout`.

### Exactly where

2) Paste the code **inside the form’s class**, typically near the top, like this:

```vbnet
Public Class FormMainMenu   ' <-- your form name may differ

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
        UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        tmrClock.Start()

        UpdateHeaderClock()
    End Sub

        Dim now = DateTime.Now
        lblDateTime.Text = $"{now:dddd}  {now:MM-dd-yyyy}          {now:HH:mm:ss}"
    End Sub

End Class
```

### Important: match names
- Replace `FormMainMenu_Load` with whatever your form is actually named (VS can do this automatically).
- Make sure the controls are named exactly:
  - `lblMainMenu`
  - `lblDateTime`
  - `tmrClock`

2) Click the form background
3) In **Properties → ⚡ Events**, double‑click **Load**

## @testsubject117

I have done everything up to double clicking tick, and verifying the code was generated. When I click the play button I only see the menu buttons, I do not see the Main Menu text or the date and time

## Copilot

This almost always means `pnlHeader` is either (a) **inside the wrong container** (e.g., inside `flpLeft`), (b) **behind** `tlpRoot` due to docking/order, or (c) its **Height is effectively 0** / it’s not docked.

Do these checks in this order (they’re quick and will pinpoint it):

## 1) Verify the control hierarchy (most important)
Open **View → Other Windows → Document Outline**.

You should see something like:

- `FormMainMenu` (or whatever your main form is)
  - `pnlHeader`
    - `lblMainMenu`
    - `lblDateTime`
  - `tlpRoot` (or your main layout)

If `pnlHeader` is under `tlpRoot` or `flpLeft`, that’s the issue.

### Fix
In Document Outline, **drag `pnlHeader`** so it is a **direct child of the form**, *above* `tlpRoot`.

## 2) Verify Dock settings
Click each control and confirm properties:

### `pnlHeader`
- `Dock = Top`
- `Height = 48` (or at least ~30)
- `BackColor = Black` (so you can see it)

### `tlpRoot`
- `Dock = Fill` (not Top)
- It should **not** be Dock=Fill *and* “covering” the header due to order (next step handles that)

## 3) Fix docking order (header can be hidden even if Dock is right)
Even with correct Dock, **z-order matters**.

- Select `pnlHeader` in the designer
- **Right-click → Bring to Front**

Also try:
- Select `tlpRoot`
- **Right-click → Send to Back**

## 4) Make sure labels aren’t invisible
Click `lblMainMenu`:
- `ForeColor = Gold/Yellow`
- `BackColor = Black` or `Transparent`
- `Dock = Left` (or None is OK, but Dock is easiest)
- `Width` not tiny (e.g., 250)

Click `lblDateTime`:
- `ForeColor = Gold/Yellow`
- `Dock = Fill` (or Right)
- `TextAlign = TopRight` or `MiddleRight`

## 5) Confirm the code is running on the correct form
Make sure the `Load` handler is on the **startup main menu form**, not another form.

In your main menu form code, add this temporarily:

```vbnet
Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    MessageBox.Show("Main menu form loaded")
    lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
    UpdateHeaderClock()
    tmrClock.Interval = 1000
    tmrClock.Start()
End Sub
```

If you **don’t** see that message box, you edited the wrong form (or the form isn’t the startup form).

## 6) Quick “force visible” test
Temporarily set the header to something unmistakable:

- `pnlHeader.BackColor = Color.Red`
- `lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.White`

If you still can’t see it, it’s definitely hierarchy/order/docking.

---

### Tell me these 3 things and I’ll give the exact fix
1) What is your startup form name (Project Properties → Application → Startup form)?
2) In **Document Outline**, where does `pnlHeader` appear (under the form, under `tlpRoot`, under `flpLeft`, etc.)?
3) What are the Dock values for `pnlHeader` and `tlpRoot`?

## @testsubject117

Startup Object is FormMainMenu. pnlHeader appears under the form, dock value for pnlHeader is Top and Dock value for tlpRoot is fill

## Copilot

Given that hierarchy and docking are correct, the remaining common causes are:

1) `pnlHeader` is there but **its height is too small / collapsed**
2) `tlpRoot` has a **negative/top margin** or the form’s **AutoScale** / layout is pushing the header out
3) `pnlHeader` is visible but the **labels have 0 size** or are positioned off-screen
4) Another control (often a menu/toolbar) is occupying the top and your header is docked under it but you don’t notice

Do these targeted checks/fixes:

## 1) Force the header to be obvious (quick test)
In `FormMainMenu_Load`, add this temporarily:

```vbnet
pnlHeader.BackColor = Color.Red
pnlHeader.Height = 60
pnlHeader.Visible = True

lblMainMenu.BackColor = Color.Blue
lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.White
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
lblMainMenu.AutoSize = False
lblMainMenu.Dock = DockStyle.Left
lblMainMenu.Width = 300

lblDateTime.BackColor = Color.Green
lblDateTime.ForeColor = Color.White
lblDateTime.AutoSize = False
lblDateTime.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleRight

UpdateHeaderClock()
```

Run it. If you still don’t see a **red bar** at the top, it’s being covered by something or not actually on that form at runtime.

## 2) Check if `pnlHeader` is accidentally behind despite the outline
In the designer:
- Click `pnlHeader`
- Right-click → **Bring to Front**
Then click `tlpRoot`
- Right-click → **Send to Back**

Even with correct docking, z-order can hide it.

## 3) Make sure `pnlHeader` isn’t inside another top-docked container
In Document Outline, confirm there isn’t something like:
- `ToolStripContainer`
- `MenuStrip`
- Another `Panel` docked `Fill`

If you have a `MenuStrip`, the form’s `MainMenuStrip` can reserve top space. That’s fine—but you should still see the header. The “force red” test above will clarify.

## 4) Verify you’re not creating a new instance of the form without the header
This happens if somewhere you do `Dim f As New FormMainMenu` and show that, but you edited a different form file/class or the designer partial didn’t match.

Add this (temporary) at the top of `FormMainMenu_Load`:

```vbnet
MessageBox.Show("Loaded: " & Me.Name & "  Class=" & Me.GetType().FullName)
```

If it shows the right class name, you’re on the right form.

## 5) Most likely fix: Dock order
In WinForms, when you have Dock=Top and Dock=Fill, the order the controls are added matters. The easiest way to correct it:

- Open **Document Outline**
- Drag `pnlHeader` **above** `tlpRoot` (so it’s listed first)
- Save All
- Run again

Even if both are children of the form, if `tlpRoot` was added after, it can occupy the full client area and appear to cover the top until order is fixed.

## 6) Check `pnlHeader` Height in designer
In the Properties window for `pnlHeader`, confirm:
- `Size` shows something like `Width, 48`
- Not `Height = 0` or `1`

---

### If you do just one thing
Do the **red/blue/green force test** (step 1) and tell me what you see:
- Do you see the red bar?
- Do you see blue/green areas?
- Or still only buttons?

That single result tells us exactly whether it’s a layout/z-order issue vs. the header not being on the runtime form.

## @testsubject117

I cannot find FormMainMenu_Load

## Copilot

You don’t need to “find” it manually—Visual Studio will generate it for you. Right now, you likely only have the `tmrClock_Tick` handler, but no `Load` handler yet.

## Create the `FormMainMenu_Load` event handler (easy way)
1) Open **FormMainMenu** in **Design** view.
2) Click a blank area of the form (so the form itself is selected).
3) In the **Properties** window, click the **⚡ Events** icon.
4) Find **Load** in the list.
5) Double‑click the empty space next to **Load**.

Visual Studio will create:

```vbnet
Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load

End Sub
```

Now paste inside it:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
UpdateHeaderClock()
tmrClock.Interval = 1000
tmrClock.Start()
```

## If you prefer: create it from the code window
Open **View Code** for FormMainMenu and add this anywhere inside `Public Class FormMainMenu ... End Class`:

```vbnet
Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
    UpdateHeaderClock()
    tmrClock.Interval = 1000
    tmrClock.Start()
End Sub
```

## Why you couldn’t find it
The `..._Load` method only exists if you (or the designer) created an event handler for the form’s Load event. The timer Tick handler gets generated when you double-click the Timer, but the form Load handler does not unless you create it.

If you tell me what your form class line says exactly (e.g., `Public Class FormMainMenu`), I can give you the exact code block to paste with correct names.

## @testsubject117

lets review so I can get an understanding of what document to look at. I currently have FormMainMenu (working version after removing un-needed entries, sub menu's not yet connected).vb, FormMainMenu.Designer.vb, AMiOffice, FormMainMenu.vb (Design)* and FormMainMenu.vb*

## Copilot

Look at (and edit) **`FormMainMenu.vb`** — the one that opens when you click **View Code** on the form in Solution Explorer. That is the *code-behind* where event handlers like `FormMainMenu_Load`, `tmrClock_Tick`, and `UpdateHeaderClock()` belong.

Here’s what each of the things you listed means, and which one matters.

## The files you should care about

### 1) `FormMainMenu.vb`  ✅ (this is where you put the code)
- Contains `Public Class FormMainMenu`
- This is where you add:
  - `Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load ... Handles MyBase.Load`
  - `Private Sub tmrClock_Tick ... Handles tmrClock.Tick`
  - `Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()`

You should **not** have multiple *different* `FormMainMenu.vb` files in the same project unless they’re different names.

### 2) `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`  ⚠️ (don’t edit by hand)
- Auto-generated by the WinForms designer
- Contains the control declarations and `InitializeComponent()`
- This is where the panel/labels/timer are *declared* and created
- You normally **never edit** this manually (use the Designer instead)

### 3) `FormMainMenu.vb (Design)*` ✅ (this is the Designer view)
- This is not a separate file; it’s the *designer surface* for `FormMainMenu.vb`
- You use this to drag/drop `pnlHeader`, labels, timer, etc.

### 4) `FormMainMenu.vb*` ✅ (same file, unsaved changes)
- The `*` just means “unsaved changes”
- It’s still the same `FormMainMenu.vb` file.

## The odd one: `FormMainMenu (working version after removing ...).vb`
This is a **separate VB file** (a copy/backup) and **may not be the form that is actually running**.

Two possibilities:
- It’s just a spare backup file (not used by the project), OR
- It’s included in the project and might even define another form/class, which can cause confusion.

### What you should do next (to avoid chasing the wrong file)
1) In Solution Explorer, right-click **FormMainMenu.vb** (the one that has the designer)  
   → **View Code**
2) At the very top, confirm you see:

```vbnet
Public Class FormMainMenu
```

3) Add the Load handler **in that file**:

```vbnet
Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
    UpdateHeaderClock()
    tmrClock.Interval = 1000
    tmrClock.Start()
End Sub
```

## Quick sanity check: make sure you’re editing the one that runs
Add this temporarily inside `FormMainMenu_Load`:

```vbnet
MessageBox.Show("Running " & Me.GetType().FullName)
```

Run (F5). If you see the message box, you’re editing the correct form.

---

If you want, tell me what you see when you open **View Code** for the designer form: the first few lines (just the `Public Class ...` line is enough). That will confirm which file is the real startup form code-behind.

## @testsubject117

FormMainMenu.vb: Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    ' NOTE:
    ' - True GUI menu (no terminal emulation).
    ' - Assumes FormMainMenu.Designer.vb defines:
    '     Friend WithEvents flpLeft As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents flpRight As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents tlpRoot As TableLayoutPanel
    ' - This code will enforce the layout at runtime.

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        EnsureLayout()
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub EnsureLayout()
        If tlpRoot Is Nothing Then Return
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        tlpRoot.SuspendLayout()

        tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Clear()
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Clear()

        tlpRoot.ColumnCount = 2
        tlpRoot.RowCount = 1
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        tlpRoot.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        If flpLeft.Parent IsNot tlpRoot OrElse flpRight.Parent IsNot tlpRoot Then
            tlpRoot.Controls.Clear()
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
            tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)
        Else
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpLeft, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(0, 0))
            tlpRoot.SetCellPosition(flpRight, New TableLayoutPanelCellPosition(1, 0))
        End If

        If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
        End If

        For i As Integer = Me.Controls.Count - 1 To 0 Step -1
            Dim c = Me.Controls(i)
            If c IsNot tlpRoot Then
                Me.Controls.RemoveAt(i)
            End If
        Next

        tlpRoot.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.SuspendLayout()

        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.Padding = New Padding(10)

        panel.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Optional headers
        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        ' Left column (unchanged)
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (REMOVED: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
            "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
            "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try
            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            ' REMOVED hotkeys: S, U, V, W, +, 5

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")
            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick

    End Sub

End Class

## Copilot

Got it—this `FormMainMenu.vb` explains exactly why you don’t see the header: **your `EnsureLayout()` method is removing every control on the form except `tlpRoot`.** So even if `pnlHeader` is correctly added in the Designer, it gets removed at runtime on load.

Specifically, this block deletes the header:

```vbnet
For i As Integer = Me.Controls.Count - 1 To 0 Step -1
    Dim c = Me.Controls(i)
    If c IsNot tlpRoot Then
        Me.Controls.RemoveAt(i)
    End If
Next
```

### What to change (minimum fix)
Modify `EnsureLayout()` so it keeps both `pnlHeader` and `tlpRoot`, and make sure the docking order is correct (header Top, root Fill).

Here’s the corrected `EnsureLayout()` section to replace the “remove everything” portion with:

```vbnet
' Ensure tlpRoot is on the form
If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
    Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
End If

' Keep the header panel if present
If pnlHeader IsNot Nothing AndAlso Not Me.Controls.Contains(pnlHeader) Then
    Me.Controls.Add(pnlHeader)
End If

' Docking
If pnlHeader IsNot Nothing Then
    pnlHeader.Dock = DockStyle.Top
    pnlHeader.BringToFront()
End If

tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
tlpRoot.SendToBack()
```

And **delete** (or comment out) the loop that removes other controls.

### Next: add the clock update code
Right now your `tmrClock_Tick` is empty. You need:
- a `FormMainMenu_Load` (or add into your `OnLoad`) to start the timer
- an `UpdateHeaderClock()` method
- the Tick handler should call it

Because you’re overriding `OnLoad`, you can do it there (no separate Load handler required). Add these into your class:

```vbnet
Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
    MyBase.OnLoad(e)

    Me.KeyPreview = True
    EnsureLayout()
    BuildMenu()

    lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
    UpdateHeaderClock()
    tmrClock.Interval = 1000
    tmrClock.Start()
End Sub

Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick
    UpdateHeaderClock()
End Sub

Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
    Dim now = DateTime.Now
    lblDateTime.Text = $"{now:dddd}  {now:MM-dd-yyyy}          {now:HH:mm:ss}"
End Sub
```

### One dependency: pnlHeader/lblMainMenu/lblDateTime must exist in Designer
To reference `pnlHeader`, `lblMainMenu`, and `lblDateTime` from this code, they must be defined as `Friend WithEvents` (or `Friend`) controls in `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`.

---

Send me **`FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`** next (and also your “working version” file), and I will:
1) Fix the designer to contain `pnlHeader`, `lblMainMenu`, `lblDateTime`, `tmrClock`
2) Fix `FormMainMenu.vb` so it doesn’t delete the header and it updates the clock
3) Tell you exactly what goes in which file, cleanly, with no duplicates/conflicts

FormMainMenu.vb: Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    ' NOTE:
    ' - True GUI menu (no terminal emulation).
    ' - Assumes FormMainMenu.Designer.vb defines:
    '     Friend WithEvents flpLeft As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents flpRight As FlowLayoutPanel
    '     Friend WithEvents tlpRoot As TableLayoutPanel
    ' - This code will enforce the layout at runtime.

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        EnsureLayout()
        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub EnsureLayout()
        ' Ensure tlpRoot is on the form
        If Not Me.Controls.Contains(tlpRoot) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
        End If

        ' Keep the header panel if present
        If pnlHeader IsNot Nothing AndAlso Not Me.Controls.Contains(pnlHeader) Then
            Me.Controls.Add(pnlHeader)
        End If

        ' Docking
        If pnlHeader IsNot Nothing Then
            pnlHeader.Dock = DockStyle.Top
            pnlHeader.BringToFront()
        End If

        tlpRoot.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        tlpRoot.SendToBack()
    End Sub

    Private Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.SuspendLayout()

        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.Padding = New Padding(10)

        panel.ResumeLayout(performLayout:=True)
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Optional headers
        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        ' Left column (unchanged)
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (REMOVED: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
            "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
            "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try
            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            ' REMOVED hotkeys: S, U, V, W, +, 5

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")
            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick

    End Sub

End Class

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)

        Me.KeyPreview = True

        ' To avoid "Overload resolution failed..." we do NOT call any EnsureLayout method here.

        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Optional headers

        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")

        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")
            Case "I" : NotYet("Backup Price List & Rolodex (implement in .NET)")
            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            ' REMOVED hotkeys: 5, +, S, U, V, W

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case Else
                ' ignore
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub
    Private Sub MainForm_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
        UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        tmrClock.Start()
    End Sub

    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick
        UpdateHeaderClock()
    End Sub

    Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now = DateTime.Now
        lblDateTime.Text = $"{now:dddd}  {now:MM-dd-yyyy}          {now:HH:mm:ss}"
    End Sub

End Class

## @testsubject117

FormAbout.vb: Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Const AppTitle As String = "About Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"

    Private Const AuthorName As String = "Kirk Saffell"
    Private Const DeveloperCompany As String = "Enterprise Services & Consulting"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "capnkirk@capnkirk.com"
    Private Const AuthorPhone As String = "(661) 478-0990"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        lblTitle.Text = AppTitle

        LoadDeveloperImage()

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            "",
            "",
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            $"Build date: {buildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}",
            $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

        rtbInfo.SelectAll()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
        rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

        rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Try
            If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

            Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
            Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

            Dim resName As String = Nothing

            ' Most common case: spaces become underscores in the manifest name
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If

            ' Fallbacks
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                         (n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) OrElse
                                                          n.EndsWith(".png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
            End If

            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
                If s Is Nothing Then
                    picDeveloper.Visible = False
                    Return
                End If

                Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                    s.CopyTo(ms)
                    ms.Position = 0
                    picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
                End Using
            End Using

            picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
            picDeveloper.Visible = True

        Catch ex As Exception
#If DEBUG Then
            MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")
#End If
            If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class

## @testsubject117

MigrationService.vb: Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO

Public Module MigrationService

    Private ReadOnly LegacyRoot As String = "\\invoice"
    Private ReadOnly MarkerPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, ".migrated")

    Public Sub EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.AppRoot)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' If we've already migrated on this share, do nothing.
        If File.Exists(MarkerPath) Then Return

        Dim patterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT", "*.CUR", "*.LST", "*.PRC",
            "PACK.NUM", "BILLS.*", "*.CHK", "*.TXT"
        }

        Dim movedAny As Boolean = False

        For Each pattern In patterns
            Dim files As String()

            Try
                files = Directory.GetFiles(LegacyRoot, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
            Catch
                Continue For
            End Try

            For Each src In files
                Dim dest As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, Path.GetFileName(src))

                ' Skip if already migrated
                If File.Exists(dest) Then Continue For

                Try
                    File.Move(src, dest)
                    movedAny = True
                Catch
                    ' ignore (in use / permissions / etc.)
                End Try
            Next
        Next

        ' Create marker even if nothing moved, so we don't keep scanning \\invoice\ forever.
        Try
            File.WriteAllText(MarkerPath, $"migrated={DateTime.Now:yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss}")
        Catch
            ' If we can't write marker, migration will try again next time.
        End Try
    End Sub

End Module

## @testsubject117

BackupService.vb: Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO
Imports System.IO.Compression
Imports System.Linq

Public Module BackupService

    Public Sub RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.DataDir)
        Directory.CreateDirectory(AppPaths.BackupDir)

        ' 1) Word docs => WORD_yyyy-MM-dd_HHmmss.zip (timestamped)
        Dim stamp As String = DateTime.Now.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd_HHmmss")
        Dim wordZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"WORD_{stamp}.zip")
        CreateZipFromPattern(wordZip, AppPaths.WordDocsDir, "*.doc")

        ' 2) Rotating data backup => BACKUP##.zip (LOCKED)
        Dim lockPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "backup.lock")

        Using lockStream As FileStream = AcquireBackupLock(lockPath)

            Dim packNumPath As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.DataDir, "PACK.NUM")
            Dim n As Integer = GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath)
            Dim dataZip As String = Path.Combine(AppPaths.BackupDir, $"BACKUP{n}.zip")

            CreateDataZip(dataZip, AppPaths.DataDir)

        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateZipFromPattern(zipPath As String, folder As String, pattern As String)
        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                If Directory.Exists(folder) Then
                    For Each f In Directory.GetFiles(folder, pattern, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly)
                        archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, Path.GetFileName(f), CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                    Next
                End If
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Sub CreateDataZip(zipPath As String, dataDir As String)
        Dim includePatterns As String() = {
            "*.DAT", "*.CUR", "PRC*.PRC", "PHONE*.LST", "BILLS.*", "PACK.NUM"
        }

        Dim files As New System.Collections.Generic.List(Of String)

        For Each pat In includePatterns
            If Directory.Exists(dataDir) Then
                Try
                    files.AddRange(Directory.GetFiles(dataDir, pat, SearchOption.TopDirectoryOnly))
                Catch
                End Try
            End If
        Next

        files = files.Distinct(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase).ToList()

        Using fs As New FileStream(zipPath, FileMode.Create, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
            Using archive As New ZipArchive(fs, ZipArchiveMode.Create)
                For Each f In files
                    archive.CreateEntryFromFile(f, Path.GetFileName(f), CompressionLevel.Optimal)
                Next
            End Using
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function GetNextPackNumberAndAdvance(packNumPath As String) As Integer
        Dim n As Integer = 1

        Try
            If File.Exists(packNumPath) Then
                Integer.TryParse(File.ReadAllText(packNumPath).Trim(), n)
            End If
        Catch
            n = 1
        End Try

        If n < 1 OrElse n > 99 Then n = 1

        Dim nextN As Integer = n + 1
        If nextN > 99 Then nextN = 1

        Try
            File.WriteAllText(packNumPath, nextN.ToString())
        Catch
        End Try

        Return n
    End Function
    Private Function AcquireBackupLock(lockPath As String) As FileStream
        ' Creating with FileShare.None makes it a cross-machine mutex on a UNC share.
        ' If another PC holds it, this throws an IOException.
        Return New FileStream(lockPath, FileMode.OpenOrCreate, FileAccess.ReadWrite, FileShare.None)
    End Function

End Module

## Copilot

You currently have **two different implementations of `FormMainMenu` logic in two different .vb files**, both marked `Partial Public Class FormMainMenu`, and both contain overlapping members (`OnLoad`, `BuildMenu`, `HandleMenuKey`, etc.). That’s the core problem: you’re effectively “merging” two forms into one class, which causes conflicting behavior and prevents the header/time logic from being reliably executed.

Below is exactly what needs to be **removed**, **moved/merged**, and **added** to get a clean, correct setup that shows:

- **MAIN MENU** (left)
- **Day / date / time** (right, ticking every second)
- Your existing two-column button menu

---

## 1) Files to keep vs. delete

### Keep (authoritative)
- **`FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`** (Designer-generated controls: `pnlHeader`, `lblMainMenu`, `lblDateTime`, `tmrClock`, `tlpRoot`, `flpLeft`, `flpRight`, `rtbScreen`)
- **`FormMainMenu.vb`** (ONE code-behind file for all your runtime logic)
- `FormAbout.vb`
- `FormAbout.Designer.vb`
- `AppPaths.vb`
- `MigrationService.vb`
- `BackupService.vb`

### Remove from project (or rename so it is NOT partial / NOT FormMainMenu)
- **`FormMainMenu (working version after removing un-needed entries, sub menu's not yet connected).vb`**

If you want to keep it as a reference, that’s fine, but you must:
- either **exclude it from the project**, or
- rename the class inside it to something else (NOT `FormMainMenu`), or
- remove the `Partial Public Class FormMainMenu` wrapper

Because as long as it stays `Partial Public Class FormMainMenu`, it will keep contributing duplicate methods/events.

---

## 2) Fix your designer layout (critical)
Right now `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb` adds **four** top-level controls:

```vbnet
Controls.Add(pnlHeader)
Controls.Add(flpRight)
Controls.Add(flpLeft)
Controls.Add(tlpRoot)
```

But your code expects `flpLeft` and `flpRight` to be **inside `tlpRoot`**, and `tlpRoot` to be the main container.

### What to remove from `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`
Remove these two lines from the bottom of `InitializeComponent()`:

- `Controls.Add(flpRight)`
- `Controls.Add(flpLeft)`

### What to add/ensure in `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`
Make sure `tlpRoot` actually contains `flpLeft` and `flpRight` (not `rtbScreen`), and has **2 columns, 1 row**, like:

- Column 0: `flpLeft`
- Column 1: `flpRight`

Also, **remove `rtbScreen` from tlpRoot** unless you truly still need it. Right now it’s inside `tlpRoot` and will fight your intended layout.

In short, decide one of these two approaches:

**Approach A (recommended for your two-column button UI):**
- `tlpRoot` holds `flpLeft` and `flpRight`
- delete `rtbScreen` from the main menu form

**Approach B (if you still want the RichTextBox “screen”):**
- Keep `rtbScreen` and do NOT use `flpLeft/flpRight` as separate top-level menus
- but then your BuildMenu code needs to write text to `rtbScreen` instead of building buttons

Right now your code is clearly written for Approach A.

---

## 3) Fix `FormMainMenu.vb` (the code-behind)
Your current `FormMainMenu.vb` includes:
- `EnsureLayout()` (but it only docks; it doesn’t place flpLeft/flpRight into tlpRoot)
- `ConfigureMenuPanel()` (but it is never called)
- `tmrClock_Tick` exists but is empty
- no `UpdateHeaderClock()`

### Remove from `FormMainMenu.vb`
- Remove the empty Tick handler body and replace it (see “Add” below).
- Remove (or stop using) `rtbScreen` related assumptions (since you’re building buttons).

### Add to `FormMainMenu.vb`
Add these methods and call them from `OnLoad`:

**A) Update the header text + start the timer**
- Set `lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"`
- Start timer (or keep designer Enabled=True)
- Update header clock immediately

**B) Implement `UpdateHeaderClock()`**
**C) Implement `tmrClock_Tick` so it calls `UpdateHeaderClock()`**
**D) Ensure `EnsureLayout()` actually configures `tlpRoot` and puts `flpLeft/flpRight` into it** (or do it purely in designer; but don’t do half/half).

### Important: choose ONE layout owner
Right now you have mixed responsibilities:
- Designer creates tlpRoot with rtbScreen (1 column)
- Code intends 2 columns with flpLeft/flpRight

You should pick one:

#### Recommended: keep layout in designer, simplify EnsureLayout
If you fix the designer so tlpRoot already has flpLeft/flpRight set correctly, then `EnsureLayout()` should only do:
- docking order (header top, tlpRoot fill)
- optional FlowLayoutPanel configuration (padding, scroll, wrap)

This avoids runtime surprises.

---

## 4) What to do with the “working version” file’s extra code
Your “working version” contains the clock code, but it’s incorrectly named:

```vbnet
Private Sub MainForm_Load(...) Handles MyBase.Load
```

That name is fine, but it’s inside a partial class that already overrides `OnLoad`, and it duplicates load logic across partial files.

### Move from the “working version” into `FormMainMenu.vb`
Move these three members into the single authoritative `FormMainMenu.vb`:

- `UpdateHeaderClock()`
- `tmrClock_Tick` (the version that calls UpdateHeaderClock)
- the load/start logic (but put it into your existing `OnLoad` override)

### Remove from the “working version”
All of it (since the whole file should be removed/excluded), but if you insist on keeping it included, at minimum remove:
- `Partial Public Class FormMainMenu` wrapper OR rename the class
- `OnLoad` override
- `MainForm_Load`
- `tmrClock_Tick`
- `UpdateHeaderClock`
- any duplicates of BuildMenu/HandleMenuKey/etc.

---

## 5) One more problem you’ll hit: duplicate event handlers
Right now you have:
- `tmrClock.Enabled = True` in Designer
- `tmrClock.Interval = 1000` in Designer
- a Tick handler in code

That’s OK. But if you accidentally have **two Tick handlers** across partial files, you’ll get weird behavior or compile errors.

So the rule: **only one `Handles tmrClock.Tick` in the entire project**.

---

## Summary checklist (actionable)

### A) Remove/Exclude file
- Exclude `FormMainMenu (working version after removing un-needed entries, sub menu's not yet connected).vb` from the project (or rename its class).

### B) Fix `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`
- Remove `Controls.Add(flpLeft)` and `Controls.Add(flpRight)` (top-level)
- Change `tlpRoot` to 2 columns, 1 row and add:
  - `tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)`
  - `tlpRoot.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)`
- Remove `rtbScreen` from `tlpRoot` (unless you truly want it)

### C) Fix `FormMainMenu.vb`
- Keep only one `OnLoad` override
- In that `OnLoad`, do:
  - `EnsureLayout()`
  - `BuildMenu()`
  - set header text + start timer + call `UpdateHeaderClock()`
- Implement:
  - `UpdateHeaderClock()`
  - `tmrClock_Tick` to call it

---

If you want, I can provide the **final cleaned versions** of:
- `FormMainMenu.vb`
- `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb` (only the relevant parts, since it’s large)

…but I didn’t do that yet because you asked specifically for what to **move/add/remove**.

Partial Class FormMainMenu
    Inherits System.Windows.Forms.Form
