## File 2 — `FormMainMenu.vb` (REPLACE ENTIRE FILE)

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True

        ' Build button menus
        BuildMenu()
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)

        ' Header text + clock
        lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
        UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        tmrClock.Start()
    End Sub

    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick
        UpdateHeaderClock()
    End Sub

    Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now = DateTime.Now
        lblDateTime.Text = $"{now:dddd}  {now:MM-dd-yyyy}          {now:HH:mm:ss}"
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Optional headers (remove these two lines if you don't want headers above the buttons)
        flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
        flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))

        ' Left column
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (removed: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        lbl.Width = 1000 ' will be resized later
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 34
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - 5
        If targetWidth < 50 Then targetWidth = 50

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")

            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try

            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

---

## Notes / Expected result
- The header should always show at the top because it’s **Dock=Top** and `tlpRoot` is **Dock=Fill**.
- The time updates every second because `tmrClock_Tick` calls `UpdateHeaderClock()`.

---

## @testsubject117

The Menu buttons have no text, only the hotkey representation. uploading a screenshot for you to review

## Copilot

Upload the screenshot and I’ll confirm, but based on your description (“buttons have no text, only the hotkey representation”), the most likely cause is:

- Your **button text color is the same as the button background** (so only a small part is visible), **or**
- You’re using the OS visual style and the theme is drawing text oddly, **or**
- The button **Text is being overwritten after creation** (unlikely here), **or**
- You’re seeing the **mnemonic/accelerator** only because the rest is clipped (width too small / layout issue).

Before the screenshot arrives, do these two fixes (they’re safe and usually solve it immediately):

## Fix 1: Force the button to show full text + proper colors
In `FormMainMenu.vb`, in `AddMenuButton`, set these properties:

```vbnet
btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
btn.BackColor = SystemColors.Control
btn.ForeColor = SystemColors.ControlText
btn.AutoEllipsis = True
btn.TextImageRelation = TextImageRelation.Overlay
```

So your `AddMenuButton` becomes:

```vbnet
Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
    Dim btn As New Button()

    btn.AutoSize = False
    btn.Height = 34
    btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
    btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
    btn.Tag = key
    btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

    btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
    btn.BackColor = SystemColors.Control
    btn.ForeColor = SystemColors.ControlText
    btn.AutoEllipsis = True

    AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

    panel.Controls.Add(btn)
End Sub
```

## Fix 2: Remove the “optional headers” (for now) and verify widths
Temporarily comment out these two lines in `BuildMenu()`:

```vbnet
'flpLeft.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Left)"))
'flpRight.Controls.Add(MakeHeaderLabel("Main Menu (Right)"))
```

Then run again. If the panel width calculation was being thrown off by the labels or layout, this can expose it.

## If it’s actually clipping (too narrow), force button width once after layout
In `OnLoad`, after `BuildMenu()`, add:

```vbnet
Me.PerformLayout()
ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
```

(WinForms sometimes reports a tiny `ClientSize.Width` on first pass until layout completes.)

---

### Next step
Please upload the screenshot, and also tell me:
1) Are the buttons the standard gray WinForms buttons, or custom themed?
2) Do you see the text if you click a button (focus rectangle) or resize the window?

Once I see the screenshot, I can tell whether it’s a **color/visual styles issue** vs. a **layout/clipping issue**, and give the exact final code change.

## @testsubject117

Describe this image

## Copilot



## @testsubject117

Describe this image

## Copilot

In your screenshot, the buttons are being created correctly, but they’re **far too narrow**, so the text is getting clipped and you only see the first part (e.g., `(A)`). On the right column you can see a little extra like `(Y) Ed`, which confirms it’s purely a **width/layout** problem, not a font/color problem. (Also note your “Main / Menu” header label is wrapping because it’s narrow too.) ![image2](image2)

### Why it’s happening
Your `ResizeButtonsToPanel()` uses `panel.ClientSize.Width`, but you call it **before the layout has finalized**, so the `ClientSize.Width` is still small. Then the buttons keep that tiny width.

---

## Fix (do this): resize after the form finishes laying out
Replace your `OnLoad` with this version (or add the shown lines). The key parts are:
- call `BeginInvoke` so resizing runs **after** WinForms layout completes
- also handle panel `SizeChanged` so it keeps correct widths

```vbnet
Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
    MyBase.OnLoad(e)

    Me.KeyPreview = True

    BuildMenu()

    lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
    UpdateHeaderClock()
    tmrClock.Interval = 1000
    tmrClock.Start()

    ' IMPORTANT: run resize after layout is finalized
    Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                  ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                  ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                              End Sub))
End Sub
```

### Add these two event handlers (recommended)
They ensure if the user resizes the window (or scrollbars appear), button widths stay correct:

```vbnet
Private Sub flpLeft_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpLeft.SizeChanged
    ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
End Sub

Private Sub flpRight_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpRight.SizeChanged
    ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
End Sub
```

---

## Also fix one thing in `ResizeButtonsToPanel`
You’re subtracting only 5 pixels; that’s a bit tight when scrollbars appear. Update this line:

```vbnet
Dim targetWidth As Integer = panel.ClientSize.Width - panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right - SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6
```

Full method:

```vbnet
Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
    If panel Is Nothing Then Return

    Dim targetWidth As Integer =
        panel.ClientSize.Width -
        panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
        SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

    If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

    For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
        Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
        Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

        If btn IsNot Nothing Then
            btn.Width = targetWidth
        ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
            lbl.Width = targetWidth
        End If
    Next
End Sub
```

---

### After this change
Your buttons will expand to the full panel width and you’ll see e.g.:
`(A) Shop Card Generator`, `(B) Invoice Generator`, etc.

If you want, I can also adjust the designer so the form opens larger by default (e.g., 1200×700), which will match the “big menu” look you’re going for.

## @testsubject117

ok, the date and time text needs to move down a line

## Copilot

Change the **alignment** and add a little **top padding** to `lblDateTime` so it sits lower inside the 48px header.

## Easiest fix (no custom painting): add padding + middle-right
In `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`, in the `lblDateTime` section, change:

```vbnet
Me.lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.TopRight
```

to:

```vbnet
Me.lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleRight
Me.lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 10, 0)
```

Also (optional but usually looks better), give it a little right padding even without the top move:

- right padding `10` (as above)

### Result
This effectively “moves it down a line” by pushing the text down ~10 pixels and centering vertically.

---

## Alternative (if you want *exactly* one text-line lower)
If you want it to behave like two lines (blank first line, date/time on second), you can force a leading newline in code:

```vbnet
lblDateTime.Text = Environment.NewLine & $"{now:dddd}  {now:MM-dd-yyyy}          {now:HH:mm:ss}"
```

But I recommend the padding/alignment approach because it’s cleaner and doesn’t change the text content.

Tell me which look you prefer (padding vs actual newline).

## @testsubject117

I would like for all buttons to be black with white text

## Copilot

Update your button creation in **`FormMainMenu.vb`** so every menu button is forced to a black background with white text.

Replace your current `AddMenuButton` with this:

```vbnet
Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
    Dim btn As New Button()

    btn.AutoSize = False
    btn.Height = 34
    btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
    btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
    btn.Tag = key
    btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

    ' Force black buttons with white text
    btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
    btn.BackColor = Color.Black
    btn.ForeColor = Color.White
    btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
    btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
    btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1

    ' Optional: nicer hover / click feedback
    btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
    btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)

    btn.AutoEllipsis = True

    AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

    panel.Controls.Add(btn)
End Sub
```

If you also want the **(focused)** button to be more visible (since the default focus rectangle is subtle on dark buttons), tell me and I’ll add a simple focus style (e.g., a yellow border on focus).

```vbnet
' -------------------------
```

```vbnet name=FormAbout.vb

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        ' Title labels are handled by the designer now:
        '   lblAbout  => "About:"
        '   lblAppName => app name (DarkRed)
        '   lblDevelopedBy => "Developed By:"

        ' If you want to adjust these at runtime, you can:
        ' lblAppName.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"

        ' Populate the info box (edit as desired)

        rtbInfo.AppendText("Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application" & Environment.NewLine)
        rtbInfo.AppendText(Environment.NewLine)
        rtbInfo.AppendText("Main Menu port / modernization UI." & Environment.NewLine)
        rtbInfo.AppendText(Environment.NewLine)
        rtbInfo.AppendText("Data folders:" & Environment.NewLine)
        rtbInfo.AppendText("  AppRoot:   " & AppPaths.AppRoot & Environment.NewLine)
        rtbInfo.AppendText("  DataDir:   " & AppPaths.DataDir & Environment.NewLine)
        rtbInfo.AppendText("  BackupDir: " & AppPaths.BackupDir & Environment.NewLine)
        rtbInfo.AppendText("  WordDocs:  " & AppPaths.WordDocsDir & Environment.NewLine)

        ' Optional: show a developer image if you have one.
        ' If you already set the image in the designer/resources, you can remove this block.
        Try
            If picDeveloper.Image Is Nothing Then
                ' Example: load from a shared location (edit/remove as needed)
                ' Dim candidate As String = "\\invoice\MainMenu\developer.png"
                ' If IO.File.Exists(candidate) Then picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromFile(candidate)
            End If
        Catch
            ' ignore image load failures
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

## @testsubject117

I need all of the buttons to have larger text

## Copilot

Set a larger `Font` on the buttons when you create them.

In **`FormMainMenu.vb`**, inside `AddMenuButton`, add (or change) the font line:

```vbnet
btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
```

Example `AddMenuButton` with the key parts:

```vbnet
Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
    Dim btn As New Button()

    btn.AutoSize = False
    btn.Height = 44   ' optional: increase height so bigger font fits
    btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))

    btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
    btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
    btn.Tag = key
    btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

    btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
    btn.BackColor = Color.Black
    btn.ForeColor = Color.White
    btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
    btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
    btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
    btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
    btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)

    btn.AutoEllipsis = True

    AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

    panel.Controls.Add(btn)
End Sub
```

If you tell me the exact font size you want (e.g., 14, 16, etc.), I can tune the recommended `Height` and padding so nothing clips.

## @testsubject117

Can the time at upper right be changed to 12 hr format with am or pm

## Copilot

Yes—change the time format string in your header update code.

In **`FormMainMenu.vb`**, find where you set the header date/time text (likely in `UpdateHeaderClock()`), and change the time portion from 24-hour (`HH:mm:ss`) to 12-hour with AM/PM (`hh:mm:ss tt`).

Example:

```vbnet
Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
    Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

    ' 12-hour time with AM/PM:
    lblDateTime.Text = $"{now:dddd}  {now:MM-dd-yyyy}          {now:hh:mm:ss tt}"
End Sub
```

This will display like: `02:16:06 PM` instead of `14:16:06`.

If you want “AM/PM” lowercase (`am/pm`), WinForms formats `tt` based on culture; we can force lowercase with `.ToLowerInvariant()` on just the time part if needed.

```vbnet

```

and you’re set.

## @testsubject117

how do I publish this so I can test it

## Copilot

To test it like a real installed app, publish a **Release build** and run it from the publish folder.

## Option A (recommended): Publish as a folder (no installer)
1. In Visual Studio, set the build to **Release**:
   - Top toolbar: change **Debug** → **Release**
2. Right‑click your project in **Solution Explorer** → **Publish…**
3. Choose **Folder** as the target.
4. Pick an output folder (example): `C:\Publish\MainMenuTest`
5. Click **Finish** (or **Next/Publish**).
6. When it completes, open the publish folder and run the generated **`.exe`**.

### If it asks deployment mode
- Choose **Self-contained** if you want to run it on machines without .NET installed (bigger output).
- Choose **Framework-dependent** if the target PC already has the right .NET Desktop Runtime.

## Option B: Quick test without publishing
1. Build **Release**
2. Run the compiled EXE from:
   - `bin\Release\<target>\` (for example `bin\Release\net6.0-windows\YourApp.exe`)

## What I need from you to give exact clicks
Tell me:
1) Are you using **VB.NET WinForms** on **.NET Framework (4.x)** or **.NET (6/7/8/9)**?  
2) What Visual Studio version (2022/2019)?  

Then I’ll give the exact publish steps for your project type (ClickOnce vs “Publish” vs Setup project).

## @testsubject117

how do I get version, file version and build date to update

## Copilot

You usually want **Version** and **File Version** to auto-increment, and **Build Date** to be generated at build time and shown in your About box.

Below are the common ways (pick the one that matches your project type).

---

## 1) Version + File Version (auto-increment)

### If your project is **.NET Framework WinForms** (classic)
1. In Solution Explorer: **My Project** → **Application** tab  
2. Click **Assembly Information…**
3. Set **Assembly version** and **File version** to something like:

- `1.0.*`

That `*` makes Visual Studio auto-generate build/revision numbers each build (so it changes automatically).

**Note:** This typically updates *only on build*, and the exact numbers are based on date/time-derived values.

### If your project is **SDK-style .NET (6/7/8/9) WinForms**
Edit your `.vbproj` and set:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <Version>1.2.0</Version>
  <FileVersion>1.2.0.0</FileVersion>
  <AssemblyVersion>1.2.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Auto-increment isn’t built-in the same “`1.0.*`” way for SDK-style projects; typical approach is CI/build scripts (GitVersion/Nerdbank.GitVersioning), but you *can* still manually bump these.

If you tell me which project type you have, I’ll give the exact best practice.

---

## 2) Build Date (updates every build)
The simplest reliable method: generate a build timestamp **at runtime** from the assembly file itself.

Add this helper somewhere (e.g., in `FormAbout.vb` or a module):

```vbnet
Private Function GetBuildDateTime() As DateTime
    Return IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(System.Reflection.Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().Location)
End Function
```

Then in `FormAbout_Load`, append:

```vbnet
Dim ver = System.Reflection.Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetName().Version
rtbInfo.AppendText(Environment.NewLine)
rtbInfo.AppendText("Version:      " & ver.ToString() & Environment.NewLine)
rtbInfo.AppendText("Build date:   " & GetBuildDateTime().ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm") & Environment.NewLine)
```

This updates every time the EXE is rebuilt/published because the file write time changes.

---

## 3) Display **File Version** separately (optional)
If you specifically want *File Version* (not assembly version):

```vbnet
Dim fvi = FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(Application.ExecutablePath)
rtbInfo.AppendText("File version: " & fvi.FileVersion & Environment.NewLine)
```

---

## Quick question (so I give you the exact steps)
Are you building this as **.NET Framework** (My Project → Assembly Information exists) or **.NET 6/7/8/9** (SDK-style `.vbproj` with `<TargetFramework>`)?

## @testsubject117

We'll revisit that later

## Copilot

Okay. When you’re ready, tell me which you’re using (**.NET Framework** vs **.NET 6/7/8/9**) and where you want the values displayed (About box only vs also title bar), and I’ll give you the exact edit steps and code.

In the meantime, the one approach that works in either project type without changing versioning is:
- **Build date** = `File.GetLastWriteTime(Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().Location)`
- **File version** = `FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(Application.ExecutablePath).FileVersion`

## @testsubject117

the about box states the shared paths data is ok, backups is ok, but word is not found

## Copilot

That means your code is successfully finding the **Data** and **Backup** shared folders, but whatever path you’re using for **Word** documents (your `WordDocsDir`) does **not exist** on that machine (or you don’t have access to it).

### What to check/fix (fast)
1. **Confirm the exact path the app is checking**
   - Open the About box and look for the line like:
     - `WordDocs:  ...`
   - That is the path your app is trying to use.

2. **Verify the folder exists**
   - Paste that path into Windows File Explorer.
   - If it’s a UNC path (`\\server\share\...`) make sure you can browse it.
   - If it’s a drive letter (`W:\...`) make sure that drive is mapped for your user.

3. **Common causes**
   - The “Word” folder name is different on this PC (e.g., `Word`, `WordDocs`, `WORDDOCS`, `Word Documents`).
   - The share isn’t available off-network/VPN.
   - You have permissions for Data/Backups but not the Word share.
   - The app is using a mapped drive letter that isn’t mapped when launched (especially if run elevated).

### Quick improvement (recommended)
Change the code that sets `WordDocsDir` so it tries **multiple candidates** (UNC first), and reports which it selected.

If you tell me what the About box shows for **WordDocs:** (copy/paste that one line), I can tell you exactly what to change in `AppPaths.WordDocsDir` (or wherever you define it) to make it resolve correctly.

## @testsubject117

on all machines when I double click the exe to launch it I get a Publisher could not be verified message and prompted to run

## Copilot

That prompt is Windows SmartScreen / “Open File – Security Warning” and it happens when the EXE **is not digitally signed** (and/or it has a “Mark of the Web” because it came from a network share, email, or download). To get rid of it consistently across machines, you need to **sign the EXE** (best) or change how/where it’s delivered (workarounds).

## Best fix: code sign the EXE
1. Get a **Code Signing Certificate** (commercial CA, or an internal enterprise CA if your org uses one).
2. After building/publishing, sign the output EXE (and any installer) with **signtool**.
   - Typical command (example):
     ```powershell
     signtool sign /fd SHA256 /tr http://timestamp.digicert.com /td SHA256 /a "path\to\YourApp.exe"
     ```
3. Distribute the signed EXE. SmartScreen reputation improves over time; with a trusted cert it generally stops warning much sooner.

If you use ClickOnce/MSIX/Installer, you sign the deployment package too (not just the EXE).

## Workarounds (don’t require a cert)
### A) If you’re launching from a network share
- Windows often warns when running executables from `\\server\share\...`.
- Copy the EXE locally (e.g., `C:\Apps\YourApp\`) and run it, or deploy with an installer.

### B) Unblock the file (if it was downloaded)
On the machine where you built/copied it:
1. Right-click the EXE → **Properties**
2. If you see **Unblock**, click it → Apply

For multiple files via PowerShell:
```powershell
Unblock-File "C:\Path\YourApp.exe"
```

### C) Use Group Policy (enterprise)
If this is internal-only, IT can reduce prompts via policy (SmartScreen, attachment manager, trusted zones). This is environment-specific and usually not recommended vs signing.

## Quick question so I can tell you the exact path
When the warning appears, does it say:
- **“Windows protected your PC”** (SmartScreen blue window), or
- **“Open File – Security Warning / Publisher could not be verified”** (classic dialog)?

And are you running it from:
- a **network share** (`\\...`) or
- a **local folder** (`C:\...`)?

Those two details determine the most effective fix for your situation.

## @testsubject117

ok, I unchecked the box always ask this and now it doesn't do it anymore

## Copilot

That makes sense: you cleared the per-file “Open File – Security Warning” prompt for that specific EXE on that machine.

A couple important notes so it doesn’t surprise you later:

- That checkbox setting is **stored per file** (and can change if you rebuild/republish and copy a *new* EXE, or if you download it again). A newly copied EXE may prompt again.
- If you distribute via a **network share/email/download**, Windows may re-mark the file and re-enable the prompt.

If you want it to *never* happen across machines/users without relying on that checkbox, the durable solution is still **code signing** (or packaging via an installer like MSIX/ClickOnce and signing that).

2) **Add proper version / file version / build date into the About box** so when you test on multiple PCs you can instantly confirm which build is running (even if we don’t do auto-increment yet).

Which one do you want to do first—**WordDocs path** or **Version/Build info**?

Do this in two parts: (A) make version auto-update via AssemblyInfo, and (B) display **Version / File Version / Build Date** in both the **About box** and **main menu title**.

---

## A) Make Version + File Version update (auto-increment) — .NET Framework
In Visual Studio:

1. **Project → Properties** (or **My Project**)  
2. **Application** tab → click **Assembly Information…**
3. Set:
   - **Assembly version**: `1.0.*`
   - **File version**: `1.0.*`  (or set this explicitly if VS won’t accept `*` here)

Click OK and rebuild.

*Note:* `1.0.*` will auto-generate build/revision numbers when you compile.

---

## B) Code to display Version / File Version / Build Date

### 1) Add a small helper module (recommended)
Create a new file: **`BuildInfo.vb`**

```vbnet name=BuildInfo.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Diagnostics
Imports System.Reflection

Public Module BuildInfo

    Public Function AssemblyVersionString() As String
        Dim v = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().GetName().Version
        Return If(v Is Nothing, "", v.ToString())
    End Function

    Public Function FileVersionString() As String
        Dim fvi = FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(Application.ExecutablePath)
        Return If(fvi Is Nothing, "", fvi.FileVersion)
    End Function

    Public Function BuildDateTime() As DateTime
        ' Uses the EXE last write time (updates on rebuild/publish)
        Dim exePath = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly().Location
        Return IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(exePath)
    End Function

End Module
```

---

### 2) Update **FormAbout.vb** to show it
In your **`FormAbout_Load`**, append these lines (near the top of the info text is fine):

```vbnet
rtbInfo.AppendText("Assembly Version: " & BuildInfo.AssemblyVersionString() & Environment.NewLine)
rtbInfo.AppendText("File Version:     " & BuildInfo.FileVersionString() & Environment.NewLine)
rtbInfo.AppendText("Build Date:       " & BuildInfo.BuildDateTime().ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm") & Environment.NewLine)
rtbInfo.AppendText(Environment.NewLine)
```

(Keep the rest of your About text after that.)

---

### 3) Update the **Main Menu title** to include version/build date
Wherever you set the main title label (for example `lblTitle.Text = ...` in `FormMainMenu_Load`), set it like this:

```vbnet
lblTitle.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application" &
                "  v" & BuildInfo.AssemblyVersionString() &
                "  (" & BuildInfo.BuildDateTime().ToString("yyyy-MM-dd") & ")"
```

If your title is in the **form caption** instead of a label, use:

```vbnet
Me.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application" &
          "  v" & BuildInfo.AssemblyVersionString() &
          "  (" & BuildInfo.BuildDateTime().ToString("yyyy-MM-dd") & ")"
```

---

### One question so I point you to the exact line:
On the main menu, is the “title” you mean a **Label** (like `lblTitle`) or the **window caption** (`Me.Text`)? If you tell me the control name, I’ll give the exact replacement line for your case.

## @testsubject117

Question: once we're done compiling the entire visual basic app, what will we need to do to get the visual basic app updated to the latest data found in the production machine .bas files?

## Copilot

You’ll need a repeatable **data-refresh pipeline** from the production machine’s `.bas` files into your VB.NET app. Exactly what that looks like depends on what those `.bas` files really are in your environment (in many shops they’re *BASIC source code*; in others they’re treated like *data/config scripts*).

Here are the practical options, in order of “most maintainable”:

## 1) Best long-term: stop treating `.bas` as “data” (convert to a real data format)
If the `.bas` files contain tables/parameters (limits, part numbers, recipes, menu text, etc.), convert them once into something like:
- JSON / XML / INI, or
- a small SQLite database, or
- CSV files

Then your VB.NET app loads that format at runtime from a shared location.

**Outcome:** Updating “latest production data” becomes “drop new JSON/XML/DB on the share”, no recompile needed.

## 2) If you must keep `.bas` as the source of truth: extract data at build time
Make a **build step** that pulls the latest `.bas` files from the production machine/share and generates a VB.NET-friendly output:
- a `.vb` module with constants/arrays, or
- embedded resource files, or
- JSON/XML shipped with the app

This is basically: **Production `.bas` → generator → your app artifacts → build/publish**.

**Outcome:** Every time you publish, it bakes in the latest production `.bas`-derived data (but still requires a rebuild to refresh).

## 3) Easiest operationally: load the production `.bas` files at runtime
Your VB.NET app reads the `.bas` files directly from the production machine (or a replicated share) when it starts.

**Outcome:** No rebuild needed to pick up updates.  
**Tradeoffs:** You must handle:
- file access permissions
- offline/VPN scenarios
- file locking / partial writes (need retry + read-only copies)
- validation (don’t crash if a file changes format)

Often the best version of this is: production machine writes to a “staging” folder, then atomically copies to a “published” folder that clients read.

## 4) Hybrid (common): production `.bas` → nightly export → shared “data package”
A scheduled job (Task Scheduler) on the production machine exports/compiles the `.bas` content into a stable “data package” (JSON/CSV/SQLite) in a network share. Your app consumes the package.

**Outcome:** reliable updates without rebuilding, and you don’t read raw `.bas` on client machines.

---

## What I need from you to recommend the right one
1) What are the `.bas` files exactly in your case?
   - Are they **VB6/QBasic source modules** that contain constants and code?
   - Or are they “data files” in a BASIC-like syntax?
2) Do you want the app to update **without republishing** (automatic), or is it okay that updates happen **only when you publish a new build**?
3) Where do the `.bas` files live (local disk on production PC, or a network share path like `\\server\share\...`)?

If you paste a small representative snippet (10–30 lines) from one `.bas` (remove sensitive values), I can tell you the cleanest extraction/loading approach and sketch the exact VB.NET code to implement it.

## @testsubject117

.bas files have to stay same. we will have to import the latest data from .bas files at the time of completion and test. does that work?

## Copilot

Yes—that works, as long as you’re clear about **what “import” means operationally**:

- If you import the latest `.bas` data **at completion/test time** and **embed it into the compiled app**, then you’ll need to **re-import + rebuild/re-publish** any time production `.bas` changes.
- If you import the latest `.bas` data **at completion/test time** but keep it as **external files the app reads at runtime**, then you can keep the `.bas` files unchanged and simply **replace/copy the `.bas` files** in the app’s data folder for testing (no rebuild required).

Given what you said (“have to stay same” and “import at time of completion and test”), the simplest, safest workflow is usually:

## Recommended workflow: “Import by copying” (runtime load; no rebuild)
1) Decide a single “authoritative” production folder, e.g.  
   `\\PRODPC\Share\BasData\`

2) When you’re ready to test, copy the current `.bas` set to your test location, e.g.  
   `\\TESTSHARE\AppData\BasData\` (or `C:\ProgramData\YourApp\BasData\`)

3) Configure your app to look for `.bas` files in **one configurable path**:
   - `app.config` setting like `BasDataPath`
   - default to a known share, but allow override for testing

4) On startup (or when opening a screen that needs it), the app:
   - reads the `.bas` files,
   - parses the values it needs,
   - validates them (missing keys/format),
   - then runs using those values.

**Result:** “Import latest data” is just “copy latest `.bas` files” before testing.

---

## Alternative workflow: “Import by generating VB module” (baked into the app)
1) At completion/test time, run an “import” script/tool that:
   - reads the `.bas` files
   - generates a `BasDataGenerated.vb` (constants/arrays/etc.)
2) Rebuild the app and test.

**Result:** No runtime parsing, but you must rebuild to refresh.

---

## Quick decision question
When you say “import”, do you want:
1) **No rebuild** during testing (just copy updated `.bas` files), or  
2) It’s fine to **rebuild** once at the end to bake them in?

If you answer that, I can give you the exact implementation plan (either: `app.config` + runtime loader/parser, or: a generator step + where to hook it into the build).

## @testsubject117

I tried changing the version by right clicking the project, then properties, application tab, changing the versions to assembly version 1.0.* and file version 1.0.*. There is no OK button, but if I right click and choose rebuild on the project, I get errors: BC37330 the specified version string contains wildcards which are not compatible with determinism and BC42366 the specified version string does not conform to the recommended format - major.minor.build.revision

## Copilot

Those errors are expected in modern VB builds when **deterministic compilation** is enabled: `1.0.*` (wildcard versioning) relies on the compiler generating version parts from date/time, which is **not compatible with determinism**, so the build fails with **BC37330**. The BC42366 is basically “don’t use `*` here / format not recommended”.

### Fix option 1 (recommended): keep determinism, stop using `*`
Do **not** use `1.0.*`. Instead:

1. Set versions to explicit values, e.g.
   - Assembly version: `1.0.0.0`
   - File version: `1.0.0.0`

2. Then, if you still want “changes every build”, show **Build Date** in the About box (and title) using the EXE timestamp (that updates every build) — no wildcard needed.

This is the least painful and works reliably.

### Fix option 2: disable determinism so `1.0.*` works again
If you truly want wildcard auto-incrementing:

1. Open your **project properties** (or the `.vbproj` in a text editor).
2. Set **Deterministic** to false. Depending on project style, you can do one of these:

**A) In the `.vbproj`** add/set:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <Deterministic>false</Deterministic>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Then rebuild. Now `1.0.*` should compile.

(Where this goes exactly depends on your project file style, but inside a `<PropertyGroup>` is fine.)

### About the missing “OK” button
The Application tab changes are saved immediately—there often isn’t an OK button; closing the properties window is effectively “OK”.

---

### What I suggest you do
For your testing needs, I’d do **Option 1**:
- Keep versions explicit (no `*`)
- Display **Build Date** + **File Version** in About/title (changes every build anyway)

If you want, paste your **.vbproj** contents (or tell me VS version + whether it’s SDK-style or classic .NET Framework project), and I’ll tell you exactly where to put `<Deterministic>false</Deterministic>` if you prefer Option 2.

## @testsubject117

is this replacing code or do I add it to what exists?: Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
Dim asmVer = asm.GetName().Version.ToString()

Dim fvi = FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(Application.ExecutablePath)
Dim fileVer = fvi.FileVersion

Dim buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location)

rtbInfo.AppendText("Assembly Version: " & asmVer & Environment.NewLine)
rtbInfo.AppendText("File Version:     " & fileVer & Environment.NewLine)

rtbInfo.AppendText(Environment.NewLine)

## Copilot

Add it to what exists (don’t replace your whole `FormAbout_Load`).

### Where to put it
Put that block **inside** your `FormAbout_Load` (or wherever you build the About text), **before** you append the rest of your existing About information—so the version/build info appears near the top.

Typical pattern:

```vbnet
Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
    rtbInfo.Clear()

    ' ADD this block near the top:
    Dim asm = Reflection.Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
    Dim asmVer = asm.GetName().Version.ToString()

    Dim fvi = Diagnostics.FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(Application.ExecutablePath)
    Dim fileVer = fvi.FileVersion

    Dim buildDate = IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location)

    rtbInfo.AppendText("Assembly Version: " & asmVer & Environment.NewLine)
    rtbInfo.AppendText("File Version:     " & fileVer & Environment.NewLine)
    rtbInfo.AppendText("Build Date:       " & buildDate.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm") & Environment.NewLine)
    rtbInfo.AppendText(Environment.NewLine)

    ' then keep your existing lines that append paths/status/etc below...
    ' rtbInfo.AppendText("Shared Paths Data: OK" & Environment.NewLine)
    ' ...
End Sub
```

### Notes
- If your About box uses a **Label** (like `lblInfo.Text = ...`) instead of `rtbInfo.AppendText`, tell me and I’ll adapt it.
- You can avoid adding `Imports ...` by using the fully-qualified names like I did above (`Reflection.`, `Diagnostics.`, `IO.`).

## @testsubject117

Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Diagnostics
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Const AppTitle As String =
    "About:" & vbCrLf & """Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"""

    Private Const AuthorName As String = "Kirk Saffell"
    Private Const DeveloperCompany As String = "Enterprise Services & Consulting"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "capnkirk@capnkirk.com"
    Private Const AuthorPhone As String = "(661) 478-0990"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        ' Title handled by designer labels now

        LoadDeveloperImage()

        Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()


        Dim product As String = Application.ProductName
        Dim ver As String = asm.GetName().Version?.ToString()

        Dim fileVer As String = ""
        Try
            fileVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyFileVersionAttribute)()?.Version
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim infoVer As String = ""
        Try
            infoVer = asm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim buildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            buildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(asm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            "",
            "",
            $"{product}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(ver), "(unknown)", ver)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fileVer), "(not set)", fileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(infoVer), "(not set)", infoVer)}",
            $"Build date: {buildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}",
            $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

        rtbInfo.SelectAll()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
        rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

        rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Try
            If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

            Dim asm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
            Dim names = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

            Dim resName As String = Nothing

            ' Most common case: spaces become underscores in the manifest name
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If

            ' Fallbacks
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                         (n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) OrElse
                                                          n.EndsWith(".png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
            End If

            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using s = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
                If s Is Nothing Then
                    picDeveloper.Visible = False
                    Return
                End If

                Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                    s.CopyTo(ms)
                    ms.Position = 0
                    picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
                End Using
            End Using

            picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
            picDeveloper.Visible = True

        Catch ex As Exception
#If DEBUG Then
            MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")
#End If
            If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class

## Copilot

Here’s a full replacement **`FormAbout.vb`** that keeps your existing behavior but fixes the “already defined” problem by:

- using unique variable names (`aboutAsm`, `aboutVer`, etc.)
- getting **File Version** via `FileVersionInfo` (more reliable than attributes)
- keeping your centered RichTextBox output and shared paths check exactly as you had it

```vbnet name=FormAbout.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Diagnostics
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Const AppTitle As String =
    "About:" & vbCrLf & """Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"""

    Private Const AuthorName As String = "Kirk Saffell"
    Private Const DeveloperCompany As String = "Enterprise Services & Consulting"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "capnkirk@capnkirk.com"
    Private Const AuthorPhone As String = "(661) 478-0990"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        ' Title handled by designer labels now
        LoadDeveloperImage()

        Dim aboutAsm As Assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim aboutProduct As String = Application.ProductName

        Dim aboutVer As String = ""
        Try
            aboutVer = aboutAsm.GetName().Version?.ToString()
        Catch
            aboutVer = ""
        End Try

        ' File Version (best pulled from the built EXE metadata)
        Dim aboutFileVer As String = ""
        Try
            Dim fvi As FileVersionInfo = FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(Application.ExecutablePath)
            aboutFileVer = fvi.FileVersion
        Catch
            aboutFileVer = ""
        End Try

        ' Informational Version (optional; often blank in .NET Framework unless set)
        Dim aboutInfoVer As String = ""
        Try
            aboutInfoVer = aboutAsm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
            aboutInfoVer = ""
        End Try

        ' Build date based on EXE last write time (updates each rebuild/publish)
        Dim aboutBuildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            aboutBuildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(aboutAsm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
            aboutBuildDate = "unknown"
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            "",
            "",
            $"{aboutProduct}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(aboutVer), "(unknown)", aboutVer)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(aboutFileVer), "(not set)", aboutFileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(aboutInfoVer), "(not set)", aboutInfoVer)}",
            $"Build date: {aboutBuildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}",
            $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

        rtbInfo.SelectAll()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
        rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

        rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Try
            If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

            Dim imgAsm As Assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
            Dim names = imgAsm.GetManifestResourceNames()

            Dim resName As String = Nothing

            ' Most common case: spaces become underscores in the manifest name
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If

            ' Fallbacks
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                         (n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) OrElse
                                                          n.EndsWith(".png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
            End If

            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using s = imgAsm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
                If s Is Nothing Then
                    picDeveloper.Visible = False
                    Return
                End If

                Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                    s.CopyTo(ms)
                    ms.Position = 0
                    picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
                End Using
            End Using

            picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
            picDeveloper.Visible = True

        Catch ex As Exception
#If DEBUG Then
            MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")
#End If
            If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

### After you replace it
- Rebuild.
- Open About: you should now see Version/File Version/Build date populated.
- If **File Version** still shows `(not set)`, that means the project’s file version isn’t being stamped—then we’ll set it explicitly in AssemblyInfo / Project properties.

If you want, paste the *main menu title code* next and I’ll show you the cleanest way to add `vX.Y.Z` + build date there too (without duplicating logic).

```vbnet
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Diagnostics
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Const AppTitle As String =
    "About:" & vbCrLf & """Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"""

    Private Const AuthorName As String = "Kirk Saffell"
    Private Const DeveloperCompany As String = "Enterprise Services & Consulting"
    Private Const ContactEmail As String = "capnkirk@capnkirk.com"
    Private Const AuthorPhone As String = "(661) 478-0990"
    Private Const CopyrightYear As String = "2026"

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        ' Title handled by designer labels now
        LoadDeveloperImage()

        Dim aboutAsm As Assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim aboutProduct As String = Application.ProductName

        Dim aboutVer As String = ""
        Try
            aboutVer = aboutAsm.GetName().Version?.ToString()
        Catch
            aboutVer = ""
        End Try

        ' File Version (best pulled from the built EXE metadata)
        Dim aboutFileVer As String = ""
        Try
            Dim fvi As FileVersionInfo = FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(Application.ExecutablePath)
            aboutFileVer = fvi.FileVersion
        Catch
            aboutFileVer = ""
        End Try

        ' Informational Version (optional; often blank in .NET Framework unless set)
        Dim aboutInfoVer As String = ""
        Try
            aboutInfoVer = aboutAsm.GetCustomAttribute(Of AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute)()?.InformationalVersion
        Catch
            aboutInfoVer = ""
        End Try

        ' Build date based on EXE last write time (updates each rebuild/publish)
        Dim aboutBuildDate As String = "unknown"
        Try
            aboutBuildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(aboutAsm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss")
        Catch
            aboutBuildDate = "unknown"
        End Try

        Dim shareOk As Func(Of String, String) =
            Function(p As String)
                Try
                    Return If(Directory.Exists(p), "OK", "NOT FOUND")
                Catch
                    Return "ERROR"
                End Try
            End Function

        Dim lines As New List(Of String) From {
            "",
            "",
            $"{aboutProduct}",
            $"Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(aboutVer), "(unknown)", aboutVer)}",
            $"File Version: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(aboutFileVer), "(not set)", aboutFileVer)}",
            $"Build: {If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(aboutInfoVer), "(not set)", aboutInfoVer)}",
            $"Build date: {aboutBuildDate}",
            "",
            $"Author/Developer: {AuthorName}",
            $"Company: {DeveloperCompany}",
            $"Phone: {AuthorPhone}",
            $"Contact: {ContactEmail}",
            $"Year: {CopyrightYear}",
            "",
            $"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}",
            $"User: {Environment.UserName}",
            $"OS: {Environment.OSVersion}",
            $"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}",
            "",
            "Shared paths:",
            $"  Data:    {AppPaths.DataDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.DataDir)}]",
            $"  Backups: {AppPaths.BackupDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.BackupDir)}]",
            $"  Word:    {AppPaths.WordDocsDir}  [{shareOk(AppPaths.WordDocsDir)}]"
        }

        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.Text = String.Join(Environment.NewLine, lines)

        rtbInfo.SelectAll()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center
        rtbInfo.DeselectAll()

        rtbInfo.SelectionStart = 0
        rtbInfo.SelectionLength = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDeveloperImage()
        Try
            If picDeveloper Is Nothing Then Return

            Dim imgAsm As Assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
            Dim names = imgAsm.GetManifestResourceNames()

            Dim resName As String = Nothing

            ' Most common case: spaces become underscores in the manifest name
            resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(".ESC_Logo.png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If

            ' Fallbacks
            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                resName = names.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.IndexOf("esc", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0 AndAlso
                                                         (n.EndsWith(".jpg", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) OrElse
                                                          n.EndsWith(".png", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)))
            End If

            If String.IsNullOrEmpty(resName) Then
                picDeveloper.Visible = False
                Return
            End If

            Using s = imgAsm.GetManifestResourceStream(resName)
                If s Is Nothing Then
                    picDeveloper.Visible = False
                    Return
                End If

                Using ms As New MemoryStream()
                    s.CopyTo(ms)
                    ms.Position = 0
                    picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromStream(ms)
                End Using
            End Using

            picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
            picDeveloper.Visible = True

        Catch ex As Exception
#If DEBUG Then
            MessageBox.Show(ex.ToString(), "LoadDeveloperImage error")
#End If
            If picDeveloper IsNot Nothing Then picDeveloper.Visible = False
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

You get the “main menu title” code from the **main menu form**, not the About form.

### Where to look
1) In **Solution Explorer**, open the form that is your main menu (often named something like):
- `FormMainMenu.vb`
- `MainMenu.vb`
- `frmMain.vb`

2) In that file, look for one of these places:
- `Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load(...) Handles MyBase.Load`
- `Shown` event (`Handles MyBase.Shown`)
- A method named like `InitUI()`, `SetupUI()`, `UpdateHeaderClock()`

3) Search inside that file for any of these strings:
- `Me.Text =`  (this is the window title/caption)
- `.Text = "Active Magnetic` (or whatever your title text is)
- `lblTitle.Text =` / `lblHeader.Text =` (if you use a label at the top of the form)

### Fastest way
Press **Ctrl+Shift+F** (Find in Files) and search for:
- `Active Magnetic Inspection`
or search for:
- `Me.Text =`

Whichever file contains that is where the “main menu title code lines” live.

If you paste the snippet that sets the title (even 10–20 lines around it), I’ll show you exactly how to add the version + build date there.

## @testsubject117

I am not finding the main menu title code

## Copilot

The “main menu title” might not be set in code at all—it’s often set in the **Designer** (so there’s nothing to find in `.vb`).

Here are the quickest ways to locate it.

## 1) Check the form’s caption (window title) in the Designer
1. Open your main menu form in **Design** view (double‑click the form, then click the designer surface).
2. Click the **form background** (not a button/label).
3. Press **F4** to open the **Properties** window.
4. Look for the property **Text**.
   - Whatever is in **Text** is the window title.
   - If it’s set here, there may be **no code line** like `Me.Text = ...`.

If you want to add version/build date, you’ll add code in the form’s `Load` event to override this.

## 2) The title might be a Label, not the form caption
If you have a big title at the top of the form, click that label in the designer and check:
- its **(Name)** (e.g., `lblTitle`, `lblHeader`)
- its **Text**

If you update it in code, you’ll use that control name.

## 3) Use “Find All References” / search for the control name
If you find a label called `lblTitle`:
- Right‑click `lblTitle` in the designer (or click it, then go to Properties window)
- Copy the **(Name)** value
- Press **Ctrl+Shift+F** and search for `lblTitle.Text`

## 4) If nothing shows up, you may be using a custom header/usercontrol
Sometimes the “title” is inside a `Panel` / `UserControl` at the top. In that case:
- In the designer, click the header area and look at the **(Name)** in Properties
- If it’s something like `ucHeader1` / `HeaderControl1`, the title is inside that control’s code.

---

### Fastest next step
Open the main menu form in the designer and tell me **one** of these:

- The form’s **Name** (top of Properties window) and its **Text** property value, **or**
- The title label’s **Name** (e.g., `lblTitle`) and whether it’s a Label/RichTextBox/etc.

Then I’ll give you the exact `Form_Load` code to set either `Me.Text` or `lblTitle.Text` to include:
`v<assembly version> (<yyyy-mm-dd>)`.

## @testsubject117

here is contents of FormMainMenu.vb (I'm not seeing Private Sub FormMainMenu_Load in it: Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True

        BuildMenu()

        lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
        UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        tmrClock.Start()

        ' IMPORTANT: run resize after layout is finalized
        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub
    Private Sub flpLeft_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpLeft.SizeChanged
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
    End Sub

    Private Sub flpRight_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpRight.SizeChanged
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub
    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick
        UpdateHeaderClock()
    End Sub

    Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now = DateTime.Now
        lblDateTime.Text = $"{now:dddd}  {now:MM-dd-yyyy}          {now:hh:mm:ss tt}"
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        ' Left column
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (removed: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        lbl.Width = 1000 ' will be resized later
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        ' Force black buttons with white text
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1

        ' Optional: nicer hover / click feedback
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)

        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
        panel.ClientSize.Width -
        panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
        SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")

            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try

            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class

## Copilot

Your “main menu title” *is* being set in code already—just not as a form caption. It’s this line in `OnLoad`:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
```

So you have two “titles” you can update:

1) **The big on-screen title label**: `lblMainMenu.Text`  
2) **The window title bar caption**: `Me.Text` (currently not set in this file, so it stays whatever the designer set)

### Add version + build date (safe, minimal change)
Add these `Imports` at the top (since this file currently only imports System/Drawing/Windows.Forms):

```vbnet
Imports System.Diagnostics
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.IO
```

Then replace the single line:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
```

with:

```vbnet
Dim mainAsm = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
Dim mainVer = mainAsm.GetName().Version?.ToString()
Dim mainBuildDate = File.GetLastWriteTime(mainAsm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")

lblMainMenu.Text = $"MAIN MENU   v{mainVer}   ({mainBuildDate})"
Me.Text = $"Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application   v{mainVer}   ({mainBuildDate})"
```

That will update both the visible label and the window caption.

### Why this won’t conflict with “already defined”
These variable names (`mainAsm`, `mainVer`, `mainBuildDate`) don’t exist elsewhere in your `OnLoad`, so you won’t get the “already defined” error.

---

If you want the clock line (`lblDateTime.Text`) to also show the version (optional), tell me and I’ll show the cleanest way without making it redraw oddly every second.

## @testsubject117

when I replace that I get bc30451 file is not declared it may be inaccessbile due to its protection level and also BC37251 and BC30205

## Copilot

Those errors are because `File`, string interpolation (`$"..."`), and/or the null-conditional (`?.`) aren’t available with your current settings.

### What’s going on
- **BC30451 “File is not declared”**: you didn’t import `System.IO`, *or* you’re on a VB language version where it’s not resolving. Easiest fix: use `IO.File` (fully qualified) so no Imports are required.
- **BC37251 / BC30205**: these commonly show up when VB doesn’t understand **string interpolation** (`$"..."`) and/or the **null conditional** (`?.`). That usually means your project is using an older VB compiler/language level.

### Fix: use “old VB-safe” code (no `$"..."`, no `?.`, no `File` symbol)
Replace your `lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"` line with this block (exactly), and **don’t add any Imports**:

```vbnet
Dim mainAsm As System.Reflection.Assembly = System.Reflection.Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

Dim mainVer As String = ""
Try
    mainVer = mainAsm.GetName().Version.ToString()
Catch
    mainVer = "(unknown)"
End Try

Dim mainBuildDate As String = ""
Try
    mainBuildDate = System.IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(mainAsm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
Catch
    mainBuildDate = "(unknown)"
End Try

lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
Me.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
```

That avoids:
- `File` needing an import (uses `System.IO.File`)
- `$"..."` interpolation
- `?.` null conditional

### If you still see errors
Tell me the *exact* text for:
- **BC37251** line (it usually points at a specific character)
- Which Visual Studio version you’re compiling with (2010/2013/2015/2017/2019/2022)

…and I’ll match the syntax to what your compiler supports.

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36

        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft

        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)
