        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
        panel.ClientSize.Width -
        panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
        SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")

            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try

            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class

## Copilot

You’re in good shape—your main menu now shows version + build date in both **`lblMainMenu`** and the **window title (`Me.Text`)**.

Two quick cleanups I recommend based on the code you pasted:

## 1) Fix `UpdateHeaderClock()` to avoid string interpolation (so it matches your older-compiler-safe style)
Right now you still have:

```vbnet
lblDateTime.Text = $"{now:dddd}  {now:MM-dd-yyyy}          {now:hh:mm:ss tt}"
```

If your environment previously threw interpolation-related errors, this line can bite you later. Replace `UpdateHeaderClock()` with:

```vbnet
Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
    Dim now = DateTime.Now
    lblDateTime.Text = now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                       now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                       now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
End Sub
```

## 2) Optional: also show File Version (instead of Assembly version) on the main menu
If you prefer the **File Version** (what Windows shows in file properties) rather than Assembly version, swap the version part to:

```vbnet
Dim fvi As System.Diagnostics.FileVersionInfo = System.Diagnostics.FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(Application.ExecutablePath)
Dim mainVer As String = fvi.FileVersion
```

(Keep the rest the same.)

---

### Confirm the UI intent
Do you want the main menu label to stay as **“MAIN MENU vX (date)”**, or would you rather keep the label just **“MAIN MENU”** and only put version/date in the **window title bar**?

## Copilot

You currently have **two** `UpdateHeaderClock()` methods in the *same* `FormMainMenu` partial class (very likely one in `FormMainMenu.vb` and another in `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`, or another thread-scoped file for the same form). That’s why VB says “multiple definitions with identical signatures”, and why calls like `UpdateHeaderClock()` become ambiguous (“overload resolution failed”).

### Fix (best): edit the existing method, don’t add a new one
1) In Visual Studio press **Ctrl+Shift+F** (Find in Files)
2) Search for:
   - `Sub UpdateHeaderClock`
3) You’ll see **2 results**. Open both and you’ll find two blocks like:

```vbnet
Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
    ...
End Sub
```

4) **Keep only one**. In the one you want to keep, change its body to the non-interpolated version:

```vbnet
Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
    Dim now = DateTime.Now
    lblDateTime.Text = now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                       now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                       now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
End Sub
```

5) In the other file, either:
- delete the duplicate method entirely, **or**
- rename it (e.g., `UpdateHeaderClock_OLD`) if you want to keep it temporarily.

### If you can’t find the duplicate easily
In the editor, right-click `UpdateHeaderClock` (on the method name or call site) → **Go To Definition**. If it takes you to one, repeat from another call site; or use Find in Files as above.

### Important: don’t put helper methods in the Designer file
If one of the duplicates is in `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`, delete/move it to `FormMainMenu.vb`. Designer files can get regenerated.

---

If you paste the second `UpdateHeaderClock()` definition (the one you *didn’t* edit) and the file it’s in, I’ll tell you exactly which one to remove and the safest way to do it without fighting the designer.

ok, here is what I have now: Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True

        BuildMenu()

        Dim mainAsm As System.Reflection.Assembly = System.Reflection.Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim mainVer As String = ""
        Try
            mainVer = mainAsm.GetName().Version.ToString()
        Catch
            mainVer = "(unknown)"
        End Try

        Dim mainBuildDate As String = ""
        Try
            mainBuildDate = System.IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(mainAsm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
        Catch
            mainBuildDate = "(unknown)"
        End Try

        lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
        Me.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
        UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        tmrClock.Start()

        ' IMPORTANT: run resize after layout is finalized
        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Private Sub flpLeft_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpLeft.SizeChanged
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
    End Sub

    Private Sub flpRight_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpRight.SizeChanged
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub
    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick
        UpdateHeaderClock()
    End Sub
    Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now = DateTime.Now
        lblDateTime.Text = now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                       now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                       now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub
    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        ' Left column
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (removed: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        lbl.Width = 1000 ' will be resized later
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        ' Force black buttons with white text
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1

        ' Optional: nicer hover / click feedback
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)

        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
        panel.ClientSize.Width -
        panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
        SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")

            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try

            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class

## Copilot

This `FormMainMenu.vb` looks correct now (your `UpdateHeaderClock()` is the non-interpolated version and should compile fine).

The only remaining landmine I see is that you **still have string interpolation** here:

```vbnet
btn.Text = $"({key}) {text}"
```

If your compiler is old enough to choke on `$"..."` (which it did earlier), you should change that line too, otherwise you may get BC37251/BC30205 again later.

Replace just that one line with:

```vbnet
btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
```

Everything else in the file is already “old VB-safe”.

### If you still get the “multiple definitions” error
That means there is *still* another `Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()` in some other `Partial Class FormMainMenu` file (not the Designer file you pasted). To find it:

1) **Ctrl+Shift+F**  
2) search: `Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock`  
3) delete the duplicate one.

If you paste the search results list (filenames) or the other file contents, I can tell you exactly which one to keep/remove.

   - press **Ctrl+Shift+F**
   - search `Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock`
   - you should have **exactly 1** result across the whole solution.
3) If you see any error like **BC37251** again:
   - it’s almost always another `$"..."` somewhere else.
   - search the solution for ` $"` (space then dollar quote) and also just `$"`.

If you want, paste the build errors (if any) after rebuild and I’ll point to the exact remaining line(s).

- **No wildcard versioning**, so no determinism-related build errors

If you want the **About box** and **Main menu** to always match formatting exactly (same version/date logic), next step would be to put the version/date retrieval into a single shared helper (e.g., `BuildInfo.vb`) and call it from both forms—but only if you want that cleanup.

## @testsubject117

here is a screenshot of the running code. I see the version number and build date after the window title, however I do not see the version or build after the Main Menu title in the app

## Copilot

Most likely `lblMainMenu` is being **clipped**: in your designer it has a fixed width/max width of **400 px**, so when you set a long string (`MAIN MENU   v... (date)`), only “MAIN MENU” fits and the rest is cut off.

In your `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`, you currently have (from what you pasted earlier):

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.MaximumSize = New Size(400, 50)
lblMainMenu.MinimumSize = New Size(400, 50)
lblMainMenu.Size = New Size(400, 50)
lblMainMenu.Dock = DockStyle.Left
```

That guarantees it will never grow wider than 400.

## Fix option A (recommended): remove the fixed 400px constraint (Designer)
In the Designer (not by editing the `.Designer.vb` by hand if you can avoid it):

1. Open **FormMainMenu** in Design view
2. Click the **MAIN MENU** label (lblMainMenu)
3. In Properties:
   - set **AutoSize** = `False` (can stay false)
   - clear **MaximumSize** (set to `0, 0`)
   - clear **MinimumSize** (set to `0, 0`)
   - set **Width** to something larger (or let Dock handle it)
4. Consider changing:
   - **Dock** from `Left` to `None`, and set `Anchor = Top, Left, Right`
   - OR leave Dock=Left but give it a larger Width (like 700–900)

Then it will have room to show the version text.

## Fix option B: keep the label as “MAIN MENU”, put version/build on the clock label instead
Since `lblDateTime` is docked fill and has lots of space, you could append version/build there (and keep lblMainMenu short). Example:

- Keep: `lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"`
- In `UpdateHeaderClock()`, set `lblDateTime.Text` to include version/build on the left or right.

(This avoids layout issues entirely.)

## Quick confirm
Do you want the version/build to appear:
1) **On the left label (`lblMainMenu`)** (we’ll widen it / remove max size), or  
2) **On the right clock label (`lblDateTime`)** (more space, easiest), or  
3) **Split**: “MAIN MENU” left, “vX (date)” right under/near the clock?

If you tell me which, I’ll give you the exact property changes / code. If you upload the screenshot, I can point to the best layout choice.

  so the version/build date is successfully appended to the *window caption*.

- **Inside the app**, at the very top-left, there is a large yellow header text that says **“MAIN MENU”** (with a decorative font and outline). There is **no visible version/build date appended** to that in-app header.

- At the top-right of the header area, there’s a yellow **date/time display** showing:  
  `Thursday  04-16-2026          11:57:58 AM`

## @testsubject117

ok, I expanded MainMenu Max width to 700 and I now see the version, but it's the same size as the Main Menu text, I want it smaller, the same size as the day and date text or even smaller

## Copilot

To make only the version/date smaller, you can’t do it with a single `Label`—a label uses **one font for all its text**. You have two good options:

## Option A (recommended): split into two labels (big “MAIN MENU” + small version)
Keep your existing big `lblMainMenu` as just the words, and add a second label for the version/build date with a smaller font.

### 1) Designer changes
In `pnlHeader`, add a new label named **`lblBuildInfo`**:
- `AutoSize = True`
- `ForeColor = Color.Yellow`
- `BackColor = Color.Transparent` (or Black)
- `Font = Consolas, 12pt Bold` (or smaller, e.g. 10pt)
- `Location` somewhere to the right of MAIN MENU (or below it)

If you want it aligned neatly, set:
- `Anchor = Top, Left`
- Place it just to the right of `lblMainMenu`.

### 2) Code changes (FormMainMenu.vb)
Change this:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
```

to:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
lblBuildInfo.Text = "v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
```

That’s it—now you control the size by the `lblBuildInfo.Font`.

## Option B (more advanced): draw mixed-size text yourself
You could owner-draw the header (Graphics.DrawString with two fonts), but it’s more work and less maintainable than two labels.

---

### Quick question so I generate exact settings
Do you want the small version/build text:
1) **On the same line** as MAIN MENU (to the right), or  
2) **Under** MAIN MENU (like a subtitle)?

Tell me 1 or 2 and what font size you want (e.g., 12pt / 10pt), and I’ll give you the exact Designer property values (and a code snippet that positions it nicely even if you resize the form).

## @testsubject117

would it be easier to add version to day and date?

## Copilot

Yes—much easier. Since `lblDateTime` is already the smaller font and has plenty of space, you can append the version/build info there and keep the big `MAIN MENU` text untouched.

### What to change
1) In `OnLoad`, **store** the version/build date in class-level fields (so you don’t recompute every second).
2) In `UpdateHeaderClock()`, append them to `lblDateTime.Text`.

Here’s the exact code.

#### 1) Add these fields near the top of `FormMainMenu` (inside the class, above `OnLoad`)
```vbnet
Private _buildInfo As String = ""
```

#### 2) In `OnLoad`, after you compute `mainVer` and `mainBuildDate`, set `_buildInfo`
Add this line:

```vbnet
_buildInfo = "   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
```

Then change your label line back to just:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
```

(Keep `Me.Text = ...` as you already have it, if you still want it in the title bar.)

#### 3) Replace `UpdateHeaderClock()` with this
```vbnet
Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
    Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now
    lblDateTime.Text = now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                       now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                       now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt") &
                       _buildInfo
End Sub
```

### Result
Top-right line will read like:
`Thursday  04-16-2026          11:57:58 AM   v1.0.0.0   (2026-04-16)`

and the big left header stays just `MAIN MENU`.

If you want the version on a **second line** (even cleaner), tell me and I’ll give you the two-line version using `vbCrLf` and label padding/alignment.

## @testsubject117

added code but getting bc30188 delcaration expected. here's the current FormMainMenu.vb code: Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Private _buildInfo As String = ""
    _buildInfo = "   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True

        BuildMenu()

        Dim mainAsm As System.Reflection.Assembly = System.Reflection.Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()

        Dim mainVer As String = ""
        Try
            mainVer = mainAsm.GetName().Version.ToString()
        Catch
            mainVer = "(unknown)"
        End Try

        Dim mainBuildDate As String = ""
        Try
            mainBuildDate = System.IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(mainAsm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
        Catch
            mainBuildDate = "(unknown)"
        End Try

        lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
        Me.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
        UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        tmrClock.Start()

        ' IMPORTANT: run resize after layout is finalized
        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Private Sub flpLeft_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpLeft.SizeChanged
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
    End Sub

    Private Sub flpRight_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpRight.SizeChanged
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub
    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick
        UpdateHeaderClock()
    End Sub
    Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now
        lblDateTime.Text = now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                       now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                       now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt") &
                       _buildInfo
    End Sub
    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        ' Left column
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (removed: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        lbl.Width = 1000 ' will be resized later
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        ' Force black buttons with white text
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1

        ' Optional: nicer hover / click feedback
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)

        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
        panel.ClientSize.Width -
        panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
        SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")

            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try

            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class

## Copilot

BC30188 is because you put this line **at class scope**, where only declarations are allowed:

```vbnet
_buildInfo = "   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
```

At that point, `mainVer` / `mainBuildDate` don’t even exist yet (they’re local variables inside `OnLoad`). The fix is:

- Keep only the **field declaration** at class scope
- Move the **assignment** into `OnLoad` after you compute `mainVer` and `mainBuildDate`

Here’s the corrected top part of your file (showing exactly what to remove/add).

### Replace this (your current)
```vbnet
Private _buildInfo As String = ""
_buildInfo = "   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
```

### With this
```vbnet
Private _buildInfo As String = ""

Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
```

…and then **inside `OnLoad`**, after you set `mainVer` and `mainBuildDate`, add:

```vbnet
_buildInfo = "   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
```

So it will look like:

```vbnet
Dim mainBuildDate As String = ""
Try
    mainBuildDate = System.IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(mainAsm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
Catch
    mainBuildDate = "(unknown)"
End Try

_buildInfo = "   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"

lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
Me.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
UpdateHeaderClock()
```

After that, your `UpdateHeaderClock()` method is fine as-is and it will append `_buildInfo` to the date/time display.

If you want, I can paste a full “entire `FormMainMenu.vb`” replacement with the corrected placement so you can copy/paste once.

## @testsubject117

that worked, but the version is to the right of the day date, I want it to the left of day date, right after the main menu text

## Copilot

Do it by **prepending** `_buildInfo` before the day/date/time string (instead of appending it at the end).

Replace your `UpdateHeaderClock()` with this version:

```vbnet
Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
    Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

    lblDateTime.Text = _buildInfo & "          " &
                       now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                       now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                       now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
End Sub
```

That puts `v1.0.0.0 (2026-04-16)` at the **left side** of the `lblDateTime` content.

### If it still appears on the far right
Your `lblDateTime` in the designer is set to **right-aligned**:

- `lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleRight`

Change it to left-aligned so text starts right after “MAIN MENU”:

- In Designer: select `lblDateTime`
- Set **TextAlign** to `MiddleLeft`

(or set it in code once in `OnLoad`):

```vbnet
lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
```

With `MiddleLeft`, the build info will appear immediately after the left side of the `lblDateTime` area (which begins right after the `lblMainMenu` label).

## @testsubject117

it's at left now but not far enough left it's still about 2 inches from Main Menu text, I want it about .5 inches away

## Copilot

That gap isn’t coming from your string—it’s from the **layout/padding** on `lblDateTime` (and possibly the extra left inset created by where it starts).

From your `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`, `lblDateTime` has:

```vbnet
lblDateTime.Location = New Point(400, 0)
lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 10, 0)
lblDateTime.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
```

Because `lblMainMenu` is fixed at 400px wide and docked left, `lblDateTime` starts at **x=400**. Then **your text itself begins at the left edge of lblDateTime**, so any visible “gap” is basically the width of the empty space between the end of the big “MAIN MENU” letters and the start of lblDateTime.

To reduce it to ~0.5", you have two simple options:

## Option A (best): make `lblMainMenu` narrower
Right now it’s fixed at 400px. Make it smaller so `lblDateTime` starts closer.

In the Designer, select `lblMainMenu` and change:
- `MinimumSize` from `400, 50` to something like `300, 50`
- `MaximumSize` from `400, 50` to something like `300, 50`
- and/or set its `Size` width to ~300

This is the cleanest because it actually moves the start of `lblDateTime` left.

## Option B: add left padding *negative?* (can’t) — instead, shift text with spaces (hacky)
If you don’t want to change layout, you can “pull” it left visually only by adjusting the main label width. Labels can’t have negative padding, so code-only can’t truly move it left without changing control sizes/positions.

## Quick “.5 inch” target
At 96 DPI, 0.5 inch ≈ **48 pixels**. You currently have a 400px header area reserved. If you want the build info to start ~48px after the end of “MAIN MENU”, you’ll probably want `lblMainMenu` width closer to **300–320** (depends on font rendering).

### What I recommend
Set `lblMainMenu` width to **300**:

- `lblMainMenu.MaximumSize = New Size(300, 50)`
- `lblMainMenu.MinimumSize = New Size(300, 50)`
- `lblMainMenu.Size = New Size(300, 50)`

Then your `lblDateTime` will start at x=300 instead of x=400, pulling your version text ~100px (~1 inch) to the left.

If you tell me what DPI you’re running at (or just whether 300 is too tight), I can suggest the exact width to use.

## @testsubject117

the minimum I can set to is 365 or it hides the main menu text. the version is still about an inch away from starting at 365

## Copilot

What you’re seeing makes sense: the **label’s left edge** starts at 365px, but the **visible “MAIN MENU” letters** don’t reach the right edge of that label because the Castellar font has a lot of built-in side bearing and your label also effectively reserves blank space. So even at 365px, there’s still “air” after the last letter before `lblDateTime` begins.

You have 3 practical ways to get the version to sit ~0.5" from the visible text:

## Option 1 (best/clean): make `lblMainMenu` autosize to its text width
If you let `lblMainMenu` size itself to exactly the rendered “MAIN MENU”, `lblDateTime` will start immediately after it.

In Designer for `lblMainMenu`:
- set **AutoSize = True**
- set **Dock = Left** (keep)
- remove **MinimumSize** and **MaximumSize** constraints (set them to `0, 0`)

Then in code you keep:
```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
```

If AutoSize can’t be used with your current constraints/layout, use option 2.

## Option 2 (very reliable): measure text and set lblMainMenu.Width at runtime
This works even if the designer fights you.

Add this inside `OnLoad` **after** `lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"`:

```vbnet
Try
    ' Measure the actual pixel width of the rendered text and size the label to fit.
    Dim w As Integer = TextRenderer.MeasureText(lblMainMenu.Text, lblMainMenu.Font).Width
    lblMainMenu.Width = w + 20 ' +padding so it doesn't clip (tweak 10-40)
Catch
End Try
```

This forces `lblMainMenu` to hug the text, so `lblDateTime` begins much closer.

## Option 3 (quick hack): remove the extra spacing in your string
Right now you have `"          "` between version and day/date. If that’s still in your `UpdateHeaderClock`, reduce it:

```vbnet
lblDateTime.Text = _buildInfo & "  " & now.ToString("dddd") & "  " & ...
```

But based on what you described (“about an inch away from starting at 365”), the bigger issue is still the `lblMainMenu` width/whitespace, so I’d do option 2.

---

### Exact code to use (Option 2)
Put this in `OnLoad` right after `lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"`:

```vbnet
Try
    Dim w As Integer = TextRenderer.MeasureText(lblMainMenu.Text, lblMainMenu.Font).Width
    lblMainMenu.Width = w + 20
Catch
End Try
```

And make sure `lblDateTime.TextAlign = MiddleLeft` (you already did or can do once in `OnLoad`):

```vbnet
lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
```

If you paste your current `UpdateHeaderClock()` too, I’ll adjust the spacing string so the version sits exactly where you want without excessive gaps.

## @testsubject117

but the size of lblMainMenu.Text is fine, it's lblDateTime that's not placing the version text far enough to the left. within lblDateTime there is an inch of empty space before the version

## Copilot

That “inch of empty space” is coming from **`lblDateTime.Padding`** and/or **text alignment** inside `lblDateTime`, not from your code string.

In the designer file you pasted earlier, `lblDateTime` has:

```vbnet
lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 10, 0)
lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleRight
```

Even if you changed TextAlign, you may still have padding or a different alignment being set elsewhere.

## Do this (one-time) in `OnLoad` to force it
Add these lines in `OnLoad` (after `MyBase.OnLoad(e)` and after `InitializeComponent` has already run, which it has by then):

```vbnet
' Force the version/date text to start as far left as possible in lblDateTime
lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)  ' remove right padding; keep top padding
lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)
```

### If there’s still a gap
It’s likely because `lblDateTime` is **Dock=Fill** and your `lblMainMenu` is Dock=Left with a fixed width; the “gap” you’re seeing could actually be **inside lblMainMenu**, not lblDateTime.

To prove which control is the culprit quickly:
- Temporarily set in `OnLoad`:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.BackColor = Color.DarkRed
lblDateTime.BackColor = Color.DarkBlue
```

Run it and you’ll visually see where the empty space is.

## Also remove extra spaces from your build string
Make sure `_buildInfo` doesn’t start with a bunch of spaces. Right now you build it with leading spaces:

```vbnet
_buildInfo = "   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
```

Change it to:

```vbnet
_buildInfo = "v" & mainVer & " (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
```

Then in `UpdateHeaderClock()` add only the spacing you actually want:

```vbnet
lblDateTime.Text = _buildInfo & "    " & now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                   now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                   now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
```

That gives you precise control over the gap.

---

If you want, paste your current `lblDateTime` properties as shown in the Designer (Padding, Margin, TextAlign, Dock, Location), and I’ll tell you exactly which one is creating the left inset.

## @testsubject117

are we able to change only the version and build text color or does it have to stay the same as day and date?

## Copilot

With a **Label** (`lblDateTime`), the text is rendered with **one ForeColor for the entire string**—so you **cannot** make only the version/build part a different color *within the same Label*.

You have 3 workable alternatives:

## 1) Add a second label for build info (easiest + cleanest)
Put a new label (e.g. `lblBuildInfo`) in the header panel between `lblMainMenu` and `lblDateTime`.

- `lblBuildInfo.ForeColor` = (your version color)
- `lblDateTime.ForeColor` = (your date/time color)

This is the normal WinForms way.

## 2) Use a RichTextBox instead of a Label (supports per-range color)
A `RichTextBox` lets you color different parts, but it’s heavier, and you’d typically lock it down (ReadOnly, BorderStyle None, etc.). Usually overkill for a header.

## 3) Owner-draw the header (custom painting)
Handle `pnlHeader.Paint` and draw the version in one color and the day/date/time in another using `Graphics.DrawString`. Most control, but more code.

**Recommendation:** Option 1 (two labels). It’s the least brittle and simplest to maintain.

If you tell me:
- the color you want for version/build (e.g., white, gray, green),
- and whether it should sit immediately after MAIN MENU or under it,

I’ll give you the exact Designer property settings + the 5–10 lines of VB code to position it and update it.

## @testsubject117

lets just remove the version and build number from the main menu and leave it on the about dialogue

## Copilot

Remove it from the main menu by undoing the places you added it:

## 1) In `FormMainMenu.vb`, remove the build-info field
Delete this line from the class:

```vbnet
Private _buildInfo As String = ""
```

## 2) In `OnLoad`, stop building/using `_buildInfo`, and stop appending version/date to the window caption
In `OnLoad`, change it to just:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
Me.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"
```

…and **delete** the line where you set:

```vbnet
_buildInfo = ...
```

(You can also delete the `mainAsm/mainVer/mainBuildDate` block entirely if you’re no longer using it anywhere in this form.)

## 3) In `UpdateHeaderClock()`, remove `_buildInfo` from the clock text
Make it only date/time again:

```vbnet
Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
    Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now
    lblDateTime.Text = now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                       now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                       now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
End Sub
```

After those changes, the main menu will show only “MAIN MENU” + date/time, and no version/build anywhere on that screen.

If you paste your current `FormAbout` code (or tell me the control name where you want the version shown), I’ll tell you exactly where to add the version/build text so it only appears on the About dialog.

## @testsubject117

can the version and build be moved to sit below the buttons at the bottom of the screen?

## Copilot

Yes. Easiest is to add a **footer label** docked to the bottom, and set its text once in `OnLoad`. That keeps it out of the header entirely.

## 1) Designer change (recommended)
In `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb` / designer view:

- Add a new `Label` named **`lblBuildInfo`**
- Set properties:
  - `Dock = Bottom`
  - `Height = 20` (or 24)
  - `TextAlign = MiddleRight` (or MiddleLeft if you prefer)
  - `ForeColor = Color.Silver` (or Yellow if you want)
  - `BackColor = Color.Black`
  - `Font = New Font("Consolas", 9.0F, FontStyle.Bold)` (or 8.25)

Make sure it’s added to the form **after** the main content so it appears at the bottom (Designer will handle order usually).

## 2) Code change (FormMainMenu.vb)
Keep your version/build computation, but instead of putting it in `lblMainMenu` or `lblDateTime`, do:

Inside `OnLoad`, after you compute `mainVer` and `mainBuildDate`:

```vbnet
lblBuildInfo.Text = "v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
```

And set the main header back to:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
```

## Important note about layout
Right now your layout is:

- `pnlHeader` docked **Top**
- `tlpRoot` docked **Fill**

Adding `lblBuildInfo` docked **Bottom** will automatically make `tlpRoot` fill the remaining space between header and footer—so your buttons won’t be covered.

---

### One question
Do you want the footer text aligned:
- **bottom-right** (common for version/build), or
- **bottom-left**?

If you tell me which, I’ll give you the exact property values (TextAlign + Padding) for that look.

## @testsubject117

so if I publish a new AMiOffice.exe and run it on a workstation I see 2 things. Main Menu text does not appear in the font I chose, and the version is there, but build currently says ((unknown))

## Copilot

Both symptoms point to “works on dev machine, different on target workstation” issues:

## 1) “MAIN MENU” font not showing = the font isn’t installed (or isn’t available) on the workstation
Your designer sets:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold, ...)
```

If **Castellar** isn’t installed/available on that workstation, Windows will silently substitute a different font.

**Fix options:**
- **Install the font** on the workstation (if licensing allows).
- **Change to a font that exists everywhere**, e.g. `"Segoe UI"`, `"Microsoft Sans Serif"`, etc.
- **Provide a fallback in code**:

```vbnet
Try
    lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold)
Catch
    lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold)
End Try
```

(You can also check `FontFamily.Families` to see if “Castellar” exists before using it.)

## 2) Build date showing “(unknown)” = `mainAsm.Location` / file timestamp retrieval is failing on the workstation
Your build date logic is:

```vbnet
mainBuildDate = System.IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(mainAsm.Location).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
```

That will return “(unknown)” only if an exception is thrown—commonly because:
- `mainAsm.Location` is blank/not usable in that deployment style, or
- the app is running from a location where last-write time can’t be read reliably, or
- the file is shadow-copied / launched in a way where the assembly path isn’t what you expect.

**Most reliable fix:** use the executable path instead of `Assembly.Location`:

```vbnet
Dim exePath As String = Application.ExecutablePath
mainBuildDate = System.IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(exePath).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
```

That usually works for published WinForms apps.

### If you want truly correct “build date”
File last write time can be changed by copying/unzipping. If you want an actual build timestamp, the best approach is to embed it at build time (AssemblyInfo attribute or generated source constant). But for “date of the exe on disk”, `Application.ExecutablePath` is simplest.

---

## What I need to be precise
1) How are you publishing? (ClickOnce, MSIX, plain folder copy, installer, etc.)
2) What Windows version on the workstation?
3) If you can, tell me what `mainAsm.Location` is on the workstation (you can temporarily `MessageBox.Show(mainAsm.Location)`).

If you answer those, I’ll give you the exact safest code for that publish method.

## @testsubject117

Folder copy. Windows 10. 

## Copilot

For a plain **folder copy on Windows 10**:

## 1) Font issue (“Castellar” not showing)
That means **Castellar isn’t installed** on that workstation, so Windows substitutes another font.

**Fix choices:**
- Install the Castellar font on that PC (if permitted), **or**
- Switch to a standard font (recommended), **or**
- Add a runtime fallback (so it looks good everywhere):

```vbnet
Private Sub ApplyMainMenuFont()
    Dim desired As String = "Castellar"
    Dim fallback As String = "Segoe UI"

    If FontFamily.Families.Any(Function(f) f.Name.Equals(desired, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)) Then
        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font(desired, 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold)
    Else
        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font(fallback, 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold)
    End If
End Sub
```

Call `ApplyMainMenuFont()` in `OnLoad` after `MyBase.OnLoad(e)`.

(If your project doesn’t have LINQ enabled, tell me and I’ll rewrite without `.Any()`.)

## 2) Build date showing “(unknown)”
For folder copy, the simplest reliable source is the EXE file itself:

Replace your build-date line with:

```vbnet
mainBuildDate = System.IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(Application.ExecutablePath).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
```

Also, while you’re at it, don’t swallow the exception silently—at least capture the message during troubleshooting:

```vbnet
Try
    mainBuildDate = System.IO.File.GetLastWriteTime(Application.ExecutablePath).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd")
Catch ex As Exception
    mainBuildDate = "(unknown)"
    ' Optional while debugging:
    ' MessageBox.Show("Build date read failed: " & ex.Message)
End Try
```

That should stop “(unknown)” on a normal workstation folder-copy run.

If you paste the 10–15 lines where you compute `mainVer` / `mainBuildDate`, I’ll return the exact edited block (drop-in replacement) with these fixes.

## Copilot

Good—then the `(unknown)` was coming from the `Assembly.Location` path not being usable in that environment, and switching to `Application.ExecutablePath` fixed it.

If you still see the “MAIN MENU” font mismatch on the workstation, that’s still the separate issue: the chosen font isn’t installed there. The two clean ways forward are:

1) **Install the font** on each workstation (if your org/licensing allows), or  
2) **Keep the fallback code** so it uses Castellar when available, otherwise uses something like Segoe UI.

If you tell me whether you want to standardize on a single font (same everywhere) or keep “best effort” with fallback, I’ll give you the exact final snippet to keep in `OnLoad` (and what font I’d recommend as the fallback to match your look).

Great—then both deployment issues are resolved:

- **Font:** Castellar wasn’t present on the Windows 10 workstation, so WinForms substituted a different font; installing Castellar fixed the header appearance.

## @testsubject117

lets Lock down version/build info (so it’s consistent and never “wrong”)

## Copilot

To “lock down” version/build so it’s **consistent and can’t be changed by copying files**, stop using file timestamps and instead **embed build metadata into the assembly at build time**, then read it at runtime.

### Recommended approach (robust, simple): use `AssemblyInformationalVersion`
You put both:
- semantic version (`1.2.3.4` or `1.2.3`)
- build identifier (date/time and/or git commit)

into **AssemblyInformationalVersionAttribute**, which is *meant* for display.

#### 1) Add build metadata at build time (MSBuild)
In your `.vbproj`, add something like this inside a `<PropertyGroup>`:

```xml
<GenerateAssemblyInfo>true</GenerateAssemblyInfo>

<!-- Use your normal versioning here -->
<Version>1.0.0.0</Version>

<!-- Embed a stable “build stamp” at build time -->
<InformationalVersion>$(Version) (built $(MSBuildThisFileName) $(MSBuildProjectName))</InformationalVersion>
```

But we actually want the **timestamp**. MSBuild doesn’t have a perfect built-in “now” property, so the usual reliable methods are:

- **A)** Add a tiny pre-build step that writes a `.vb` file containing a constant timestamp (my favorite for VB projects), or  
- **B)** If you’re using git, use a git commit hash via a script, or  
- **C)** Use a CI pipeline variable (if you later move to CI).

For your folder-copy workflow, **A** is the most straightforward.

#### 2) Generate a `BuildInfo.vb` at build time (best for WinForms)
Create a file `BuildInfo.vb` in your project and *auto-generate* it on each build.

Example content:

```vbnet
Friend Module BuildInfo
    Friend Const BuildTimestamp As String = "2026-04-16 12:34:56"
    Friend Const VersionText As String = "1.0.0.0"
End Module
```

Then your UI uses `BuildInfo.VersionText` and `BuildInfo.BuildTimestamp`—no file timestamps, no “unknown”, no copying effects.

### What I need from you to give exact, copy/paste steps
1) Is this **.NET Framework WinForms** or **.NET (Core/5/6/7/8) WinForms**?
   - (You can check in project properties or the `.vbproj` TargetFramework.)
2) Do you have an `AssemblyInfo.vb` file currently, or are you relying on auto-generated assembly info?

Once I know those two things, I’ll give you the exact recommended implementation (either:
- pre-build event command that writes `BuildInfo.vb`, **or**
- MSBuild `<Target>` that generates it), and the exact runtime code to display it in About/Main Menu.

## @testsubject117

how do I create buildinfo.vb

## Copilot

Create it as a normal VB file, then (optionally) add a build step later to auto-update the timestamp.

## Step 1: Create the file
In Visual Studio:

1. In **Solution Explorer**, right‑click your project
2. **Add** → **New Item…**
3. Choose **Class** (or “Module” if it’s offered), name it: `BuildInfo.vb`
4. Click **Add**

Replace the contents with this:

```vbnet
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Friend Module BuildInfo
    ' Update these when you release/build (or automate later)
    Friend Const VersionText As String = "1.0.0.0"
    Friend Const BuildDateText As String = "2026-04-16"   ' yyyy-MM-dd
End Module
```

## Step 2: Use it in your UI (example)
Anywhere you currently build the strings, use:

```vbnet
Dim mainVer As String = BuildInfo.VersionText
Dim mainBuildDate As String = BuildInfo.BuildDateText
```

…and then:

```vbnet
Me.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application   v" & mainVer & "   (" & mainBuildDate & ")"
```

## Step 3 (optional, but what “locked down” really means): automate the build date
Right now it’s manual. If you want it to automatically update on every build *without relying on file timestamps*, tell me which you use:

- Visual Studio “classic” **.NET Framework** project with a **Pre-build event**, or
- SDK-style project (.NET 6/7/8) using MSBuild targets

…and I’ll give you a copy/paste pre-build script that regenerates `BuildInfo.vb` with the current date/time and (optionally) the git commit hash.

## @testsubject117

using visual studio 2026 .net

## Copilot

Create `BuildInfo.vb` as a **generated file at build time** using an MSBuild target in your `.vbproj`. This is the cleanest way on modern “.NET (SDK-style)” projects and it avoids file timestamps entirely.

## 1) Add `BuildInfo.vb` as a generated file (do NOT hand-edit)
1. In your project, create a folder (optional but nice): `Generated`
2. Do **not** add a manually-maintained `BuildInfo.vb` to the project root. We’ll generate it into `obj\` each build.

## 2) Edit your `.vbproj`
Right-click project → **Edit Project File** and add this near the bottom (before `</Project>`):

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <!-- Where the generated VB file will go -->
  <BuildInfoFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)BuildInfo.g.vb</BuildInfoFile>
</PropertyGroup>

<ItemGroup>
  <!-- Compile the generated file -->
  <Compile Include="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
</ItemGroup>

<Target Name="GenerateBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
  <!-- UTC timestamp is stable across machines/timezones -->
  <PropertyGroup>
    <BuildTimestamp>$([System.DateTime]::UtcNow.ToString("yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss 'UTC'"))</BuildTimestamp>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <WriteLinesToFile
    File="$(BuildInfoFile)"
    Overwrite="true"
    Lines=
"Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Friend Module BuildInfo
    Friend Const BuildTimestamp As String = &quot;$(BuildTimestamp)&quot;
    Friend Const VersionText As String = &quot;$(Version)&quot;
End Module
" />
</Target>
```

### Notes
- `$(Version)` comes from your project’s `<Version>...</Version>` (or defaults). If you don’t have one, add e.g.:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
</PropertyGroup>
```

## 3) Use it in code
Now anywhere (MainMenu, About dialog, etc.):

```vbnet
Dim v = BuildInfo.VersionText
Dim built = BuildInfo.BuildTimestamp
```

Example:

```vbnet
lblBuildInfo.Text = "v" & v & " (" & built & ")"
```

## 4) Important: “locked down” meaning
This timestamp is the **time the project was built**, not the file copy time—so copying the folder to a workstation won’t change it.

## Quick question (so I tailor it perfectly)
Is your project file **SDK-style** (it will start with something like `<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk.WindowsDesktop">`)? If you paste the first ~10 lines of the `.vbproj`, I’ll confirm and adjust the MSBuild snippet if needed.

## @testsubject117

3) use it in code - what do you mean Now anywhere? Where do I place Dim v = BuildInfo.VersionText
Dim built = BuildInfo.BuildTimestamp

## Copilot

“Anywhere” means **inside whatever method/event is building the text you display** (Main Menu header, window title, About dialog label, etc.). You **don’t** put `Dim v = ...` at the top of the class body—VB only allows declarations there (that’s the BC30188 problem you hit earlier).

### Rule of thumb
Put:

```vbnet
Dim v = BuildInfo.VersionText
Dim built = BuildInfo.BuildTimestamp
```

**inside a Sub/Function**, right before you set a label’s `.Text` (or the form’s `.Text`).

---

## Example 1: Main menu form (`FormMainMenu.vb`)
Put it in `OnLoad` (or `FormMainMenu_Load`) because that runs once when the form opens.

```vbnet
Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
    MyBase.OnLoad(e)

    Dim v = BuildInfo.VersionText
    Dim built = BuildInfo.BuildTimestamp

    lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
    Me.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application   v" & v & "   (" & built & ")"

    ' If you also want it in your date label (optional)
    ' _buildInfo = "v" & v & " (" & built & ")"

    UpdateHeaderClock()
    tmrClock.Interval = 1000
    tmrClock.Start()
End Sub
```

If you want version/build to show in your `lblDateTime`, **don’t** recalc it every tick—set a field once in `OnLoad`:

```vbnet
Private _buildInfo As String = ""

Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
    MyBase.OnLoad(e)
    _buildInfo = "v" & BuildInfo.VersionText & " (" & BuildInfo.BuildTimestamp & ")"
End Sub
```

Then `UpdateHeaderClock()` just appends `_buildInfo`.

---

## Example 2: About dialog (`FormAbout.vb`)
In the About form’s `OnLoad`:

```vbnet
Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
    MyBase.OnLoad(e)

    lblVersion.Text = "Version: " & BuildInfo.VersionText
    lblBuild.Text = "Built: " & BuildInfo.BuildTimestamp
End Sub
```

---

### Where you should *not* put it
Not here (class scope):

```vbnet
Partial Public Class FormMainMenu
    Dim v = BuildInfo.VersionText   ' ❌ invalid in VB at class scope
End Class
```

---

If you tell me **exactly where you want the version/build displayed** (title bar only? About only? date line? footer?), I’ll point to the exact method in your current code and show the exact 3–5 lines to paste.

## @testsubject117

I inserted Dim v = BuildInfo.VersionText
Dim built = BuildInfo.BuildTimestamp below MyBase.OnLoad(e) and I now get BC31429 'VersionText' is ambiguous because multiple kinds of members with this name exist in module 'BuildInfo'.

## Copilot

That error means your project now has **two different `VersionText` members** visible under the name `BuildInfo`—most commonly because you have **two `BuildInfo` modules/files** getting compiled (or one is being generated twice), each defining `Friend Const VersionText ...`.

### Fix it (quick and definitive)

## 1) Make sure there is only ONE `BuildInfo` module
Search your project for `Module BuildInfo` and `VersionText`.

- In Visual Studio: **Edit → Find and Replace → Find in Files**
  - Find: `Module BuildInfo`
  - Find: `VersionText`

You should end up with exactly **one** module defining those constants.

**Common culprit:** you still have a manually created `BuildInfo.vb` (from earlier) *and* the new auto-generated `BuildInfo.g.vb` is also being compiled.

### What to do
- If you created a manual `BuildInfo.vb`, **delete it** (or at least rename the module inside it).
- Keep only the generated one.

## 2) Fix the `.vbproj` so the generated file is included only once
If you used both:
- an `<ItemGroup><Compile Include="...BuildInfo.g.vb" /></ItemGroup>` AND
- also generated it into a folder that VS auto-includes,

you can accidentally compile it twice.

The safest pattern is: generate into `obj\` and include it explicitly **once**.

In your `.vbproj`, keep it like this:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <BuildInfoFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)BuildInfo.g.vb</BuildInfoFile>
</PropertyGroup>

<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

…and ensure you **do not** also have another `<Compile Include="BuildInfo.vb" ...>` defining the same module.

## 3) Clean + rebuild
After removing the duplicate:
- **Build → Clean Solution**
- **Build → Rebuild Solution**

That clears out stale generated files too.

---

### If you want a “belt and suspenders” workaround (not the real fix)
You *can* disambiguate by using the global namespace, but it won’t help if there are literally two members with the same fully-qualified name:

```vbnet
Dim v = Global.<YourRootNamespace>.BuildInfo.VersionText
```

So the real solution is removing the duplicate module.

---

If you paste your `.vbproj` section where you added the MSBuild target + Compile Include, and tell me whether you still have a manual `BuildInfo.vb` in the project, I can point to the exact duplicate and tell you exactly what to delete.

## @testsubject117

I do not have a generated BuildInfo.g.vb only the manually created BuildInfo.vb module buildinfo is located within that file as well as AMiOffice.vbproj

## Copilot

BC31429 in this situation almost always means you have **two `VersionText` definitions inside the same `BuildInfo` module** (or something equivalent like a `Property VersionText` plus a `Const VersionText`). Since you only have the manual `BuildInfo.vb`, the next step is to look at that file.

### 1) Open `BuildInfo.vb` and check for duplicates
You should have **exactly one** of each name.

It must look like this (only one `VersionText`, only one `BuildTimestamp`):

```vbnet
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Friend Module BuildInfo
    Friend Const VersionText As String = "1.0.0.0"
    Friend Const BuildTimestamp As String = "2026-04-16 12:00:00"
End Module
```

If you see any of these, delete/rename one of them:
- a second `Friend Const VersionText ...`
- a `Friend ReadOnly Property VersionText ...`
- a `Function VersionText() ...`
- a field like `Friend VersionText As String = ...`

Even **different kinds** (Const vs Property) with the same name will produce the “multiple kinds of members” ambiguity.

### 2) If `BuildInfo.vb` looks clean, you likely have another `BuildInfo` module elsewhere
Even if you didn’t create `BuildInfo.g.vb`, you might still have:
- another file named `BuildInfo.vb` in a different folder, or
- another `Module BuildInfo` inside some other `.vb` file.

Do a **Find in Files** for this exact text:

- `Friend Module BuildInfo`
- `Module BuildInfo`
- `VersionText`

and make sure there’s only one hit for the module and only one hit for `VersionText` within it.

### 3) Don’t put these at class scope
Just to re-clarify: your usage lines must be inside `OnLoad` (which you did):

```vbnet
Dim v = BuildInfo.VersionText
Dim built = BuildInfo.BuildTimestamp
```

That part is correct—this error is about the module contents.

---

## Fastest way to resolve
Paste your **entire `BuildInfo.vb`** here (it should be small). I’ll point out the exact duplicate member name and what to rename/remove.

Also tell me your project’s **Root Namespace** (Project Properties → Application → Root namespace) if you have it set, because occasionally people end up with `Namespace BuildInfo` + `Module BuildInfo` patterns that create confusing name resolution.

## @testsubject117

actually I'm wrong. there is also a buildinfo.g.vb

## Copilot

That explains it: you now have **two modules named `BuildInfo`** (manual `BuildInfo.vb` and generated `BuildInfo.g.vb`), each defining `VersionText` / `BuildTimestamp`. VB then sees multiple members with the same name and reports BC31429.

### Fix (pick ONE source of truth)

## Option A (recommended): keep the generated one, delete the manual one
1) In Solution Explorer, delete (or exclude from project) your manual file:
- `BuildInfo.vb`

2) Rebuild:
- **Build → Clean Solution**
- **Build → Rebuild Solution**

After that, `BuildInfo.VersionText` will refer to the generated module only.

## Option B: keep the manual one, stop compiling the generated one
If you prefer manual values, remove the generation from the `.vbproj`:
- delete the `<Target Name="GenerateBuildInfo" ...>` section
- delete the `<Compile Include="$(BuildInfoFile)" />` line

Then Clean/Rebuild.

---

### How to verify which one you’re using
Open `BuildInfo.g.vb` and check where it lives. If it’s under `obj\...` it’s a generated intermediate file (expected).

### My recommendation for “locked down”
Use **generated** build info (Option A), but improve it slightly:

- **VersionText** from `$(Version)`
- **BuildTimestamp** set at build time
- optionally add a **git commit hash** later

---

If you paste your `.vbproj` snippet you added (the BuildInfoFile / Compile Include / Target), I can tell you exactly what to keep/remove so it generates cleanly and only once.

## @testsubject117

Duplicate 'Compile' items were included. The .NET SDK includes 'Compile' items from your project directory by default. You can either remove these items from your project file, or set the 'EnableDefaultCompileItems' property to 'false' if you want to explicitly include them in your project file. For more information, see https://aka.ms/sdkimplicititems. The duplicate items were: 'BuildInfo.g.vb'

## Copilot

You’re generating `BuildInfo.g.vb` **into your project directory**, so the SDK is picking it up automatically *and* you’re also explicitly including it with `<Compile Include=...>`, causing the duplicate Compile item error.

Fix it by doing **one** of these (recommended first):

## Fix (recommended): generate into `obj\` (IntermediateOutputPath)
1) In your `.vbproj`, set the generated file path to `$(IntermediateOutputPath)` and **keep** the explicit `<Compile Include=...>`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <BuildInfoFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)BuildInfo.g.vb</BuildInfoFile>
</PropertyGroup>

<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

2) Make sure you are **not** writing `BuildInfo.g.vb` into the project folder anywhere else.

3) Delete any stray `BuildInfo.g.vb` that already exists in your project directory (next to your `.vbproj`), then:
- **Build → Clean Solution**
- **Build → Rebuild Solution**

This avoids implicit inclusion because files under `obj\` are not part of the default compile globs.

---

## Alternate fix: keep generating in project directory, remove explicit Compile include
If you insist on generating it into the project folder, then remove the explicit include:

- Delete this:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Include="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
</ItemGroup>
```

…and let the SDK implicitly include it. (I don’t recommend this because generated source files in the project root are easy to accidentally commit and cause confusion.)

---

## Also: remove the manual `BuildInfo.vb`
Once the generated file is working, delete/exclude your manual `BuildInfo.vb` so you don’t reintroduce ambiguity.

---

If you paste your current `.vbproj` `GenerateBuildInfo` target block, I can edit it to the exact correct version (so it generates under `obj\` and compiles exactly once).
