      <CopyToOutputDirectory>Never</CopyToOutputDirectory>
    </EmbeddedResource>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Update="My Project\Application.Designer.vb">
      <DesignTime>True</DesignTime>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Application.myapp</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>

    <Compile Update="My Project\Resources.Designer.vb">
      <DesignTime>True</DesignTime>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Resources.resx</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>

    <Compile Update="My Project\Settings.Designer.vb">
      <DesignTimeSharedInput>True</DesignTimeSharedInput>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Settings.settings</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <EmbeddedResource Update="My Project\Resources.resx">
      <CustomToolNamespace>My.Resources</CustomToolNamespace>
      <Generator>VbMyResourcesResXFileCodeGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Resources.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </EmbeddedResource>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <None Update="My Project\Application.myapp">
      <Generator>MyApplicationCodeGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Application.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </None>

    <None Update="My Project\Settings.settings">
      <CustomToolNamespace>My</CustomToolNamespace>
      <Generator>SettingsSingleFileGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Settings.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </None>
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- Prevent compiling any stray BuildInfo file from the project root -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Remove="BuildInfo.g.vb" />
    <Compile Remove="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <Target Name="IncrementBuildCounter" BeforeTargets="Publish">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildCounterFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\build-counter.txt</BuildCounterFile>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      Condition="!Exists('$(BuildCounterFile)')"
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="0" />

    <ReadLinesFromFile File="$(BuildCounterFile)">
      <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildCounterLines" />
    </ReadLinesFromFile>

<PropertyGroup>
  <BuildNumber>$([System.Int32]::Parse('%(BuildCounterLines.Identity)'))</BuildNumber>
  <BuildNumber>$([MSBuild]::Add($(BuildNumber), 1))</BuildNumber>
</PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="$(BuildNumber)" />
  </Target>

  <Target Name="GenerateBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildCounterFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\build-counter.txt</BuildCounterFile>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      Condition="!Exists('$(BuildCounterFile)')"
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="0" />

    <ReadLinesFromFile Condition="'$(BuildNumber)' == ''" File="$(BuildCounterFile)">
      <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildCounterLines2" />
    </ReadLinesFromFile>

    <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(BuildNumber)' == ''">
      <BuildNumber>%(BuildCounterLines2.Identity)</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <MakeDir Directories="$([System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName('$(BuildInfoFile)'))" />

    <WriteLinesToFile
      File="$(BuildInfoFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="Option Strict On&#xD;&#xA;Option Explicit On&#xD;&#xA;&#xD;&#xA;Friend Module BuildInfo&#xD;&#xA;    Friend Const ProductVersion As String = &quot;$(ProductVersion)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;    Friend Const BuildNumber As String = &quot;$(BuildNumber)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;&#xD;&#xA;    Friend ReadOnly Property DisplayVersion As String&#xD;&#xA;        Get&#xD;&#xA;            Return &quot;AMiOffice v&quot; &amp; ProductVersion &amp; &quot; (Build &quot; &amp; BuildNumber &amp; &quot;)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;        End Get&#xD;&#xA;    End Property&#xD;&#xA;End Module&#xD;&#xA;" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Remove="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
      <Compile Include="$(BuildInfoFile)" Visible="false" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

</Project>

## Copilot

You’re extremely close—your `.vbproj` just has **one big metadata bug**: you define `<InformationalVersion>` **twice**, and the second one overwrites the first. Right now you have:

- `InformationalVersion = $(ProductVersion) (Build $(BuildNumber))`  ✅ (what you want)
- later: `InformationalVersion = $(Version)` ❌ (overrides it)

So your built EXE’s product/info version won’t include the build number, and depending on what your About form reads, you’ll see inconsistent results like “(not set)”.

## 1) Fix the duplicate InformationalVersion
Delete this line entirely:

```xml
<InformationalVersion>$(Version)</InformationalVersion>
```

Keep only this one (near the top is fine):

```xml
<InformationalVersion>$(ProductVersion) (Build $(BuildNumber))</InformationalVersion>
```

## 2) Add a deterministic fallback BuildNumber for normal builds
Right now `BuildNumber` is often empty unless Publish ran (or the read happens). To ensure your assembly metadata always has a number even on a normal build, add this to your main `<PropertyGroup>`:

```xml
<BuildNumber Condition="'$(BuildNumber)'==''">0</BuildNumber>
```

Then your informational version is always well-formed.

## 3) Optional: make ProductVersion match your assembly Version
You currently have:
- `<ProductVersion>1.0</ProductVersion>`
- `<Version>1.0.0.0</Version>`

That’s fine, but if you want consistency, you can do:

```xml
<ProductVersion>$(Version)</ProductVersion>
```

(or set `Version` to `1.0.0` and keep `ProductVersion` as `1.0`—your call).

## Minimal corrected header block
Here’s the part of your project that should be changed (keep the rest as-is):

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>WinExe</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0-windows</TargetFramework>
    <StartupObject>AMiOffice.My.MyApplication</StartupObject>
    <EnableNETAnalyzers>false</EnableNETAnalyzers>

    <UseWindowsForms>true</UseWindowsForms>
    <MyType>WindowsForms</MyType>

    <AssemblyTitle>AMiOffice</AssemblyTitle>
    <Product>AMiOffice</Product>
    <Company>Kirk Saffell</Company>
    <Description>AMiOffice Application</Description>

    <AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
    <FileVersion>1.0.0.0</FileVersion>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>

    <ProductVersion>1.0</ProductVersion>

    <!-- Ensure BuildNumber is never blank for normal builds -->
    <BuildNumber Condition="'$(BuildNumber)'==''">0</BuildNumber>

    <!-- Keep ONLY ONE InformationalVersion -->
    <InformationalVersion>$(ProductVersion) (Build $(BuildNumber))</InformationalVersion>

    <GenerateAssemblyInfo>true</GenerateAssemblyInfo>

    <BuildInfoFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\Generated\BuildInfo.g.vb</BuildInfoFile>

    <Copyright>Copyright Kirk Saffell 2026</Copyright>
    <Title>AMiOfficeApplication</Title>
  </PropertyGroup>
```

## 4) After editing: do a clean rebuild
Delete `bin` + `obj` and rebuild so the EXE metadata is regenerated.

---

If you tell me exactly what you want the About box to show (e.g., “Version 1.0.0.0”, “File version 1.0.0.0”, “Product version 1.0 (Build 14)”), I can suggest the cleanest mapping between `Version`, `FileVersion`, `ProductVersion`, and `InformationalVersion` so they’re consistent everywhere (Explorer details pane, About box, and your UI).

## Copilot

You currently have **two different `FormAbout` classes** being compiled, so the Designer partial class can’t “see” the controls and even thinks it’s not a Form anymore. That’s why you’re getting:

- `class 'FormAbout' and class 'FormAbout' conflict`
- Designer says `Event 'Load' cannot be found`
- Designer says `Text is not a member of 'FormAbout'`
- controls like `lblAppName` “not declared”

This happens when **FormAbout.vb is not declared as a Partial class**, or there is **another FormAbout class in another file/namespace**.

## Fix (do exactly this)

### 1) Make `FormAbout.vb` a `Partial Class FormAbout`
Replace your entire `FormAbout.vb` with this version (note the **Partial** keyword):

```vbnet name=FormAbout.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Diagnostics
Imports System.Text

Partial Public Class FormAbout

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        ' Title bar text
        Me.Text = "About AMiOffice"

        ' Big red app name line (your designer label)
        lblAppName.Text = "AMiOffice"

        ' "About:" header include build display
        lblAbout.Text = "About: " & BuildInfo.DisplayVersion

        ' Developed by line
        lblDevelopedBy.Text = "Developed By: Kirk Saffell"

        ' Fill the info box
        Dim exePath As String = Application.ExecutablePath
        Dim fvi As FileVersionInfo = FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(exePath)

        Dim sb As New StringBuilder()
        sb.AppendLine(BuildInfo.DisplayVersion)
        sb.AppendLine()
        sb.AppendLine($"Assembly Version : {My.Application.Info.Version}")
        sb.AppendLine($"File Version     : {Safe(fvi.FileVersion)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Product Version  : {Safe(fvi.ProductVersion)}")
        sb.AppendLine()
        sb.AppendLine($"Product Name     : {Safe(fvi.ProductName, "AMiOffice")}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Company          : {Safe(fvi.CompanyName)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Copyright        : {Safe(fvi.LegalCopyright)}")
        sb.AppendLine()
        sb.AppendLine($"Executable Path  : {exePath}")

        rtbInfo.Text = sb.ToString()
    End Sub

    Private Shared Function Safe(value As String, Optional fallback As String = "") As String
        If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) Then
            If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fallback) Then
                Return "(not set)"
            End If
            Return fallback
        End If
        Return value.Trim()
    End Function

End Class
```

**Key detail:** `Partial Public Class FormAbout` must match the designer’s `Partial Class FormAbout`.

### 2) Ensure there is NOT another `FormAbout` defined elsewhere
In Solution Explorer, use **Search** (Ctrl+Shift+F) for:

```
Class FormAbout
```

If you find another file defining `Class FormAbout` (not the Designer file), it must be deleted/renamed, or moved into a different namespace.

### 3) Clean rebuild
Close VS, delete `bin` and `obj` under `...\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\`, reopen, rebuild.

---

## Why “Load cannot be found” happened
When the partial class linkage breaks (because your code-behind wasn’t `Partial`, or there’s a second class with the same name), the designer half is no longer inheriting from `System.Windows.Forms.Form` as a single combined type, so VB complains that Form events/properties don’t exist.

---

If after Step 1 you still get the conflict error, paste the results of the “Class FormAbout” search (file paths + the matching lines). That will identify the duplicate definition instantly.

## Copilot

You **still have two copies of the code-behind being compiled** (or the same file is included twice), and **VB is binding your `FormAbout` code to a different class than the designer-generated `FormAbout`**, which is why:

- `FormAbout_Load` is “defined multiple times”
- `Safe` is “defined multiple times”
- *and at the same time* the designer controls (`lblAppName`, `rtbInfo`, etc.) are “not declared”
- plus “`Load` cannot be found” / “`Text` is not a member of `FormAbout`” (meaning the `FormAbout` it’s compiling against is not the WinForms `Form` type)

We fix this by doing two things **deterministically**:

1) make sure there is exactly **one** `FormAbout` partial class definition in non-designer code
2) ensure the file is compiled **once** and the designer file is paired correctly

## Step 1 — Find the duplicates (do this first)
In Visual Studio: **Edit → Find and Replace → Find in Files** (Ctrl+Shift+F)

Search for each of these strings:

1. `Partial Public Class FormAbout`
2. `Partial Class FormAbout`
3. `Class FormAbout`
4. `FormAbout_Load`
5. `Private Shared Function Safe`

For each match, list the **file path(s)** it finds.

You should only have:
- `FormAbout.Designer.vb` (contains `Partial Class FormAbout`)
- `FormAbout.vb` (contains `Partial Public Class FormAbout` and the Load handler)
- optionally `FormAbout.resx` (resource file)

If you have **anything like**:
- `FormAbout (1).vb`
- `FormAbout - Copy.vb`
- `FormAbout.bak.vb`
- a second `FormAbout.vb` under `..\My Project\` or elsewhere  
…that is the cause.

### Quick Windows check (often reveals the culprit immediately)
In File Explorer, go to:
`C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\`

Sort by name and see if you have:
- multiple `FormAbout*.vb` files

## Step 2 — The “Load cannot be found” / “Text not a member” smoking gun
That specific symptom usually happens when you accidentally created a **Module** or **Class** named `FormAbout` somewhere *without* inheriting `Form`, so the compiler is binding your `Handles MyBase.Load` against the wrong type.

So in the same Find-in-Files results, look for:
- `Module FormAbout`
- `Class FormAbout` (without `Inherits System.Windows.Forms.Form` in the Designer file)

## Step 3 — Most likely immediate fix
Once you find the extra file, do one of these:
- **remove it from the project** (right-click → Exclude From Project), or
- **rename it** so it doesn’t define `FormAbout` anymore, or
- delete it if it’s junk

Then clean/rebuild.

---

## If you want a “no-thinking” solution right now
Do this:

1) Temporarily rename your current code-behind file on disk:
   - `FormAbout.vb` → `FormAbout_codebehind.bak`

2) In Visual Studio, right-click the project → **Add → Windows Form…**
   - Name it: `FormAbout.vb`  
   (this will regenerate a correct partial linkage)

3) Copy only your UI logic into the new file (I’ll provide it once you paste the *new* generated file).

But before we do that, the faster route is: **identify the duplicate file**.

---

### Reply with this
Paste the results of Ctrl+Shift+F for `FormAbout_Load` (it will show all files where it appears). That one search alone will tell us exactly which file is duplicating the handler.

## @testsubject117

1. Partial Public Class FormAbout was found 1 time in FormAbout.vb., 2. Partial Class FormAbout was not found at all, 3. Class FormAbout was found in 2 places - FormAbout.Designer.vb and FormAbout.vb, 4. FormAbout_Load was found in 2 places - FormAbout.Designer.vb and FormAbout.vb, 5. Private Shared Function Safe was found in 2 places - FormAbout.Designer.vb and FormAbout.vb

## Copilot

Your `FormAbout.Designer.vb` **should not** contain `FormAbout_Load` or `Safe` at all. That’s the root cause.

So your designer file has been accidentally contaminated with code-behind content (or you pasted the replacement into the designer file at some point). That explains *everything*:

- duplicate `FormAbout_Load` / `Safe`
- designer controls not recognized (because the partial class structure got broken)
- “Load cannot be found” / “Text is not a member” cascading errors

## Fix: restore `FormAbout.Designer.vb` to “designer-only”
Do this in order:

### 1) Revert `FormAbout.Designer.vb` to ONLY designer code
Open `FormAbout.Designer.vb` and delete **anything** that is not designer-generated field declarations or `InitializeComponent`, `Dispose`, etc.

Specifically, `FormAbout.Designer.vb` should contain:
- `<DesignerGenerated()> Partial Class FormAbout Inherits System.Windows.Forms.Form`
- `Dispose`
- `InitializeComponent`
- `Friend WithEvents ...` control fields

It should **NOT** contain:
- `Private Sub FormAbout_Load...`
- `Private Shared Function Safe...`
- any `Imports ...` (designer usually doesn’t need them)
- any other methods besides `Dispose` and `InitializeComponent`

### Easiest safe method (recommended): let Visual Studio regenerate it
1) In Solution Explorer, expand `FormAbout.vb` (if it shows children).
2) Right-click `FormAbout.Designer.vb` → **Delete**
   - Choose “Remove” (or delete from disk).
3) Right-click `FormAbout.vb` → **View Designer**
4) Make **any tiny change** (move a control 1px) and save.

Visual Studio will regenerate a clean `FormAbout.Designer.vb`.

If VS won’t regenerate automatically, then:

- Right-click project → Add → **New Item** → Windows Form
- Name it `FormAbout2.vb`
- Copy the fresh generated `FormAbout2.Designer.vb` content over your broken `FormAbout.Designer.vb`
- Then delete `FormAbout2.*`

### 2) Ensure your code-behind is in `FormAbout.vb` only
Your `FormAbout.vb` should contain your `Partial Public Class FormAbout` and the `FormAbout_Load` + `Safe` function—**only there**.

### 3) Rebuild

## @testsubject117

when i right click View Designer is not there

## Copilot

That happens when Visual Studio no longer recognizes `FormAbout.vb` as a WinForms designer file—usually because one (or more) of these is true:

- `FormAbout.vb` is missing the `Partial Class FormAbout` (or the class name doesn’t match the designer)
- the `.vbproj` doesn’t have the WinForms “subtype” metadata for that file (rare in SDK-style)
- the `.Designer.vb` / `.resx` relationship (“DependentUpon”) is broken
- the designer file is corrupted (very likely, given it contains `FormAbout_Load`/`Safe`)

We can fix this without relying on “View Designer” at all.

## Do this: regenerate FormAbout cleanly via “Add → Windows Form”
### Step 1) Create a new clean form
In Visual Studio:
1. Right-click the **project** `AMiOffice`
2. **Add → New Item…**
3. Choose **Windows Form**
4. Name it: `FormAboutNew.vb`
5. Click Add

This should create:
- `FormAboutNew.vb`
- `FormAboutNew.Designer.vb`
- `FormAboutNew.resx`

### Step 2) Move your UI layout over (pick one)
**Option A (fastest):** Rebuild the About UI quickly by copying settings manually (since it’s a simple layout).

**Option B (best):** Copy the *designer code* from the old About form into the new one, but only after we clean it.

Given your designer file is contaminated, do this instead:

1. Open **old** `FormAbout.Designer.vb`
2. Copy only what’s inside `InitializeComponent()` *from the first line `Private Sub InitializeComponent()` down to `End Sub`*, and the `Friend WithEvents ...` declarations at the bottom.
3. Paste that into `FormAboutNew.Designer.vb`, replacing its `InitializeComponent` and field declarations.
4. Ensure the class name in the new designer file remains `Partial Class FormAboutNew` (or rename consistently).

### Step 3) Swap names (make the new form become FormAbout)
Once `FormAboutNew` builds and displays correctly:
1. Delete/exclude the broken old `FormAbout.vb`, `FormAbout.Designer.vb`, `FormAbout.resx`
2. Rename `FormAboutNew.vb` → `FormAbout.vb` (and same for `.Designer.vb` / `.resx`)
3. In both files, rename the class from `FormAboutNew` to `FormAbout`

### Step 4) Rebuild

---

## Even simpler: fix recognition by adding DependentUpon entries
If you’d rather keep the existing files, we can reattach them in the `.vbproj` by explicitly declaring the designer relationships.

Add this to your `.vbproj`:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Update="FormAbout.Designer.vb">
    <DependentUpon>FormAbout.vb</DependentUpon>
  </Compile>
  <EmbeddedResource Update="FormAbout.resx">
    <DependentUpon>FormAbout.vb</DependentUpon>
  </EmbeddedResource>
</ItemGroup>
```

This often brings “View Designer” back *after* the designer file is clean.

No problem—we can recover without it.

## Path 1 (fastest): Restore the deleted file from the Recycle Bin / Git / VS undo
1) Check **Recycle Bin** for `FormAbout.Designer.vb` (and `FormAbout.resx` if you deleted it).
2) If it’s there, **Restore** it.
3) Then we’ll clean it (remove the accidental `FormAbout_Load` / `Safe` code) and reattach designer support.

If you don’t have it there, go to Path 2.

## Path 2 (clean rebuild): Create a new About form and reapply your layout
Since you pasted the designer layout earlier in this chat, you can reconstruct the form quickly without needing the old file.

### Step A — Add a new Windows Form
In Visual Studio:
- Right-click project **AMiOffice** → **Add → New Item… → Windows Form**
- Name it **FormAbout.vb** (if it complains the name already exists, name it `FormAbout2.vb` for now)

This will create a new designer-backed form and bring back “View Designer” for that new form.

### Step B — Replace the *designer code* with the layout you posted
Open the newly created `FormAbout.Designer.vb` (or `FormAbout2.Designer.vb`) and replace its contents with the clean designer-only code you pasted earlier.

If you want, paste the name of the new generated form file (`FormAbout2` vs `FormAbout`) and I’ll rewrite the designer code with the correct class name to match.

### Step C — Add your code-behind
Put the code-behind (the `FormAbout_Load` etc.) in `FormAbout.vb` (not the designer file).

### Step D — Wire up DependentUpon so it nests properly (optional but helpful)
Add this to your `.vbproj`:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Update="FormAbout.Designer.vb">
    <DependentUpon>FormAbout.vb</DependentUpon>
  </Compile>
  <EmbeddedResource Update="FormAbout.resx">
    <DependentUpon>FormAbout.vb</DependentUpon>
  </EmbeddedResource>
</ItemGroup>
```

Option Explicit On

Imports System.Diagnostics
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.Text

Public Class FormAbout

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        ' Title bar text
        Me.Text = "About AMiOffice"

        ' Big red app name line (your designer label)
        lblAppName.Text = "AMiOffice"

        ' Optional: make the "About:" header include the build display
        lblAbout.Text = "About: " & BuildInfo.DisplayVersion

        ' Show detailed info in the rich text box
        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Left
        rtbInfo.Font = New Font("Consolas", 9.75F, FontStyle.Regular)

        Dim exePath As String = Application.ExecutablePath
        Dim fvi As FileVersionInfo = FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(exePath)

        Dim sb As New StringBuilder()

        sb.AppendLine(BuildInfo.DisplayVersion)
        sb.AppendLine()

        sb.AppendLine($"Assembly Version : {My.Application.Info.Version}")
        sb.AppendLine($"File Version     : {Safe(fvi.FileVersion)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Product Version  : {Safe(fvi.ProductVersion)}")
        sb.AppendLine()

        sb.AppendLine($"Product Name     : {Safe(fvi.ProductName, "AMiOffice")}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Company          : {Safe(fvi.CompanyName)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Copyright        : {Safe(fvi.LegalCopyright)}")
        sb.AppendLine()

        sb.AppendLine($"Executable Path  : {exePath}")

        rtbInfo.Text = sb.ToString()

        ' If you want a nicer "Developed By" line, set it here:
        ' (Change the name to whatever you want displayed.)
        lblDevelopedBy.Text = "Developed By: Kirk Saffell"

        ' If you want to show an embedded image in picDeveloper, do it here.
        ' (Only if you have one available. Otherwise leave it blank.)
        ' picDeveloper.Image = My.Resources.YourImageName
    End Sub

    Private Shared Function Safe(value As String, Optional fallback As String = "") As String
        If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) Then
            If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fallback) Then
                Return "(not set)"
            End If
            Return fallback
        End If
        Return value.Trim()
    End Function

End Class

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
```

That file you restored as `FormAbout.Designer.vb` is **not** a designer file at all — it’s the **code-behind** (what belongs in `FormAbout.vb`). That’s exactly why you were getting all the partial/designer errors earlier.

## Do this exactly

### 1) Replace `FormAbout.Designer.vb` with the real designer-only content
Overwrite `C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\FormAbout.Designer.vb` with this (designer-only) file:

```vbnet name=FormAbout.Designer.vb
<Global.Microsoft.VisualBasic.CompilerServices.DesignerGenerated()>
Partial Class FormAbout
    Inherits System.Windows.Forms.Form

    <System.Diagnostics.DebuggerNonUserCode()>
    Protected Overrides Sub Dispose(ByVal disposing As Boolean)
        Try
            If disposing AndAlso components IsNot Nothing Then
                components.Dispose()
            End If
        Finally
            MyBase.Dispose(disposing)
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private components As System.ComponentModel.IContainer

    <System.Diagnostics.DebuggerStepThrough()>
    Private Sub InitializeComponent()
        Me.lblAbout = New Label()
        Me.lblAppName = New Label()
        Me.lblDevelopedBy = New Label()
        Me.picDeveloper = New PictureBox()
        Me.rtbInfo = New RichTextBox()
        Me.pnlBottom = New Panel()
        Me.btnOK = New Button()

        CType(Me.picDeveloper, ComponentModel.ISupportInitialize).BeginInit()
        Me.pnlBottom.SuspendLayout()
        Me.SuspendLayout()

        ' -------------------------
        ' lblAbout
        ' -------------------------
        Me.lblAbout.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        Me.lblAbout.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 8.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        Me.lblAbout.Location = New Point(0, 0)
        Me.lblAbout.Name = "lblAbout"
        Me.lblAbout.Size = New Size(800, 22)
        Me.lblAbout.TabIndex = 0
        Me.lblAbout.Text = "About:"
        Me.lblAbout.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleCenter

        ' -------------------------
        ' lblAppName
        ' -------------------------
        Me.lblAppName.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        Me.lblAppName.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        Me.lblAppName.ForeColor = Color.DarkRed
        Me.lblAppName.Location = New Point(0, 0)
        Me.lblAppName.Name = "lblAppName"
        Me.lblAppName.Size = New Size(800, 30)
        Me.lblAppName.TabIndex = 1
        Me.lblAppName.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"
        Me.lblAppName.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleCenter

        ' -------------------------
        ' lblDevelopedBy
        ' -------------------------
        Me.lblDevelopedBy.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        Me.lblDevelopedBy.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 8.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        Me.lblDevelopedBy.ForeColor = SystemColors.ControlText
        Me.lblDevelopedBy.BackColor = SystemColors.Control
        Me.lblDevelopedBy.Location = New Point(0, 0)
        Me.lblDevelopedBy.Name = "lblDevelopedBy"
        Me.lblDevelopedBy.Size = New Size(800, 22)
        Me.lblDevelopedBy.TabIndex = 2
        Me.lblDevelopedBy.Text = "Developed By:"
        Me.lblDevelopedBy.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleCenter

        ' -------------------------
        ' picDeveloper
        ' -------------------------
        Me.picDeveloper.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        Me.picDeveloper.Name = "picDeveloper"
        Me.picDeveloper.Size = New Size(800, 60)
        Me.picDeveloper.SizeMode = PictureBoxSizeMode.Zoom
        Me.picDeveloper.TabIndex = 3
        Me.picDeveloper.TabStop = False

        ' -------------------------
        ' pnlBottom
        ' -------------------------
        Me.pnlBottom.Dock = DockStyle.Bottom
        Me.pnlBottom.Name = "pnlBottom"
        Me.pnlBottom.Size = New Size(800, 45)
        Me.pnlBottom.TabIndex = 6

        ' btnOK
        Me.btnOK.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Right Or AnchorStyles.Bottom
        Me.btnOK.DialogResult = DialogResult.OK
        Me.btnOK.Location = New Point(673, 11)
        Me.btnOK.Name = "btnOK"
        Me.btnOK.Size = New Size(75, 23)
        Me.btnOK.TabIndex = 0
        Me.btnOK.Text = "OK"
        Me.btnOK.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True

        Me.pnlBottom.Controls.Add(Me.btnOK)

        ' -------------------------
        ' rtbInfo
        ' -------------------------
        Me.rtbInfo.BorderStyle = BorderStyle.FixedSingle
        Me.rtbInfo.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        Me.rtbInfo.Font = New Font("Consolas", 9.75F, FontStyle.Regular, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        Me.rtbInfo.Name = "rtbInfo"
        Me.rtbInfo.ReadOnly = True
        Me.rtbInfo.TabIndex = 5
        Me.rtbInfo.Text = ""

        ' -------------------------
        ' FormAbout
        ' -------------------------
        Me.AcceptButton = Me.btnOK
        Me.AutoScaleDimensions = New SizeF(7.0F, 15.0F)
        Me.AutoScaleMode = AutoScaleMode.Font
        Me.ClientSize = New Size(760, 550)

        Me.Controls.Add(Me.rtbInfo)
        Me.Controls.Add(Me.pnlBottom)
        Me.Controls.Add(Me.picDeveloper)
        Me.Controls.Add(Me.lblDevelopedBy)
        Me.Controls.Add(Me.lblAppName)
        Me.Controls.Add(Me.lblAbout)

        Me.FormBorderStyle = FormBorderStyle.FixedDialog
        Me.MaximizeBox = False
        Me.MinimizeBox = False
        Me.Name = "FormAbout"
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
        Me.Text = "About"

        CType(Me.picDeveloper, ComponentModel.ISupportInitialize).EndInit()
        Me.pnlBottom.ResumeLayout(False)
        Me.ResumeLayout(False)
    End Sub

    Friend WithEvents lblAbout As Label
    Friend WithEvents lblAppName As Label
    Friend WithEvents lblDevelopedBy As Label
    Friend WithEvents picDeveloper As PictureBox
    Friend WithEvents rtbInfo As RichTextBox
    Friend WithEvents pnlBottom As Panel
    Friend WithEvents btnOK As Button
End Class
```

### 2) Put your restored code into `FormAbout.vb` (and make it Partial)
Overwrite `C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\FormAbout.vb` with this:

```vbnet name=FormAbout.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Diagnostics
Imports System.Text

Partial Public Class FormAbout

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        Me.Text = "About AMiOffice"

        lblAppName.Text = "AMiOffice"
        lblAbout.Text = "About: " & BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        lblDevelopedBy.Text = "Developed By: Kirk Saffell"

        Dim exePath As String = Application.ExecutablePath
        Dim fvi As FileVersionInfo = FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(exePath)

        Dim sb As New StringBuilder()
        sb.AppendLine(BuildInfo.DisplayVersion)
        sb.AppendLine()
        sb.AppendLine($"Assembly Version : {My.Application.Info.Version}")
        sb.AppendLine($"File Version     : {Safe(fvi.FileVersion)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Product Version  : {Safe(fvi.ProductVersion)}")
        sb.AppendLine()
        sb.AppendLine($"Product Name     : {Safe(fvi.ProductName, "AMiOffice")}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Company          : {Safe(fvi.CompanyName)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Copyright        : {Safe(fvi.LegalCopyright)}")
        sb.AppendLine()
        sb.AppendLine($"Executable Path  : {exePath}")

        rtbInfo.Text = sb.ToString()
    End Sub

    Private Shared Function Safe(value As String, Optional fallback As String = "") As String
        If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) Then
            If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fallback) Then
                Return "(not set)"
            End If
            Return fallback
        End If
        Return value.Trim()
    End Function

End Class
```

### 3) Clean + rebuild
Close VS, delete `AMiOffice\bin` and `AMiOffice\obj`, reopen, rebuild.

### 4) (Optional but recommended) Reattach designer nesting in the `.vbproj`
Add:

```xml
<ItemGroup>
  <Compile Update="FormAbout.Designer.vb">
    <DependentUpon>FormAbout.vb</DependentUpon>
  </Compile>
  <EmbeddedResource Update="FormAbout.resx">
    <DependentUpon>FormAbout.vb</DependentUpon>
  </EmbeddedResource>
</ItemGroup>
```

If you don’t have `FormAbout.resx`, omit that EmbeddedResource entry.

If you do the above, your compile errors about duplicate Load/Safe and missing controls should disappear, and “View Designer” should return once VS recognizes the proper designer file again.

## Copilot

You didn’t break anything “mysteriously” — two specific regressions happened:

1) **Build number went back to 0** because your `IncrementBuildCounter` target only runs on the MSBuild `Publish` target, and **your batch file `dotnet publish ... /p:PublishProfile=...` is very likely not invoking the exact MSBuild target you think** (or it is, but the build counter file being read is `0`). The Learn docs even note the `Publish` target is ClickOnce-oriented in some contexts. ([learn.microsoft.com](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/visualstudio/msbuild/msbuild-targets?view=visualstudio&utm_source=openai))  
2) **About dialog lost the picture + share paths** because the code-behind you restored into the designer file didn’t include any of the logic that was previously setting `picDeveloper.Image` and writing the share paths into the rich text box. In other words: we restored layout + version text, but not your old “content” logic.

I can fix both, but I need the two images you mentioned (before/after) to reconstruct the exact About content. Please upload them.

Meanwhile, here are the concrete fixes.

---

## 1) Fix BuildNumber showing 0 (make it reliable for your batch publish)

### A. Make sure `build-counter.txt` lives where you think
Your project reads:

- `$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\build-counter.txt`

So it must be here:
`C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\build-counter.txt`

Open it and confirm it contains (for example):
```
14
```
(no extra blank lines).

### B. Don’t rely on `BeforeTargets="Publish"` for filesystem publish
In SDK-style projects, depending on how publish is invoked, `Publish` is not always the hook you expect. ([learn.microsoft.com](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/visualstudio/msbuild/msbuild-targets?view=visualstudio&utm_source=openai))  
Instead, hook into a publish pipeline target that **always** runs when you do `dotnet publish`.

Replace your counter target header:

```xml
<Target Name="IncrementBuildCounter" BeforeTargets="Publish">
```

with:

```xml
<Target Name="IncrementBuildCounter" BeforeTargets="Publish;PublishOnly;ComputeFilesToPublish">
```

That makes it fire for more publish paths.

### C. Add a message so you can see it running
Inside the target add:

```xml
<Message Text="IncrementBuildCounter running. BuildCounterFile=$(BuildCounterFile)" Importance="high" />
<Message Text="New BuildNumber=$(BuildNumber)" Importance="high" />
```

Then run your batch file and you should see those lines.

If you want, paste the console output around those messages and I’ll confirm it’s running.

---

## 2) Fix About dialog: restore developer picture + share paths

Right now your **Designer** only creates the `PictureBox`; it does not set the image. So the image must be assigned in code (or via resources). Your current `FormAbout.vb` replacement intentionally left that as a comment.

### What I need from you
To restore it exactly as before, please paste **your previous `FormAbout.vb`** (the one that showed the picture + share paths) OR upload the two screenshots so I can mirror the previous text.

### Immediate likely fix for the picture (two common cases)

**Case 1: picture is a file in the project**
If you previously loaded from a file path, you need something like:

```vb
picDeveloper.Image = Image.FromFile("C:\path\to\developer.png")
```

**Case 2: picture is a resource**
If the developer image is in `My Project\Resources.resx` as (say) `DeveloperPhoto`, then:

```vb
picDeveloper.Image = My.Resources.DeveloperPhoto
```

Tell me which one you used before (or paste the old code) and I’ll put it back.

### Share paths
Same story: those were almost certainly hardcoded or computed strings appended into `rtbInfo`. Once you upload the “before” screenshot (or paste the old code), I’ll rebuild that section verbatim.

---

## Quick questions (answer these and upload the screenshots)
1) What is the current content of `AMiOffice\build-counter.txt`?
2) When you run the publish batch file, does it print any of your MSBuild messages (if you add them)?
3) Upload the two “before/after” screenshots so I can restore the About dialog picture + share path text exactly.

## Replacement `FormAbout.vb` (use this)
This uses your existing controls from the designer you posted (`lblAbout`, `lblAppName`, `lblDevelopedBy`, `picDeveloper`, `rtbInfo`).

```vbnet name=FormAbout.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Diagnostics
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Reflection
Imports System.Runtime.InteropServices
Imports System.Text

Partial Public Class FormAbout

    Private Sub FormAbout_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        ' Window / header labels
        Me.Text = "About"
        lblAbout.Text = "About:"
        lblAppName.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application"
        lblDevelopedBy.Text = "Developed By:"

        ' Restore the ESC logo
        picDeveloper.Image = LoadEmbeddedImage({"ESC Logo.jpg", "AMiOffice.ESC Logo.jpg", "AMiOffice.ESC_Logo.jpg"})
        picDeveloper.Visible = (picDeveloper.Image IsNot Nothing)

        ' Build the info block (RichTextBox)
        rtbInfo.Clear()
        rtbInfo.SelectionAlignment = HorizontalAlignment.Center

        Dim exePath As String = Application.ExecutablePath
        Dim fvi As FileVersionInfo = FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(exePath)

        Dim sb As New StringBuilder()

        ' App identity
        sb.AppendLine("AMiOffice")
        sb.AppendLine($"Version: {My.Application.Info.Version}")
        sb.AppendLine($"File Version: {Safe(fvi.FileVersion)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Build: {BuildInfo.DisplayVersion}")
        sb.AppendLine()

        ' Author/company info (from your screenshot)
        sb.AppendLine("Author/Developer: Kirk Saffell")
        sb.AppendLine("Company: Enterprise Services & Consulting")
        sb.AppendLine("Phone: (661) 478-0990")
        sb.AppendLine("Contact: capnkirk@capnkirk.com")
        sb.AppendLine("Year: 2026")
        sb.AppendLine()

        ' Machine info
        sb.AppendLine($"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}")
        sb.AppendLine($"User: {Environment.UserName}")
        sb.AppendLine($"OS: {RuntimeInformation.OSDescription}")
        sb.AppendLine($".NET Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}")
        sb.AppendLine()

        ' Shared paths (adjust these strings if needed)
        sb.AppendLine("Shared paths:")

        Dim dataPath As String = "\\invoice\MainMenu\Data"
        Dim backupsPath As String = "\\invoice\MainMenu\Backups"
        Dim wordPath As String = "\\invoice\word"

        sb.AppendLine($"Data: {dataPath} {ExistsTag(dataPath)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Backups: {backupsPath} {ExistsTag(backupsPath)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Word: {wordPath} {ExistsTag(wordPath)}")

        rtbInfo.Text = sb.ToString()
    End Sub

    Private Shared Function ExistsTag(path As String) As String
        Try
            If Directory.Exists(path) Then
                Return ""
            End If
        Catch
            ' ignore (permissions/network issues) and treat as not found
        End Try

        Return "[NOT FOUND]"
    End Function

    Private Shared Function Safe(value As String, Optional fallback As String = "") As String
        If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) Then
            If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fallback) Then Return "(not set)"
            Return fallback
        End If
        Return value.Trim()
    End Function

    Private Shared Function LoadEmbeddedImage(candidateNames As IEnumerable(Of String)) As Image
        Dim asm As Assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim allNames As String() = asm.GetManifestResourceNames()

        For Each candidate In candidateNames
            ' Try exact match first
            Dim exact As String = allNames.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) String.Equals(n, candidate, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            If exact Is Nothing Then
                ' Try "ends with" match (common with VB root namespace prefixes)
                exact = allNames.FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(candidate, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            End If

            If exact IsNot Nothing Then
                Using s As Stream = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(exact)
                    If s IsNot Nothing Then
                        Return Image.FromStream(s)
                    End If
                End Using
            End If
        Next

        Return Nothing
    End Function

End Class
```

### One small compile note
That code uses `FirstOrDefault`, so you need LINQ. If you don’t already have it globally imported, add:

```vbnet
Imports System.Linq
```

at the top, or add `<Import Include="System.Linq" />` in the vbproj. (Your vbproj currently doesn’t import Linq.)

```vbnet name=FormAbout.vb

        Dim fvi As FileVersionInfo = FileVersionInfo.GetVersionInfo(exePath)

        Dim sb As New StringBuilder()

        sb.AppendLine("AMiOffice")
        sb.AppendLine($"Version: {My.Application.Info.Version}")
        sb.AppendLine($"File Version: {Safe(fvi.FileVersion)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Build: {BuildInfo.DisplayVersion}")
        sb.AppendLine()

        sb.AppendLine("Author/Developer: Kirk Saffell")
        sb.AppendLine("Company: Enterprise Services & Consulting")
        sb.AppendLine("Phone: (661) 478-0990")
        sb.AppendLine("Contact: capnkirk@capnkirk.com")
        sb.AppendLine("Year: 2026")
        sb.AppendLine()

        sb.AppendLine($"Machine: {Environment.MachineName}")
        sb.AppendLine($"User: {Environment.UserName}")
        sb.AppendLine($"OS: {RuntimeInformation.OSDescription}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Runtime: {RuntimeInformation.FrameworkDescription}")
        sb.AppendLine()

        sb.AppendLine("Shared paths:")

        Dim dataPath As String = "\\invoice\MainMenu\Data"
        Dim backupsPath As String = "\\invoice\MainMenu\Backups"
        Dim wordPath As String = "\\invoice\word"

        sb.AppendLine($"Data:    {dataPath}  {ExistsTag(dataPath)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Backups: {backupsPath}  {ExistsTag(backupsPath)}")
        sb.AppendLine($"Word:    {wordPath}  {ExistsTag(wordPath)}")

        rtbInfo.Text = sb.ToString()
    End Sub

    Private Shared Function ExistsTag(path As String) As String
        Try
            If Directory.Exists(path) Then Return ""
        Catch
            ' network/permission issues => treat as not found
        End Try
        Return "[NOT FOUND]"
    End Function

    Private Shared Function Safe(value As String, Optional fallback As String = "") As String
        If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(value) Then
            If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(fallback) Then Return "(not set)"
            Return fallback
        End If
        Return value.Trim()
    End Function

    Private Shared Function LoadEmbeddedImage(fileName As String) As Image
        Dim asm As Assembly = Assembly.GetExecutingAssembly()
        Dim resourceName As String =
            asm.GetManifestResourceNames().
                FirstOrDefault(Function(n) n.EndsWith(fileName, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))

        If resourceName Is Nothing Then Return Nothing

        Using s As Stream = asm.GetManifestResourceStream(resourceName)
            If s Is Nothing Then Return Nothing
            Return Image.FromStream(s)
        End Using
    End Function

End Class
```

## @testsubject117

yes, for Number 1 those are the correct paths for number 2, I've set the file at 15

## Copilot

If `build-counter.txt` is **15** and you still see **Build 0**, then your build is not reading the same file (or it’s not getting the value into `BuildInfo.g.vb` at compile time).

Here’s the quickest, deterministic way to fix it.

## 1) Ensure MSBuild reads the counter line as a *property* (not an item expression)
Right now in `GenerateBuildInfo` you do:

```xml
<BuildNumber>%(BuildCounterLines2.Identity)</BuildNumber>
```

That `%(...)` item metadata syntax is fragile inside a `PropertyGroup` and is the #1 reason people end up with blank/0 values.

### Replace your `GenerateBuildInfo` read section with this
In your `.vbproj`, inside `Target Name="GenerateBuildInfo"`, replace everything from the `<ReadLinesFromFile ...>` through the conditional `<PropertyGroup ...>` with this block:

```xml
    <ReadLinesFromFile File="$(BuildCounterFile)">
      <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildCounterLines2" />
    </ReadLinesFromFile>

    <!-- Convert first line item into a real property -->
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.String]::Copy('%(BuildCounterLines2.Identity)'))</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <!-- Trim + fallback -->
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.String]::Copy('$(BuildNumber)').Trim())</BuildNumber>
      <BuildNumber Condition="'$(BuildNumber)'==''">0</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>
```

That forces the first line into a normal MSBuild property and trims whitespace.

## 2) Add a high-importance message so you can SEE what it read
Still inside `GenerateBuildInfo` (after the property group above), add:

```xml
    <Message Text="GenerateBuildInfo: BuildCounterFile=$(BuildCounterFile) BuildNumber=$(BuildNumber)" Importance="high" />
```

Now when you build, the Output window / build log will literally show the number MSBuild is using.

## 3) Confirm the generated VB file contains Build 15
After a rebuild, open:

`C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\Generated\BuildInfo.g.vb`

You should see a line like:

```vb
Friend Const BuildNumber As String = "15"
```

If it still shows `"0"`, the message we added will tell us why.

## 4) About box + shared paths + logo
Since you confirmed the UNC paths, the `FormAbout.vb` I gave you will restore:
- the ESC logo (from embedded `ESC Logo.jpg`)
- the shared paths with `[NOT FOUND]`

If the logo still doesn’t show after this: tell me whether the file name is exactly `ESC Logo.jpg` (case/spaces) and whether it’s in the project root or a subfolder.

`GenerateBuildInfo: BuildCounterFile=... BuildNumber=...`

That will let me finish this in one step if anything’s still off.

## Copilot

Good—when you publish, here are the exact checks to confirm everything is behaving the way you want (and how to interpret the result):

## On your dev machine (right after Publish)
1) Confirm the counter incremented:
- Open `C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\build-counter.txt`
- It should now be **16** (since you said it was 15 before publish).

2) Confirm the published EXE has the right versions:
- In the publish output folder, right-click the EXE → **Properties → Details**
- Check:
  - **File version** should be `1.0.0.0` (per your vbproj)
  - **Product version** should show something like `1.0 (Build 16)` if your `InformationalVersion` is being applied as you intended.

3) Run the published EXE locally and verify:
- Main menu title shows `AMiOffice v1.0 (Build 16)`
- About box shows the logo + shared paths + the same build number.

## On the workstation
If the workstation shows a different build number than what you published, it almost always means the workstation is running an **older copy** from a different folder/shortcut.

Quick verification on the workstation:

- Make sure it points to the folder you just copied to.

## @testsubject117

yes, on the workstation everything appears fine except the shared data paths do not saying either OK or Not found

## Copilot

That’s just the `ExistsTag()` logic: right now it returns **blank when the folder exists**, and only prints `[NOT FOUND]` when it doesn’t. On your workstation the shares likely exist, so you’re seeing nothing.

Change it so it explicitly shows `[OK]` when the path exists.

## Replace `ExistsTag` with this
In `FormAbout.vb`, replace your current function:

```vbnet
Private Shared Function ExistsTag(path As String) As String
    Try
        If Directory.Exists(path) Then Return ""
    Catch
    End Try
    Return "[NOT FOUND]"
End Function
```

with:

```vbnet
Private Shared Function ExistsTag(path As String) As String
    Try
        If Directory.Exists(path) Then Return "[OK]"
    Catch
        ' network/permission issues => treat as not found
    End Try
    Return "[NOT FOUND]"
End Function
```

Now your About box will show:

- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data  [OK]` (when reachable)
- `... [NOT FOUND]` (when not)

If you’d like it to distinguish **“Access denied”** vs **“Not found”**, tell me and I’ll add a more detailed check (attempt a directory listing and catch `UnauthorizedAccessException` separately).

## @testsubject117

ok, are we ready to start bringing in the other items such as ledger.bas etc.?

## Copilot

Yes—ready. To bring `ledger.bas` (and the other legacy modules) in cleanly, I need a couple specifics so we don’t reintroduce the “duplicate class/module / designer breaks / wrong build” kind of problems.

### 1) What exactly are we importing?
- Where are the source files right now (folder path)?
- List the filenames you want to add (e.g., `ledger.bas`, `inventory.bas`, etc.).

### 2) What target are we importing *from*?
Are these from:
- VB6 / VBA (`.bas` with VB6 syntax), or
- VB.NET (`.vb`) modules that just happen to be named `.bas`?

This matters because VB.NET won’t compile VB6/VBA `.bas` as-is.

### 3) What do you want the end state to be?
Pick one:
- **A)** “Make it compile first” (minimal changes; stubs/wrappers allowed)
- **B)** “Port properly” (convert to VB.NET patterns, types, IO, DB access, etc.)

### Recommended approach (once I know the above)
- Create a folder like `Legacy\` and add modules one-by-one.
- If they’re VB6/VBA: convert each to a VB.NET `Module` (`.vb`) and fix:
  - `Declare`/API calls
  - default properties
  - `Variant`, `Currency`, `Date` semantics
  - file IO (`Open #` → `System.IO`)
  - database access (DAO/ADO → ADO.NET or OleDb)
- Add a quick “smoke test” call path from the main menu so we can validate behavior incrementally.

### Next step
Paste the contents of `ledger.bas` (or upload it), and tell me whether it’s VB6/VBA or already VB.NET style. Then I’ll tell you exactly what to rename/change and where to place it in the project.

## @testsubject117

all .bas files are from DOS 5.0 GWBasic.exe

## Copilot

No—we’re not ready to “bring in ledger.bas” directly as source code, because **DOS GW-BASIC `.BAS` is not compatible with VB.NET** (or even VB6) and cannot be compiled/imported into your WinForms project as-is. We *can* bring the functionality in, but it’s a **port/rewrite** effort.

### What this means in practice
GW-BASIC programs typically rely on:
- line numbers (`10 PRINT ...`)
- `GOTO/GOSUB/RETURN`
- `ON ERROR GOTO`
- `DEF FN`
- `DATA/READ/RESTORE`
- `INPUT`, `INKEY$`, screen/keyboard control, and direct console UI
- fixed-length record files / random access (`OPEN "R"`, `FIELD`, `PUT/GET`)
- sometimes `CHAIN`, `COMMON`, etc.

None of that maps 1:1 to WinForms.

---

## Best path forward (recommended)
### Phase 1: Decide your “ground truth”
Pick one:
1) **Keep GW-BASIC as the reference** and port module-by-module to VB.NET, validating outputs match.
2) **Treat the data as the reference** (ledgers/files) and re-implement the business rules in VB.NET, validating against known reports.

### Phase 2: Get it running in a controlled way
For `ledger.bas`, we start by extracting:
- Inputs (files, parameters, user prompts)
- Outputs (reports, files, totals)
- Data formats (record layouts)

Then we build a VB.NET “ledger engine” library (plain classes/modules) and call it from your WinForms UI.

---

## What I need from you to start
Upload/paste **ledger.bas** (and any files it depends on), plus answer these:

1) Does `ledger.bas` read/write data files? If yes, what are their names/extensions (e.g., `.DAT`, `.IDX`, `.TXT`)?
2) How big is it (roughly how many lines)?
3) What does “ledger” do in your workflow—post transactions, print reports, update balances?
4) Do you still have sample data files we can test against?

---

## If you want the fastest possible “first win”
We can start with a **GW-BASIC compatibility runner** approach:

- Step A: Identify exactly what ledger.bas *produces* (e.g., a printed report / text file).
- Step B: Port only the part that generates that output (no UI yet).
- Step C: Verify output matches the old program with the same inputs.

---

### Next message
Paste the first ~80 lines of `ledger.bas` (top of file) and the section where it opens files (look for `OPEN` statements). That’s enough for me to tell you:
- what data formats you have
- whether it’s sequential or random access
- the right porting strategy for this module

## @testsubject117

are you still aware of the contents of mainmenu.bas and it's dependancies?

## Copilot

No—I’m not currently aware of the contents of `mainmenu.bas` (or its dependencies) because you haven’t provided that code in this chat, and I don’t have access to your local files unless you paste/upload them (or point me at a GitHub repo and branch so I can use repo tools).

If you want, I can become “aware” of it quickly in either of these ways:

### Option A (fastest)
Paste:
1) the first ~100 lines of `mainmenu.bas`
2) every line that contains any of these keywords (these usually reveal dependencies):
- `CHAIN`, `COMMON`, `GOSUB`, `GOTO`
- `OPEN`, `FIELD`, `GET`, `PUT`, `CLOSE`
- `DEF FN`, `DECLARE`, `CALL`, `SHELL`
- `DATA`, `READ`, `RESTORE`
- any `RUN`, `SYSTEM`, `ON ERROR`

### Option B (best)
Upload `mainmenu.bas` plus the other `.bas` files it uses (zip is fine), and if there are data/layout files (e.g., `.DAT`, `.IDX`, `.CFG`), include at least one sample set.

Once I have it, I can:
- map the dependency graph (which modules call which)
- identify file formats and record layouts
- propose a VB.NET module breakdown (ledger/reporting/data access)
- suggest a step-by-step port plan that keeps your current WinForms app stable while adding features incrementally.

2040 OPEN "NOTES-4.ED" FOR OUTPUT LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:Q$="BACKUP ERROR! on "+DATE$+" "+Q$:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1:OPEN "ERRORS.LST" FOR APPEND LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1:GOTO 490
2041 REM ############ ADD IN DAILY JOKE ##############
2042 PRINT:PRINT"Please Wait...":OPEN "JOKE.NUM" FOR INPUT AS 1:INPUT#1,JN:CLOSE 1:JN=JN+1:IF TODAY$="Sunday" OR TODAY$="Saturday" THEN 2060

2044 IF EOF(1) THEN JN=1:RESET:GOTO 2046

2046 RESET:OPEN "NOTES-4.ED" FOR OUTPUT LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1
2047 OPEN "JOKE.NUM" FOR OUTPUT LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,JN:CLOSE 1
2060 IF JOKE<>1 THEN SHELL "UNPAID"
2065 JOKE=0:GOTO 490
2070 REM ##################################################################              ###############  Change Main Menu Message  ######################              ##################################################################
2080 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"<<< Change Main Menu Message >>>":PRINT:PRINT:PRINT"Current Message is:":PRINT:PRINT A1$:PRINT:PRINT:PRINT:PRINT"Enter New Message. [ENTER = Keep Current Message]  [N = No Message]":PRINT:PRINT"  ";
2090 FOR X=1 TO 129:PRINT"_";:NEXT:PRINT CHR$(30);CHR$(30):INPUT Q$
2100 IF LEN(Q$)>129 THEN 2080
2110 IF Q$="" THEN 20
2120 IF Q$="N" OR Q$="n" THEN Q$=""
2125 OPEN "MSGCHNG.DAT" FOR APPEND LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1
2130 OPEN "NOTES-4.ED" FOR OUTPUT LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1:GOTO 20

2150 LPRINT CHR$(27);"i";CHR$(1)
2160 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"<<< TYPE WRITTER MODE >>>      [TAB = Move to Right 5 Spaces]  [ESC = Exit]":PRINT:PRINT
2170 PRINT"....A....B....C....D....E....F....G....H....I....J....K....L....M....N....O....P"
2180 LN$="":COLOR 0,7:PRINT" ";:COLOR 7,0
2190 Q$=INKEY$:IF Q$="" THEN 2190
2200 IF Q$=CHR$(27) THEN LPRINT CHR$(27);"@":GOTO 490
2210 IF Q$=CHR$(9) THEN Q$="     ":REM *** TAB KEY ***
2220 IF Q$=CHR$(13) THEN LPRINT LN$:PRINT:GOTO 2160
2230 IF ASC(Q$)=8 AND LEN(LN$)=0 THEN 2190
2240 IF ASC(Q$)=8 THEN LN$=LEFT$(LN$,LEN(LN$)-1):PRINT:PRINT CHR$(30);TAB(80);:PRINT:PRINT CHR$(30);LN$;:COLOR 0,7:PRINT" ";:COLOR 7,0:GOTO 2190
2250 LN$=LN$+Q$
2260 IF LEN(LN$)>79 THEN LN$=(LEFT$(LN$,79))
2270 PRINT:PRINT CHR$(30);LN$;:COLOR 0,7:PRINT" ";:COLOR 7,0
2280 GOTO 2190
2290 REM ##################################################################              ####################   subliminal messages  ######################              ##################################################################
2300 INPUT "  Enter Password ";Q$:IF Q$="dean" OR Q$="DEAN" THEN 2320
2310 GOTO 490
2320 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"<<< SUBLIMINAL MESSAGE GENERATOR >>>":PRINT:PRINT:PRINT"Current message: ";
2330 OPEN "SUBLIMAL.MES" FOR INPUT AS 1:INPUT#1,MESS$:CLOSE 1:PRINT MESS$:PRINT:PRINT
2340 INPUT "Enter new message [Enter = Current Message] ";Q$:IF Q$="" THEN 490
2350 IF LEN(Q$)>35 THEN PRINT"Message too long.":GOTO 2340
2360 OPEN "SUBLIMAL.MES" FOR OUTPUT LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1
2370 OPEN "SUBLIMAL.LOG" FOR APPEND LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$;DATE$:CLOSE 1
2380 GOTO 490
2399 REM ##################################################################              ####################   BACKUP FILES TO D: DRIVE   ################              ##################################################################
2400 OPEN "D:\DDRIVE.CHK" FOR INPUT AS 1:CLOSE 1
2410 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"The computer is preparing to backup all the important data.":PRINT:PRINT"This process takes about 30 minutes.":PRINT
2420 SHELL"DT D: /D /M"
2430 SHELL"XCOPY PRC\*.PRC D:\PRC\*.PRC"
2440 SHELL"XCOPY *.DAT D:\"
2450 SHELL"XCOPY *.CUR D:\"
2460 SHELL"XCOPY *.TOT D:\"
2470 SHELL"XCOPY *.INV D:\"
2480 SHELL"XCOPY *.DEP D:\"
2490 SHELL"XCOPY *.CHK D:\"
2500 SHELL"XCOPY *.BAS D:\"
2501 SHELL"XCOPY *.PMS D:\ /M"
2510 SHELL"XCOPY BILLS.* D:\ /M"
2512 SHELL"XCOPY F:\WORD\*.DOC D:\WORD /M"
2520 Q$="XCOPY *."+RIGHT$(DATE$,2)+" D:\":SHELL Q$
2522 SHELL"XCOPY F:\PHONE\*.BAS D:\PHONE"
2523 SHELL"XCOPY F:\PHONE\*.LST D:\PHONE"
2524 SHELL"XCOPY F:\PHONE\*.EXE D:\PHONE"
2525 SHELL"XCOPY F:\PHONE\*.OVL D:\PHONE"
2530 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\0\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2531 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\1\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2532 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\2\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2533 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\3\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2534 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\4\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2535 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\5\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2536 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\6\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2537 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\7\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2538 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\8\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2539 SHELL"AUTOBOOT /NIL":REM ******* REBOOT SYSTEM AND GO TO TIMECARD ****
2540 OPEN "crdnumbr.dat" FOR INPUT AS 2
2550 INPUT#2,CDN$:CLOSE 2
2560 CDN=VAL(CDN$)
2570 X=CDN-480
2580 IF X<0 THEN X=3000+X
2590 IF X=CDN+1 THEN 490
2595 X$=STR$(X):X$=RIGHT$(X$,LEN(X$)-1)
2600 Q$="COPY F:\SHOPCARD\"+X$+".CRD D:\SHOPCARD":PRINT Q$
2605 SHELL Q$
2610 X=X+1:IF X>3000 THEN X=1
2620 GOTO 2590
2699 REM ##################################################################              ####################   CALC THE DAY OF THE WEEK   ################              ##################################################################
2710 DAYS31$(0)="Saturday":DAYS31$(1)="Sunday":DAYS31$(2)="Monday":DAYS31$(3)="Tuesday":DAYS31$(4)="Wednesday":DAYS31$(5)="Thursday":DAYS31$(6)="Friday"
2720 S31$=DATE$
2730 PS31=INSTR(S31$,"-"):MONTH31= VAL(LEFT$(S31$,PS31-1)):  S31$= MID$(S31$,PS31+1)
2740 PS31=INSTR(S31$,"-"):  DAY =  VAL(LEFT$(S31$,PS31-1)):  S31$= MID$(S31$,PS31+1)
2750 YEAR31=VAL(S31$)
2760 IF MONTH31>2 THEN 2780
2770 MONTH31=MONTH31+12 : YEAR31=YEAR31-1
2780 N31=DAY+2*MONTH31+INT(.6*(MONTH31+1))+YEAR31+INT(YEAR31/4)-INT(YEAR31/100)+INT(YEAR31/400)+  2
2790 N31=INT((N31/7-INT(N31/7))*7+.5)
2800 TODAY$=DAYS31$(N31)
2810 RETURN


## Copilot

Start with **LEDGER** (the one invoked by `CHAIN "LEDGER"` / menu option C). It’s the fastest/easiest to implement first because it’s a “business-logic + file I/O” program you can port into a **single VB.NET workflow** without having to recreate the entire DOS UI/keyboard behavior.

### Why LEDGER is the best first file (based on your `MAINMENU.BAS` snippet)
Your main menu does a lot of DOS-only things that are **hard** to port directly:

- `DEF SEG=&H40 : POKE/PEEK` (keyboard LEDs / caps/num lock)
- `KEY` macro programming
- `INKEY$` loops + screen blanker
- `LOCATE`, `COLOR`, ASCII art UI
- heavy `SHELL`, `CHAIN`, `SYSTEM`, floppy/drive assumptions (`A:\`, `F:\`, `D:\`)
- lots of “operator experience” features (beeps, time-based logic)

Those are “polish” features and **not** where you want to start.

By contrast, the “real work” programs behind menu items (like `LEDGER`, `SALES`, `LOGBOOK`, `QUOTE`, `PHONE/ROLODEX`) usually have:
- defined inputs/outputs
- persistent files
- deterministic calculations
- report printing

Those are much more straightforward to port and test.

---

## What I need from you to begin porting LEDGER
To port `LEDGER.BAS` into VB.NET, I need just two things from you first:

1) **Convert `LEDGER.BAS` to ASCII and paste/upload it** (or upload the file).
2) Also paste/upload any **data file specs** it touches (even if you don’t know the format yet).  
   I’ll discover them from `OPEN` statements, but sample files help.

### When you paste it, include at least:
- the top of the file
- every `OPEN` / `FIELD` / `GET` / `PUT` / `INPUT #` / `WRITE #` line
- any `TYPE`-like record layout comments (sometimes they’re in comments)

---

## After LEDGER, the next easiest set
Once LEDGER is in, good next targets (usually) are:

1) `SALES` (Sales Journal) — often similar file/report patterns
2) `LOGBOOK` (view/add) — typically append-only text/data
3) `QUOTE` (form generator) — might be print formatting heavy but doable
4) `PLIST` (price list) — can be more UI-heavy depending on editing/search

I’d postpone anything that is mostly DOS shell/utility work, like:
- “backup/restore” routines (lots of path assumptions and `SHELL ZIP/UNZIP/XCOPY`)
- keyboard cleaning mode / typewriter mode / screen saver
- “send a message” batch-file generation
- anything that launches external DOS apps

---

## Concrete next step
Upload/paste `LEDGER.BAS` (ASCII) and tell me:
- what VB.NET action should happen from the WinForms menu click?
  - open a new form to “View Ledger”
  - generate a report to screen
  - print
  - export to text/PDF

Once I see `LEDGER.BAS`, I’ll reply with:
- a dependency map (what other `.BAS`/files it expects)
- the VB.NET module/classes we should create first
- a “minimum viable” port plan that compiles and runs in AMiOffice quickly.

## @testsubject117

do I need to convert ledger.bas to ascii then copy to you?

## Copilot

Yes—convert `LEDGER.BAS` to plain ASCII text and then paste it here (or upload the `.bas` text file). I can’t read the original GW-BASIC “tokenized” format reliably, and VB.NET can’t either.

### What “ASCII” means here
You want the file to be human-readable in Notepad and look like:

- line numbers (`10`, `20`, `30`…)
- keywords like `OPEN`, `INPUT#`, `GOSUB`, etc.
- quoted strings visible

### How to convert (pick whichever you already have)
**Option A (best, if you have GW-BASIC/QB available):**
- Open the program in GW-BASIC/QBASIC/QuickBASIC
- Use `SAVE "LEDGER.ASC", A` (the `,A` is the key: ASCII format)

**Option B (if you only have the tokenized file):**
- Use whatever tool you used for `MAINMENU.BAS` to dump it to text (same process).

### What to send me
To start, you don’t have to paste the whole thing if it’s huge. Send:

1) The first ~150 lines, and  
2) Every block that contains file I/O, especially lines with:
- `OPEN`
- `FIELD`
- `GET` / `PUT`
- `INPUT#` / `LINE INPUT#`
- `WRITE#` / `PRINT#`
- `CLOSE`
- `KILL`, `NAME`, `FILES`

And tell me what the original filename is (`LEDGER.BAS` vs `LEDGER`), because `CHAIN "LEDGER"` depends on that.

Once I see that, I’ll tell you exactly what the dependencies are (data files, other BAS modules) and what the fastest VB.NET “first slice” should be.

## @testsubject117

I'm having trouble converting ledger.bas. I'm loading gwbasic, the loading ledger.bas, then F4 for save, saving the file like this: SAVE"LEDGER.BAS,A and it's not saving as text

## Copilot

In GW-BASIC the ASCII flag is **not** part of the filename string. It’s a second argument to `SAVE`.

Use this exact syntax (note the comma is **outside** the quotes):

```basic
SAVE "LEDGER.ASC", A
```

or, if you want to keep the `.BAS` extension:

```basic
SAVE "LEDGER.BAS", A
```

What you typed:

```basic
SAVE"LEDGER.BAS,A
```

puts `,A` *inside* the filename (and also has quoting issues), so it won’t do an ASCII save.

## Step-by-step (GW-BASIC)
1) Run `GWBASIC`
2) `LOAD "LEDGER.BAS"`
3) At the `Ok` prompt, type:

```basic
SAVE "LEDGER.ASC", A
```

4) You should see `Ok` again.
5) Then `SYSTEM` (or exit) and check the output file in the same directory GW-BASIC is using.

## How to confirm it worked
Open `LEDGER.ASC` in Notepad:
- you should see line numbers like `10`, `20`, `30`…
- readable keywords (`PRINT`, `OPEN`, `GOSUB`, etc.)

## Common gotcha: wrong working directory
If you don’t see the file where you expect, inside GW-BASIC do:

```basic
FILES
```

That will show the current directory contents so you can confirm where it saved.

## If `SAVE "LEDGER.ASC", A` errors
Tell me the exact error message (verbatim) and:
- whether you’re using GW-BASIC, QBASIC, or QuickBASIC
- what DOS version/environment (real DOS, DOSBox, etc.)
- and whether the file is tokenized or already ASCII

Then I’ll give the exact compatible command for your version.

## Copilot

That output is exactly what a **tokenized GW-BASIC program** looks like when it’s opened/printed as raw bytes (mojibake). It means the “save as ASCII” did **not** happen, or you’re viewing the wrong file after saving.

Here’s how to fix it reliably.

## 1) Use the correct GW-BASIC command (don’t use the F4 dialog)
In GW-BASIC, do this at the `Ok` prompt:

```basic
LOAD "LEDGER.BAS"
SAVE "LEDGER.TXT", A
```

Key points:
- The comma and `A` are **outside** the quotes.
- Use a *new* filename like `LEDGER.TXT` so you don’t accidentally re-open the original tokenized file.

Then:

```basic
SYSTEM
```

## 2) Verify you’re looking at the ASCII file, not the original
Back in DOS (or File Explorer), make sure you open **LEDGER.TXT** in Notepad, not `LEDGER.BAS`.

Inside GW-BASIC you can confirm the file exists with:

```basic
FILES "LEDGER.*"
```

You should see both `LEDGER.BAS` and `LEDGER.TXT`.

## 3) If `SAVE "LEDGER.TXT", A` gives an error
Tell me the exact message. Common ones:

### A) “Bad file name”
You’re in the wrong directory or using a path GW-BASIC can’t write to.
Fix: `CHDIR` to a writable folder, or use a simple name with no path.

### B) “Direct statement in file”
The program contains something GW-BASIC can’t store in ASCII format (rare, but it happens).
Fix: We can still extract it using `LIST` to a file (next section).

## 4) Fallback method that always works: redirect LIST to a file
If for any reason `SAVE ..., A` won’t work, do:

```basic
LOAD "LEDGER.BAS"
FILES
LIST
```

(Confirm it displays readable code on screen. If it does, it can be captured.)

Then do:

```basic
OPEN "LEDGER.TXT" FOR OUTPUT AS #1
PRINT #1, " "   ' (optional, just to create/truncate)
CLOSE #1
```

Unfortunately GW-BASIC doesn’t have an easy built-in “LIST to file” like some BASICs, but there is a common workaround using `LLIST` (printer) if your environment supports redirecting LPT1 to a file:

```basic
LOAD "LEDGER.BAS"
LLIST
```

And in DOS you can redirect printer output to a file if LPT1 is captured. Whether this works depends on DOSBox/Windows setup.

So: **try `SAVE "LEDGER.TXT", A` first**—it’s the correct and simplest approach.

## 5) One quick question (this matters)
What environment are you using to run GW-BASIC?

- Real DOS
- DOSBox
- Windows NTVDM
- Something else

And when you run:

```basic
LOAD "LEDGER.BAS"
LIST 1-20
```

Do you see readable BASIC lines (with line numbers) or do you see garbage?

### If `LIST 1-20` is readable
Then the program is fine and we just need to ensure you’re opening the correct ASCII output file.

### If `LIST 1-20` is garbage
Then `LEDGER.BAS` is not a GW-BASIC text/token file you can load (it might be compressed/encrypted/renamed binary), and we’ll need a different extraction approach.

Reply with:
1) the output of `LIST 1-20`
2) the output of `FILES "LEDGER.*"` after you run the SAVE command

Great—then we’re ready to start.

### What to send first (so we can pick the fastest “first slice”)
Paste (or upload) **just these parts** of the ASCII `LEDGER` text:

1) **Lines 1–120** (top of file: constants, menus, globals)
2) **Every place it does file I/O**, i.e. all lines containing any of:
   - `OPEN`
   - `FIELD`
   - `GET` / `PUT`
   - `INPUT#` / `LINE INPUT#`
   - `WRITE#` / `PRINT#`
   - `CLOSE`
   - `KILL` / `NAME`
3) Any lines that mention other programs/modules, e.g.:
   - `CHAIN`
   - `RUN`
   - `SHELL`

That’s enough for me to map:
- which data files the ledger uses
- whether it’s sequential or random-access records
- what dependencies (other `.BAS`) it calls
- the best minimal VB.NET port we can implement first (view-only ledger vs posting vs reports)

### One quick note
When you paste it here, don’t worry about perfect formatting—just keep the line numbers if possible. If it’s huge, uploading the `.txt` is easier.

Send that and I’ll reply with:
- a dependency list (“you’ll need these files present: …”)
- the VB.NET structures/classes to create
- and the exact first feature we should implement to get a working menu link quickly.
