485 PRINT TAB(34);"____";CHR$(30):INPUT "Enter Year [Enter = All Years] ";YEAR$:IF YEAR$="" THEN 490
486 IF YEAR$>"2010" OR YEAR$<"1986" THEN 485
487 PRINT TAB(36);"__";CHR$(30):INPUT "Enter Month [Enter = All Months] ";MTH$:IF LEN(MTH$)=1 THEN MTH$="0"+MTH$
488 IF MTH$="" THEN 490
489 IF LEN(MTH$)>1 THEN IF VAL(MTH$)<1 OR VAL(MTH$)>12 THEN 487
490 OPEN "LEDGER.CUR" FOR INPUT AS #2
500 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"Please Wait... [ESC = Exit]";CHR$(30);CHR$(30):Q=0
510 IF EOF(2) THEN CLOSE 2:BEEP:PRINT"<<<End of Ledger>>>,  [Q = Quit]";TAB(55)"Grand total ";USING"$########.##";TOTAL#:GOTO 670
520 INPUT#2,N2$:INPUT#2,DT$:INPUT#2,CNUM$:INPUT#2,INUMS$:INPUT#2,AMT:INPUT#2,RF$
530 IF INKEY$=CHR$(27) THEN RESET:GOTO 200
540 IF INSTR(INUMS$,INVC$)>0 THEN 590
550 IF N$="A" AND CKN$="A" THEN 590
560 IF CKN$=CNUM$ THEN IF N$="A" OR N$=N2$ THEN 590
570 IF N2$=N$ THEN IF CKN$=CNUM$ OR CKN$="A" THEN 590
580 GOTO 510
590 IF YEAR$<>"" AND RIGHT$(YEAR$,2)<>RIGHT$(DT$,2) THEN 510
592 IF MTH$<>"" AND MTH$<>LEFT$(DT$,2) THEN 510
595 PRINT"Customer: ";N2$ TAB(35) "Date: ";DT$
600 PRINT"Check #: ";CNUM$;"     Reference: ";RF$:PRINT"Difference Between Total of Invoices & Check Amount: ";INUMS$:TOTAL#=TOTAL#+AMT
610 PRINT"Amount: ";:PRINT USING"$######.##";AMT:PRINT"*****************************************************"
620 Q=Q+1:IF Q<4 THEN 510
630 PRINT"Hit [ENTER] for more, or [Q] to quit..."
640 Q$=INKEY$:IF Q$=CHR$(13) THEN 500
650 IF Q$="Q" OR Q$="q" THEN CLOSE 2:GOTO 200
660 GOTO 640
670 IF INKEY$<>"Q" THEN 670
680 GOTO 200
690 REM #########  ADD A CHECK  #########
700 PRINT
710 TOT=0:DEAN$="":INUMS$="":CT=0:CLS:PRINT:PRINT"***** Add A Check *****":PRINT
720 PRINT TAB(27);"________";CHR$(30):INPUT "Company Name [Q = Quit] ";N2$:N2$=LEFT$(N2$,8):N5$=N2$+".PRC":IF ASC(LEFT$(N2$,1))>96 THEN PRINT CHR$(30);"HIT THE CAPS LOCK KEY...                        ":GOTO 720
722 IF N2$="Q" OR N2$="q" THEN 200
725 IF LEN(N2$)<3 OR LEN(N2$)>8 THEN 720
740 OPEN "PRC\"+N5$ FOR INPUT AS #2:CLOSE 2
750 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"***** Add A Check *****":PRINT:PRINT"Company Name: ";N2$
780 INPUT "Check Amount ";CHKAMT:IF CHKAMT<.1 THEN PRINT CHR$(30);:GOTO 780
785 GOTO 3500: REM ####### GOTO AUTO SCAN ######
790 RESET:OPEN "INVOICE.CHK" SHARED AS #1 LEN=26
800 FIELD #1,9 AS INUM2$,8 AS AMT2$,8 AS CO$,1 AS FLAG$
810 REM ######## COMPARE $ IN INVOICE.CHK FILE ###########
820 CT=CT+1:PRINT"(";CT;:IF CT<10 THEN PRINT" ";
830 PRINT ")  Enter Invoice # [-1 = Quit] [9 = Go Back One]";:INPUT INUM1:IF INUM1 = -1 THEN CT=CT-1:GOTO 1000
840 IF INUM1=9 AND CT>1 THEN BEEP:CT=CT-2:PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(79);CHR$(30);CHR$(30);SPACE$(79);CHR$(30):GOTO 820
850 IF INUM1<75000! OR INUM1>999999! THEN BEEP:CT=CT-1:PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(79);CHR$(30):GOTO 820
860 IFIND=INT(INUM1-75000!)
870 LOCK#1,IFIND:GET#1,IFIND
880 IF IFIND<>CVS(INUM2$) THEN BEEP:PRINT CHR$(30);"INVOICE #";INUM1;" DOESNT EXIST                                 ":CT=CT-1:GOTO 980
890 IF N2$<>LEFT$(CO$,LEN(N2$)) THEN BEEP:PRINT CHR$(30);"INVOICE #";INUM1;" COMPANY IS ";CO$;"                        ":IF CT<>1 THEN CT=CT-1:GOTO 980:ELSE GOTO 892
891 GOTO 900
892 CO7$=CO$:IF INSTR(CO$," ")>0 THEN CO7$=LEFT$(CO$,INSTR(CO$," ")-1)
893 PRINT:PRINT"You told me the customer was ";N2$:PRINT"Is the customer really ";CO7$;" (Y/N) ? ";
894 Q$=INKEY$:IF Q$="Y" OR Q$="y" THEN PRINT"YES,  Name Changed.":N2$=CO7$:GOTO 900
895 IF Q$="n" OR Q$="N" THEN PRINT"NO":CT=CT-1:GOTO 980
898 GOTO 894
900 X=0
910 X=X+1:IF X=>CT THEN 940
920 IF HOLD(X)=IFIND THEN BEEP:PRINT CHR$(30);"INVOICE #";INUM1;" HAS ALREADY BEEN ENTERED                       ":CT=CT-1:GOTO 980
930 GOTO 910
940 IF FLAG$="P" THEN BEEP:PRINT CHR$(30);"INVOICE #";INUM1;" HAS ALREADY BEEN PAYED WITH A CHECK.                ":CT=CT-1:GOTO 980
950 IF FLAG$="C" THEN BEEP:PRINT CHR$(30);"INVOICE #";INUM1;" IS A CORRECTED CERT.                                ":CT=CT-1:GOTO 980
960 IF FLAG$="V" THEN BEEP:PRINT CHR$(30);"INVOICE #";INUM1;" IS VOID.                                            ":CT=CT-1:GOTO 980
970 HOLD(CT)=IFIND:INUM$="0":Q$=STR$(INUM1):N$(CT)=RIGHT$(Q$,LEN(Q$)-1):N#(CT)=CVS(AMT2$):GOTO 990
980 FOR X=1 TO 6500:NEXT:PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(78);CHR$(30)
990 UNLOCK#1,IFIND:GOTO 820
1000 CLS:X=0:TOT=0:IF PRI=1 THEN LPRINT N2$;"     Date: ";DT$;"     Check #";CNUM$;"    Ref: ";RF$:LPRINT:LPRINT
1010 X=X+1:TOT=TOT+N#(X)
1020 PRINT N$(X);" ";USING"####.##";N#(X);:PRINT CHR$(186);
1030 IF PRI=1 THEN LPRINT N$(X);"  ";USING"####.##";N#(X)
1040 IF POS(0)>65 THEN PRINT
1050 IF X=>CT AND PRI=1 THEN LPRINT"TOTAL";USING"$######.##";TOT:LPRINT:LPRINT:LPRINT:LPRINT
1060 IF X=>CT THEN PRINT"TOTAL";USING"$#####.##";TOT:GOTO 1080
1070 GOTO 1010
1080 IF PRI=1 THEN FOR SPL=1 TO 12:LPRINT:NEXT
1081 FFGG=0:INUMS$="0":NOTE$="":IF ABS(TOT-CHKAMT)<1 THEN 1200:REM ####### CHECK IS CORRECT #######
1090 KEY OFF:H9=CSRLIN:H8=POS(0):LOCATE 25,1:FFGG=1:PERCENT=(100/ABS(TOT))*CHKAMT:PERCENT$=LEFT$(STR$(PERCENT),6)
1100 BEEP:COLOR 0,7:PRINT"TOTAL OF ALL INVOICES IS DIFFERENT FROM THE CHECK AMOUNT by ";USING"$#####.##";ABS(CHKAMT-TOT);:PRINT"  ";PERCENT$;"%":COLOR 7,0:LOCATE H9,H8:INPUT YYY$
1110 PRINT"Figure out whats wrong BEFORE you hit Enter";:FOR X=1 TO 1199:Q$=INKEY$:NEXT:INPUT Q$:PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(78)
1120 LOCATE 25,1:PRINT SPACE$(79):LOCATE H9,H8
1130 DIFF=TOT-CHKAMT:A$=STR$(DIFF):A$=RIGHT$(A$,LEN(A$)-1)
1140 X=INSTR(A$,"."):IF X=0 THEN A$=A$+".00":GOTO 1160
1150 A$=A$+"00":X=INSTR(A$,"."):X=X+2:A$=LEFT$(A$,X)
1160 IF DIFF>0 THEN A$=A$+" DEBIT"
1170 IF DIFF<0 THEN A$=A$+" CREDIT"
1171 A$=A$+" "+PERCENT$+"% Discount"
1180 NOTE$="Make a Invoice with a Total of "
1190 INUMS$=A$
1200 PRINT"==============================================================================":PRINT ;N2$;"  REF:";RF$;"  Check Amount:";USING"$#####.##";CHKAMT;:PRINT "   {";CT;"Invoices }"
1210 PRINT"Difference Between Total of Invoices & Check Amount: ";INUMS$
1220 IF NOTE$<>"" THEN PRINT:COLOR 23,0:PRINT"IMPORTANT";:COLOR 7,0:PRINT": ";NOTE$;USING"$#####.##";DIFF;:PRINT" if ";N2$;" is wrong!"
1230 PRINT:PRINT"(C) Check is Correct, add to the ledger":PRINT"(P) Print list of invoices"
1240 IF FFGG=1 THEN PRINT"(A) Add an Invoice number":PRINT"(D) Delete an Invoice number"
1250 PRINT"(Q) Quit to Main Menu":PRINT:PRI=0
1260 Q$=INKEY$
1270 IF Q$="A" OR Q$="a" THEN IF FFGG=1 THEN 820
1280 IF Q$="D" OR Q$="d" THEN IF FFGG=1 THEN 1420
1290 IF Q$="Q" OR Q$="q" THEN 10
1300 IF Q$="C" OR Q$="c" THEN 1320
1305 IF Q$="P" OR Q$="p" THEN PRI=1:GOTO 1000
1310 GOTO 1260
1320 PRINT:INPUT "Check # (Top Right)";CNUM$:IF CNUM$="" THEN 1320
1321 PRINT"Date [ENTER = ";DATE$;"] ";:INPUT DT$:IF DT$="" THEN DT$=DATE$
1322 INPUT "Check Reference (numbers on bottom right of check) ";RF$:IF RF$="" THEN 1322
1323 PRINT:PRINT:PRINT"Is EVERYTHING Correct ?  [Y = Yes] [N = NO]";:IF TOT<>CHKAMT THEN PRINT" [1 = Discount] [2 = Debit]":ELSE PRINT
1324 Q$=INKEY$:IF Q$="y" OR Q$="Y" THEN 1329
1325 IF Q$="n" OR Q$="N" THEN 1000
1326 IF Q$="1" THEN Q$="A":GOTO 1329
1327 IF Q$="2" THEN Q$="2":GOTO 1329
1328 GOTO 1324
1329 PRINT"ADDING...":FOR X=1 TO CT:HOLD=INT(HOLD(X)):LOCK#1,HOLD:GET#1,HOLD:LSET FLAG$="P"
1330 PUT#1,HOLD:UNLOCK#1,HOLD:NEXT
1332 IF TOT<>CHKAMT AND Q$="A" THEN RF$=RF$+" ("+PERCENT$+"% Discount)"
1333 IF TOT<>CHKAMT AND Q$="2" THEN RF$=RF$+" (Debit)"
1340 RESET:OPEN "LEDGER.CUR" FOR APPEND LOCK WRITE AS #1
1350 WRITE#1,N2$:WRITE#1,DT$:WRITE#1,CNUM$:WRITE#1,"":WRITE#1,CHKAMT:WRITE#1,RF$:CLOSE 1
1360 OPEN "CHECK.INV" FOR APPEND LOCK READ WRITE AS #1
1370 WRITE#1,N2$;CNUM$;RF$;DT$;CHKAMT;CT
1380 FOR X=1 TO CT:Y=VAL(N$(X)):WRITE#1,Y:NEXT:CLOSE 1
1390 PRINT:PRINT"Check #";CNUM$;" Has Been Entered...."
1400 GOTO 10
1410 REM ###### DELETE A INVOICE NUMBER #######
1420 X=0
1430 CLS:PRINT:COLOR 0,7:PRINT" #   Invoice Number":COLOR 7,0:BEG=X+1
1440 X=X+1:IF X>CT THEN LST=X-1:X=0:GOTO 1480
1450 PRINT X;TAB(7);HOLD(X)+75000!;"  ";USING"$#####.##";N#(X)
1460 IF X/20=INT(X/20) THEN LST=X:GOTO 1480
1470 GOTO 1440
1480 INPUT "              Enter # to delete [Enter = Next Page] [-1 = Quit] ";DNUM
1490 IF DNUM=0 THEN 1430
1500 IF DNUM=-1 THEN 1000
1510 IF DNUM<BEG OR DNUM>LST THEN PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(79);CHR$(30):GOTO 1480
1520 PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(79);CHR$(30):PRINT"Are you sure you want to delete #";DNUM;"Invoice Number";HOLD(DNUM)+75000!;" (Y/N)?";
1530 TEMP9=0:Q$=INKEY$:IF Q$="Y" OR Q$="y" THEN TEMP9=X:GOTO 1570
1540 IF Q$="N" OR Q$="n" THEN 1560
1550 GOTO 1530
1560 PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(79);CHR$(30):GOTO 1480
1570 X=DNUM
1580 X=X+1:IF X>CT THEN CT=CT-1:GOTO 1601
1590 N$(X-1)=N$(X):N#(X-1)=N#(X)
1600 HOLD(X-1)=HOLD(X):GOTO 1580
1601 IF TEMP9>0 AND CT>20 THEN X=TEMP9-20:IF X<1 THEN GOTO 1420:ELSE GOTO 1430
1605 GOTO 1420
1610 REM ###### DELETE A CHECK #######
1620 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"***** Delete A Check *****":PRINT:PRINT:Q2$="":Q3$="":Q4=0
1630 INPUT "Check # [ENTER = Quit]";C1$:PRINT"Please wait..."
1640 IF INU=-1 OR C1$="" OR VAL(C1$)<1 THEN 10
1650 OPEN "LEDGER.CUR" FOR INPUT AS #2
1660 OPEN "LEDGER.TMP" FOR OUTPUT LOCK WRITE AS #1
1670 IF EOF(2) THEN GOSUB 1770:GOTO 10
1680 INPUT#2,N2$:INPUT#2,DT$:INPUT#2,CNUM$:INPUT#2,INUMS$:INPUT#2,AMT:INPUT#2,RF$
1690 IF CNUM$=C1$ THEN 1720
1700 WRITE#1,N2$:WRITE#1,DT$:WRITE#1,CNUM$:WRITE#1,INUMS$:WRITE#1,AMT:WRITE#1,RF$
1710 GOTO 1670
1720 Q4=1:CLS:PRINT:PRINT"Customer: ";N2$ TAB(35) "Date: ";DT$
1730 PRINT"Check #: ";CNUM$;"     Reference: ";RF$:PRINT"Paid Invoice(s): ";INUMS$
1740 PRINT"Amount: ";:PRINT USING"$######.##";AMT:PRINT:PRINT
1745 PRINT"Delete the check Above & Reset the Invoices to UnPaid (Y/N) ?"
1750 Q2$=INKEY$:IF Q2$="Y" OR Q2$="y" THEN N0$=N2$:Q3$="Y":PRINT"Deleting Check...":GOTO 1670
1755 IF Q2$="N" OR Q2$="n" THEN 1700
1760 GOTO 1750
1770 CLOSE 1:CLOSE 2
1780 IF Q3$="Y" THEN PRINT:PRINT"Please Wait..":GOTO 3800
1790 IF Q4=0 THEN PRINT"CHECK DOES NOT EXIST, ";:GOSUB 160
1800 RETURN
1810 REM ##### CO. NAME DOES'NT EXISTENCE ######
1820 PRINT:PRINT N2$;" does not exist...  Would you like to see a list (Y/N)";:INPUT Q$
1830 IF LEFT$(Q$,1)="y" OR LEFT$(Q$,1)="Y" THEN 1860
1840 IF LEFT$(Q$,1)="n" OR LEFT$(Q$,1)="N" THEN RESUME 700
1850 GOTO 1820
1860 CLS:Q$="PRC\"+LEFT$(N2$,1)+"*.PRC":FILES Q$:LOCATE 24,1:GOSUB 160:RESUME 700
1870 PRINT"error at line 1050":STOP
1880 REM ###### VIEW COMPANY TOTAL #######
1890 CLS:PRINT:CT=0:TTL=0
1900 OPEN "LEDGER.CUR" FOR INPUT AS #2
1910 IF EOF(2) THEN 2000
1920 TTL=TTL+1:PRINT"LOADING ENTRY #";TTL;CHR$(30)
1930 INPUT#2,N2$:INPUT#2,DT$:INPUT#2,CNUM$:INPUT#2,INUMS$:INPUT#2,AMT#:INPUT#2,RF$
1940 X=0:Y=0
1950 X=X+1:IF X>CT THEN CT=CT+1:N$(X)=N2$:GOTO 1980
1960 IF N2$=N$(X) THEN 1980
1970 GOTO 1950
1980 N#(X)=N#(X)-AMT#
1990 GOTO 1910
2000 CLOSE 2
2010 TOT#=0:FOR X=1 TO CT
2020 PRINT N$(X);TAB(10);:PRINT USING"$#######.##";N#(X)
2030 TOT#=TOT#+N#(X):Y=Y+1:IF Y=20 THEN Y=0:PRINT:GOSUB 160
2040 NEXT:PRINT"GRAND TOTAL: ";USING"$######.##";TOT#
2050 PRINT:GOSUB 160:GOTO 10
2060 A$=STR$(AMT):A$=RIGHT$(A$,LEN(A$)-1):TOT#=TOT#+AMT
2070 X=INSTR(A$,"."):IF X=0 THEN A$=A$+".00":GOTO 1100
2080 A$=A$+"00":X=INSTR(A$,"."):X=X+2:A$=LEFT$(A$,X)
2090 REM #########  ADD OTHER CHECKS #########
2100 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"***** Add A Check *****":PRINT:COLOR 0,7:PRINT" This is usually for over payments or checks that are Not paying for invoices ":COLOR 7,0:PRINT:PRINT
2110 INPUT "Company Name [Q = Quit] ";N2$:IF N2$="" THEN 2100
2120 IF ASC(LEFT$(N2$,1))>96 THEN PRINT CHR$(30);"HIT THE CAPS LOCK KEY...                        ":GOTO 2110
2130 IF N2$="Q" OR N2$="q" THEN 200
2140 INPUT "Check # ";CNUM$:IF CNUM$="" THEN PRINT CHR$(30);:GOTO 2140
2150 INPUT "Check Reference ";RF$:IF RF$="" THEN PRINT CHR$(30);:GOTO 2150
2160 PRINT
2170 PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(79):PRINT CHR$(30);:PRINT TAB(29);"##-##-####";CHR$(30):PRINT"Date [Enter = ";DATE$;"] ";:INPUT DT2$
2180 IF DT2$="" THEN DT2$=DATE$
2190 IF INSTR(DT2$,"-")<>3 OR INSTR(4,DT2$,"-")<>6 OR LEN(DT2$)<>10 THEN 2170
2200 Q=VAL(LEFT$(DT2$,2)):IF Q<1 OR Q>12 THEN 2170
2210 Q=VAL(MID$(DT2$,4,2)):IF Q<1 OR Q>31 THEN 2170
2220 Q=VAL(RIGHT$(DT2$,4)):IF Q<1988 OR Q>2099 THEN 2170
2230 DT$=DT2$
2240 INPUT "Check Amount ";CHKAMT:IF CHKAMT<.1 THEN PRINT CHR$(30);:GOTO 2240
2250 INPUT "Reason Why ";RW$:IF RW$="" THEN PRINT CHR$(30);:GOTO 2250
2260 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"***** Add A Check *****":PRINT:COLOR 0,7:PRINT" This is usually for over payments or checks that are Not paying for invoices ":COLOR 7,0:PRINT:PRINT
2270 PRINT "Company Name: ";N2$
2280 PRINT "Check #: ";CNUM$
2290 PRINT "Check Reference: ";RF$
2300 PRINT"Date: ";DT$
2310 PRINT "Check Amount: ";CHKAMT
2320 PRINT "Reason Why: ";RW$:PRINT:PRINT:PRINT"[A = Add this Check]   [R = Re-enter Check]   [Q = Quit]    ";
2330 Q$=INKEY$:IF Q$="a" OR Q$="A" THEN 2370
2340 IF Q$="r" OR Q$="R" THEN 2100
2350 IF Q$="q" OR Q$="Q" THEN 200
2360 GOTO 2330
2370 PRINT "Adding..."
2380 OPEN "other.chk" FOR APPEND LOCK WRITE AS #1
2390 WRITE#1,N2$:WRITE#1,DT$:WRITE#1,CNUM$:WRITE#1,CHKAMT:WRITE#1,RF$:WRITE#1,RW$:CLOSE 1:GOTO 200
2400 REM #########  VIEW OTHER CHECKS #########
2410 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"***** VIEW OTHER CHECKS *****":PRINT:COLOR 0,7:PRINT" This is usually for over payments or checks that are Not paying for invoices ":COLOR 7,0:PRINT:PRINT
2420 INPUT "Enter All or Part of Company Name [Q = Quit]  [A = All Companys]";N2$:IF N2$="" THEN 2410
2430 IF N2$="a" THEN N2$="A"
2440 IF N2$="q" OR N2$="Q" THEN 200
2450 IF ASC(LEFT$(N2$,1))>96 THEN PRINT CHR$(30);"HIT THE CAPS LOCK KEY...                        ":GOTO 2420
2460 PRINT:PRINT
2470 PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(79):PRINT CHR$(30);:PRINT TAB(35);"##-##-####";CHR$(30):PRINT"BEGINNING DATE [1 = 01-01-1988] ";:INPUT DT2$
2480 IF DT2$="1" THEN DT2$="01-01-1988"
2490 IF DT2$="-1" THEN 2470
2500 IF INSTR(DT2$,"-")<>3 OR INSTR(4,DT2$,"-")<>6 OR LEN(DT2$)<>10 THEN 2470
2510 Q=VAL(LEFT$(DT2$,2)):IF Q<1 OR Q>12 THEN 2470
2520 Q=VAL(MID$(DT2$,4,2)):IF Q<1 OR Q>31 THEN 2470
2530 Q=VAL(RIGHT$(DT2$,4)):IF Q<1988 OR Q>2099 THEN 2470
2540 BEGDT$=DT2$:PRINT:PRINT:PRINT
2550 DT1=(VAL(LEFT$(DT2$,2))*32) + VAL(MID$(DT2$,4,2)) + (VAL(RIGHT$(DT2$,4))*420)
2560 PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(79):PRINT CHR$(30);:PRINT TAB(35);"##-##-####";CHR$(30):PRINT"   ENDING DATE [1 = 12-31-2000] ";:INPUT DT2$
2570 IF DT2$="1" THEN DT2$="12-31-2000"
2580 IF DT2$="-1" THEN 2470
2590 IF INSTR(DT2$,"-")<>3 OR INSTR(4,DT2$,"-")<>6 OR LEN(DT2$)<>10 THEN 2560
2600 Q=VAL(LEFT$(DT2$,2)):IF Q<1 OR Q>12 THEN 2560
2610 Q=VAL(MID$(DT2$,4,2)):IF Q<1 OR Q>31 THEN 2560
2620 Q=VAL(RIGHT$(DT2$,4)):IF Q<1988 OR Q>2099 THEN 2560
2630 ENDDT$=DT2$
2640 DT2=(VAL(LEFT$(DT2$,2))*32) + VAL(MID$(DT2$,4,2)) + (VAL(RIGHT$(DT2$,4))*420)
2650 IF DT2<DT1 THEN PRINT:INPUT "ENDING DATE IS BEFORE BEGINNING, hit [ENTER]";Q$:CLS:GOTO 2460
2660 OPEN "other.chk" FOR INPUT AS #1:CLS:PRINT:PRINT"Please Wait... [ESC = Exit]";CHR$(30);CHR$(30):X=0:TOT#=0
2670 IF EOF(1) THEN RESET:GOTO 2810
2680 IF INKEY$=CHR$(27) THEN RESET:GOTO 2810
2690 INPUT#1,C$:INPUT#1,DT$:INPUT#1,CNUM$:INPUT#1,CHKAMT:INPUT#1,RF$:INPUT#1,RW$
2700 DT=(VAL(LEFT$(DT$,2))*32) + VAL(MID$(DT$,4,2)) + (VAL(RIGHT$(DT$,4))*420):IF DT<DT1 OR DT>DT2 THEN 2670
2710 IF N2$="A" THEN 2730
2720 IF INSTR(C$,N2$)=0 THEN 2670
2730 PRINT"Customer: ";C$ TAB(35) "Date: ";DT$:TOT#=TOT#+CHKAMT
2740 PRINT"Check #: ";CNUM$;"   Reference: ";RF$;
2750 PRINT"   Amount: ";:PRINT USING"$#######.##";CHKAMT:PRINT"Reason Why: "RW$:PRINT STRING$(79,"*"):PRINT"Please Wait... [ESC = Exit]";CHR$(30)
2760 X=X+1:IF X<5 THEN 2670
2770 X=0:PRINT"[N = Next Page]   [ESC = Exit]";
2780 Q$=INKEY$:IF Q$="N" OR Q$="n" THEN CLS:PRINT:PRINT"Please Wait... [ESC = Exit]";CHR$(30);CHR$(30):GOTO 2670
2790 IF Q$=CHR$(27) THEN RESET:GOTO 200
2800 GOTO 2780
2810 PRINT"TOTAL from ";BEGDT$;" to ";ENDDT$:PRINT"for ";:IF N2$="A" THEN PRINT"ALL CUSTOMERS: ";:ELSE PRINT N2$;": ";
2820 PRINT USING"$########.##";TOT#;:PRINT"   End of List [ESC = Exit]";
2830 Q$=INKEY$
2840 IF Q$=CHR$(27) THEN RESET:GOTO 200
2850 GOTO 2830
2860 REM #########================================================##########             #######  Find the Invoices Payed with a Check           ###########             ########===============================================############
2870 PRINT:PRINT "<<<  Find the Invoices that a Check Payed for  >>>":PRINT
2880 PRINT SPACE$(79);CHR$(30):INPUT "Enter Part or All of Check Number [ENTER = All Checks] [Q = Quit] ";CNUM2$
2890 IF CNUM2$="Q" OR CNUM2$="q" THEN 200
2900 PRINT"Please Wait..."
2910 OPEN "CHECK.INV" FOR INPUT AS #1:FL=0
2920 IF EOF(1) THEN CLOSE 1:IF FL=0 THEN PRINT "Check number ";CNUM2$;" does not exist, hit Enter";:INPUT Q$:GOTO 200:ELSE GOTO 200
2930 INPUT#1,N2$,CNUM$,RF$,DT$,CHKAMT,CT
2940 FOR X=1 TO CT:INPUT#1,HOLD(X):NEXT
2950 IF INSTR(CNUM$,CNUM2$)=0 THEN 2920
2960 FL=1:CLS:PRINT:PRINT N2$;TAB(65);"Date: ";DT$:PRINT"Check # ";CNUM$;"   Reference: ";RF$;"   Amount ";USING"$######.##";CHKAMT
2970 PRINT STRING$(35,"=");" Invoices ";STRING$(35,"=")
2980 Y=0:FOR X=1 TO CT:Q$=STR$(HOLD(X)):Q$=RIGHT$(Q$,LEN(Q$)-1):PRINT Q$;SPACE$(7-LEN(Q$));
2990 IF POS(0)>71 THEN PRINT:Y=Y+1
3000 IF Y>18 THEN Y=0:INPUT "Hit ENTER for Next Page";Q$:PRINT CHR$(30);SPACE$(40);CHR$(30)
3010 NEXT:PRINT:PRINT "Hit ENTER to look for another check [ESC = Quit] ";
3020 Q$=INKEY$:IF Q$=CHR$(13) THEN 2920
3030 IF Q$=CHR$(27) THEN RESET:GOTO 200
3040 GOTO 3020
3050 REM #########================================================##########             #######  Find the Check that Payed for an Invoice       ###########             ########===============================================############
3060 PRINT:PRINT "<<<  Find the Check that Payed for an Invoice >>>":PRINT
3070 PRINT SPACE$(79);CHR$(30):INPUT "Enter the Invoice Number [-1 = Quit] ";INUM
3080 IF INUM=-1 THEN 200
3090 PRINT"Please Wait..."
3100 OPEN "CHECK.INV" FOR INPUT AS #1:FL=0
3110 IF EOF(1) THEN CLOSE 1:IF FL=0 THEN PRINT "Invoice number ";INUM;" does not exist, hit Enter";:INPUT Q$:GOTO 200:ELSE GOTO 200
3120 INPUT#1,N2$,CNUM$,RF$,DT$,CHKAMT,CT
3130 Y=0:FOR X=1 TO CT:INPUT#1,HOLD(X):IF HOLD(X)=INUM THEN Y=1
3140 NEXT
3150 IF Y=0 THEN 3110

## Copilot

Perfect—this ASCII dump is exactly what we need.

### What we should implement first (fastest “win”)
From `LEDGER.BAS`, the fastest/easiest feature to port into your VB.NET app is:

**“View Checks”** (menu option **3**, starts at line **460**), because it:
- only reads `LEDGER.CUR` (sequential text records)
- doesn’t require touching `INVOICE.CHK` random-access records
- doesn’t need to modify anything (safe, low risk)
- gives you an immediate “menu item → new screen → real legacy data” integration

Once that works, next easiest are:
- **View Company Totals** (option **5**, starts at **1890**) – also read-only from `LEDGER.CUR`
- **Find invoices paid by a check** (option **9**, `CHECK.INV`, sequential)
- **Find check that paid an invoice** (option **0**, `CHECK.INV`, sequential)

Harder (save for later):
- “Add check” / “Delete check” because they touch both:
  - `LEDGER.CUR` (append/replace)
  - `CHECK.INV` (append/replace)
  - `INVOICE.CHK` (random-access fixed records with `FIELD`, `GET/PUT`, flags)

---

## Dependency map (from your code)
You will eventually need these files present somewhere accessible:

### Sequential text-ish files (easy)
- `LEDGER.CUR` (records: customer, date, check#, invoice-diff string, amount, reference)
- `CHECK.INV` (records per check: customer, check#, ref, date, amount, count, then invoice list)
- `OTHER.CHK` (other checks)
- `EMPNAME.DAT` (employee + indented customer list)
- `ERRORS.LST` (append log)

### Random-access file (harder)
- `INVOICE.CHK` **LEN=26** with fields:
  - 9 bytes invoice #
  - 8 bytes amount
  - 8 bytes company
  - 1 byte flag (`J` unpaid, `P` paid, `V` void, `C` corrected, etc.)

### Other legacy dependencies
- `PRC\*.PRC` existence check for customers
- It `CHAIN`s back to `MAINMENU.BAS` (we’ll replace that with returning to your WinForms main menu)

---

   - `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data` ?
2) Or are they local per workstation?

## What I propose for the VB.NET structure
Create a small “legacy data” layer:

- `LegacyDataPaths` (knows base folder like `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data`)
- `LedgerCurReader` (reads `LEDGER.CUR` into a `LedgerEntry` model)
- `CheckInvReader` (reads `CHECK.INV` into a `CheckInvoiceEntry` model)
- (later) `InvoiceChkFile` (random access reader/writer for `INVOICE.CHK`)

And a WinForms screen:
- `FormLedgerView` with filters:
  - Customer (partial or exact)
  - Check number
  - Year / Month
  - A results grid
  - optional details pane (reference, invoice-diff string)

This is much faster and more maintainable than trying to recreate the `INKEY$` / paging UI.

## Copilot

Start by porting **LEDGER “View Checks” (option 3, line ~460)** reading `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\LEDGER.CUR`. It’s read-only, sequential, and will let you prove the new WinForms menu wiring + file-path strategy immediately.

Below is a clean VB.NET implementation you can drop in **without touching the hard parts** (`INVOICE.CHK` random access, flags, PUT/GET) yet.

---

## 1) Create a single place for legacy data paths
Create a new file:

```vbnet name=LegacyDataPaths.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO

Public NotInheritable Class LegacyDataPaths
    Private Sub New()
    End Sub

    ' Centralized so every converted module uses the same base
    Public Shared ReadOnly Property BaseDataDir As String =
        "\\invoice\MainMenu\Data"

    Public Shared ReadOnly Property LedgerCur As String =
        Path.Combine(BaseDataDir, "LEDGER.CUR")

    Public Shared ReadOnly Property CheckInv As String =
        Path.Combine(BaseDataDir, "CHECK.INV")

    Public Shared ReadOnly Property OtherChk As String =
        Path.Combine(BaseDataDir, "OTHER.CHK")

    Public Shared ReadOnly Property EmpNameDat As String =
        Path.Combine(BaseDataDir, "EMPNAME.DAT")

    Public Shared ReadOnly Property InvoiceChk As String =
        Path.Combine(BaseDataDir, "INVOICE.CHK")
End Class
```

---

## 2) Implement a reader for `LEDGER.CUR`
`LEDGER.BAS` writes to `LEDGER.CUR` using multiple `WRITE#` statements, which in BASIC produces **comma-delimited / quoted** fields. We’ll parse it robustly with `TextFieldParser`.

Add:

```vbnet name=LedgerCurReader.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Globalization
Imports System.IO
Imports Microsoft.VisualBasic.FileIO

Public NotInheritable Class LedgerCurReader
    Private Sub New()
    End Sub

    Public Shared Function ReadAll(path As String) As List(Of LedgerEntry)
        Dim results As New List(Of LedgerEntry)()

        If Not File.Exists(path) Then
            Return results
        End If

        ' GW-BASIC wrote 6 logical fields per entry, but they may be split across lines.
        ' We'll read *fields* continuously and group by 6.
        Dim buffer As New List(Of String)()

        Using p As New TextFieldParser(path)
            p.TextFieldType = FieldType.Delimited
            p.SetDelimiters(",")
            p.HasFieldsEnclosedInQuotes = True
            p.TrimWhiteSpace = True

            While Not p.EndOfData
                Dim fields = p.ReadFields()
                If fields Is Nothing Then Continue While

                For Each f In fields
                    buffer.Add(If(f, ""))
                    If buffer.Count = 6 Then
                        results.Add(ParseEntry(buffer))
                        buffer.Clear()
                    End If
                Next
            End While
        End Using

        ' If buffer leftover exists, file is malformed; ignore tail.
        Return results
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseEntry(fields As List(Of String)) As LedgerEntry
        Dim e As New LedgerEntry()

        e.Customer = fields(0).Trim()
        e.DateText = fields(1).Trim()
        e.CheckNumber = fields(2).Trim()
        e.InvoiceDiffText = fields(3).Trim()
        e.Amount = ParseDecimal(fields(4))
        e.Reference = fields(5).Trim()

        Return e
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseDecimal(s As String) As Decimal
        s = (If(s, "")).Trim()

        Dim d As Decimal
        If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, d) Then Return d
        If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, d) Then Return d

        Return 0D
    End Function
End Class

Public Class LedgerEntry
    Public Property Customer As String
    Public Property DateText As String
    Public Property CheckNumber As String
    Public Property InvoiceDiffText As String
    Public Property Amount As Decimal
    Public Property Reference As String
End Class
```

**Why this approach works:** BASIC `WRITE#` can split fields across multiple lines; treating the file as a stream of CSV fields and grouping by 6 matches how the program writes entries (6 `WRITE#` calls per check entry).

---

## 3) Add a WinForms form to view checks (`FormLedgerView.vb`)
This is the modern replacement for the old “paged print” view at lines 460–680.

Create a new form with:
- `TextBox` `txtCustomer`
- `TextBox` `txtCheck`
- `ComboBox` `cmbYear` (blank + years)
- `ComboBox` `cmbMonth` (blank + 01-12)
- `Button` `btnRefresh`
- `DataGridView` `dgv`
- `Label` `lblTotal`

Then add this code:

```vbnet name=FormLedgerView.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Public Class FormLedgerView

    Private _all As List(Of LedgerEntry) = New List(Of LedgerEntry)()

    Private Sub FormLedgerView_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        Me.Text = "Ledger - View Checks"

        cmbMonth.Items.Clear()
        cmbMonth.Items.Add("") ' all
        For m As Integer = 1 To 12
            cmbMonth.Items.Add(m.ToString("00"))
        Next

        cmbYear.Items.Clear()
        cmbYear.Items.Add("") ' all
        For y As Integer = Date.Today.Year To 1986 Step -1
            cmbYear.Items.Add(y.ToString())
        Next

        LoadData()
        ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub btnRefresh_Click(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles btnRefresh.Click
        LoadData()
        ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub txtCustomer_TextChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles txtCustomer.TextChanged
        ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub txtCheck_TextChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles txtCheck.TextChanged
        ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub cmbYear_SelectedIndexChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles cmbYear.SelectedIndexChanged
        ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub cmbMonth_SelectedIndexChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles cmbMonth.SelectedIndexChanged
        ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadData()
        _all = LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur)

        dgv.AutoGenerateColumns = True
        dgv.DataSource = _all
    End Sub

    Private Sub ApplyFilters()
        Dim cust As String = txtCustomer.Text.Trim()
        Dim chk As String = txtCheck.Text.Trim()
        Dim year As String = If(TryCast(cmbYear.SelectedItem, String), "").Trim()
        Dim month As String = If(TryCast(cmbMonth.SelectedItem, String), "").Trim()

        Dim filtered = _all.AsEnumerable()

        If cust <> "" Then
            ' DOS used LEFT$(cust,8) exact-ish; we’ll do “contains” but you can switch to StartsWith.
            filtered = filtered.Where(Function(e) e.Customer IsNot Nothing AndAlso e.Customer.IndexOf(cust, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
        End If

        If chk <> "" Then
            filtered = filtered.Where(Function(e) e.CheckNumber IsNot Nothing AndAlso e.CheckNumber.IndexOf(chk, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
        End If

        If year <> "" Then
            ' GW-BASIC checked RIGHT$(DT$,2) vs RIGHT$(YEAR$,2). DT$ is MM-DD-YYYY.
            Dim yy As String = year.Substring(year.Length - 2)
            filtered = filtered.Where(Function(e) e.DateText IsNot Nothing AndAlso e.DateText.Length >= 2 AndAlso e.DateText.EndsWith(yy, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If

        If month <> "" Then
            filtered = filtered.Where(Function(e) e.DateText IsNot Nothing AndAlso e.DateText.Length >= 2 AndAlso e.DateText.StartsWith(month, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If

        Dim list = filtered.ToList()
        dgv.DataSource = list

        Dim total As Decimal = list.Sum(Function(e) e.Amount)
        lblTotal.Text = $"Total: {total:C}"
    End Sub

End Class
```

---

## 4) Wire it into your Main Menu
Wherever your WinForms “Ledger” menu item/button is, do:

```vbnet
Using f As New FormLedgerView()
    f.ShowDialog(Me)
End Using
```

---

- it’s truly CSV with quotes (from `WRITE#`), or
- it’s plain lines (from `PRINT#`/manual edits)

If it’s plain lines, I’ll adjust the reader to “6 lines per entry” instead of CSV-field grouping.

### Option C: paste 3 complete “entries”
From your GW-BASIC code, one ledger entry is **6 fields** in this order:
1) customer (e.g., `N2$`)
2) date (`DT$`, typically `MM-DD-YYYY`)
3) check number (`CNUM$`)
4) invoice-diff text (`INUMS$`, sometimes blank)
5) amount (`CHKAMT`)
6) reference (`RF$`)

So you can paste 3 entries worth of data (however they’re stored in the file). Feel free to redact names/amounts.

Great—this confirms the format precisely: **`LEDGER.CUR` is not CSV.** It is **6 lines per entry**, where each entry is:

1. Customer (quoted)
2. Date (quoted, `MM-DD-YYYY`)
3. Check number (quoted)
4. Invoice diff text (quoted; often `""`)
5. Amount (number, unquoted)
6. Reference (quoted)

So we should **not** use `TextFieldParser`/CSV parsing for this file. We should read **lines** and group them by 6.

Below is the corrected reader (drop-in replacement), plus one small improvement: it handles blank lines and strips surrounding quotes safely.

---

## Replace `LedgerCurReader.vb` with this line-based reader

```vbnet name=LedgerCurReader.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Globalization
Imports System.IO

Public NotInheritable Class LedgerCurReader
    Private Sub New()
    End Sub

    Public Shared Function ReadAll(path As String) As List(Of LedgerEntry)
        Dim results As New List(Of LedgerEntry)()

        If Not File.Exists(path) Then
            Return results
        End If

        Dim lines As String() = File.ReadAllLines(path)

        ' Collect non-null lines; keep empty string lines (they matter as fields like "")
        Dim cleaned As New List(Of String)(lines.Length)
        For Each raw In lines
            If raw Is Nothing Then Continue For
            Dim s As String = raw.Trim()
            If s = "" Then
                ' skip truly blank lines (not a data line)
                Continue For
            End If
            cleaned.Add(s)
        Next

        ' Each entry is 6 lines
        Dim i As Integer = 0
        While i + 5 < cleaned.Count
            Dim e As New LedgerEntry() With {
                .Customer = Unquote(cleaned(i)),
                .DateText = Unquote(cleaned(i + 1)),
                .CheckNumber = Unquote(cleaned(i + 2)),
                .InvoiceDiffText = Unquote(cleaned(i + 3)),
                .Amount = ParseDecimal(cleaned(i + 4)),
                .Reference = Unquote(cleaned(i + 5))
            }
            results.Add(e)
            i += 6
        End While

        Return results
    End Function

    Private Shared Function Unquote(s As String) As String
        If s Is Nothing Then Return ""
        s = s.Trim()

        ' Handle GW-BASIC style: "TEXT" or ""
        If s.Length >= 2 AndAlso s.StartsWith("""", StringComparison.Ordinal) AndAlso s.EndsWith("""", StringComparison.Ordinal) Then
            s = s.Substring(1, s.Length - 2)
        End If

        Return s
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseDecimal(s As String) As Decimal
        If s Is Nothing Then Return 0D
        s = s.Trim()

        Dim d As Decimal
        If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, d) Then Return d
        If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, d) Then Return d
        Return 0D
    End Function
End Class

Public Class LedgerEntry
    Public Property Customer As String
    Public Property DateText As String
    Public Property CheckNumber As String
    Public Property InvoiceDiffText As String
    Public Property Amount As Decimal
    Public Property Reference As String
End Class
```

### Why this matches your file
Your sample shows exactly this repeating pattern:

```text
"CBSFASTE"
"01-02-1992"
"1578"
""
775
"90-3731/1222"
```

That’s line-grouped records, not comma-delimited records.

---

## Optional: make date filtering easier later
If you want, we can add a computed property:

- `EntryDate As Date?` parsed from `DateText` (`MM-dd-yyyy`)

so we can filter by year/month properly instead of string slicing.

## @testsubject117

do I add ledgercurreader.vb as a right click, add new, item, windows form?

## Copilot

No—`LedgerCurReader.vb` is **not** a Windows Form. Add it as a **Class** (or a plain code file), not a form.

### How to add it in Visual Studio
1) In Solution Explorer, right‑click your project **AMiOffice** (not the “Forms” folder unless you want it there).
2) **Add → New Item…**
3) Choose **Class** (VB) (or “Class File”).
4) Name it: `LedgerCurReader.vb`
5) Paste the code I gave you.

Do the same for `LegacyDataPaths.vb` (also a **Class** item).

### What *is* a Windows Form?
Only `FormLedgerView.vb` should be added as:
- **Add → Windows Form (VB)**

That form will *use* `LedgerCurReader` to load and display data.

If you tell me whether you already have a “Ledger” menu item/button in your WinForms main menu (and what it’s called), I’ll tell you exactly where to add:

```vbnet
Using f As New FormLedgerView()
    f.ShowDialog(Me)
End Using
```

## @testsubject117

how do I determine which buttons from mainmenu.bas attach to ledger.bas and ledger.cur?

## Copilot

You determine that by mapping **the GW-BASIC menu choices** in `MAINMENU.BAS` to the programs it launches via **`CHAIN`** (and sometimes `SHELL "QB /RUN ..."`). Anything that `CHAIN`s to `LEDGER` is what you wire to your new “Ledger” WinForms feature—and anything inside `LEDGER.BAS` that reads/writes `LEDGER.CUR` is part of that feature.

### 1) In `MAINMENU.BAS`, the Ledger entry is already visible
From the snippet you posted:

```basic
860 IF Q$="C" THEN CHAIN "LEDGER"
```

So the **Main Menu option C** (the line that prints “Checks and Cash Receipts”) is the one that launches the ledger system.

In the printed menu above it:

```basic
520 PRINT"(C) Checks and Cash Reciepts"
```

So in WinForms:

- Whatever button/menu item corresponds to **“Checks and Cash Receipts”** should open your new Ledger UI (starting with “View Checks” backed by `LEDGER.CUR`).

### 2) `LEDGER.BAS` confirms it owns `LEDGER.CUR`
Inside `LEDGER.BAS` you have:

- Read/view checks (option 3):
  ```basic
  490 OPEN "LEDGER.CUR" FOR INPUT AS #2
  ```
- Add a check (writes):
  ```basic
  1340 OPEN "LEDGER.CUR" FOR APPEND ... AS #1
  1350 WRITE#1, ...
  ```

So `LEDGER.CUR` is absolutely part of the program launched by **MainMenu option C**.

### 3) What to do in the VB.NET app (practical mapping)
You don’t need to recreate the letter-based DOS routing. Instead:

- In your VB.NET main menu, locate the control with text like:
  - “Checks and Cash Receipts”
  - “Ledger”
  - “Add checks to ledger…”
- That control’s click handler is the one you attach to the new form(s).

If you’re not sure which control that is:

#### Method A (fastest): search in the WinForms designer code
Use Visual Studio **Find in Files** for:
- `"Checks"`  
- `"Cash"`  
- `"Ledger"`

It’ll show you where the button/menu text is set, and you can identify the control name (e.g., `btnLedger`, `mnuLedger`, etc.).

#### Method B: click the button in the designer
- Open your main menu form in the Designer
- Click the button/menu item
- In the Properties window, look at **(Name)** and **Text**
- Double-click it to generate/open its Click event handler

### 4) Recommended WinForms structure (matches your legacy menu)
Main Menu button **Checks and Cash Receipts** → opens `FormLedgerMenu` with options:

- View Checks (reads `LEDGER.CUR`) — implement first
- View Company Totals (reads `LEDGER.CUR`)
- Find Check → Invoice list (reads `CHECK.INV`)
- Find Invoice → Check (reads `CHECK.INV`)
- Other checks (reads/writes `OTHER.CHK`)
- Add/Delete checks (later: touches `INVOICE.CHK` random access)

This mirrors `LEDGER.BAS` options 1/2/3/5/6/7/8/9/0/A/S without having to emulate the DOS UI.

---

## Copilot

From this `InitializeComponent`, you currently **don’t have any of the menu buttons created in the designer**—only the layout containers (`flpLeft`, `flpRight`) that the buttons will be added to at runtime.

So the answer is:

- In the DOS code, **MainMenu option C** (“Checks and Cash Receipts”) launches Ledger via `CHAIN "LEDGER"`.
- In your WinForms code, the equivalent is **whatever runtime-created button you add for “Checks and Cash Receipts”**. That button’s Click handler should open your new Ledger form(s) which read `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\LEDGER.CUR`.

### How to find which WinForms button corresponds to “Ledger”
You need to locate the code that adds buttons into `flpLeft` / `flpRight`. Look for any of these patterns in `FormMainMenu.vb`:

- `flpLeft.Controls.Add(...)` / `flpRight.Controls.Add(...)`
- `New Button() With {.Text = ...}`
- `.Text = "(C) Checks and Cash Receipts"` or similar
- `AddHandler btn.Click, AddressOf ...`

#### Quick way (Visual Studio)
Use **Ctrl+Shift+F** (Find in Files) for:
- `flpLeft.Controls.Add`
- `flpRight.Controls.Add`
- `New Button`
- `Checks and Cash`
- `Ledger`

That will take you to the method that builds the menu.

### What you should wire to Ledger
In that runtime menu-building code, when you create the “Checks and Cash Receipts” button, wire it like this:

```vbnet
AddHandler btnChecksAndCashReceipts.Click,
    Sub()
        Using f As New FormLedgerView()
            f.ShowDialog(Me)
        End Using
    End Sub
```

(or if you want a Ledger submenu later, open `FormLedgerMenu` instead).

## Copilot

Button **C** is the one you want: **“Checks and Cash Receipts”**. In the original DOS `MAINMENU.BAS`, that exact option did:

```basic
IF Q$="C" THEN CHAIN "LEDGER"
```

So in your WinForms version, you attach **C → open the Ledger UI** (starting with `LEDGER.CUR` “View Checks”).

## How to wire it up
You’ll do this in your `AddMenuButton(...)` helper (best, because it centralizes the routing).

### Step 1: Make `AddMenuButton` return the created Button (or accept a click handler)
If your helper currently is `Sub AddMenuButton(parent, key, text)`, change it to **return** the button:

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
Private Function AddMenuButton(parent As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, caption As String) As Button
    Dim btn As New Button() With {
        .Text = $"({key}) {caption}",
        .Tag = key,
        .AutoSize = False
        ' ... your existing styling ...
    }

    AddHandler btn.Click, AddressOf MenuButton_Click
    parent.Controls.Add(btn)
    Return btn
End Function
```

### Step 2: Route keys in one place
Add this click handler:

```vbnet name=FormMainMenu.vb
Private Sub MenuButton_Click(sender As Object, e As EventArgs)
    Dim btn = TryCast(sender, Button)
    Dim key = TryCast(btn?.Tag, String)

    Select Case key
        Case "C"
            Using f As New FormLedgerView()
                f.ShowDialog(Me)
            End Using

        Case "?"
            Using f As New FormAbout()
                f.ShowDialog(Me)
            End Using

        Case Else
            MessageBox.Show(Me, $"Menu item ({key}) not implemented yet.", "AMiOffice")
    End Select
End Sub
```

That’s it: **Button C now launches your Ledger viewer** that reads `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\LEDGER.CUR`.

---

## Alternative (if you don’t want to change AddMenuButton)
If `AddMenuButton` already wires a click event and you can’t/don’t want to refactor, then the rule is the same: wherever it handles clicks, route `"C"` to `FormLedgerView`.

---

## Quick sanity check
Since you also have button **P** = “Entire Ledger Viewing” (in DOS: `CHAIN "ENTIRE"`), you have two choices:

- Use **C** for “checks/ledger operations” (matches your `LEDGER.BAS`)
- Use **P** later for the “entire ledger viewing” module (whatever `ENTIRE.BAS` used to do)

## Copilot

Keep your existing `BuildMenu()` exactly as-is. **Do not replace it.**  
What you need to add/change is **how `AddMenuButton` handles clicks**, so that when the user clicks **C**, it opens the Ledger form.

All of this goes in **`FormMainMenu.vb`** (the code-behind for your main menu form), because that’s where the buttons are created.

## What to do (minimal, safe steps)

### Step 1 — Find `AddMenuButton` in `FormMainMenu.vb`
In the same file, you should already have something like:

- `Private Sub AddMenuButton(...)` or
- `Private Function AddMenuButton(...) As Button`

If you paste that method, I can tell you the absolute smallest edit.

### Step 2 — Add ONE click handler that routes by key
Add this method anywhere inside `FormMainMenu` (same file, outside `BuildMenu()`):

```vbnet
Private Sub MenuButton_Click(sender As Object, e As EventArgs)
    Dim btn = TryCast(sender, Button)
    Dim key = TryCast(btn?.Tag, String)

    Select Case key
        Case "C"
            Using f As New FormLedgerView()
                f.ShowDialog(Me)
            End Using

        Case "?"
            Using f As New FormAbout()
                f.ShowDialog(Me)
            End Using

        Case Else
            MessageBox.Show(Me, $"Menu item ({key}) not implemented yet.", "AMiOffice")
    End Select
End Sub
```

### Step 3 — Ensure each menu button calls that handler
Inside **`AddMenuButton`**, make sure you set `.Tag = key` and wire the click:

```vbnet
btn.Tag = key
AddHandler btn.Click, AddressOf MenuButton_Click
```

That’s the only change needed to make **C → Ledger** work.

---

## Where exactly does this code go?
- **File:** `FormMainMenu.vb`
- **Inside:** `Public Class FormMainMenu ... End Class`
- **Not** in the Designer file (`FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`)
- **Not** in `BuildMenu()` (BuildMenu just creates buttons)

ugh, sorry, wrong paste, I meant this: Private Function AddMenuButton(parent As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, caption As String) As Button

## Copilot

Use that `AddMenuButton` function, but you need to **fix it and place it in `FormMainMenu.vb`** (not the Designer file). As written, it won’t compile because VB object initializers need commas between properties, and the comment line can’t sit inside the initializer like that.

### Put this in `FormMainMenu.vb` inside `Public Class FormMainMenu`
(You can put it right under `BuildMenu()`.)

```vbnet
Private Function AddMenuButton(parent As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, caption As String) As Button
    Dim btn As New Button() With {
        .Text = $"({key}) {caption}",
        .Tag = key,
        .AutoSize = False
    }

    ' Apply whatever styling you want here (outside the initializer)
    ' Example:
    btn.Width = parent.ClientSize.Width - 25
    btn.Height = 44
    btn.Margin = New Padding(5)
    btn.BackColor = Color.Black
    btn.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
    btn.Font = New Font("Consolas", 12.0!, FontStyle.Bold)

    AddHandler btn.Click, AddressOf MenuButton_Click
    parent.Controls.Add(btn)
    Return btn
End Function
```

### Also add this click router in the same file/class
(Anywhere inside `FormMainMenu`.)

```vbnet
Private Sub MenuButton_Click(sender As Object, e As EventArgs)
    Dim btn = TryCast(sender, Button)
    Dim key = TryCast(btn?.Tag, String)

    Select Case key
        Case "C"
            Using f As New FormLedgerView()
                f.ShowDialog(Me)
            End Using

        Case "?"
            Using f As New FormAbout()
                f.ShowDialog(Me)
            End Using

        Case Else
            MessageBox.Show(Me, $"Menu item ({key}) not implemented yet.", "AMiOffice")
    End Select
End Sub
```

### Important notes
- **Do not** put either of these in `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb` (it will get overwritten).
- You don’t need to replace `BuildMenu()`—it already calls `AddMenuButton(...)` correctly.

If you tell me what your existing button styling is (or paste your current `AddMenuButton`), I can match your current look exactly instead of the example styling above.

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Private _buildInfo As String = ""

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Dim V = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Dim built = BuildInfo.BuildNumber

        Me.KeyPreview = True

        BuildMenu()


        _buildInfo = "   " & BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Me.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application   " & BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        ' Force the Show of a small window with version and build - 'MessageBox.Show(BuildInfo.DisplayVersion, "DisplayVersion")

        lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
        UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        tmrClock.Start()

        ' Force the version/date text to start as far left as possible in lblDateTime
        lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)  ' remove right padding; keep top padding
        lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)

        ' IMPORTANT: run resize after layout is finalized
        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Private Sub flpLeft_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpLeft.SizeChanged
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
    End Sub

    Private Sub flpRight_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpRight.SizeChanged
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub
    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick
        UpdateHeaderClock()
    End Sub
    Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

        lblDateTime.Text = _buildInfo & "          " &
                       now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                       now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                       now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub
    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        ' Left column
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (removed: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        lbl.Width = 1000 ' will be resized later
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        ' Force black buttons with white text
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1

        ' Optional: nicer hover / click feedback
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)

        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
        panel.ClientSize.Width -
        panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
        SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")

            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try

            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class

## Copilot

You already have everything you need wired up — the only change is to replace the **`Case "C"`** branch in `HandleMenuKey` so it opens your new Ledger form instead of calling `NotYet(...)`.

### What to change (single edit)
In **`FormMainMenu.vb`**, find this line:

```vbnet
Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
```

Replace it with:

```vbnet
Case "C"
    Using f As New FormLedgerView()
        f.ShowDialog(Me)
    End Using
```

That’s it. You do **not** need to touch `BuildMenu()` or `AddMenuButton()`—your buttons already call `HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))`, so clicking **(C)** will now launch the Ledger view.

---

## Where does `FormLedgerView` live?
- `FormLedgerView.vb` should be a **Windows Form** in the same project.
- `LedgerCurReader.vb` and `LegacyDataPaths.vb` should be **Class** files (not forms).

If `FormLedgerView` doesn’t exist yet, create it (Add → Windows Form) and we’ll hook it up to the `LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur)` call.

---

## One more small recommendation
At the top of the project, you currently have `Option Strict Off`. If possible, keep your new files (`LedgerCurReader.vb`, etc.) with `Option Strict On` at the top (as I provided). They can coexist fine.

---

If you paste your current `FormLedgerView` (even if empty), I’ll tell you the minimal controls + code to display the `LEDGER.CUR` entries in a grid and filter by customer/check/year/month like the DOS version.

End Class

## Copilot

Put the “View Checks” implementation into `FormLedgerView.vb` as a real WinForms screen. Minimal version: a grid + a Refresh button + a couple filters. This will prove the end-to-end wiring for **Main Menu (C) → read `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\LEDGER.CUR` → show rows**.

## 1) Ensure these files exist (Class files)
- `LegacyDataPaths.vb` (points to `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data`)
- `LedgerCurReader.vb` (the **6-lines-per-entry** reader we corrected)

## 2) Replace `FormLedgerView.vb` with this
(Add this in the same file: `FormLedgerView.vb`. You can paste over the empty class.)

```vbnet name=FormLedgerView.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormLedgerView

    Private ReadOnly _all As New List(Of LedgerEntry)()

    ' UI controls (created in code so you don’t have to design it yet)
    Private ReadOnly txtCustomer As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly txtCheck As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly cmbYear As New ComboBox()
    Private ReadOnly cmbMonth As New ComboBox()
    Private ReadOnly btnRefresh As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly dgv As New DataGridView()
    Private ReadOnly lblTotal As New Label()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Ledger - View Checks"
        Width = 1100
        Height = 750
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        BuildUi()
        PopulatePicklists()

        LoadData()
        ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildUi()
        Dim topPanel As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Top,
            .Height = 70,
            .ColumnCount = 9,
            .RowCount = 2
        }

        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Customer lbl
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 35)) ' Customer txt
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Check lbl
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 20)) ' Check txt
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Year lbl
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 15)) ' Year cmb
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Month lbl
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 15)) ' Month cmb
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Refresh btn

        Dim lblCustomer As New Label() With {.Text = "Customer:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}
        Dim lblCheck As New Label() With {.Text = "Check #:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}
        Dim lblYear As New Label() With {.Text = "Year:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}
        Dim lblMonth As New Label() With {.Text = "Month:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}

        txtCustomer.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        txtCheck.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

        cmbYear.DropDownStyle = ComboBoxStyle.DropDownList
        cmbMonth.DropDownStyle = ComboBoxStyle.DropDownList
        cmbYear.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        cmbMonth.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

        btnRefresh.Text = "Refresh"
        btnRefresh.AutoSize = True
        btnRefresh.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left

        lblTotal.Text = "Total: $0.00"
        lblTotal.AutoSize = True
        lblTotal.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblTotal.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft

        dgv.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        dgv.ReadOnly = True
        dgv.AllowUserToAddRows = False
        dgv.AllowUserToDeleteRows = False
        dgv.AutoGenerateColumns = True
        dgv.SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect

        ' Row 0: filters
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblCustomer, 0, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(txtCustomer, 1, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblCheck, 2, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(txtCheck, 3, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblYear, 4, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(cmbYear, 5, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblMonth, 6, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(cmbMonth, 7, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(btnRefresh, 8, 0)

        ' Row 1: total
        topPanel.SetColumnSpan(lblTotal, 9)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblTotal, 0, 1)

        Controls.Add(dgv)
        Controls.Add(topPanel)

        AddHandler btnRefresh.Click, Sub() LoadData() : ApplyFilters()
        AddHandler txtCustomer.TextChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
        AddHandler txtCheck.TextChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
        AddHandler cmbYear.SelectedIndexChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
        AddHandler cmbMonth.SelectedIndexChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub PopulatePicklists()
        cmbMonth.Items.Clear()
        cmbMonth.Items.Add("") ' all
        For m As Integer = 1 To 12
            cmbMonth.Items.Add(m.ToString("00"))
        Next
        cmbMonth.SelectedIndex = 0

        cmbYear.Items.Clear()
        cmbYear.Items.Add("") ' all
        ' We don’t know min/max upfront; just provide a reasonable range + all.
        For y As Integer = Date.Today.Year To 1986 Step -1
            cmbYear.Items.Add(y.ToString())
        Next
        cmbYear.SelectedIndex = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadData()
        _all.Clear()
        _all.AddRange(LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur))
    End Sub

    Private Sub ApplyFilters()
        Dim cust As String = txtCustomer.Text.Trim()
        Dim chk As String = txtCheck.Text.Trim()
        Dim year As String = If(TryCast(cmbYear.SelectedItem, String), "").Trim()
        Dim month As String = If(TryCast(cmbMonth.SelectedItem, String), "").Trim()

        Dim q = _all.AsEnumerable()

        If cust <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(e) (e.Customer ?? "").IndexOf(cust, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
        End If

        If chk <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(e) (e.CheckNumber ?? "").IndexOf(chk, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
        End If

        If year <> "" Then
            Dim yy As String = year.Substring(year.Length - 2) ' DOS compared last 2 digits
            q = q.Where(Function(e) (e.DateText ?? "").EndsWith(yy, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If

        If month <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(e) (e.DateText ?? "").StartsWith(month, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If

        Dim list = q.ToList()
        dgv.DataSource = list

        Dim total As Decimal = list.Sum(Function(e) e.Amount)
        lblTotal.Text = $"Total: {total:C}"
    End Sub

End Class
```

### Tiny fix if your VB version doesn’t like `??`
If you get an error on `??`, replace `(e.Customer ?? "")` with `If(e.Customer, "")` (same for other fields). Some VB configurations don’t support that operator.

## Copilot

Fixing this is straightforward—your errors come from **(1)** VB lambda syntax, **(2)** the `??` operator I accidentally used (that’s C#, not VB), and **(3)** you don’t yet have `LegacyDataPaths.vb` in the project (or it’s in a namespace you’re not importing).

Below are the exact changes.

---

## 1) Fix the lambda error (BC36918)
This line is the problem:

```vbnet
AddHandler btnRefresh.Click, Sub() LoadData() : ApplyFilters()
```

VB single-line lambdas can only contain **one** statement. Make it multi-line:

```vbnet
AddHandler btnRefresh.Click,
    Sub()
        LoadData()
        ApplyFilters()
    End Sub
```

---

## 2) Remove the `??` operator and the “String to Boolean” errors (BC36637 / BC30512)
These come from code like:

```vbnet
(e.Customer ?? "")
```

That operator doesn’t exist in VB. Replace all of them with `If(e.Customer, "")` etc.

---

## 3) Fix `LegacyDataPaths` not declared (BC30451)
You need to **add** `LegacyDataPaths.vb` as a Class file (Add → New Item → Class) into your project.

Use this exact file:

```vbnet
' LegacyDataPaths.vb  (Add as Class, NOT a form)
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO

Public NotInheritable Class LegacyDataPaths
    Private Sub New()
    End Sub

    Public Shared ReadOnly Property BaseDataDir As String = "\\invoice\MainMenu\Data"

    Public Shared ReadOnly Property LedgerCur As String =
        Path.Combine(BaseDataDir, "LEDGER.CUR")
End Class
```

If you already created it but still get the error, then it’s likely in a namespace. In that case tell me:
- the project’s **Root Namespace** (Project Properties), or
- paste the top of `LegacyDataPaths.vb`

…and I’ll tell you the correct `Imports ...` or fully-qualified name to use.

---

## 4) Replace your `FormLedgerView.vb` with this corrected version
This compiles under VB with `Option Strict On` and doesn’t use unsupported operators.

```vbnet name=FormLedgerView.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormLedgerView

    Private ReadOnly _all As New List(Of LedgerEntry)()

    Private ReadOnly txtCustomer As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly txtCheck As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly cmbYear As New ComboBox()
    Private ReadOnly cmbMonth As New ComboBox()
    Private ReadOnly btnRefresh As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly dgv As New DataGridView()
    Private ReadOnly lblTotal As New Label()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Ledger - View Checks"
        Width = 1100
        Height = 750
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        BuildUi()
        PopulatePicklists()

        LoadData()
        ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildUi()
        Dim topPanel As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Top,
            .Height = 70,
            .ColumnCount = 9,
            .RowCount = 2
        }

        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 35))
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 20))
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 15))
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 15))
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

        Dim lblCustomer As New Label() With {.Text = "Customer:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}
        Dim lblCheck As New Label() With {.Text = "Check #:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}
        Dim lblYear As New Label() With {.Text = "Year:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}
        Dim lblMonth As New Label() With {.Text = "Month:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}

        txtCustomer.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        txtCheck.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

        cmbYear.DropDownStyle = ComboBoxStyle.DropDownList
        cmbMonth.DropDownStyle = ComboBoxStyle.DropDownList
        cmbYear.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        cmbMonth.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

        btnRefresh.Text = "Refresh"
        btnRefresh.AutoSize = True
        btnRefresh.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left

        lblTotal.Text = "Total: $0.00"
        lblTotal.AutoSize = True
        lblTotal.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblTotal.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft

        dgv.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        dgv.ReadOnly = True
        dgv.AllowUserToAddRows = False
        dgv.AllowUserToDeleteRows = False
        dgv.AutoGenerateColumns = True
        dgv.SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect

        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblCustomer, 0, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(txtCustomer, 1, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblCheck, 2, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(txtCheck, 3, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblYear, 4, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(cmbYear, 5, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblMonth, 6, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(cmbMonth, 7, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(btnRefresh, 8, 0)

        topPanel.SetColumnSpan(lblTotal, 9)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblTotal, 0, 1)

        Controls.Add(dgv)
        Controls.Add(topPanel)

        AddHandler btnRefresh.Click,
            Sub()
                LoadData()
                ApplyFilters()
            End Sub

        AddHandler txtCustomer.TextChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
        AddHandler txtCheck.TextChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
        AddHandler cmbYear.SelectedIndexChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
        AddHandler cmbMonth.SelectedIndexChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub PopulatePicklists()
        cmbMonth.Items.Clear()
        cmbMonth.Items.Add("")
        For m As Integer = 1 To 12
            cmbMonth.Items.Add(m.ToString("00"))
        Next
        cmbMonth.SelectedIndex = 0

        cmbYear.Items.Clear()
        cmbYear.Items.Add("")
        For y As Integer = Date.Today.Year To 1986 Step -1
            cmbYear.Items.Add(y.ToString())
        Next
        cmbYear.SelectedIndex = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadData()
        _all.Clear()
        _all.AddRange(LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur))
    End Sub

    Private Sub ApplyFilters()
        Dim cust As String = txtCustomer.Text.Trim()
        Dim chk As String = txtCheck.Text.Trim()
        Dim year As String = If(TryCast(cmbYear.SelectedItem, String), "").Trim()
        Dim month As String = If(TryCast(cmbMonth.SelectedItem, String), "").Trim()

        Dim q = _all.AsEnumerable()

        If cust <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(e) If(e.Customer, "").IndexOf(cust, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
        End If

        If chk <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(e) If(e.CheckNumber, "").IndexOf(chk, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
        End If

        If year <> "" Then
            Dim yy As String = year.Substring(year.Length - 2)
            q = q.Where(Function(e) If(e.DateText, "").EndsWith(yy, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If

        If month <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(e) If(e.DateText, "").StartsWith(month, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If

        Dim list = q.ToList()
        dgv.DataSource = list

        Dim total As Decimal = list.Sum(Function(e) e.Amount)
        lblTotal.Text = $"Total: {total:C}"
    End Sub

End Class
```

---

## If you still get 1–2 errors after this
The most likely remaining issue will be **namespace/root namespace** for `LegacyDataPaths` or `LedgerCurReader`. Paste the top of those files (first ~10 lines) and your project’s Root Namespace, and I’ll tell you exactly what `Imports` or qualifier to add.

## @testsubject117

what file and where do I replace (e.Customer ?? "") with If(e.Customer, "")

## Copilot

In **`FormLedgerView.vb`** (the WinForms form you’re building). It’s inside the `ApplyFilters()` method, on the lines where you filter with `q.Where(...)`.

### Where exactly
Open **`FormLedgerView.vb`** and search (Ctrl+F) for either:
- `??` (best), or
- `.Customer ??` / `.CheckNumber ??` / `.DateText ??`

Then replace each occurrence.

### The exact replacements
Replace:

- `(e.Customer ?? "")` → `If(e.Customer, "")`
- `(e.CheckNumber ?? "")` → `If(e.CheckNumber, "")`
- `(e.DateText ?? "")` → `If(e.DateText, "")`

### Example (before/after)
**Before (wrong for VB):**
```vbnet
q = q.Where(Function(e) (e.Customer ?? "").IndexOf(cust, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
```

**After (correct VB):**
```vbnet
q = q.Where(Function(e) If(e.Customer, "").IndexOf(cust, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
```

### Note
You should only see `??` in code you pasted from me earlier. If you already replaced your `FormLedgerView.vb` with the corrected version I sent, it **won’t contain `??` at all**. If it still does, you’re editing the older version of the file.

Add this temporary debug line inside `LoadData()` in **FormLedgerView.vb**:

Run again and click **C**:

- If it says `False`, the UNC path is wrong or the app doesn’t have access.

### 3) Most common cause right now: filename casing / extension mismatch
Your DOS code uses `LEDGER.CUR`. On Windows shares it could be:
- `Ledger.cur`
- `LEDGER.CUR`
- `LEDGER.CUR.txt` (if it got renamed during copying)

Make sure the file on `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data` is exactly named **`LEDGER.CUR`**.

Your file is **6 lines per entry**, not CSV. So your `LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(...)` should be the line-grouping version we wrote.

## @testsubject117

question. will this ledger view be pulling data from the original dos ledger.cur file?

## Copilot

Yes—**it’s pulling from whatever file is currently at the path you configured**, which right now is:

`\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\LEDGER.CUR`

So:

- If you copied the original DOS `C:\LEDGER.CUR` up to that share (unchanged), then **you are viewing the original DOS data** (just from the network location).
- If `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\LEDGER.CUR` is a different/new file, then you’re viewing that one instead.

Nothing in the current `FormLedgerView`/`LedgerCurReader` writes back—**it’s read-only**.

### How to be 100% sure it’s the original data
On the machine/share:
1) Check the file’s **timestamp** and **size**.
2) Open it in Notepad and confirm it looks like your DOS lines (6-line repeating pattern).
3) In the app, temporarily show the full path being used (optional) by popping:
   ```vbnet

   ```

## Copilot

Yes—if you want the new **Ledger – View Checks** screen to show real historical data, you should copy the original DOS `LEDGER.CUR` into `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\LEDGER.CUR`.

### What to do (safe approach)
1) On the server/share, create a folder for legacy imports, e.g.
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\legacy\`

2) Copy the DOS file up **without changing it**:
- from DOS: `C:\LEDGER.CUR`
- to: `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\legacy\LEDGER.CUR`

3) Then copy it into the “active” location your app currently reads:
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\LEDGER.CUR`

This gives you a preserved original plus the active one.

### Important notes
- Your app currently is **read-only**, so copying it there is low risk.
- Make sure the file keeps the exact name `LEDGER.CUR` (not `LEDGER.CUR.txt`).

### Quick verification
After copying, open your app → click **C**:
- you should see rows populate immediately.

### One question before you do this
Is `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data` meant to be a **shared live data folder** (multi-user), or just a staging folder during conversion?

If it’s live/shared, we should also add a simple “file locked / file missing” friendly error message in `LoadData()` so users get a clear prompt rather than an exception.

## @testsubject117

dos ledger.bas also need to be at \\invoice\MainMenu\Data?

## Copilot

No. Your VB.NET program does **not** need `LEDGER.BAS` to be present at `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data`.

- `LEDGER.BAS` was only needed by GW-BASIC to run the DOS program.
- Your VB.NET conversion only needs the **data files** that it reads/writes.

### What you *do* need on `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data` (for the ledger module)
For the parts we’re implementing now (read-only “View Checks”):
- `LEDGER.CUR`

For later features you’ll likely port:
- `CHECK.INV` (used by “Find Check #” and “Find Invoice #” in your DOS code)
- `OTHER.CHK` (other checks)
- `EMPNAME.DAT` (sales employee checks feature)
- `INVOICE.CHK` (random-access invoice status file; needed for add/delete checks)
- `ERRORS.LST` (optional; you can log elsewhere in VB.NET)

### Where should `LEDGER.BAS` go then?
Keep it in your source archive (Git repo, a `Legacy` folder, or documentation), because it’s your “spec” for behavior—but it doesn’t belong in the runtime data folder.

If you want, I can give you a “Data folder checklist” (exact filenames + purpose + whether required for each menu option) so you know what to copy when you implement each feature.

### Two quick hardening steps I recommend next

1) **Graceful “file missing / access denied” message**
In `FormLedgerView.LoadData()`, wrap the read in `Try/Catch` so users get a friendly error if the share is down or permissions change.

```vbnet
Private Sub LoadData()
    Try
        _all.Clear()
        _all.AddRange(LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur))
    Catch ex As Exception
        MessageBox.Show(Me,
                        "Unable to read LEDGER.CUR from:" & Environment.NewLine &
                        LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                        ex.Message,
                        "Ledger", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
    End Try
End Sub
```

2) **Sort newest-first (so it feels modern)**
Right now it will display in file order (oldest → newest). If you want newest-first without parsing dates yet, you can just reverse after load:

```vbnet
_all.Reverse()
```

(or later we’ll parse `DateText` into a real `Date` and sort properly).

# A) (5) View Company Totals — add to `FormLedgerView`
This is derived from `LEDGER.BAS` lines ~1890–2050: sum check amounts per customer.

### Add a new button and handler in `FormLedgerView`
In `BuildUi()`, add:

- `btnTotals` (a new Button)
- and a handler to open a totals form

To keep things clean, I recommend a new form:

## Create a new Windows Form: `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb`

```vbnet name=FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormLedgerCompanyTotals

    Private ReadOnly dgv As New DataGridView()
    Private ReadOnly btnClose As New Button()

    Public Sub New()
        Text = "Ledger - Company Totals"
        Width = 800
        Height = 700
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        dgv.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        dgv.ReadOnly = True
        dgv.AllowUserToAddRows = False
        dgv.AllowUserToDeleteRows = False
        dgv.AutoGenerateColumns = True
        dgv.SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect

        btnClose.Text = "Close"
        btnClose.Dock = DockStyle.Bottom
        btnClose.Height = 40
        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Close()

        Controls.Add(dgv)
        Controls.Add(btnClose)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnShown(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnShown(e)

        Try
            Dim entries = LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur)

            Dim totals =
                entries.
                GroupBy(Function(x) (If(x.Customer, "")).Trim()).
                Select(Function(g) New With {
                    .Customer = g.Key,
                    .Total = g.Sum(Function(x) x.Amount),
                    .Count = g.Count()
                }).
                OrderBy(Function(x) x.Customer).
                ToList()

            dgv.DataSource = totals
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show(Me, ex.Message, "Company Totals", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

### Wire it from `FormLedgerView`
Add a button (simplest: put it next to Refresh). In `FormLedgerView`, add:

1) Field:
```vbnet
Private ReadOnly btnTotals As New Button()
```

2) In `BuildUi()` after `btnRefresh` setup:
```vbnet
btnTotals.Text = "Company Totals"
btnTotals.AutoSize = True
btnTotals.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left
```

3) Add it to the top panel (you’ll need one more column or just stack buttons). Easiest: put totals on row 1:

```vbnet
topPanel.Controls.Add(btnTotals, 8, 1)
```

4) Handler:
```vbnet
AddHandler btnTotals.Click,
    Sub()
        Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
            f.ShowDialog(Me)
        End Using
    End Sub
```
