# B) (8) View OTHER Checks — new form reading `OTHER.CHK`
DOS stores 6 fields per entry (from `WRITE#1` at line ~2380):
1) company
2) date
3) check#
4) amount
5) reference
6) reason-why

So `OTHER.CHK` is also “one field per line” style on disk (same pattern as `LEDGER.CUR`).

## Add reader + model: `OtherChkReader.vb`
(Add as **Class** file.)

```vbnet name=OtherChkReader.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Globalization
Imports System.IO

Public NotInheritable Class OtherChkReader
    Private Sub New()
    End Sub

    Public Shared Function ReadAll(path As String) As List(Of OtherCheckEntry)
        Dim results As New List(Of OtherCheckEntry)()
        If Not File.Exists(path) Then Return results

        Dim rawLines = File.ReadAllLines(path)
        Dim lines As New List(Of String)()

        For Each raw In rawLines
            If raw Is Nothing Then Continue For
            Dim s = raw.Trim()
            If s = "" Then Continue For
            lines.Add(s)
        Next

        Dim i As Integer = 0
        While i + 5 < lines.Count
            results.Add(New OtherCheckEntry With {
                .Company = Unquote(lines(i)),
                .DateText = Unquote(lines(i + 1)),
                .CheckNumber = Unquote(lines(i + 2)),
                .Amount = ParseDecimal(lines(i + 3)),
                .Reference = Unquote(lines(i + 4)),
                .ReasonWhy = Unquote(lines(i + 5))
            })
            i += 6
        End While

        Return results
    End Function

    Private Shared Function Unquote(s As String) As String
        If s Is Nothing Then Return ""
        s = s.Trim()
        If s.Length >= 2 AndAlso s.StartsWith("""") AndAlso s.EndsWith("""") Then
            Return s.Substring(1, s.Length - 2)
        End If
        Return s
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseDecimal(s As String) As Decimal
        If s Is Nothing Then Return 0D
        s = s.Trim()
        Dim d As Decimal
        If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, d) Then Return d
        If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, d) Then Return d
        Return 0D
    End Function
End Class

Public Class OtherCheckEntry
    Public Property Company As String
    Public Property DateText As String
    Public Property CheckNumber As String
    Public Property Amount As Decimal
    Public Property Reference As String
    Public Property ReasonWhy As String
End Class
```

## New Windows Form: `FormOtherChecksView.vb`
```vbnet name=FormOtherChecksView.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormOtherChecksView

    Private ReadOnly txtCompany As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly btnRefresh As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly dgv As New DataGridView()
    Private ReadOnly lblTotal As New Label()

    Private _all As New List(Of OtherCheckEntry)()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Other Checks (overpayments / non-invoice checks)"
        Width = 1100
        Height = 750
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim top As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Top, .Height = 45}
        top.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.Text = "Company contains:", .AutoSize = True, .Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)})
        txtCompany.Width = 250
        top.Controls.Add(txtCompany)

        btnRefresh.Text = "Refresh"
        top.Controls.Add(btnRefresh)

        lblTotal.AutoSize = True
        lblTotal.Padding = New Padding(20, 10, 0, 0)
        top.Controls.Add(lblTotal)

        dgv.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        dgv.ReadOnly = True
        dgv.AllowUserToAddRows = False
        dgv.AllowUserToDeleteRows = False
        dgv.AutoGenerateColumns = True

        Controls.Add(dgv)
        Controls.Add(top)

        AddHandler btnRefresh.Click, Sub() LoadData() : ApplyFilter()
        AddHandler txtCompany.TextChanged, Sub() ApplyFilter()

        LoadData()
        ApplyFilter()
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadData()
        Try
            _all = OtherChkReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.OtherChk)
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show(Me, ex.Message, "Other Checks", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
            _all = New List(Of OtherCheckEntry)()
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub ApplyFilter()
        Dim s = txtCompany.Text.Trim()
        Dim q = _all.AsEnumerable()

        If s <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(x) If(x.Company, "").IndexOf(s, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
        End If

        Dim list = q.ToList()
        dgv.DataSource = list

        Dim total = list.Sum(Function(x) x.Amount)
        lblTotal.Text = $"Total: {total:C}"
    End Sub

End Class
```

Wire this from **Main Menu** or from inside `FormLedgerView` with a button like “Other Checks”.

### (5) View Company Totals (from `LEDGER.CUR`)
Create a new **Windows Form** named `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb` and paste this:

```vbnet name=FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormLedgerCompanyTotals

    Private ReadOnly dgv As New DataGridView()
    Private ReadOnly lblGrandTotal As New Label()
    Private ReadOnly btnClose As New Button()

    Public Sub New()
        Text = "Ledger - Company Totals"
        Width = 900
        Height = 700
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim top As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Top, .Height = 40}
        lblGrandTotal.AutoSize = True
        lblGrandTotal.Padding = New Padding(10, 10, 0, 0)
        top.Controls.Add(lblGrandTotal)

        dgv.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        dgv.ReadOnly = True
        dgv.AllowUserToAddRows = False
        dgv.AllowUserToDeleteRows = False
        dgv.AutoGenerateColumns = True
        dgv.SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect

        btnClose.Text = "Close"
        btnClose.Dock = DockStyle.Bottom
        btnClose.Height = 40
        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Close()

        Controls.Add(dgv)
        Controls.Add(btnClose)
        Controls.Add(top)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnShown(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnShown(e)

        Try
            Dim entries = LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur)

            Dim totals =
                entries.
                GroupBy(Function(x) (If(x.Customer, "")).Trim()).
                Select(Function(g) New With {
                    .Customer = g.Key,
                    .Total = g.Sum(Function(x) x.Amount),
                    .Count = g.Count()
                }).
                OrderBy(Function(x) x.Customer).
                ToList()

            dgv.DataSource = totals

            Dim grand = totals.Sum(Function(x) x.Total)
            lblGrandTotal.Text = $"Grand total: {grand:C}   (Customers: {totals.Count})"
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show(Me, ex.Message, "Company Totals", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

### (8) View OTHER Checks (from `OTHER.CHK`)
Add **Class file** `OtherChkReader.vb`:

```vbnet name=OtherChkReader.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Globalization
Imports System.IO

Public NotInheritable Class OtherChkReader
    Private Sub New()
    End Sub

    Public Shared Function ReadAll(path As String) As List(Of OtherCheckEntry)
        Dim results As New List(Of OtherCheckEntry)()
        If Not File.Exists(path) Then Return results

        Dim rawLines = File.ReadAllLines(path)
        Dim lines As New List(Of String)()

        For Each raw In rawLines
            If raw Is Nothing Then Continue For
            Dim s = raw.Trim()
            If s = "" Then Continue For
            lines.Add(s)
        Next

        Dim i As Integer = 0
        While i + 5 < lines.Count
            results.Add(New OtherCheckEntry With {
                .Company = Unquote(lines(i)),
                .DateText = Unquote(lines(i + 1)),
                .CheckNumber = Unquote(lines(i + 2)),
                .Amount = ParseDecimal(lines(i + 3)),
                .Reference = Unquote(lines(i + 4)),
                .ReasonWhy = Unquote(lines(i + 5))
            })
            i += 6
        End While

        Return results
    End Function

    Private Shared Function Unquote(s As String) As String
        If s Is Nothing Then Return ""
        s = s.Trim()
        If s.Length >= 2 AndAlso s.StartsWith("""", StringComparison.Ordinal) AndAlso s.EndsWith("""", StringComparison.Ordinal) Then
            Return s.Substring(1, s.Length - 2)
        End If
        Return s
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseDecimal(s As String) As Decimal
        If s Is Nothing Then Return 0D
        s = s.Trim()

        Dim d As Decimal
        If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, d) Then Return d
        If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, d) Then Return d
        Return 0D
    End Function
End Class

Public Class OtherCheckEntry
    Public Property Company As String
    Public Property DateText As String
    Public Property CheckNumber As String
    Public Property Amount As Decimal
    Public Property Reference As String
    Public Property ReasonWhy As String
End Class
```

Add **Windows Form** `FormOtherChecksView.vb`:

```vbnet name=FormOtherChecksView.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormOtherChecksView

    Private ReadOnly txtCompany As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly btnRefresh As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly dgv As New DataGridView()
    Private ReadOnly lblTotal As New Label()

    Private _all As New List(Of OtherCheckEntry)()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Other Checks"
        Width = 1100
        Height = 750
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim top As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Top, .Height = 45}
        top.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.Text = "Company contains:", .AutoSize = True, .Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)})

        txtCompany.Width = 250
        top.Controls.Add(txtCompany)

        btnRefresh.Text = "Refresh"
        top.Controls.Add(btnRefresh)

        lblTotal.AutoSize = True
        lblTotal.Padding = New Padding(20, 10, 0, 0)
        top.Controls.Add(lblTotal)

        dgv.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        dgv.ReadOnly = True
        dgv.AllowUserToAddRows = False
        dgv.AllowUserToDeleteRows = False
        dgv.AutoGenerateColumns = True

        Controls.Add(dgv)
        Controls.Add(top)

        AddHandler btnRefresh.Click,
            Sub()
                LoadData()
                ApplyFilter()
            End Sub
        AddHandler txtCompany.TextChanged, Sub() ApplyFilter()

        LoadData()
        ApplyFilter()
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadData()
        Try
            _all = OtherChkReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.OtherChk)
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show(Me, ex.Message, "Other Checks", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
            _all = New List(Of OtherCheckEntry)()
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub ApplyFilter()
        Dim s = txtCompany.Text.Trim()
        Dim q = _all.AsEnumerable()

        If s <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(x) If(x.Company, "").IndexOf(s, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
        End If

        Dim list = q.ToList()
        dgv.DataSource = list

        Dim total = list.Sum(Function(x) x.Amount)
        lblTotal.Text = $"Total: {total:C}"
    End Sub

End Class
```

### Update `LegacyDataPaths.vb` (add these properties)
```vbnet
Public Shared ReadOnly Property CheckInv As String = Path.Combine(BaseDataDir, "CHECK.INV")
Public Shared ReadOnly Property OtherChk As String = Path.Combine(BaseDataDir, "OTHER.CHK")
```

## @testsubject117

you said Create a new Windows Form: FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb, is this a windows form?

## Copilot

Yes — **`FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb` should be added as a Windows Form**.

### In Visual Studio
1) Right‑click your project **AMiOffice**
2) **Add → New Item…**
3) Pick **Windows Form (Visual Basic)** (sometimes shown as “Windows Form”)
4) Name it **`FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb`**
5) Click **Add**
6) Open the new `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb` (the code-behind, not the designer) and **replace its contents** with the code I provided.

### Why it’s a Windows Form
It’s a UI screen (window) that shows a DataGridView of totals and a Close button—so it should inherit from `System.Windows.Forms.Form`, which is what a Windows Form item gives you automatically.

If you instead add it as a “Class”, you *can* still make it inherit `Form`, but using the **Windows Form** template is the simplest because Visual Studio also creates the `.Designer.vb` file (even though our code builds the UI dynamically).

## @testsubject117

what do you mean you can expose then under c (ledger) as buttons on formledgerview or temporarily map them to keys in HandleMenuKey, what's the difference?

## Copilot

They’re two different ways to let the user *reach* the new features.

## Option 1 (recommended): expose them **under C** inside the Ledger area
Meaning: the user clicks **(C) Checks and Cash Receipts** and then, *within that Ledger window*, they click buttons like:

- “View Checks” (what you already built)
- “Company Totals” (option 5)
- “Other Checks” (option 8)
- “Find by Check #” (option 9)
- “Find by Invoice #” (option 0)

**Pros**
- Matches the DOS flow: C → LEDGER menu → options 5/8/9/0.
- Keeps the main menu clean.
- You won’t “steal” other main menu keys.

**Cons**
- Requires a tiny bit of UI work: add a few buttons to `FormLedgerView` (or make a `FormLedgerMenu`).

**How it looks in code**
In `FormLedgerView`, add a button `btnTotals` that opens `FormLedgerCompanyTotals`.

---

## Option 2 (temporary / quick wiring): map them directly in **Main Menu** `HandleMenuKey`
Meaning: you temporarily pick a main menu key (say `"P"` or `"O"`) and make it open your new forms **directly**, like:

- Press/click **P** → Company Totals
- Press/click **O** → Other Checks

**Pros**
- Fastest way to test the new forms without adding UI inside Ledger.
- Good during development.

**Cons**
- Not true to the DOS menu meaning (you’d be repurposing keys).
- Confusing for users (“P says Entire Ledger Viewing but opens Totals”).
- You’ll have to undo/re-map later.

**How it looks in code**
In `FormMainMenu.vb` → `HandleMenuKey`:
```vbnet
Case "P"
    Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
        f.ShowDialog(Me)
    End Using
```

---

## My recommendation
Do **Option 1**: keep everything “Ledger-related” reachable from **C**.

### Easiest way to do Option 1 (clean UX)
Create a small Ledger submenu form:

- `FormLedgerMenu` with four buttons:
  - View Checks
  - Company Totals
  - Other Checks
  - Find (Check/Invoice)

Then in `FormMainMenu.HandleMenuKey` for `"C"`, open `FormLedgerMenu` instead of `FormLedgerView`.

If you want, I can give you `FormLedgerMenu.vb` (very small) and the exact one-line change to `Case "C"` in the main menu.

## Step 1 — Add a new Windows Form: `FormLedgerMenu.vb`
In Visual Studio: **Add → New Item → Windows Form (VB)**, name it `FormLedgerMenu.vb`, then replace its code with:

```vbnet name=FormLedgerMenu.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Public Class FormLedgerMenu

    Private ReadOnly btnViewChecks As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnCompanyTotals As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnOtherChecks As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnFindByCheck As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnFindByInvoice As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnClose As New Button()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Ledger Menu"
        Width = 520
        Height = 420
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim flp As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown,
            .WrapContents = False,
            .AutoScroll = True,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }

        Configure(btnViewChecks, "View Checks (LEDGER.CUR)")
        Configure(btnCompanyTotals, "View Company Totals (Option 5)")
        Configure(btnOtherChecks, "View OTHER Checks (Option 8)")
        Configure(btnFindByCheck, "Find by Check # (Option 9)")
        Configure(btnFindByInvoice, "Find by Invoice # (Option 0)")
        Configure(btnClose, "Close")

        AddHandler btnViewChecks.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormLedgerView()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnCompanyTotals.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnOtherChecks.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormOtherChecksView()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        ' These two forms we will add AFTER you paste a sample of CHECK.INV
        AddHandler btnFindByCheck.Click, Sub() MessageBox.Show(Me, "Next: implement CHECK.INV parser + Find by Check # form.")
        AddHandler btnFindByInvoice.Click, Sub() MessageBox.Show(Me, "Next: implement CHECK.INV parser + Find by Invoice # form.")

        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Close()

        flp.Controls.Add(btnViewChecks)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnCompanyTotals)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnOtherChecks)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnFindByCheck)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnFindByInvoice)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnClose)

        Controls.Add(flp)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub Configure(btn As Button, text As String)
        btn.Text = text
        btn.Width = 460
        btn.Height = 44
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
    End Sub

End Class
```

## Step 2 — Change Main Menu (C) to open `FormLedgerMenu`
In **`FormMainMenu.vb`**, in `HandleMenuKey`, change `Case "C"` to:

```vbnet
Case "C"
    Using f As New FormLedgerMenu()
        f.ShowDialog(Me)
    End Using
```

Now the user goes:
**Main Menu → (C) → Ledger Menu → pick totals/other checks/etc.**

## Copilot

Great — `CHECK.INV` is very clear now.

### Confirmed file format
It’s a repeating **record block**:

**Header line (CSV produced by GW-BASIC `WRITE#`)**
```
"ACCT","CHECKNO","SALESMAN?","MM-DD-YYYY",Amount,InvoiceCount
```

Followed by **InvoiceCount lines**, each containing a single invoice number (no quotes).

Example:
- Header says `...,196.75,4`
- Then exactly 4 invoice lines follow.

That means we can implement both:
- **(9) Find a Check #**: find header where CheckNo matches → show its invoice list.
- **(0) Find an Invoice #**: scan blocks, find invoice line matching → show the header (customer, check#, date, amount).

---

## 1) Update `LegacyDataPaths.vb`
Make sure it has this:

```vbnet
Public Shared ReadOnly Property CheckInv As String = Path.Combine(BaseDataDir, "CHECK.INV")
```

(You said the file is copied into `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data`, so this will resolve correctly.)

---

## 2) Add a parser: `CheckInvReader.vb` (Class file)
Add → New Item → **Class** → name `CheckInvReader.vb`:

```vbnet name=CheckInvReader.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Globalization
Imports System.IO

Public NotInheritable Class CheckInvReader
    Private Sub New()
    End Sub

    Public Shared Function ReadAll(path As String) As List(Of CheckInvBlock)
        Dim results As New List(Of CheckInvBlock)()
        If Not File.Exists(path) Then Return results

        Dim lines = File.ReadAllLines(path)
        Dim i As Integer = 0

        While i < lines.Length
            Dim line = lines(i).Trim()
            If line = "" Then
                i += 1
                Continue While
            End If

            ' Header lines start with a quote in your sample.
            If Not line.StartsWith("""", StringComparison.Ordinal) Then
                ' Skip junk/unexpected
                i += 1
                Continue While
            End If

            Dim header = ParseHeader(line)
            i += 1

            Dim invoices As New List(Of String)()
            For n As Integer = 1 To header.InvoiceCount
                If i >= lines.Length Then Exit For
                Dim inv = lines(i).Trim()
                If inv <> "" Then invoices.Add(inv)
                i += 1
            Next

            header.Invoices = invoices
            results.Add(header)
        End While

        Return results
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseHeader(line As String) As CheckInvBlock
        ' Example:
        ' "NATAIRCR","8508","4","01-03-1995",196.75,4
        Dim parts = SplitCsvWriteLine(line)

        ' parts should be: 6 items
        ' 0 customer, 1 checkno, 2 salesman?, 3 date, 4 amount, 5 invoiceCount
        Dim b As New CheckInvBlock()

        b.CustomerCode = GetPart(parts, 0)
        b.CheckNumber = GetPart(parts, 1)
        b.SalesmanCode = GetPart(parts, 2)
        b.DateText = GetPart(parts, 3)
        b.Amount = ParseDecimal(GetPart(parts, 4))
        b.InvoiceCount = ParseInt(GetPart(parts, 5))

        Return b
    End Function

    Private Shared Function GetPart(parts As List(Of String), idx As Integer) As String
        If parts Is Nothing OrElse idx < 0 OrElse idx >= parts.Count Then Return ""
        Return parts(idx)
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseDecimal(s As String) As Decimal
        If s Is Nothing Then Return 0D
        s = s.Trim()
        Dim d As Decimal
        If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, d) Then Return d
        If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, d) Then Return d
        Return 0D
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseInt(s As String) As Integer
        If s Is Nothing Then Return 0
        s = s.Trim()
        Dim n As Integer
        If Integer.TryParse(s, n) Then Return n
        Return 0
    End Function

    ' Splits a GW-BASIC WRITE# style CSV-ish line:
    ' - quoted strings may contain commas (unlikely here, but we support it)
    ' - numeric fields are unquoted
    Private Shared Function SplitCsvWriteLine(line As String) As List(Of String)
        Dim result As New List(Of String)()
        Dim cur As String = ""
        Dim inQuotes As Boolean = False

        Dim i As Integer = 0
        While i < line.Length
            Dim ch = line(i)

            If ch = """"c Then
                inQuotes = Not inQuotes
                i += 1
                Continue While
            End If

            If ch = ","c AndAlso Not inQuotes Then
                result.Add(cur.Trim())
                cur = ""
                i += 1
                Continue While
            End If

            cur &= ch
            i += 1
        End While

        If cur.Length > 0 OrElse line.EndsWith(",", StringComparison.Ordinal) Then
            result.Add(cur.Trim())
        End If

        Return result
    End Function
End Class

Public Class CheckInvBlock
    Public Property CustomerCode As String
    Public Property CheckNumber As String
    Public Property SalesmanCode As String
    Public Property DateText As String
    Public Property Amount As Decimal
    Public Property InvoiceCount As Integer
    Public Property Invoices As List(Of String)
End Class
```

---

## 3) (9) Find a Check # — new Windows Form `FormFindByCheckNumber.vb`
Add → **Windows Form** → `FormFindByCheckNumber.vb`:

```vbnet name=FormFindByCheckNumber.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormFindByCheckNumber

    Private ReadOnly txtCheck As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly btnFind As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly lblHeader As New Label()
    Private ReadOnly lstInvoices As New ListBox()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Find by Check # (CHECK.INV)"
        Width = 700
        Height = 650
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim top As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Top, .Height = 45, .Padding = New Padding(10)}
        top.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.Text = "Check #:", .AutoSize = True, .Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)})
        txtCheck.Width = 150
        top.Controls.Add(txtCheck)
        btnFind.Text = "Find"
        top.Controls.Add(btnFind)

        lblHeader.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lblHeader.Height = 50
        lblHeader.Padding = New Padding(10)
        lblHeader.Text = "Enter a check # and click Find."

        lstInvoices.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

        Controls.Add(lstInvoices)
        Controls.Add(lblHeader)
        Controls.Add(top)

        AddHandler btnFind.Click, Sub() DoFind()
        AddHandler txtCheck.KeyDown,
            Sub(sender, args)
                If args.KeyCode = Keys.Enter Then
                    args.SuppressKeyPress = True
                    DoFind()
                End If
            End Sub
    End Sub

    Private Sub DoFind()
        Dim checkNo = txtCheck.Text.Trim()
        If checkNo = "" Then Return

        Try
            Dim blocks = CheckInvReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.CheckInv)
            Dim hit = blocks.FirstOrDefault(Function(b) String.Equals(If(b.CheckNumber, "").Trim(), checkNo, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))

            lstInvoices.Items.Clear()

            If hit Is Nothing Then
                lblHeader.Text = $"No match for check #{checkNo}."
                Return
            End If

            lblHeader.Text = $"Customer: {hit.CustomerCode}    Check#: {hit.CheckNumber}    Date: {hit.DateText}    Amount: {hit.Amount:C}    Invoices: {hit.InvoiceCount}"

            For Each inv In hit.Invoices
                lstInvoices.Items.Add(inv)
            Next
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show(Me, ex.Message, "Find by Check #", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

---

## 4) (0) Find an Invoice # — new Windows Form `FormFindByInvoiceNumber.vb`
Add → **Windows Form** → `FormFindByInvoiceNumber.vb`:

```vbnet name=FormFindByInvoiceNumber.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormFindByInvoiceNumber

    Private ReadOnly txtInvoice As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly btnFind As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly lblResult As New Label()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Find by Invoice # (CHECK.INV)"
        Width = 750
        Height = 300
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim top As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Top, .Height = 45, .Padding = New Padding(10)}
        top.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.Text = "Invoice #:", .AutoSize = True, .Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)})
        txtInvoice.Width = 150
        top.Controls.Add(txtInvoice)
        btnFind.Text = "Find"
        top.Controls.Add(btnFind)

        lblResult.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblResult.Padding = New Padding(10)
        lblResult.Text = "Enter an invoice # and click Find."

        Controls.Add(lblResult)
        Controls.Add(top)

        AddHandler btnFind.Click, Sub() DoFind()
        AddHandler txtInvoice.KeyDown,
            Sub(sender, args)
                If args.KeyCode = Keys.Enter Then
                    args.SuppressKeyPress = True
                    DoFind()
                End If
            End Sub
    End Sub

    Private Sub DoFind()
        Dim invNo = txtInvoice.Text.Trim()
        If invNo = "" Then Return

        Try
            Dim blocks = CheckInvReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.CheckInv)

            Dim hit = blocks.FirstOrDefault(
                Function(b) b.Invoices IsNot Nothing AndAlso b.Invoices.Any(Function(x) String.Equals(x.Trim(), invNo, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
            )

            If hit Is Nothing Then
                lblResult.Text = $"No match for invoice #{invNo}."
                Return
            End If

            lblResult.Text =
                $"Invoice {invNo} was paid by:" & Environment.NewLine &
                $"Customer: {hit.CustomerCode}" & Environment.NewLine &
                $"Check #:  {hit.CheckNumber}" & Environment.NewLine &
                $"Date:     {hit.DateText}" & Environment.NewLine &
                $"Amount:   {hit.Amount:C}" & Environment.NewLine &
                $"Invoices on that check: {hit.InvoiceCount}"
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show(Me, ex.Message, "Find by Invoice #", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

## 1) Update `LegacyDataPaths.vb` (Class file)
Add these properties (keep your existing `LedgerCur`):

```vbnet
Public Shared ReadOnly Property CheckInv As String = Path.Combine(BaseDataDir, "CHECK.INV")
```

## Copilot

This confirms the parser approach is right, and it also shows two important edge-cases we should handle:

1) **The 3rd field is not always numeric** (examples: `"4049516"`, `"0000193457"`, `"DIRECT DEPOSIT"`). So we should treat it as a **string “PaymentRef”** (not “salesman code”).  
2) The file ends with a **DOS EOF marker `^Z`** (that last ``). We should ignore it.

Below are the final tweaks to make everything robust.

---

## A) Update the meaning of field #3
In `CheckInvReader.vb`, rename the property logically:

- from `SalesmanCode` → `PaymentRef` (or `Ref2` if you want neutral)

So in `CheckInvBlock`:

```vbnet
Public Property PaymentRef As String
```

And in `ParseHeader(...)`:

```vbnet
b.PaymentRef = GetPart(parts, 2)
```

(If you don’t want to rename, keep your existing name, but conceptually it’s a “ref / note / method” field.)

---

## B) Ignore the DOS `^Z` at end of file
In `ReadAll(...)`, when you get a line, add:

```vbnet
line = line.Replace(ChrW(&H1A), "").Trim() ' remove DOS EOF marker ^Z
```

Also when reading invoice lines, do the same cleanup.

### Minimal safe changes (exact spots)
In `ReadAll` right after `Dim line = lines(i).Trim()`:

```vbnet
Dim line = lines(i)
If line Is Nothing Then line = ""
line = line.Replace(ChrW(&H1A), "").Trim()
```

And inside the invoice loop:

```vbnet
Dim inv = lines(i)
If inv Is Nothing Then inv = ""
inv = inv.Replace(ChrW(&H1A), "").Trim()
If inv <> "" Then invoices.Add(inv)
```

This prevents a stray `^Z` from becoming a fake invoice number.

---

## C) Display field #3 on the Find forms
### In `FormFindByCheckNumber.vb`
Update the header label line to include the reference:

```vbnet
lblHeader.Text =
    $"Customer: {hit.CustomerCode}    Check#: {hit.CheckNumber}    Ref: {hit.PaymentRef}    Date: {hit.DateText}    Amount: {hit.Amount:C}    Invoices: {hit.InvoiceCount}"
```

### In `FormFindByInvoiceNumber.vb`
Add a line:

```vbnet
$"Ref:      {hit.PaymentRef}" & Environment.NewLine &
```

---

## D) Sanity check: the format is consistent
Your last sample shows headers like:

- `"POWILLMF","DIRECT DEPOSIT","DIRECT DEPOSIT","03-31-2026",295,1`

So:
- CheckNumber can be non-numeric text (`DIRECT DEPOSIT`)
- PaymentRef can be non-numeric text
- Date is consistently quoted `MM-DD-YYYY`
- Amount can have 3 decimals (`8380.901`) — our `Decimal` parse handles this fine.

That means (9) “Find by Check #” should allow searching for text too (like `DIRECT DEPOSIT`). The form already does (it matches strings).

---

## What I need from you (1 question)
For (9), do you want “Find a Check #” to:
- match **exactly** (what we do now), or
- allow **contains** search (so typing `deposit` finds `DIRECT DEPOSIT`)?

If you want contains-search, I’ll give you the 2-line change.

## Copilot

`FormLedgerMenu` is **not something Visual Studio creates automatically**—it’s the **new Windows Form we decided to add** to implement “Option 1” (a Ledger submenu under (C)). If you don’t see it, it simply hasn’t been added to the project yet.

## Create `FormLedgerMenu` (Windows Form) and then you can wire (9)/(0)

### 1) Add the form file
In Visual Studio:
1. Right‑click the **AMiOffice** project
2. **Add → New Item…**
3. Choose **Windows Form (Visual Basic)** (or “Windows Form”)
4. Name it: **`FormLedgerMenu.vb`**
5. Click **Add**

You should now see in Solution Explorer:
- `FormLedgerMenu.vb`
- `FormLedgerMenu.Designer.vb`
- `FormLedgerMenu.resx`

### 2) Paste the Ledger menu code into `FormLedgerMenu.vb`
Open `FormLedgerMenu.vb` (the code view) and replace the class body with the code I provided earlier for the submenu.

### 3) Update Main Menu (C) to open the submenu
In `FormMainMenu.vb`, change:

```vbnet
Case "C" : NotYet("Checks and Cash Receipts (LEDGER)")
```

(or whatever your current `Case "C"` is) to:

```vbnet
Case "C"
    Using f As New FormLedgerMenu()
        f.ShowDialog(Me)
    End Using
```

### 4) Then “Wire (9) and (0) into FormLedgerMenu”
Once `FormLedgerMenu.vb` exists, you’ll edit *that* file and replace the placeholder click handlers with the real ones:

```vbnet
AddHandler btnFindByCheck.Click,
    Sub()
        Using f As New FormFindByCheckNumber()
            f.ShowDialog(Me)
        End Using
    End Sub

AddHandler btnFindByInvoice.Click,
    Sub()
        Using f As New FormFindByInvoiceNumber()
            f.ShowDialog(Me)
        End Using
    End Sub
```

---

## Alternative if you don’t want a submenu
If you’d rather **not** add `FormLedgerMenu`, you can wire (9)/(0) by adding buttons directly onto `FormLedgerView` or by adding cases in `HandleMenuKey`. But since you said “Option 1”, the submenu form is the cleanest.

If you want, paste your current `Case "C"` block from `FormMainMenu.vb` and I’ll tell you exactly what to replace it with based on your current code.

## Copilot

Those errors almost always mean **you pasted code into the file outside the class**, or you pasted the *tool header line* (like ```vbnet name=FormLedgerMenu.vb) into the `.vb` file. VB then sees garbage at line 1 and starts reporting “Declaration expected”.

### Fix (fast)
1) Open `FormLedgerMenu.vb`
2) Delete **everything** in it
3) Paste **only** the VB code below (do not include any ``` fences, and do not include `name=...`)

Also make sure you paste it into **`FormLedgerMenu.vb`** (not the `.Designer.vb` file).

---

## Correct contents for `FormLedgerMenu.vb`
Paste this as the entire file contents:

```vbnet
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormLedgerMenu

    Private ReadOnly btnViewChecks As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnCompanyTotals As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnOtherChecks As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnFindByCheck As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnFindByInvoice As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnClose As New Button()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Ledger Menu"
        Width = 520
        Height = 420
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim flp As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown,
            .WrapContents = False,
            .AutoScroll = True,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }

        Configure(btnViewChecks, "View Checks (LEDGER.CUR)")
        Configure(btnCompanyTotals, "View Company Totals (Option 5)")
        Configure(btnOtherChecks, "View OTHER Checks (Option 8)")
        Configure(btnFindByCheck, "Find by Check # (Option 9)")
        Configure(btnFindByInvoice, "Find by Invoice # (Option 0)")
        Configure(btnClose, "Close")

        AddHandler btnViewChecks.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormLedgerView()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnCompanyTotals.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnOtherChecks.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormOtherChecksView()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnFindByCheck.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormFindByCheckNumber()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnFindByInvoice.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormFindByInvoiceNumber()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Close()

        flp.Controls.Add(btnViewChecks)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnCompanyTotals)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnOtherChecks)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnFindByCheck)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnFindByInvoice)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnClose)

        Controls.Add(flp)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub Configure(btn As Button, text As String)
        btn.Text = text
        btn.Width = 460
        btn.Height = 44
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft

        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
    End Sub

End Class
```

---

## After that: update Main Menu “C”
In `FormMainMenu.vb` → `HandleMenuKey`:

```vbnet

        f.ShowDialog(Me)
    End Using
```

5. **Find by Invoice #** → enter something like `190815` (from your sample)

The most common remaining issues are:

## @testsubject117

I published this and copied to a workstation and ran, appears to be working fine.

## Copilot

Good—then you’ve successfully shipped the Ledger submenu + all four features reading the live data files from `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data`.

Before you move on, I’d do these two quick “production hardening” tweaks so it behaves well on workstations when the share is unavailable or files are missing.

## 1) Friendly error if the share/file is missing
In each form where you read a file (`FormLedgerView`, `FormLedgerCompanyTotals`, `FormOtherChecksView`, the two Find forms), wrap reads with:

- check `IO.File.Exists(path)` and show a clear message (“Missing CHECK.INV at …”)
- `Try/Catch` around parsing (some of yours already has this)

If you want, I can give you a small helper function (`LegacyFileGuard.RequireFile(path, displayName)`) to reduce repeated code.

## 2) Handle DOS EOF marker `^Z` in CHECK.INV / OTHER.CHK (optional but recommended)
Your `CHECK.INV` ends with `^Z` (0x1A). If your parser doesn’t strip it, searches can occasionally fail on the very last record (or you’ll see a weird invoice entry). Easiest fix is to strip `ChrW(&H1A)` from lines before parsing.

If you tell me whether you already added the `^Z` stripping change in `CheckInvReader`, I’ll confirm you’re covered.

## Next step choice
What do you want to implement next from the DOS menus?
- Add **printing/export** (CSV/Excel) for any grid
- Implement **Add/Delete check** workflow (touches `INVOICE.CHK` and writes back)
- Implement **Invoice status viewer** (reads `INVOICE.CHK`)
- Add **customer lookup** (if you have a master file)

Tell me what’s next and which data files you have available.

## Copilot

Thanks—this confirms `OTHER.CHK` matches the **6-lines-per-record** reader we wrote, and it also includes the DOS EOF marker `^Z` (``) at the end, same as `CHECK.INV`.

### What this means for your code
- Your current `OtherChkReader` logic (group every 6 non-blank lines) is correct.
- We should harden it to **strip `^Z`** so the last record never gets corrupted (and to avoid a weird last “ReasonWhy” of ``).

## Patch: strip DOS EOF marker in `OtherChkReader.vb`
In `OtherChkReader.ReadAll`, when you build `s`, change:

```vbnet
Dim s = raw.Trim()
```

to:

```vbnet
Dim s = raw.Replace(ChrW(&H1A), "").Trim()  ' remove DOS EOF marker ^Z
```

That’s it.

### (Optional but good) Also strip before parsing numeric Amount
In `ParseDecimal`, add:

```vbnet
s = s.Replace(ChrW(&H1A), "")
```

## Quick behavioral check
After this change, the very last record (`"MINERVA VARGAS"` … `"LOAN"`) will parse cleanly and you won’t ever see a blank/odd last row.

---

## One more improvement (based on your data)
Some “amount” lines are whole numbers (`5`, `2000`, `100000`) and some have lots of decimals (`9.770001`, `9.229999`). Your `Decimal` parsing already handles that fine.

---

When you’re ready for the “friendly error” work, tell me which screens you want hardened first (I’d start with `CheckInvReader` + `OtherChkReader` callers), and whether you want:
- MessageBox-only, or
- a status label in the form (non-blocking) plus optional MessageBox.

## @testsubject117

you said: (Optional but good) Also strip before parsing numeric Amount
In ParseDecimal, add:

Visual Basic .NET
s = s.Replace(ChrW(&H1A), "") what file and where?

## Copilot

Add that line in the **reader class file** where the `ParseDecimal` helper is defined.

### If you mean **OTHER.CHK** (the `OtherChkReader.vb` file)
Open **`OtherChkReader.vb`**, find this function:

```vbnet
Private Shared Function ParseDecimal(s As String) As Decimal
    If s Is Nothing Then Return 0D
    s = s.Trim()

    Dim d As Decimal
    If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, d) Then Return d
    If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, d) Then Return d
    Return 0D
End Function
```

Insert the replace line **right after the Nothing-check**, before `Trim()`:

```vbnet
Private Shared Function ParseDecimal(s As String) As Decimal
    If s Is Nothing Then Return 0D
    s = s.Replace(ChrW(&H1A), "") ' remove DOS EOF marker ^Z
    s = s.Trim()

    Dim d As Decimal
    If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, d) Then Return d
    If Decimal.TryParse(s, NumberStyles.Any, CultureInfo.CurrentCulture, d) Then Return d
    Return 0D
End Function
```

### Do the same idea in **CHECK.INV** reader too (if you want)
In **`CheckInvReader.vb`**, in its `ParseDecimal` helper, add the same line.

That way, even if the last numeric field in the file accidentally carries the `^Z`, it won’t break parsing.

## @testsubject117

ok s = s.Replace(ChrW(&H1A), "") changes are done

## Copilot

Good—now both readers will safely ignore the DOS `^Z` end‑of‑file marker.

When you’re ready to do the **friendly error handling**, the cleanest approach is:

1) **Readers stay “quiet”** (they return an empty list if file missing, or throw on truly malformed content)
2) **Forms show a friendly message** if:
   - file is missing (`File.Exists` = False)
   - read throws an exception (permissions, share offline, parse error)

Tell me which one you want to tackle first:

- `FormFindByCheckNumber` / `FormFindByInvoiceNumber` (CHECK.INV)
- `FormOtherChecksView` (OTHER.CHK)
- `FormLedgerView` + `FormLedgerCompanyTotals` (LEDGER.CUR)

If you paste the current `LoadData()` (or equivalent) method from one of those forms, I’ll give you the exact drop-in version with a friendly message including the full UNC path.

## @testsubject117

looking at FormFindByCheckNumber.vb where is LoadData() located

## Copilot

In the version of **`FormFindByCheckNumber.vb`** we built, there is **no `LoadData()` method**.

That form loads `CHECK.INV` **inside `DoFind()`**, each time you click **Find** (or press Enter). Specifically, this line is doing the “load” work:

```vbnet
Dim blocks = CheckInvReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.CheckInv)
```

### Where to put the friendly error
You have two good options:

## Option A (simplest): add the friendly check inside `DoFind()`
Right before calling `CheckInvReader.ReadAll(...)`, add an exists check:

```vbnet
Dim path = LegacyDataPaths.CheckInv
If Not IO.File.Exists(path) Then
    MessageBox.Show(Me,
                    "CHECK.INV was not found at:" & Environment.NewLine &
                    path,
                    "Find by Check #",
                    MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                    MessageBoxIcon.Error)
    Return
End If
```

Then keep the `Try/Catch` you already have for permission/parse errors.

## Option B (faster repeated searches): add a LoadData() cache
If you want, we can refactor to:

- `Private _all As List(Of CheckInvBlock)`
- `LoadData()` reads once on form load (or refresh)
- `DoFind()` searches `_all` without re-reading the file

This is nicer for speed on large `CHECK.INV`.

If you want Option B, tell me and I’ll paste a complete updated `FormFindByCheckNumber.vb` (with `LoadData()`, Refresh button, and the same friendly errors).

## @testsubject117

which is better option a or b

## Copilot

**Option B is better overall** for this screen, because `CHECK.INV` can be large and users may do multiple searches in a row.

### Why Option B is better
- **Much faster after the first search** (loads once, then searches in memory)
- **Less network traffic** to `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data` (important on workstations)
- Lets you show a friendly “Loaded X checks” status and a **Refresh** button (if someone updates `CHECK.INV` while the form is open)

### When Option A is OK
- If the file is small, or users only search once
- If you want the simplest change possible (fewest code edits)

### What I recommend for your situation
Do **Option B** for:
- `FormFindByCheckNumber`
- `FormFindByInvoiceNumber`

And keep it simple (Option A style) for screens where the user typically opens once and closes (like Totals), unless you notice performance issues.

If you want, paste your current `FormFindByCheckNumber.vb` and I’ll return a drop-in replacement implementing Option B (cached `_all`, `LoadData()`, friendly missing-file message, and a Refresh button).

## @testsubject117

```
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormFindByCheckNumber

    Private ReadOnly txtCheck As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly btnFind As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly lblHeader As New Label()
    Private ReadOnly lstInvoices As New ListBox()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Find by Check # (CHECK.INV)"
        Width = 700
        Height = 650
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim top As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Top, .Height = 45, .Padding = New Padding(10)}
        top.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.Text = "Check #:", .AutoSize = True, .Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)})
        txtCheck.Width = 150
        top.Controls.Add(txtCheck)
        btnFind.Text = "Find"
        top.Controls.Add(btnFind)

        lblHeader.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lblHeader.Height = 50
        lblHeader.Padding = New Padding(10)
        lblHeader.Text = "Enter a check # and click Find."

        lstInvoices.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

        Controls.Add(lstInvoices)
        Controls.Add(lblHeader)
        Controls.Add(top)

        AddHandler btnFind.Click, Sub() DoFind()
        AddHandler txtCheck.KeyDown,
            Sub(sender, args)
                If args.KeyCode = Keys.Enter Then
                    args.SuppressKeyPress = True
                    DoFind()
                End If
            End Sub
    End Sub

    Private Sub DoFind()
        Dim checkNo = txtCheck.Text.Trim()
        If checkNo = "" Then Return

        Try
            Dim blocks = CheckInvReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.CheckInv)
            Dim hit = blocks.FirstOrDefault(Function(b) String.Equals(If(b.CheckNumber, "").Trim(), checkNo, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))

            lstInvoices.Items.Clear()

            If hit Is Nothing Then
                lblHeader.Text = $"No match for check #{checkNo}."
                Return
            End If

            lblHeader.Text = $"Customer: {hit.CustomerCode}    Check#: {hit.CheckNumber}    Date: {hit.DateText}    Amount: {hit.Amount:C}    Invoices: {hit.InvoiceCount}"

            For Each inv In hit.Invoices
                lstInvoices.Items.Add(inv)
            Next
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show(Me, ex.Message, "Find by Check #", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

## Copilot

Here’s a drop-in **Option B (cached LoadData + friendly errors + Refresh)** version of your form. Replace the entire contents of `FormFindByCheckNumber.vb` with this.

Key changes:
- Loads `CHECK.INV` once into `_all` (on open, and via Refresh).
- Friendly message if the file is missing.
- `DoFind()` searches `_all` (fast).
- Shows “Loaded N checks…” status in `lblHeader`.

```vbnet name=FormFindByCheckNumber.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO
Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormFindByCheckNumber

    Private ReadOnly txtCheck As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly btnFind As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnRefresh As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly lblHeader As New Label()
    Private ReadOnly lstInvoices As New ListBox()

    Private _all As List(Of CheckInvBlock) = New List(Of CheckInvBlock)()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Find by Check # (CHECK.INV)"
        Width = 700
        Height = 650
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim top As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Top, .Height = 45, .Padding = New Padding(10)}

        top.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.Text = "Check #:", .AutoSize = True, .Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)})

        txtCheck.Width = 150
        top.Controls.Add(txtCheck)

        btnFind.Text = "Find"
        top.Controls.Add(btnFind)

        btnRefresh.Text = "Refresh"
        top.Controls.Add(btnRefresh)

        lblHeader.Dock = DockStyle.Top
        lblHeader.Height = 60
        lblHeader.Padding = New Padding(10)
        lblHeader.Text = "Loading CHECK.INV..."

        lstInvoices.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

        Controls.Add(lstInvoices)
        Controls.Add(lblHeader)
        Controls.Add(top)

        AddHandler btnFind.Click, Sub() DoFind()

        AddHandler btnRefresh.Click,
            Sub()
                LoadData()
                lstInvoices.Items.Clear()
            End Sub

        AddHandler txtCheck.KeyDown,
            Sub(sender, args)
                If args.KeyCode = Keys.Enter Then
                    args.SuppressKeyPress = True
                    DoFind()
                End If
            End Sub

        LoadData()
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadData()
        Dim path = LegacyDataPaths.CheckInv

        Try
            If Not File.Exists(path) Then
                _all = New List(Of CheckInvBlock)()
                lblHeader.Text =
                    "CHECK.INV was not found at:" & Environment.NewLine &
                    path
                Return
            End If

            _all = CheckInvReader.ReadAll(path)

            lblHeader.Text = $"Loaded {_all.Count:N0} check records from CHECK.INV."
        Catch ex As Exception
            _all = New List(Of CheckInvBlock)()
            lblHeader.Text =
                "Unable to read CHECK.INV from:" & Environment.NewLine &
                path

            MessageBox.Show(Me,
                            "Unable to read CHECK.INV from:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            path & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                            ex.Message,
                            "Find by Check #",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                            MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub DoFind()
        Dim checkNo = txtCheck.Text.Trim()
        If checkNo = "" Then Return

        If _all Is Nothing OrElse _all.Count = 0 Then
            ' If the user opens the form when the share is down, allow them to try again later.
            LoadData()
            If _all Is Nothing OrElse _all.Count = 0 Then Return
        End If

        Dim hit = _all.FirstOrDefault(
            Function(b) String.Equals(If(b.CheckNumber, "").Trim(),
                                      checkNo,
                                      StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
        )

        lstInvoices.Items.Clear()

        If hit Is Nothing Then
            lblHeader.Text = $"No match for check #{checkNo}."
            Return
        End If

        lblHeader.Text =
            $"Customer: {hit.CustomerCode}    " &
            $"Check#: {hit.CheckNumber}    " &
            $"Date: {hit.DateText}    " &
            $"Amount: {hit.Amount:C}    " &
            $"Invoices: {hit.InvoiceCount}"

        If hit.Invoices IsNot Nothing Then
            For Each inv In hit.Invoices
                lstInvoices.Items.Add(inv)
            Next
        End If
    End Sub

End Class
```

If you want, I can provide the same caching + friendly error pattern for `FormFindByInvoiceNumber.vb` too (it’s basically the same `_all` list + search by invoice).

## @testsubject117

yes do the same for FormFindByInvoiceNumber.vb

## Copilot

```vbnet name=FormFindByInvoiceNumber.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.IO
Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormFindByInvoiceNumber

    Private ReadOnly txtInvoice As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly btnFind As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnRefresh As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly lblResult As New Label()

    Private _all As List(Of CheckInvBlock) = New List(Of CheckInvBlock)()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Find by Invoice # (CHECK.INV)"
        Width = 750
        Height = 320
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim top As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Top, .Height = 45, .Padding = New Padding(10)}
        top.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.Text = "Invoice #:", .AutoSize = True, .Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)})

        txtInvoice.Width = 170
        top.Controls.Add(txtInvoice)

        btnFind.Text = "Find"
        top.Controls.Add(btnFind)

        btnRefresh.Text = "Refresh"
        top.Controls.Add(btnRefresh)

        lblResult.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblResult.Padding = New Padding(10)
        lblResult.Text = "Loading CHECK.INV..."

        Controls.Add(lblResult)
        Controls.Add(top)

        AddHandler btnFind.Click, Sub() DoFind()

        AddHandler btnRefresh.Click,
            Sub()
                LoadData()
            End Sub

        AddHandler txtInvoice.KeyDown,
            Sub(sender, args)
                If args.KeyCode = Keys.Enter Then
                    args.SuppressKeyPress = True
                    DoFind()
                End If
            End Sub

        LoadData()
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadData()
        Dim path = LegacyDataPaths.CheckInv

        Try
            If Not File.Exists(path) Then
                _all = New List(Of CheckInvBlock)()
                lblResult.Text =
                    "CHECK.INV was not found at:" & Environment.NewLine &
                    path
                Return
            End If

            _all = CheckInvReader.ReadAll(path)
            lblResult.Text = $"Loaded {_all.Count:N0} check records from CHECK.INV."
        Catch ex As Exception
            _all = New List(Of CheckInvBlock)()
            lblResult.Text =
                "Unable to read CHECK.INV from:" & Environment.NewLine &
                path

            MessageBox.Show(Me,
                            "Unable to read CHECK.INV from:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            path & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                            ex.Message,
                            "Find by Invoice #",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                            MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub DoFind()
        Dim invNo = txtInvoice.Text.Trim()
        If invNo = "" Then Return

        If _all Is Nothing OrElse _all.Count = 0 Then
            LoadData()
            If _all Is Nothing OrElse _all.Count = 0 Then Return
        End If

        Dim hit = _all.FirstOrDefault(
            Function(b) b.Invoices IsNot Nothing AndAlso
                        b.Invoices.Any(Function(x) String.Equals(x.Trim(), invNo, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        )

        If hit Is Nothing Then
            lblResult.Text = $"No match for invoice #{invNo}."
            Return
        End If

        lblResult.Text =
            $"Invoice {invNo} was paid by:" & Environment.NewLine &
            $"Customer: {hit.CustomerCode}" & Environment.NewLine &
            $"Check #:  {hit.CheckNumber}" & Environment.NewLine &
            $"Date:     {hit.DateText}" & Environment.NewLine &
            $"Amount:   {hit.Amount:C}" & Environment.NewLine &
            $"Invoices on that check: {hit.InvoiceCount}"
    End Sub

End Class
```

## @testsubject117

Severity	Code	Description	Project	File	Line	Suppression State	Details
Warning (active)	BC40054	'Public Sub New()' in designer-generated type 'FormLedgerCompanyTotals' should call InitializeComponent method.	AMiOffice	C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb	12		


## Copilot

That warning is because `FormLedgerCompanyTotals` was created as a **Windows Form**, but we’re building the UI in code in `Sub New()` and **never calling `InitializeComponent()`** (the designer normally expects that call).

You have 2 good fixes:

## Fix 1 (recommended): call `InitializeComponent()` and keep your dynamic UI
Open `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb` and change your constructor to call it first:

```vbnet
Public Sub New()
    InitializeComponent()   ' add this line

    Text = "Ledger - Company Totals"
    Width = 900
    Height = 700
    StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
    ' ... rest of your existing code ...
End Sub
```

This will satisfy the designer expectation and keep everything working.

## Fix 2: suppress the warning by not having a custom constructor
Instead of using `Sub New()`, move your UI-building code into `OnLoad` (or `OnShown`) and delete the constructor. That also removes the warning, but it’s more editing.

---

### Why Fix 1 is safe
Even if your designer file is basically empty, `InitializeComponent()` will exist and just set up the form basics. Your code that adds controls still runs right after.

If you want, paste your current `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb` and I’ll show the exact edited version with `InitializeComponent()` placed correctly.

## @testsubject117

```
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormLedgerCompanyTotals

    Private ReadOnly dgv As New DataGridView()
    Private ReadOnly lblGrandTotal As New Label()
    Private ReadOnly btnClose As New Button()

    Public Sub New()
        InitializeComponent()
        Text = "Ledger - Company Totals"
        Width = 900
        Height = 700
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim top As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Top, .Height = 40}
        lblGrandTotal.AutoSize = True
        lblGrandTotal.Padding = New Padding(10, 10, 0, 0)
        top.Controls.Add(lblGrandTotal)

        dgv.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        dgv.ReadOnly = True
        dgv.AllowUserToAddRows = False
        dgv.AllowUserToDeleteRows = False
        dgv.AutoGenerateColumns = True
        dgv.SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect

        btnClose.Text = "Close"
        btnClose.Dock = DockStyle.Bottom
        btnClose.Height = 40
        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Close()

        Controls.Add(dgv)
        Controls.Add(btnClose)
        Controls.Add(top)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnShown(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnShown(e)

        Try
            Dim entries = LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur)

            Dim totals =
                entries.
                GroupBy(Function(x) (If(x.Customer, "")).Trim()).
                Select(Function(g) New With {
                    .Customer = g.Key,
                    .Total = g.Sum(Function(x) x.Amount),
                    .Count = g.Count()
                }).
                OrderBy(Function(x) x.Customer).
                ToList()

            dgv.DataSource = totals

            Dim grand = totals.Sum(Function(x) x.Total)
            lblGrandTotal.Text = $"Grand total: {grand:C}   (Customers: {totals.Count})"
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show(Me, ex.Message, "Company Totals", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

End Class
```

## Copilot

You already fixed the warning the right way by adding `InitializeComponent()` in `Sub New()`.

If you’re **still** seeing BC40054 after this change, it usually means one of these is true:

1) You edited `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb`, but the project is still compiling an **older copy** (build artifacts), or  
2) There is **another `Public Sub New()`** (maybe in a partial class file), or  
3) `InitializeComponent()` is not being recognized because the form isn’t set up as a designer form (rare), or you pasted into the wrong file earlier.

### Quick checks
1) **Clean + Rebuild**
- Build → **Clean Solution**
- Build → **Rebuild Solution**

2) Confirm there is only one constructor
Use Visual Studio “Find in Files” for:

- `Class FormLedgerCompanyTotals`
- `Sub New`

There should be exactly one `Public Sub New()` (the one you showed).

3) Confirm the designer partial exists
In Solution Explorer, expand `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb`. You should see:
- `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.Designer.vb`

And inside that designer file there should be something like:

```vbnet
<Global.Microsoft.VisualBasic.CompilerServices.DesignerGenerated()>
Partial Class FormLedgerCompanyTotals
    Inherits System.Windows.Forms.Form

    Private components As System.ComponentModel.IContainer

    <System.Diagnostics.DebuggerStepThrough()>
    Private Sub InitializeComponent()
        Me.components = New System.ComponentModel.Container()
        ' ...
    End Sub
End Class
```

If `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.Designer.vb` is missing (or `InitializeComponent()` isn’t there), tell me—that’s the one scenario where the warning can persist or turn into an error.

### Small improvement (optional, not related to the warning)
Since you’re building controls manually, you can also add:

```vbnet
top.Padding = New Padding(0)
top.BackColor = Color.Black
lblGrandTotal.ForeColor = Color.White
```

But functionally you’re good.

If you paste the **current warning line number** (it said line 12 earlier) and confirm whether `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.Designer.vb` exists, I can tell you exactly why the warning is still being reported (if it is).

## Copilot

Perfect—so `LEDGER.CUR` is loaded in **two places**:

- `FormLedgerView.vb` (loads into `_all`)
- `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb` (loads into `entries`)

To add “friendly errors”, we’ll update both forms in the same way we did for the Find forms:
1) `If Not File.Exists(path)` → show a clear message with the full path and return
2) `Try/Catch` around `LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(path)` with a friendly message

Below are the exact edits.

---

## 1) Harden `FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb`
Add `Imports System.IO` at the top.

Then replace your `OnShown` with this version:

```vbnet
Protected Overrides Sub OnShown(e As EventArgs)
    MyBase.OnShown(e)

    Dim path = LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur

    If Not File.Exists(path) Then
        dgv.DataSource = Nothing
        lblGrandTotal.Text = "LEDGER.CUR was not found at: " & path
        MessageBox.Show(Me,
                        "LEDGER.CUR was not found at:" & Environment.NewLine & path,
                        "Company Totals",
                        MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                        MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        Return
    End If

    Try
        Dim entries = LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(path)

        Dim totals =
            entries.
            GroupBy(Function(x) (If(x.Customer, "")).Trim()).
            Select(Function(g) New With {
                .Customer = g.Key,
                .Total = g.Sum(Function(x) x.Amount),
                .Count = g.Count()
            }).
            OrderBy(Function(x) x.Customer).
            ToList()

        dgv.DataSource = totals

        Dim grand = totals.Sum(Function(x) x.Total)
        lblGrandTotal.Text = $"Grand total: {grand:C}   (Customers: {totals.Count})"
    Catch ex As Exception
        dgv.DataSource = Nothing
        lblGrandTotal.Text = "Unable to read LEDGER.CUR from: " & path

        MessageBox.Show(Me,
                        "Unable to read LEDGER.CUR from:" & Environment.NewLine & path &
                        Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                        ex.Message,
                        "Company Totals",
                        MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                        MessageBoxIcon.Error)
    End Try
End Sub
```

---

## 2) Harden `FormLedgerView.vb`
I need to see your current `FormLedgerView.vb` load method to give you an exact patch (because I don’t know if you’re loading in `OnShown`, `OnLoad`, or a `LoadData()` method and what controls you have).

But the key change is: wherever you have:

```vbnet
_all.AddRange(LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur))
```

change to the same pattern:

```vbnet
Dim path = LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur
If Not IO.File.Exists(path) Then
    ' clear grid/list, show message, Return
End If

Try
    _all.Clear()
    _all.AddRange(LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(path))
Catch ex As Exception
    ' clear grid/list, show friendly message
End Try
```

- graceful clearing of the grid

## @testsubject117

```
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Linq

Public Class FormLedgerView

    Private ReadOnly _all As New List(Of LedgerEntry)()

    ' UI controls (created in code so you don’t have to design it yet)
    Private ReadOnly txtCustomer As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly txtCheck As New TextBox()
    Private ReadOnly cmbYear As New ComboBox()
    Private ReadOnly cmbMonth As New ComboBox()
    Private ReadOnly btnRefresh As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly dgv As New DataGridView()
    Private ReadOnly lblTotal As New Label()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Ledger - View Checks"
        Width = 1100
        Height = 750
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        BuildUi()
        PopulatePicklists()

        LoadData()
        ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildUi()
        Dim topPanel As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Top,
            .Height = 70,
            .ColumnCount = 9,
            .RowCount = 2
        }

        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Customer lbl
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 35)) ' Customer txt
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Check lbl
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 20)) ' Check txt
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Year lbl
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 15)) ' Year cmb
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Month lbl
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 15)) ' Month cmb
        topPanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Refresh btn

        Dim lblCustomer As New Label() With {.Text = "Customer:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}
        Dim lblCheck As New Label() With {.Text = "Check #:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}
        Dim lblYear As New Label() With {.Text = "Year:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}
        Dim lblMonth As New Label() With {.Text = "Month:", .AutoSize = True, .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left}

        txtCustomer.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        txtCheck.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

        cmbYear.DropDownStyle = ComboBoxStyle.DropDownList
        cmbMonth.DropDownStyle = ComboBoxStyle.DropDownList
        cmbYear.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        cmbMonth.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

        btnRefresh.Text = "Refresh"
        btnRefresh.AutoSize = True
        btnRefresh.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Left

        lblTotal.Text = "Total: $0.00"
        lblTotal.AutoSize = True
        lblTotal.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblTotal.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft

        dgv.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        dgv.ReadOnly = True
        dgv.AllowUserToAddRows = False
        dgv.AllowUserToDeleteRows = False
        dgv.AutoGenerateColumns = True
        dgv.SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect

        ' Row 0: filters
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblCustomer, 0, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(txtCustomer, 1, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblCheck, 2, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(txtCheck, 3, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblYear, 4, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(cmbYear, 5, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblMonth, 6, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(cmbMonth, 7, 0)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(btnRefresh, 8, 0)

        ' Row 1: total
        topPanel.SetColumnSpan(lblTotal, 9)
        topPanel.Controls.Add(lblTotal, 0, 1)

        Controls.Add(dgv)
        Controls.Add(topPanel)

        AddHandler btnRefresh.Click,
            Sub()
                LoadData()
                ApplyFilters()
            End Sub
        AddHandler txtCustomer.TextChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
        AddHandler txtCheck.TextChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
        AddHandler cmbYear.SelectedIndexChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
        AddHandler cmbMonth.SelectedIndexChanged, Sub() ApplyFilters()
    End Sub

    Private Sub PopulatePicklists()
        cmbMonth.Items.Clear()
        cmbMonth.Items.Add("") ' all
        For m As Integer = 1 To 12
            cmbMonth.Items.Add(m.ToString("00"))
        Next
        cmbMonth.SelectedIndex = 0

        cmbYear.Items.Clear()
        cmbYear.Items.Add("") ' all
        ' We don’t know min/max upfront; just provide a reasonable range + all.
        For y As Integer = Date.Today.Year To 1986 Step -1
            cmbYear.Items.Add(y.ToString())
        Next
        cmbYear.SelectedIndex = 0
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadData()
        Try
            _all.Clear()
            _all.AddRange(LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur))
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show(Me,
                        "Unable to read LEDGER.CUR from:" & Environment.NewLine &
                        LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                        ex.Message,
                        "Ledger", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub ApplyFilters()
        Dim cust As String = txtCustomer.Text.Trim()
        Dim chk As String = txtCheck.Text.Trim()
        Dim year As String = If(TryCast(cmbYear.SelectedItem, String), "").Trim()
        Dim month As String = If(TryCast(cmbMonth.SelectedItem, String), "").Trim()

        Dim q = _all.AsEnumerable()

        If cust <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(e) If(e.Customer, "").IndexOf(cust, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
        End If

        If chk <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(e) If(e.CheckNumber, "").IndexOf(chk, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) >= 0)
        End If

        If year <> "" Then
            Dim yy As String = year.Substring(year.Length - 2) ' DOS compared last 2 digits
            q = q.Where(Function(e) If(e.DateText, "").EndsWith(yy, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If

        If month <> "" Then
            q = q.Where(Function(e) If(e.DateText, "").StartsWith(month, StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
        End If

        Dim list = q.ToList()
        dgv.DataSource = list

        Dim total As Decimal = list.Sum(Function(e) e.Amount)
        lblTotal.Text = $"Total: {total:C}"
    End Sub

End Class
```
here's the entire contents of FormLedgerView.vb: 

## Copilot

Update **only `FormLedgerView.vb`**: add `Imports System.IO` and replace your `LoadData()` with a friendlier version that (a) checks for missing file, (b) clears the grid + total, and (c) avoids leaving stale data on screen.

### 1) Add this import
At the top (with the others):

```vbnet
Imports System.IO
```

### 2) Replace `LoadData()` with this
```vbnet
Private Sub LoadData()
    Dim path = LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur

    ' Clear current view so we never display stale data if the file is missing/unreadable.
    _all.Clear()
    dgv.DataSource = Nothing
    lblTotal.Text = "Total: $0.00"

    If Not File.Exists(path) Then
        MessageBox.Show(Me,
                        "LEDGER.CUR was not found at:" & Environment.NewLine &
                        path,
                        "Ledger - View Checks",
                        MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                        MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        Return
    End If

    Try
        _all.AddRange(LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(path))
    Catch ex As Exception
        MessageBox.Show(Me,
                        "Unable to read LEDGER.CUR from:" & Environment.NewLine &
                        path & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                        ex.Message,
                        "Ledger - View Checks",
                        MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                        MessageBoxIcon.Error)
    End Try
End Sub
```

That’s all you need for “friendly error” on the `LEDGER.CUR` view.

### Optional improvement (recommended)
Right now, if `LoadData()` fails, `ApplyFilters()` still runs (because you call `ApplyFilters()` after `LoadData()` in `OnLoad` and in Refresh). That’s fine, but if you want the user to *see* that there are no rows, you can add this at the end of `LoadData()`:

```vbnet
dgv.DataSource = _all.ToList()
lblTotal.Text = $"Total: {_all.Sum(Function(e) e.Amount):C}"
```

…but it’s optional since `ApplyFilters()` already sets the grid.

If you want, paste `LedgerCurReader.vb` next and we can add the same `^Z` stripping + “line number in parse error” improvements there too.

## @testsubject117

Severity	Code	Description	Project	File	Line	Suppression State	Details
Error (active)	BC30451	'File' is not declared. It may be inaccessible due to its protection level.	AMiOffice	C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\FormLedgerView.vb	135		


## Copilot

That error means VB can’t see `System.IO.File` in that file. Easiest fix: **fully-qualify it** (no import needed).

In `FormLedgerView.vb`, change:

```vbnet
If Not File.Exists(path) Then
```

to:

```vbnet
If Not System.IO.File.Exists(path) Then
```

That will compile regardless of imports.

(Alternative: add `Imports System.IO` at the top of `FormLedgerView.vb`, but the fully-qualified version is the most foolproof.)

After changing it, rebuild and it should be gone.

## Copilot

Apply this to **`FormLedgerCompanyTotals.vb`**: it will (1) set the label to a friendly message, (2) clear the grid, and (3) show a MessageBox with the full path.

### Replace `OnShown` with this

```vbnet
Protected Overrides Sub OnShown(e As EventArgs)
    MyBase.OnShown(e)

    Dim path = LegacyDataPaths.LedgerCur

    ' Clear view first so we never display stale totals if load fails.
    dgv.DataSource = Nothing
    lblGrandTotal.Text = "Grand total: $0.00"

    If Not System.IO.File.Exists(path) Then
        lblGrandTotal.Text = "LEDGER.CUR was not found at: " & path
        MessageBox.Show(Me,
                        "LEDGER.CUR was not found at:" & Environment.NewLine &
                        path,
                        "Company Totals",
                        MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                        MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        Return
    End If

    Try
        Dim entries = LedgerCurReader.ReadAll(path)

        Dim totals =
            entries.
            GroupBy(Function(x) (If(x.Customer, "")).Trim()).
            Select(Function(g) New With {
                .Customer = g.Key,
                .Total = g.Sum(Function(x) x.Amount),
                .Count = g.Count()
            }).
            OrderBy(Function(x) x.Customer).
            ToList()

        dgv.DataSource = totals

        Dim grand = totals.Sum(Function(x) x.Total)
        lblGrandTotal.Text = $"Grand total: {grand:C}   (Customers: {totals.Count})"
    Catch ex As Exception
        dgv.DataSource = Nothing
        lblGrandTotal.Text = "Unable to read LEDGER.CUR from: " & path

        MessageBox.Show(Me,
                        "Unable to read LEDGER.CUR from:" & Environment.NewLine &
                        path & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                        ex.Message,
                        "Company Totals",
                        MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                        MessageBoxIcon.Error)
    End Try
End Sub
```

That should compile without adding imports because it uses `System.IO.File.Exists`.

If you want the same “friendly error” for `OTHER.CHK` next, paste your `FormOtherChecksView.vb` (or wherever it loads `OtherChkReader.ReadAll`).
