```
  <BuildNumber>$([MSBuild]::Add($(BuildNumber), 1))</BuildNumber>
</PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="$(BuildNumber)" />
  </Target>

  <Target Name="GenerateBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildCounterFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\build-counter.txt</BuildCounterFile>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      Condition="!Exists('$(BuildCounterFile)')"
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="0" />

    <ReadLinesFromFile File="$(BuildCounterFile)">
      <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildCounterLines2" />
    </ReadLinesFromFile>

    <!-- Convert first line item into a real property -->
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.String]::Copy('%(BuildCounterLines2.Identity)'))</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>
    <Message Text="GenerateBuildInfo: BuildCounterFile=$(BuildCounterFile) BuildNumber=$(BuildNumber)" Importance="high" />
    <!-- Trim + fallback -->
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.String]::Copy('$(BuildNumber)').Trim())</BuildNumber>
      <BuildNumber Condition="'$(BuildNumber)'==''">0</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <MakeDir Directories="$([System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName('$(BuildInfoFile)'))" />

    <WriteLinesToFile
      File="$(BuildInfoFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="Option Strict On&#xD;&#xA;Option Explicit On&#xD;&#xA;&#xD;&#xA;Friend Module BuildInfo&#xD;&#xA;    Friend Const ProductVersion As String = &quot;$(ProductVersion)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;    Friend Const BuildNumber As String = &quot;$(BuildNumber)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;&#xD;&#xA;    Friend ReadOnly Property DisplayVersion As String&#xD;&#xA;        Get&#xD;&#xA;            Return &quot;AMiOffice v&quot; &amp; ProductVersion &amp; &quot; (Build &quot; &amp; BuildNumber &amp; &quot;)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;        End Get&#xD;&#xA;    End Property&#xD;&#xA;End Module&#xD;&#xA;" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Remove="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
      <Compile Include="$(BuildInfoFile)" Visible="false" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

</Project>
```

## Copilot

You *do* have versioning set up already—your `.vbproj` just has it **twice**, and one of the two sets will “win” depending on ordering/evaluation. You should consolidate to **one** place to avoid confusion.

## What to change

### 1) Remove the duplicate `Version/AssemblyVersion/FileVersion` set
Right now you have:

- In the **first** `<PropertyGroup>`:
```xml
<AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
<FileVersion>1.0.0.0</FileVersion>
<Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
<ProductVersion>1.0</ProductVersion>
```

- Then a **second** `<PropertyGroup>` with:
```xml
<Version>2026.4.23</Version>
<AssemblyVersion>2026.4.23.0</AssemblyVersion>
<FileVersion>2026.4.23.0</FileVersion>
```

Keep **one** set only. Since you’re already using a date-based scheme, I’d keep the **2026.4.23** values and delete the earlier `1.0.0.0` ones (and ideally update `ProductVersion` too—see next).

### 2) Make `ProductVersion` match `Version`
You’re building `InformationalVersion` from `$(ProductVersion)` + build number, but `ProductVersion` is currently `1.0`. That means your “About”/details may show inconsistent versions.

Set:

```xml
<ProductVersion>$(Version)</ProductVersion>
<InformationalVersion>$(Version) (Build $(BuildNumber))</InformationalVersion>
```

That way:
- Windows file “Product version” tracks your `Version`
- “Informational version” shows `2026.4.23 (Build N)`

## Suggested cleaned-up version section (drop-in)

Replace **both** of your current version blocks with this **single** block inside the first `<PropertyGroup>` (and then delete the second `<PropertyGroup>` entirely):

```xml
    <!-- Versioning -->
    <Version>2026.4.23</Version>
    <AssemblyVersion>2026.4.23.0</AssemblyVersion>
    <FileVersion>2026.4.23.0</FileVersion>

    <!-- Use Version as the human-facing product version too -->
    <ProductVersion>$(Version)</ProductVersion>

    <!-- Keep ONLY ONE InformationalVersion -->
    <InformationalVersion>$(Version) (Build $(BuildNumber))</InformationalVersion>
```

And remove these older lines from that same PropertyGroup:
```xml
    <AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
    <FileVersion>1.0.0.0</FileVersion>
    <Version>1.0.0.0</Version>
    <ProductVersion>1.0</ProductVersion>
    <InformationalVersion>$(ProductVersion) (Build $(BuildNumber))</InformationalVersion>
```

## After you change it
1) Rebuild + Publish once
2) Check the deployed EXE:
   - Properties → Details
   - Confirm **File version** = `2026.4.23.0`
   - Confirm **Product version** = `2026.4.23`
   - If you display `BuildInfo.DisplayVersion`, confirm it shows `AMiOffice v2026.4.23 (Build N)`

If you want, tell me what you *want* to display to users (just date, date+build, or semantic version), and I’ll recommend the cleanest scheme using the build counter you already have.

```xml name=AMiOffice.vbproj
      <DesignTime>True</DesignTime>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Resources.resx</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>

    <Compile Update="My Project\Settings.Designer.vb">
      <DesignTimeSharedInput>True</DesignTimeSharedInput>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Settings.settings</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <EmbeddedResource Update="My Project\Resources.resx">
      <CustomToolNamespace>My.Resources</CustomToolNamespace>
      <Generator>VbMyResourcesResXFileCodeGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Resources.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </EmbeddedResource>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <None Update="My Project\Application.myapp">
      <Generator>MyApplicationCodeGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Application.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </None>

    <None Update="My Project\Settings.settings">
      <CustomToolNamespace>My</CustomToolNamespace>
      <Generator>SettingsSingleFileGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Settings.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </None>
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- Prevent compiling any stray BuildInfo file from the project root -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Remove="BuildInfo.g.vb" />
    <Compile Remove="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <Target Name="IncrementBuildCounter" BeforeTargets="Publish">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildCounterFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\build-counter.txt</BuildCounterFile>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      Condition="!Exists('$(BuildCounterFile)')"
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="0" />

    <ReadLinesFromFile File="$(BuildCounterFile)">
      <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildCounterLines" />
    </ReadLinesFromFile>

    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.Int32]::Parse('%(BuildCounterLines.Identity)'))</BuildNumber>
      <BuildNumber>$([MSBuild]::Add($(BuildNumber), 1))</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="$(BuildNumber)" />
  </Target>

  <Target Name="GenerateBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildCounterFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\build-counter.txt</BuildCounterFile>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      Condition="!Exists('$(BuildCounterFile)')"
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="0" />

    <ReadLinesFromFile File="$(BuildCounterFile)">
      <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildCounterLines2" />
    </ReadLinesFromFile>

    <!-- Convert first line item into a real property -->
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.String]::Copy('%(BuildCounterLines2.Identity)'))</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>
    <Message Text="GenerateBuildInfo: BuildCounterFile=$(BuildCounterFile) BuildNumber=$(BuildNumber)" Importance="high" />

    <!-- Trim + fallback -->
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.String]::Copy('$(BuildNumber)').Trim())</BuildNumber>
      <BuildNumber Condition="'$(BuildNumber)'==''">0</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <MakeDir Directories="$([System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName('$(BuildInfoFile)'))" />

    <WriteLinesToFile
      File="$(BuildInfoFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="Option Strict On&#xD;&#xA;Option Explicit On&#xD;&#xA;&#xD;&#xA;Friend Module BuildInfo&#xD;&#xA;    Friend Const ProductVersion As String = &quot;$(ProductVersion)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;    Friend Const BuildNumber As String = &quot;$(BuildNumber)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;&#xD;&#xA;    Friend ReadOnly Property DisplayVersion As String&#xD;&#xA;        Get&#xD;&#xA;            Return &quot;AMiOffice v&quot; &amp; ProductVersion &amp; &quot; (Build &quot; &amp; BuildNumber &amp; &quot;)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;        End Get&#xD;&#xA;    End Property&#xD;&#xA;End Module&#xD;&#xA;" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Remove="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
      <Compile Include="$(BuildInfoFile)" Visible="false" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

</Project>
```

## @testsubject117

you changed my versioning from v1.0 to v2026.4.23. If I leave your changes in place, when will the version next be updated?

## Copilot

It won’t update automatically.

With the `.vbproj` I gave you, these three values are **hard-coded**:

```xml
<Version>2026.4.23</Version>
<AssemblyVersion>2026.4.23.0</AssemblyVersion>
<FileVersion>2026.4.23.0</FileVersion>
```

So the app will stay at **2026.4.23 / 2026.4.23.0** until **you change them again** (manually) in the project file.

What *does* auto-increment in your project today is only the **BuildNumber** (via your `build-counter.txt` + `IncrementBuildCounter` target that runs **BeforeTargets="Publish"**). That affects:

```xml
<InformationalVersion>$(Version) (Build $(BuildNumber))</InformationalVersion>
```

So on each **Publish**, you should see something like:
- `2026.4.23 (Build 17)`, then next publish `2026.4.23 (Build 18)`, etc.

### If you want to keep “v1.0” and still get auto updates
You can keep:
- `<Version>1.0.0</Version>` (or `1.0.0.0`)
…and still get “Build N” changing on every publish via `InformationalVersion`.

If you tell me what you want users to see:
- **Option A:** `v1.0 (Build 123)` forever
- **Option B:** `v1.0.1 (Build 123)` (semver, manual bump)
- **Option C:** `v2026.04.23 (Build 123)` (date-based, manual bump)

…I’ll give you the exact `.vbproj` snippet for that, with no duplicates and predictable behavior.

## @testsubject117

Option B

## Copilot

For **Option B (semver)**: nothing will bump automatically unless you add a versioning step. With your current setup, the **BuildNumber auto-increments on Publish**, but `Version / AssemblyVersion / FileVersion` do **not**.

### Recommended semver behavior (simple, reliable)
- You **manually** bump `Version` (e.g., `1.0.1`) when you want a new “release”
- `AssemblyVersion` stays **stable** (usually `1.0.0.0` or `1.0.0.0` for the major line) to avoid binding surprises
- `FileVersion` and `InformationalVersion` include the build counter so each publish is uniquely identifiable

#### Use this in `AMiOffice.vbproj` (single source of truth)
Set these properties in your main `<PropertyGroup>`:

```xml
<!-- Semver: bump Version manually when you release -->
<Version>1.0.1</Version>

<!-- Keep AssemblyVersion stable within a major/minor line -->
<AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>

<!-- Unique per publish (BuildNumber increments BeforeTargets="Publish") -->
<FileVersion>1.0.1.$(BuildNumber)</FileVersion>

<!-- What you show to humans -->
<ProductVersion>$(Version)</ProductVersion>
<InformationalVersion>$(Version) (Build $(BuildNumber))</InformationalVersion>
```

### When will it update next?
- **Every time you Publish:** `BuildNumber` increments ⇒ `FileVersion` last component changes, and `InformationalVersion` changes.
- **Only when you edit the project file:** `Version` changes (e.g., from `1.0.1` to `1.0.2`).

### One detail to confirm
Do you want `AssemblyVersion` to change when you bump minor/major (e.g., 1.1 or 2.0), or do you want it *always* pinned at `1.0.0.0`?  
Typical approach:
- bump `AssemblyVersion` only on **major** releases.

If you answer that, I can regenerate the **full** `AMiOffice.vbproj` again with semver Option B and no duplicate version fields.

## @testsubject117

what is the standard and which is better?

## Copilot

The most common “standard” in modern .NET apps is:

- **AssemblyVersion**: changes **rarely** (often only on *major* releases)
- **FileVersion**: changes **every build/release** (so you can identify exactly what EXE is deployed)
- **InformationalVersion**: the “marketing”/human version (often semver + build metadata)

That’s also usually the **better** approach, because it avoids hard-to-debug assembly binding issues while still giving you precise traceability.

## Why keep `AssemblyVersion` stable?
`AssemblyVersion` is what .NET uses for assembly identity and binding. If it changes frequently, you can run into situations where something built against an older assembly version won’t load without redirects (more common with libraries, but still a good habit).

Even though you’re deploying a WinForms EXE by copying files and likely don’t have lots of third-party consumers, the “stable AssemblyVersion” pattern is still the safest default.

## What most teams do (recommended for you)
### Recommended “better” scheme
- **Version (semver)**: you bump manually when you decide it’s a new release (1.0.1, 1.0.2, 1.1.0, 2.0.0, etc.)
- **AssemblyVersion**: keep at `1.0.0.0` until you do a major breaking change (then 2.0.0.0)
- **FileVersion**: include your publish build counter so each publish is unique
- **InformationalVersion**: show semver + build counter

Example:

```xml
<Version>1.0.1</Version>
<AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
<FileVersion>1.0.1.$(BuildNumber)</FileVersion>
<ProductVersion>$(Version)</ProductVersion>
<InformationalVersion>$(Version) (Build $(BuildNumber))</InformationalVersion>
```

### Pros
- Users see a clean semver like **1.0.1**
- You can still uniquely identify a deployed EXE by **BuildNumber**
- Lower chance of version/binding headaches

## Alternative “standard” (less recommended): everything always matches
Some teams set `AssemblyVersion = FileVersion = Version` and bump all together. It’s simpler conceptually, but can cause binding friction in larger apps and doesn’t buy you much.

## My recommendation for AMiOffice
Use the recommended scheme above:
- Bump `<Version>` when you want to “release”
- Let your existing publish step bump `BuildNumber` automatically so every publish has a unique `FileVersion` and `InformationalVersion`

If you tell me your intended policy (“bump version on every publish” vs “bump version only when a change is meaningful”), I’ll regenerate a full `AMiOffice.vbproj` that matches it and removes the duplicate version entries cleanly.

then lets do this: AssemblyVersion: changes rarely (often only on major releases)
FileVersion: changes every build/release (so you can identify exactly what EXE is deployed)
InformationalVersion: the “marketing”/human version (often semver + build metadata)

## Copilot

```xml name=AMiOffice.vbproj
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>WinExe</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0-windows</TargetFramework>
    <StartupObject>AMiOffice.My.MyApplication</StartupObject>
    <EnableNETAnalyzers>false</EnableNETAnalyzers>

    <UseWindowsForms>true</UseWindowsForms>
    <MyType>WindowsForms</MyType>

    <AssemblyTitle>AMiOffice</AssemblyTitle>
    <Product>AMiOffice</Product>
    <Company>Kirk Saffell</Company>
    <Description>AMiOffice Application</Description>

    <!-- Versioning policy:
         - AssemblyVersion: changes rarely (typically major releases only)
         - FileVersion: changes every Publish (uses BuildNumber)
         - InformationalVersion: semver + build metadata -->
    <Version>1.0.0</Version>
    <AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>

    <!-- Ensure BuildNumber is never blank for normal builds -->
    <BuildNumber Condition="'$(BuildNumber)'==''">0</BuildNumber>

    <!-- Update every Publish -->
    <FileVersion>$(Version).$(BuildNumber)</FileVersion>

    <!-- What Windows shows as "Product version" -->
    <ProductVersion>$(Version)</ProductVersion>

    <!-- Human-facing / marketing version -->
    <InformationalVersion>$(Version) (Build $(BuildNumber))</InformationalVersion>

    <GenerateAssemblyInfo>true</GenerateAssemblyInfo>

    <BuildInfoFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\Generated\BuildInfo.g.vb</BuildInfoFile>

    <Copyright>Copyright Kirk Saffell 2026</Copyright>
    <Title>AMiOfficeApplication</Title>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- VB global imports to make designer code compile reliably -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Import Include="System" />
    <Import Include="System.Windows.Forms" />
    <Import Include="System.Drawing" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Update="FormAbout.Designer.vb">
      <DependentUpon>FormAbout.vb</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>
    <EmbeddedResource Update="FormAbout.resx">
      <DependentUpon>FormAbout.vb</DependentUpon>
    </EmbeddedResource>
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- Hard-stop: never compile stray repo-root folders -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Remove="..\My Project\**\*.vb" />
    <Compile Remove="..\obj\**\*.vb" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <None Remove="ESC Logo.jpg" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <EmbeddedResource Include="ESC Logo.jpg">
      <CopyToOutputDirectory>Never</CopyToOutputDirectory>
    </EmbeddedResource>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Update="My Project\Application.Designer.vb">
      <DesignTime>True</DesignTime>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Application.myapp</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>

    <Compile Update="My Project\Resources.Designer.vb">
      <DesignTime>True</DesignTime>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Resources.resx</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>

    <Compile Update="My Project\Settings.Designer.vb">
      <DesignTimeSharedInput>True</DesignTimeSharedInput>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Settings.settings</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <EmbeddedResource Update="My Project\Resources.resx">
      <CustomToolNamespace>My.Resources</CustomToolNamespace>
      <Generator>VbMyResourcesResXFileCodeGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Resources.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </EmbeddedResource>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <None Update="My Project\Application.myapp">
      <Generator>MyApplicationCodeGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Application.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </None>

    <None Update="My Project\Settings.settings">
      <CustomToolNamespace>My</CustomToolNamespace>
      <Generator>SettingsSingleFileGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Settings.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </None>
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- Prevent compiling any stray BuildInfo file from the project root -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Remove="BuildInfo.g.vb" />
    <Compile Remove="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <Target Name="IncrementBuildCounter" BeforeTargets="Publish">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildCounterFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\build-counter.txt</BuildCounterFile>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      Condition="!Exists('$(BuildCounterFile)')"
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="0" />

    <ReadLinesFromFile File="$(BuildCounterFile)">
      <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildCounterLines" />
    </ReadLinesFromFile>

    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.Int32]::Parse('%(BuildCounterLines.Identity)'))</BuildNumber>
      <BuildNumber>$([MSBuild]::Add($(BuildNumber), 1))</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="$(BuildNumber)" />
  </Target>

  <Target Name="GenerateBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildCounterFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\build-counter.txt</BuildCounterFile>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      Condition="!Exists('$(BuildCounterFile)')"
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="0" />

    <ReadLinesFromFile File="$(BuildCounterFile)">
      <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildCounterLines2" />
    </ReadLinesFromFile>

    <!-- Convert first line item into a real property -->
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.String]::Copy('%(BuildCounterLines2.Identity)'))</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>
    <Message Text="GenerateBuildInfo: BuildCounterFile=$(BuildCounterFile) BuildNumber=$(BuildNumber)" Importance="high" />

    <!-- Trim + fallback -->
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.String]::Copy('$(BuildNumber)').Trim())</BuildNumber>
      <BuildNumber Condition="'$(BuildNumber)'==''">0</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <MakeDir Directories="$([System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName('$(BuildInfoFile)'))" />

    <WriteLinesToFile
      File="$(BuildInfoFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="Option Strict On&#xD;&#xA;Option Explicit On&#xD;&#xA;&#xD;&#xA;Friend Module BuildInfo&#xD;&#xA;    Friend Const ProductVersion As String = &quot;$(ProductVersion)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;    Friend Const BuildNumber As String = &quot;$(BuildNumber)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;&#xD;&#xA;    Friend ReadOnly Property DisplayVersion As String&#xD;&#xA;        Get&#xD;&#xA;            Return &quot;AMiOffice v&quot; &amp; ProductVersion &amp; &quot; (Build &quot; &amp; BuildNumber &amp; &quot;)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;        End Get&#xD;&#xA;    End Property&#xD;&#xA;End Module&#xD;&#xA;" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Remove="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
      <Compile Include="$(BuildInfoFile)" Visible="false" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

</Project>
```

## @testsubject117

I am not using git. I have also already tested renaming check.inv, other.chk and ledger.cur. it's all working as expected. Not sure what you mean by number 4, what do I need to do?

## Copilot

Nothing urgent—you’re already “done.” #4 is just about how you want to handle version numbers **next time you make changes**.

Right now your project is set up like this:

- `<Version>1.0.0</Version>` (this is your **release / marketing** version)
- `<AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>` (stays stable, only change on major releases)
- `<FileVersion>$(Version).$(BuildNumber)</FileVersion>` (this becomes **1.0.0.N**, and **N increases every time you Publish**)

So:

## What happens automatically
- Every time you **Publish**, your `build-counter.txt` increments and the EXE’s **File version** will move from:
  - `1.0.0.1` → `1.0.0.2` → `1.0.0.3` … (etc)
- Your in-app informational version / About text (if you show it) will also update to:
  - `1.0.0 (Build N)`

## What you need to do manually (only when you want a “new release version”)
When you make a meaningful set of changes and you want to call it **1.0.1** (a patch release):

1) Open `AMiOffice.vbproj`
2) Change:
   - `<Version>1.0.0</Version>` → `<Version>1.0.1</Version>`
3) Leave **AssemblyVersion** alone:
   - keep `<AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>`
4) Publish. Now your EXE versions will be:
   - **Product version**: `1.0.1`
   - **File version**: `1.0.1.<BuildNumber>`

### When would you change AssemblyVersion?
Only when you decide you’re doing a **major** release (breaking change / new “generation”), e.g.:
- `2.0.0` and then set `AssemblyVersion` to `2.0.0.0`

## Suggested simple policy (so you never have to think hard)
- Small bugfix: bump `Version` patch: `1.0.0 → 1.0.1`
- New features but same app: bump minor: `1.0.0 → 1.1.0`
- Big/breaking change: bump major: `1.x → 2.0.0` and also bump `AssemblyVersion` to `2.0.0.0`

If you tell me whether you expect “patch vs minor” releases, I can give you a tiny checklist you can keep in a text file for next time (since you’re not using git).

## Copilot

````text name=RELEASING-CHECKLIST.txt
AMiOffice Release Checklist (no-git workflow)
Date: ____________
Prepared by: ____________

VERSIONING POLICY (SemVer-ish)
- Patch (bugfix):        1.0.0 -> 1.0.1      (AssemblyVersion stays the same)
- Minor (new features):  1.0.0 -> 1.1.0      (AssemblyVersion stays the same)
- Major (breaking):      1.x   -> 2.0.0      (AssemblyVersion -> 2.0.0.0)

In AMiOffice.vbproj:
- Version = X.Y.Z
- AssemblyVersion = A.0.0.0 (A changes only on major)
- FileVersion = $(Version).$(BuildNumber) (auto changes on Publish)
- InformationalVersion = $(Version) (Build $(BuildNumber))

------------------------------------------------------------
1) PRE-FLIGHT
[ ] Confirm app runs locally (Debug/Release build succeeds)
[ ] Confirm data files present on dev machine OR test with missing-file behavior
[ ] Close AMiOffice on the workstation(s) you will update (avoid locked EXE)

------------------------------------------------------------
2) SET VERSION (manual)
Open AMiOffice.vbproj and update:

Patch release:
[ ] Change <Version> X.Y.Z -> X.Y.(Z+1)
[ ] Do NOT change <AssemblyVersion>

Minor release:
[ ] Change <Version> X.Y.Z -> X.(Y+1).0
[ ] Do NOT change <AssemblyVersion>

Major release:
[ ] Change <Version> X.Y.Z -> (X+1).0.0
[ ] Change <AssemblyVersion> old -> (X+1).0.0.0

Notes:
- Do not set Version to 4 parts; keep it X.Y.Z.
- AssemblyVersion should remain A.0.0.0 between majors.

------------------------------------------------------------
3) BUILD + PUBLISH (increments BuildNumber)
[ ] Build Release
[ ] Publish (this increments build-counter.txt)
[ ] Record BuildNumber after publish: ____________

Expected results:
- Product version: X.Y.Z
- File version:    X.Y.Z.<BuildNumber>
- Informational:   X.Y.Z (Build <BuildNumber>)

------------------------------------------------------------
4) VERIFY PUBLISHED EXE BEFORE DEPLOY
Locate published AMiOffice.exe and check:
[ ] Right-click EXE -> Properties -> Details
    - Product version: ____________
    - File version:    ____________
[ ] Launch the published EXE locally
[ ] Quick sanity test: open main screens and ensure no crash on startup

------------------------------------------------------------
5) DEPLOY TO WORKSTATION(S)
[ ] Copy AMiOffice.exe (and any required supporting files) to target workstation path
[ ] Launch on workstation
[ ] Confirm the "About" screen (or version display) matches X.Y.Z (Build N)

Workstations updated:
- [ ] PC name: ____________  Time: ______  Verified: (initials) ____
- [ ] PC name: ____________  Time: ______  Verified: (initials) ____
- [ ] PC name: ____________  Time: ______  Verified: (initials) ____

------------------------------------------------------------
6) SMOKE TESTS ON WORKSTATION (fast)
[ ] Find by Invoice # works
[ ] Find by Check # works
[ ] Ledger screens load

Optional resilience checks (only if you need to validate error handling):
[ ] Rename CHECK.INV temporarily -> app shows friendly missing-file message
[ ] Rename LEDGER.CUR temporarily -> app shows friendly missing-file message
[ ] Rename OTHER.CHK temporarily -> app shows friendly missing-file message
(restore files immediately after)

------------------------------------------------------------
7) ARCHIVE / BACKUP (since there is no git)
[ ] Copy the published output folder to an archive location:
    Archive path: ________________________________
    Folder name suggestion: AMiOffice_X.Y.Z_BuildN_YYYY-MM-DD
[ ] Optionally also zip the entire source folder used for this release.

------------------------------------------------------------
8) RELEASE NOTES (1-2 lines)
Version: X.Y.Z (Build N)
Date deployed: ____________
Changes:
- 1)
- 2)

END
````

## Copilot

At this point you’re finished. The only “next” thing to do is **save that checklist somewhere you’ll actually use it** and decide how you’ll archive builds since you’re not using git.

Here are the practical next steps I’d do:

1) **Create the checklist file in your solution folder**
- Save it as `RELEASING-CHECKLIST.txt` in the same folder as `AMiOffice.vbproj`.

2) **Create an “Archives” folder and start using a consistent naming scheme**
- Example:
  - `\\server\AMiOffice\Archives\AMiOffice_1.0.0_Build37_2026-04-24\`
- Copy your **published output** into that folder each release.

3) **Write down today’s deployed build info**
- In a simple `DEPLOYMENTS.txt` (or even the same checklist), record:
  - Date: **2026-04-24**
  - Version: **1.0.0**
  - Build: **(whatever BuildNumber is now)**
  - Workstation(s) updated

If you want, paste the **File version** you see on the workstation (e.g., `1.0.0.37`) and I’ll give you the exact archive folder name + a `DEPLOYMENTS.txt` template line to add.

## @testsubject117

that's ok, I will save the files and folders consistently. Lets continue building the mainmenu app, what's easiest to do on that next?

## Copilot

Easiest next step: **make the Main Menu show an explicit version string** (your new `BuildInfo.DisplayVersion`) and add a single place to verify required data files + paths.

That gives you two big wins immediately:
- You can tell exactly what build a workstation is running without digging into EXE properties.
- You can prevent “mystery behavior” by validating files/paths up front and showing one clear message.

## 1) Add a version label to the Main Menu form
On your main menu form (whatever it’s named: `FormMainMenu`, `frmMain`, etc.):

- Add a `Label` (bottom-left is typical) named something like `lblVersion`.
- In `Form_Load`, set:

```vbnet
lblVersion.Text = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
```

(You already generate `BuildInfo.g.vb`, so this should just work.)

## 2) Add a startup “environment check” in one place
In the same `Form_Load` (or application startup), check the required data files exist and are readable:

- `CHECK.INV`
- `LEDGER.CUR`
- `OTHER.CHK`

If missing, show a single MessageBox explaining what’s missing and where it’s expected, and optionally disable menu buttons that depend on it.

## @testsubject117

```
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormMainMenu

    Private _buildInfo As String = ""

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Dim V = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Dim built = BuildInfo.BuildNumber

        Me.KeyPreview = True

        BuildMenu()


        _buildInfo = "   " & BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Me.Text = "Active Magnetic Inspection Main Menu Application   " & BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        ' Force the Show of a small window with version and build - 'MessageBox.Show(BuildInfo.DisplayVersion, "DisplayVersion")

        lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
        UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        tmrClock.Start()

        ' Force the version/date text to start as far left as possible in lblDateTime
        lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)  ' remove right padding; keep top padding
        lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)

        ' IMPORTANT: run resize after layout is finalized
        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Private Sub flpLeft_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpLeft.SizeChanged
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
    End Sub

    Private Sub flpRight_SizeChanged(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles flpRight.SizeChanged
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub
    Private Sub tmrClock_Tick(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles tmrClock.Tick
        UpdateHeaderClock()
    End Sub
    Private Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

        lblDateTime.Text = _buildInfo & "          " &
                       now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                       now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                       now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub
    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        If flpLeft Is Nothing OrElse flpRight Is Nothing Then Return

        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        ' Left column
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "A", "Shop Card Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "B", "Invoice Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "C", "Checks and Cash Receipts")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "D", "View Sales Journal")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "E", "View Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "F", "Price List Program")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "G", "Print Records / Void Invoices")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "H", "Quick Message Flashing")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "I", "Backup Price List & Rolodex")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "J", "Print Out Customers Actual Names")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "K", "Cash Disbursements")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "L", "Business Expenses Account")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "M", "Quotation Form Generator")
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "N", "Rolodex")

        ' Right column (removed: S, U, V, W, +, 5)
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "O", "Copy Spec Index")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "P", "Entire Ledger Viewing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Word Processor")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "R", "Find Word Processor Text")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "T", "Change Date or Time")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "X", "Typewriter Mode")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Y", "Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Z", "Personal Calendar")

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "1", "Mileage Tracking")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "2", "Product Purchasing")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "3", "Miscellaneous Menu")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "4", "Add Entries to Log Book")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Cadmium Cards")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Emergency PAYROLL System")
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "?", "About AMiOffice Menu System")
    End Sub

    Private Function MakeHeaderLabel(text As String) As Label
        Dim lbl As New Label()
        lbl.AutoSize = False
        lbl.Height = 32
        lbl.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
        lbl.Text = text
        lbl.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        lbl.Width = 1000 ' will be resized later
        Return lbl
    End Function

    Private Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        ' Force black buttons with white text
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1

        ' Optional: nicer hover / click feedback
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)

        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub(sender, args) HandleMenuKey(CStr(btn.Tag))

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnResize(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnResize(e)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
        panel.ClientSize.Width -
        panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
        SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            Dim lbl = TryCast(c, Label)

            If btn IsNot Nothing Then
                btn.Width = targetWidth
            ElseIf lbl IsNot Nothing Then
                lbl.Width = targetWidth
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMainMenu_KeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs) Handles Me.KeyPress
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return
        HandleMenuKey(ch)
    End Sub

    Private Sub HandleMenuKey(ch As String)
        If String.IsNullOrEmpty(ch) Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then
            up = up.ToUpperInvariant()
        End If

        Select Case up
            Case "A" : NotYet("Shop Card Generator")
            Case "B" : NotYet("Invoice Generator")
            Case "C"
                Using f As New FormLedgerMenu()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            Case "D" : NotYet("View Sales Journal (SALES)")
            Case "E" : NotYet("View Log Book (LOGBOOK)")
            Case "F" : NotYet("Price List Program (plist)")
            Case "G" : NotYet("Print/Void Invoices (BOOT)")
            Case "H" : NotYet("Quick Message Flashing")

            Case "I"
                Try
                    Cursor = Cursors.WaitCursor
                    MigrationService.EnsureFoldersAndMigrateOnce()
                    BackupService.RunBackupPriceListAndRolodex()
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup complete." & Environment.NewLine & AppPaths.BackupDir,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
                Catch ex As Exception
                    MessageBox.Show("Backup failed: " & ex.Message,
                                    "Backup", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
                Finally
                    Cursor = Cursors.Default
                End Try

            Case "J" : NotYet("Print Out Customers Actual Names (spool real names)")
            Case "K" : NotYet("Cash Disbursements (BILL)")
            Case "L" : NotYet("Business Expenses Account (password)")
            Case "M" : NotYet("Quotation Form Generator (QUOTE)")
            Case "N" : NotYet("Rolodex (PHONE)")

            Case "O" : NotYet("Copy Spec Index")
            Case "P" : NotYet("Entire Ledger Viewing (ENTIRE)")
            Case "Q" : NotYet("Word Processor")
            Case "R" : NotYet("Find Word Processor Text")

            Case "T" : NotYet("Change Date or Time")
            Case "X" : NotYet("Typewriter Mode")
            Case "Y" : NotYet("Ed Dean's Personal Backup")
            Case "Z" : NotYet("Personal Calendar")

            Case "1" : NotYet("Mileage Tracking")
            Case "2" : NotYet("Product Purchasing")
            Case "3" : NotYet("Miscellaneous Menu")
            Case "4" : NotYet("Add Entries to Log Book")
            Case "6" : NotYet("Cadmium Cards")
            Case "7" : NotYet("Emergency PAYROLL System")

            Case "?"
                Using f As New FormAbout()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using

            Case Else
                ' ignore unknown keys
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

## Copilot

Create two new WinForms forms in your project, then paste the code below into each. They’ll be simple but working, and they’ll open modally from your main menu exactly like (C) and (?) do.

## 1) Create the forms (Visual Studio)
Do this twice (once for each form):

1. In **Solution Explorer**, right-click your project (AMiOffice)
2. **Add → Windows Form…**
3. Name it:
   - `FormMileageTracking.vb`
4. Click **Add**

Repeat for:
- `FormPersonalCalendar.vb`

That will create a `.vb` and a `.Designer.vb` automatically.

## 2) Paste code into the `.vb` files (not the Designer)
Open each form’s **.vb** (code-behind) file and replace its contents with the following.

### FormMileageTracking.vb
```vbnet name=FormMileageTracking.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormMileageTracking

    Public Sub New()
        InitializeComponent()
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMileageTracking_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        Me.Text = "Mileage Tracking"
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
        Me.KeyPreview = True
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormMileageTracking_KeyDown(sender As Object, e As KeyEventArgs) Handles MyBase.KeyDown
        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Escape Then
            Me.Close()
            e.Handled = True
        End If
    End Sub

End Class
```

### FormPersonalCalendar.vb
```vbnet name=FormPersonalCalendar.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormPersonalCalendar

    Public Sub New()
        InitializeComponent()
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormPersonalCalendar_Load(sender As Object, e As EventArgs) Handles MyBase.Load
        Me.Text = "Personal Calendar"
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
        Me.KeyPreview = True
    End Sub

    Private Sub FormPersonalCalendar_KeyDown(sender As Object, e As KeyEventArgs) Handles MyBase.KeyDown
        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Escape Then
            Me.Close()
            e.Handled = True
        End If
    End Sub

End Class
```

## Copilot

No—you don’t *need* to pick A or B. Blank windows just mean the forms exist and open, but you haven’t added any controls yet (which is totally fine).

If you want the **fastest** path with **zero designer work**, pick **B** and paste the code below into each form’s `.vb` file (code-behind). This will create a simple title, a “coming soon” message, and a Close button at runtime.

## FormMileageTracking.vb (code-built UI)
```vbnet name=FormMileageTracking.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormMileageTracking

    Public Sub New()
        InitializeComponent()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.Text = "Mileage Tracking"
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.MinimumSize = New Size(640, 420)

        Dim pnl As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .Padding = New Padding(16),
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 4
        }
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' header
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' body
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100)) ' filler
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' buttons

        Dim lblHeader As New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 16.0F, FontStyle.Bold),
            .Text = "Mileage Tracking"
        }

        Dim lblBody As New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 11.0F, FontStyle.Regular),
            .Text = "This screen is ready to be built." & Environment.NewLine &
                    "Press ESC or click Close to return to the Main Menu."
        }

        Dim btnClose As New Button() With {
            .Text = "Close",
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Right
        }
        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Me.Close()

        Dim buttonPanel As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.RightToLeft,
            .AutoSize = True,
            .WrapContents = False
        }
        buttonPanel.Controls.Add(btnClose)

        pnl.Controls.Add(lblHeader, 0, 0)
        pnl.Controls.Add(lblBody, 0, 1)
        pnl.Controls.Add(New Panel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill}, 0, 2)
        pnl.Controls.Add(buttonPanel, 0, 3)

        Me.Controls.Clear()
        Me.Controls.Add(pnl)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnKeyDown(e As KeyEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnKeyDown(e)
        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Escape Then
            Me.Close()
            e.Handled = True
        End If
    End Sub

End Class
```

## FormPersonalCalendar.vb (code-built UI)
```vbnet name=FormPersonalCalendar.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormPersonalCalendar

    Public Sub New()
        InitializeComponent()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.Text = "Personal Calendar"
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.MinimumSize = New Size(640, 420)

        Dim pnl As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .Padding = New Padding(16),
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 4
        }
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' header
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' body
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100)) ' filler
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' buttons

        Dim lblHeader As New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 16.0F, FontStyle.Bold),
            .Text = "Personal Calendar"
        }

        Dim lblBody As New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 11.0F, FontStyle.Regular),
            .Text = "This screen is ready to be built." & Environment.NewLine &
                    "Press ESC or click Close to return to the Main Menu."
        }

        Dim btnClose As New Button() With {
            .Text = "Close",
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Right
        }
        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Me.Close()

        Dim buttonPanel As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.RightToLeft,
            .AutoSize = True,
            .WrapContents = False
        }
        buttonPanel.Controls.Add(btnClose)

        pnl.Controls.Add(lblHeader, 0, 0)
        pnl.Controls.Add(lblBody, 0, 1)
        pnl.Controls.Add(New Panel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill}, 0, 2)
        pnl.Controls.Add(buttonPanel, 0, 3)

        Me.Controls.Clear()
        Me.Controls.Add(pnl)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnKeyDown(e As KeyEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnKeyDown(e)
        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Escape Then
            Me.Close()
            e.Handled = True
        End If
    End Sub

End Class
```

### Why this works (and why you were seeing blank windows)
A Windows Form with no controls will show as a blank window. The above code **creates controls at runtime**, so you immediately see something without touching the designer.

If you’d rather stick with the Designer long-term, you can still do that later—this is just the quickest “not blank” step.

## @testsubject117

this is excellent, looks fine, now lets get those windows working instead of blank

## Copilot

Pick one window to implement first; the easiest to make “real” quickly is **Mileage Tracking** (simple data-entry + save/load). Below is a working, minimal mileage log that:

- lets you add trips (Date, Start, End, Purpose)
- auto-calculates Miles
- shows entries in a grid
- saves/loads to a local CSV file (no database)
- has a basic “Delete selected” and “Export” behavior

You can paste this as-is into `FormMileageTracking.vb`. It’s still code-built UI, so you don’t need the designer.

## FormMileageTracking.vb (working mileage log)
```vbnet name=FormMileageTracking.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Data
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Globalization
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Text
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormMileageTracking

    Private ReadOnly _filePath As String =
        Path.Combine(Environment.GetFolderPath(Environment.SpecialFolder.LocalApplicationData),
                     "AMiOffice", "mileage.csv")

    Private _table As DataTable = Nothing!
    Private _grid As DataGridView = Nothing!

    ' Inputs
    Private _dtpDate As DateTimePicker = Nothing!
    Private _txtStart As TextBox = Nothing!
    Private _txtEnd As TextBox = Nothing!
    Private _txtPurpose As TextBox = Nothing!
    Private _lblMiles As Label = Nothing!

    Public Sub New()
        InitializeComponent()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.Text = "Mileage Tracking"
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.MinimumSize = New Size(900, 600)

        BuildUi()
        BuildTable()
        LoadFromDisk()

        RecalcMilesPreview()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnKeyDown(e As KeyEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnKeyDown(e)
        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Escape Then
            Close()
            e.Handled = True
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildUi()
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .Padding = New Padding(12),
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 3
        }
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))          ' header
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))          ' entry
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))   ' grid

        Dim lblHeader As New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 16.0F, FontStyle.Bold),
            .Text = "Mileage Tracking"
        }

        ' Entry panel
        Dim entry As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Top,
            .AutoSize = True,
            .ColumnCount = 8
        }
        entry.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Date label
        entry.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Absolute, 140)) ' Date picker
        entry.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Start label
        entry.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Absolute, 90))  ' Start
        entry.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' End label
        entry.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Absolute, 90))  ' End
        entry.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100))  ' Purpose
        entry.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' Buttons

        _dtpDate = New DateTimePicker() With {
            .Format = DateTimePickerFormat.Short,
            .Value = DateTime.Today,
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        }

        _txtStart = New TextBox() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill}
        _txtEnd = New TextBox() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill}
        _txtPurpose = New TextBox() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill}

        AddHandler _txtStart.TextChanged, Sub() RecalcMilesPreview()
        AddHandler _txtEnd.TextChanged, Sub() RecalcMilesPreview()

        _lblMiles = New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 10.0F, FontStyle.Bold),
            .Text = "Miles: —",
            .Padding = New Padding(8, 7, 8, 7)
        }

        Dim btnAdd As New Button() With {.Text = "Add", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnAdd.Click, Sub() AddEntry()

        Dim btnDelete As New Button() With {.Text = "Delete Selected", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnDelete.Click, Sub() DeleteSelected()

        Dim btnSave As New Button() With {.Text = "Save", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnSave.Click, Sub() SaveToDisk()

        Dim btnClose As New Button() With {.Text = "Close", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Close()

        Dim rightButtons As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.LeftToRight,
            .WrapContents = False,
            .Margin = New Padding(6, 0, 0, 0)
        }
        rightButtons.Controls.Add(btnAdd)
        rightButtons.Controls.Add(btnDelete)
        rightButtons.Controls.Add(btnSave)
        rightButtons.Controls.Add(btnClose)

        entry.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.AutoSize = True, .Text = "Date:", .Padding = New Padding(0, 8, 6, 0)}, 0, 0)
        entry.Controls.Add(_dtpDate, 1, 0)
        entry.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.AutoSize = True, .Text = "Start:", .Padding = New Padding(12, 8, 6, 0)}, 2, 0)
        entry.Controls.Add(_txtStart, 3, 0)
        entry.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.AutoSize = True, .Text = "End:", .Padding = New Padding(12, 8, 6, 0)}, 4, 0)
        entry.Controls.Add(_txtEnd, 5, 0)

        Dim purposePanel As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .AutoSize = True
        }
        purposePanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
        purposePanel.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        purposePanel.Controls.Add(_txtPurpose, 0, 0)
        purposePanel.Controls.Add(_lblMiles, 1, 0)

        entry.Controls.Add(purposePanel, 6, 0)
        entry.Controls.Add(rightButtons, 7, 0)

        ' Grid
        _grid = New DataGridView() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ReadOnly = False,
            .AllowUserToAddRows = False,
            .AllowUserToDeleteRows = False,
            .SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect,
            .MultiSelect = True,
            .AutoSizeColumnsMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnsMode.Fill
        }

        root.Controls.Add(lblHeader, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(entry, 0, 1)
        root.Controls.Add(_grid, 0, 2)

        Controls.Clear()
        Controls.Add(root)
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildTable()
        _table = New DataTable("Mileage")
        _table.Columns.Add("Date", GetType(DateTime))
        _table.Columns.Add("Start", GetType(Integer))
        _table.Columns.Add("End", GetType(Integer))
        _table.Columns.Add("Miles", GetType(Integer))
        _table.Columns.Add("Purpose", GetType(String))

        _grid.DataSource = _table

        _grid.Columns("Date").DefaultCellStyle.Format = "d"
        _grid.Columns("Start").FillWeight = 15
        _grid.Columns("End").FillWeight = 15
        _grid.Columns("Miles").FillWeight = 12
        _grid.Columns("Purpose").FillWeight = 58
    End Sub

    Private Sub RecalcMilesPreview()
        Dim startVal As Integer
        Dim endVal As Integer

        If Integer.TryParse(_txtStart.Text.Trim(), startVal) AndAlso Integer.TryParse(_txtEnd.Text.Trim(), endVal) Then
            Dim miles = endVal - startVal
            If miles >= 0 Then
                _lblMiles.Text = "Miles: " & miles.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
            Else
                _lblMiles.Text = "Miles: (end < start)"
            End If
        Else
            _lblMiles.Text = "Miles: —"
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub AddEntry()
        Dim startVal As Integer
        Dim endVal As Integer

        If Not Integer.TryParse(_txtStart.Text.Trim(), startVal) Then
            MessageBox.Show("Start must be a whole number (odometer).", "Mileage", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Warning)
            _txtStart.Focus()
            Return
        End If
        If Not Integer.TryParse(_txtEnd.Text.Trim(), endVal) Then
            MessageBox.Show("End must be a whole number (odometer).", "Mileage", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Warning)
            _txtEnd.Focus()
            Return
        End If

        Dim miles = endVal - startVal
        If miles < 0 Then
            MessageBox.Show("End cannot be less than Start.", "Mileage", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Warning)
            _txtEnd.Focus()
            Return
        End If

        Dim purpose = _txtPurpose.Text.Trim()

        Dim row = _table.NewRow()
        row("Date") = _dtpDate.Value.Date
        row("Start") = startVal
        row("End") = endVal
        row("Miles") = miles
        row("Purpose") = purpose
        _table.Rows.Add(row)

        _txtStart.Clear()
        _txtEnd.Clear()
        _txtPurpose.Clear()
        _txtStart.Focus()
        RecalcMilesPreview()
    End Sub

    Private Sub DeleteSelected()
        If _grid.SelectedRows.Count = 0 Then Return

        If MessageBox.Show($"Delete {_grid.SelectedRows.Count} selected row(s)?",
                           "Mileage", MessageBoxButtons.YesNo, MessageBoxIcon.Question) <> DialogResult.Yes Then
            Return
        End If

        ' Remove from bottom to top to keep indexes valid
        Dim idxs = _grid.SelectedRows.Cast(Of DataGridViewRow)().
            Select(Function(r) r.Index).
            OrderByDescending(Function(i) i).
            ToList()

        For Each i In idxs
            If i >= 0 AndAlso i < _table.Rows.Count Then
                _table.Rows.RemoveAt(i)
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub EnsureDir()
        Dim dir = Path.GetDirectoryName(_filePath)
        If Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(dir) Then
            Directory.CreateDirectory(dir)
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub SaveToDisk()
        Try
            EnsureDir()

            Using sw As New StreamWriter(_filePath, append:=False, encoding:=Encoding.UTF8)
                sw.WriteLine("Date,Start,End,Miles,Purpose")
                For Each r As DataRow In _table.Rows
                    Dim d = DirectCast(r("Date"), DateTime).ToString("yyyy-MM-dd", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
                    Dim s = CInt(r("Start")).ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
                    Dim en = CInt(r("End")).ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
                    Dim m = CInt(r("Miles")).ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
                    Dim p = EscapeCsv(CStr(r("Purpose")))
                    sw.WriteLine($"{d},{s},{en},{m},{p}")
                Next
            End Using

            MessageBox.Show("Saved to:" & Environment.NewLine & _filePath,
                            "Mileage", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show("Save failed: " & ex.Message, "Mileage", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadFromDisk()
        Try
            If Not File.Exists(_filePath) Then Return

            Dim lines = File.ReadAllLines(_filePath, Encoding.UTF8)
            If lines.Length <= 1 Then Return ' header only

            For i = 1 To lines.Length - 1
                Dim line = lines(i)
                If String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(line) Then Continue For

                Dim parts = ParseCsvLine(line)
                If parts.Length < 5 Then Continue For

                Dim d As DateTime
                Dim s As Integer
                Dim en As Integer
                Dim m As Integer

                If Not DateTime.TryParseExact(parts(0), "yyyy-MM-dd", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, DateTimeStyles.None, d) Then Continue For
                If Not Integer.TryParse(parts(1), s) Then Continue For
                If Not Integer.TryParse(parts(2), en) Then Continue For
                If Not Integer.TryParse(parts(3), m) Then Continue For

                Dim row = _table.NewRow()
                row("Date") = d.Date
                row("Start") = s
                row("End") = en
                row("Miles") = m
                row("Purpose") = parts(4)
                _table.Rows.Add(row)
            Next
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show("Load failed: " & ex.Message, "Mileage", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Shared Function EscapeCsv(value As String) As String
        If value Is Nothing Then Return ""
        Dim needsQuotes = value.Contains(","c) OrElse value.Contains(""""c) OrElse value.Contains(ControlChars.Quote) OrElse value.Contains(ControlChars.Cr) OrElse value.Contains(ControlChars.Lf)
        Dim v = value.Replace("""", """""")
        If needsQuotes Then
            Return """" & v & """"
        End If
        Return v
    End Function

    ' Minimal CSV parser for one line with quotes
    Private Shared Function ParseCsvLine(line As String) As String()
        Dim result As New List(Of String)()
        Dim sb As New StringBuilder()
        Dim inQuotes = False

        For i = 0 To line.Length - 1
            Dim ch = line(i)

            If inQuotes Then
                If ch = """"c Then
                    If i + 1 < line.Length AndAlso line(i + 1) = """"c Then
                        sb.Append(""""c) ' escaped quote
                        i += 1
                    Else
                        inQuotes = False
                    End If
                Else
                    sb.Append(ch)
                End If
            Else
                If ch = ","c Then
                    result.Add(sb.ToString())
                    sb.Clear()
                ElseIf ch = """"c Then
                    inQuotes = True
                Else
                    sb.Append(ch)
                End If
            End If
        Next

        result.Add(sb.ToString())
        Return result.ToArray()
    End Function

End Class
```

### What this gives you right now
- A real screen you can use immediately
- A data file stored at:
  - `%LOCALAPPDATA%\AMiOffice\mileage.csv`
- No designer work, no database

## @testsubject117

so does this mileage tracking work the same as the original dos gwbasic code and performs the same user functionality?

## Copilot

No — not yet.

What I gave you is a **working, modern placeholder implementation** (basic trip entry + CSV save/load). It almost certainly does **not** match the original DOS/GW-BASIC program’s behavior unless your original was also just a simple odometer log.

Whether it “performs the same user functionality” depends on what the GW-BASIC mileage program actually did.

## What my current version does
- User enters: **Date, Start Odometer, End Odometer, Purpose**
- It calculates **Miles = End − Start**
- Shows entries in a grid
- Saves/loads from a **CSV file** in `%LOCALAPPDATA%\AMiOffice\mileage.csv`

## Common GW-BASIC mileage features that are *not* implemented (yet)
Many older mileage trackers also did some/all of this:
- Prompt-driven workflow (single-line prompts, Enter to accept, Esc to cancel)
- **Client/customer/job** codes
- **From / To** locations
- Trip type (business/personal)
- Per-month or per-year totals
- Print reports
- Export formats compatible with other modules
- Editing existing entries in-place with keyboard commands
- Storing data in a specific legacy file format (random access `.DAT`, fixed-width text, etc.)
- “Next trip number” / sequence IDs

## To match the original, I need one of these from you
Pick the easiest:

1) Paste the GW-BASIC mileage code (even partial), **or**
2) Describe the original screen flow:
   - what fields it asked for, in what order
   - what keys did what (F-keys? Esc? PgUp/PgDn?)
   - what reports existed (monthly totals? yearly totals?)
   - what file name(s) it read/wrote (and where)

If you can also tell me the original data file name (e.g., `MILEAGE.DAT` / `MILES.TXT`) and whether you still have sample data, I can make the WinForms version read/write the same format so it’s truly a drop-in replacement.

If you answer just this, I can proceed with high confidence:
- What were the **exact fields** in the old mileage entry (Date/Start/End/Purpose… what else)?
- Did it produce **monthly totals** or **printouts**?

## @testsubject117

also, why does each button have text in paranthesis such as View Checks (LEDGER.CUR) or View Company Totals (Option 5) what is option 5 and why is it showing on the button?

## Copilot

Those parentheses are there because the original AMiOffice / GW-BASIC (DOS) menu system was driven by **single-key “options”**. In the DOS version you would press a key (often a number) and the program would run that function.

- **“Option 5”** means: in the old program, you’d press **5** at that menu to run **View Company Totals**.
- **“Option 8/9/0”** similarly mean you’d press **8**, **9**, or **0**.
- **“LEDGER.CUR / OTHER.CHK / CHECK.INV”** are the legacy data file names that function uses.

Why it’s showing on the button now: whoever ported/created the WinForms menu kept the old DOS labels so users could recognize the functions and/or so the keyboard shortcuts could remain the same.

### Should you keep it?
Depends on your goal:

- **If you want the WinForms app to feel like the original (keyboard-first):** keep the `(Option X)` text and make sure those keys work.
- **If you want a clean modern UI:** remove the `(Option X)`/file name text from the button label, and optionally show it as a tooltip or small grey subtext instead.

If you tell me which direction you want (keep/remove), I’ll incorporate it when I restyle `FormLedgerMenu` (center/bold + Close button grey/smaller).

## @testsubject117

```
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormLedgerMenu

    Private ReadOnly btnViewChecks As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnCompanyTotals As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnOtherChecks As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnFindByCheck As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnFindByInvoice As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnClose As New Button()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Ledger Menu"
        Width = 520
        Height = 420
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        Dim flp As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown,
            .WrapContents = False,
            .AutoScroll = True,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }

        Configure(btnViewChecks, "View Checks (LEDGER.CUR)")
        Configure(btnCompanyTotals, "View Company Totals (Option 5)")
        Configure(btnOtherChecks, "View OTHER Checks (Option 8)")
        Configure(btnFindByCheck, "Find by Check # (Option 9)")
        Configure(btnFindByInvoice, "Find by Invoice # (Option 0)")
        Configure(btnClose, "Close")

        AddHandler btnViewChecks.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormLedgerView()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnCompanyTotals.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnOtherChecks.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormOtherChecksView()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnFindByCheck.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormFindByCheckNumber()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnFindByInvoice.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormFindByInvoiceNumber()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Close()

        flp.Controls.Add(btnViewChecks)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnCompanyTotals)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnOtherChecks)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnFindByCheck)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnFindByInvoice)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnClose)

        Controls.Add(flp)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub Configure(btn As Button, text As String)
        btn.Text = text
        btn.Width = 460
        btn.Height = 44
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft

        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
    End Sub

End Class
```

## Copilot

Here’s a drop-in replacement for **FormLedgerMenu.vb** that does everything you asked:

- Menu buttons are **bold** and **centered**
- Button text shows real hotkeys like the main menu:
  - `(1) View Checks`
  - `(5) View Company Totals`
  - `(8) View OTHER Checks`
  - `(9) Find by Check #`
  - `(0) Find by Invoice #`
- **Hotkeys work**: pressing `1`, `5`, `8`, `9`, `0` activates the same actions as clicking the buttons
- **Close** button is **grey**, **black text**, and **smaller** (and `Esc` closes the window)
- Close button is no longer the big black/white menu style

Paste this over your existing file.

```vbnet name=FormLedgerMenu.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormLedgerMenu

    Private ReadOnly btnViewChecks As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnCompanyTotals As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnOtherChecks As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnFindByCheck As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnFindByInvoice As New Button()
    Private ReadOnly btnClose As New Button()

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Text = "Ledger Menu"
        Width = 520
        Height = 420
        StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        ' Enable hotkeys
        Me.KeyPreview = True

        Dim flp As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown,
            .WrapContents = False,
            .AutoScroll = True,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }

        ' Menu buttons: centered + bold + black/white
        ConfigureMenuButton(btnViewChecks, "(1) View Checks")
        ConfigureMenuButton(btnCompanyTotals, "(5) View Company Totals")
        ConfigureMenuButton(btnOtherChecks, "(8) View OTHER Checks")
        ConfigureMenuButton(btnFindByCheck, "(9) Find by Check #")
        ConfigureMenuButton(btnFindByInvoice, "(0) Find by Invoice #")

        ' Close button: smaller + grey, like your other windows
        ConfigureCloseButton(btnClose, "Close")

        AddHandler btnViewChecks.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormLedgerView()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnCompanyTotals.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnOtherChecks.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormOtherChecksView()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnFindByCheck.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormFindByCheckNumber()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnFindByInvoice.Click,
            Sub()
                Using f As New FormFindByInvoiceNumber()
                    f.ShowDialog(Me)
                End Using
            End Sub

        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Close()

        flp.Controls.Add(btnViewChecks)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnCompanyTotals)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnOtherChecks)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnFindByCheck)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnFindByInvoice)
        flp.Controls.Add(btnClose)

        Controls.Clear()
        Controls.Add(flp)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnKeyDown(e As KeyEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnKeyDown(e)

        Select Case e.KeyCode
            Case Keys.D1, Keys.NumPad1
                btnViewChecks.PerformClick()
                e.Handled = True

            Case Keys.D5, Keys.NumPad5
                btnCompanyTotals.PerformClick()
                e.Handled = True

            Case Keys.D8, Keys.NumPad8
                btnOtherChecks.PerformClick()
                e.Handled = True

            Case Keys.D9, Keys.NumPad9
                btnFindByCheck.PerformClick()
                e.Handled = True

            Case Keys.D0, Keys.NumPad0
                btnFindByInvoice.PerformClick()
                e.Handled = True

            Case Keys.Escape
                Close()
                e.Handled = True
        End Select
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub ConfigureMenuButton(btn As Button, text As String)
        btn.Text = text
        btn.Width = 460
        btn.Height = 44
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 8)

        ' Center + bold (per request)
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleCenter
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point)

        ' Black background, white text
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1

        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub ConfigureCloseButton(btn As Button, text As String)
        btn.Text = text

        ' Match width but make it shorter, like typical Close buttons
        btn.Width = 460
        btn.Height = 32
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 14, 3, 3)

        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleCenter
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 10.0F, FontStyle.Regular, GraphicsUnit.Point)

        ' Grey background, black text
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True
        btn.BackColor = SystemColors.Control
        btn.ForeColor = Color.Black

        ' Use the standard button rendering so it looks like other Close buttons
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Standard
    End Sub

End Class
```

### Notes / quick tweaks
- If you want the Close button to show its hotkey too, change the text to `"(Esc) Close"`.
- If you want **all** buttons the same font size as the main menu, tell me what size you want (your main menu uses 12 bold, so I matched that).

If you paste this and one of the hotkeys still doesn’t work, tell me whether focus is inside another control (it shouldn’t be, but we can also add `ProcessCmdKey` for guaranteed capture).

that's nice. Can you change it so the close button is smaller and the same size as the Mileage Tracking Close button? Also can you add (ESC) before the word close on the button so everyone knows escape is the hotkey?

## Copilot

Yes—make the Close button **AutoSize** (like the Mileage Tracking one) and label it **`(Esc) Close`**. Here are the minimal edits to your updated `FormLedgerMenu.vb`.

### 1) Change the Close button text in `OnLoad`
Replace:
```vbnet
ConfigureCloseButton(btnClose, "Close")
```
with:
```vbnet
ConfigureCloseButton(btnClose, "(Esc) Close")
```

### 2) Update `ConfigureCloseButton` to match the Mileage Tracking Close button (small/grey)
Replace your entire `ConfigureCloseButton` with this:

```vbnet name=FormLedgerMenu.vb
Private Shared Sub ConfigureCloseButton(btn As Button, text As String)
    btn.Text = text

    ' Match the "small grey Close" style used in the code-built forms
    btn.AutoSize = True
    btn.Height = 32
    btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 14, 3, 3)
    btn.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Right

    btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleCenter
    btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 10.0F, FontStyle.Regular, GraphicsUnit.Point)

    ' Grey background, black text (standard button rendering)
    btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = True
    btn.BackColor = SystemColors.Control
    btn.ForeColor = Color.Black
    btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Standard
End Sub
```

### One small note
Because your buttons live in a **FlowLayoutPanel** stacked top-down, the Close button will be “small” but still left-aligned unless you explicitly right-align it. The `Anchor = Right` doesn’t always do what people expect inside a FlowLayoutPanel.

If you want it visually centered under the menu (common), tell me and I’ll adjust the FlowLayoutPanel settings or wrap the close button in a right-to-left FlowLayoutPanel so it sits nicely.
