        Dim archivePath As String = Path.Combine(MileageReportsDir, archiveName)

        Try
            If Not Directory.Exists(MileageReportsDir) Then
                Directory.CreateDirectory(MileageReportsDir)
            End If

            ' Always overwrite the "current" report (in the mileage folder)
            File.WriteAllLines(MileageRptPath, reportLines, Encoding.ASCII)

            ' Also write an archived copy (in the mileage folder)
            File.WriteAllLines(archivePath, reportLines, Encoding.ASCII)

            MessageBox.Show("Report created:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            MileageRptPath & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                            "Archived copy:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            archivePath,
                            "Print Mileage",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                            MessageBoxIcon.Information)
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show("Failed to create report: " & ex.Message, "Print Mileage", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Error)
        End Try
    End Sub

    ' Fixed-width, 80-column-safe text report (dot-matrix friendly)
    Private Function BuildMileageReportLines(rows As List(Of DataRow), beg As DateTime, endd As DateTime) As List(Of String)
        Dim lines As New List(Of String)()

        lines.Add("MILEAGE REPORT")
        lines.Add($"DATE RANGE: {beg:MM-dd-yyyy} TO {endd:MM-dd-yyyy}")
        lines.Add(New String("-"c, 80))

        lines.Add(PadRight("DATE", 10) & " " &
                  PadRight("DESTINATION", 28) & " " &
                  PadLeft("MILES", 5) & " " &
                  PadLeft("START", 6) & " " &
                  PadLeft("END", 6) & " " &
                  PadRight("PAY", 6) & " " &
                  "PURPOSE")
        lines.Add(New String("-"c, 80))

        Dim totalMiles As Integer = 0

        For Each r In rows
            Dim d = DirectCast(r("Date"), DateTime).ToString("MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
            Dim dest = SafeString(CStr(r("Destination")))
            Dim purpose = SafeString(CStr(r("BusinessPurpose")))
            Dim os = CInt(r("OdometerStart"))
            Dim oe = CInt(r("OdometerEnd"))
            Dim bm = CInt(r("BusinessMiles"))
            Dim pay = SafeString(CStr(r("PaymentType")))

            totalMiles += bm

            Dim line =
                PadRight(d, 10) & " " &
                PadRight(Trunc(dest, 28), 28) & " " &
                PadLeft(bm.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture), 5) & " " &
                PadLeft(os.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture), 6) & " " &
                PadLeft(oe.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture), 6) & " " &
                PadRight(Trunc(pay, 6), 6) & " " &
                Trunc(purpose, 80 - (10 + 1 + 28 + 1 + 5 + 1 + 6 + 1 + 6 + 1 + 6 + 1))

            lines.Add(line)
        Next

        lines.Add(New String("-"c, 80))
        lines.Add("TOTAL BUSINESS MILES: " & totalMiles.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture))
        lines.Add(New String("-"c, 80))

        Return lines
    End Function

    Private Shared Function PadRight(s As String, width As Integer) As String
        If s Is Nothing Then s = ""
        If s.Length >= width Then Return s.Substring(0, width)
        Return s.PadRight(width)
    End Function

    Private Shared Function PadLeft(s As String, width As Integer) As String
        If s Is Nothing Then s = ""
        If s.Length >= width Then Return s.Substring(0, width)
        Return s.PadLeft(width)
    End Function

    Private Shared Function Trunc(s As String, maxLen As Integer) As String
        If s Is Nothing Then Return ""
        If s.Length <= maxLen Then Return s
        Return s.Substring(0, maxLen)
    End Function

    ' === BASIC WRITE# compatibility for MILEAGE.DAT ===
    Private Shared Function ToBasicWriteLine(values As IEnumerable(Of String)) As String
        Dim parts As New List(Of String)()
        For Each v In values
            If v Is Nothing Then
                parts.Add("""""")
            Else
                Dim n As Integer
                If Integer.TryParse(v, NumberStyles.Integer, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, n) AndAlso
                   v.All(Function(ch) Char.IsDigit(ch) OrElse ch = "-"c) Then
                    parts.Add(n.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture))
                Else
                    Dim escaped = v.Replace("""", """""")
                    parts.Add("""" & escaped & """")
                End If
            End If
        Next
        Return String.Join(",", parts)
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseBasicWriteLine(line As String) As List(Of String)
        Dim result As New List(Of String)()
        Dim sb As New StringBuilder()
        Dim inQuotes As Boolean = False
        Dim i As Integer = 0

        While i < line.Length
            Dim ch = line(i)

            If inQuotes Then
                If ch = """"c Then
                    If i + 1 < line.Length AndAlso line(i + 1) = """"c Then
                        sb.Append(""""c)
                        i += 2
                    Else
                        inQuotes = False
                        i += 1
                    End If
                Else
                    sb.Append(ch)
                    i += 1
                End If
            Else
                If ch = ","c Then
                    result.Add(sb.ToString().Trim())
                    sb.Clear()
                    i += 1
                ElseIf ch = """"c Then
                    inQuotes = True
                    i += 1
                Else
                    sb.Append(ch)
                    i += 1
                End If
            End If
        End While

        result.Add(sb.ToString().Trim())
        Return result
    End Function

End Class
```

2014 IF LEFT$(Q$,7)<>"Testing" THEN 2000
2015 PRINT Q$:IF RIGHT$(Q$,2)="OK" THEN 2000
2020 RESET
2040 OPEN "NOTES-4.ED" FOR OUTPUT LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:Q$="BACKUP ERROR! on "+DATE$+" "+Q$:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1:OPEN "ERRORS.LST" FOR APPEND LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1:GOTO 490
2041 REM ############ ADD IN DAILY JOKE ##############
2042 PRINT:PRINT"Please Wait...":OPEN "JOKE.NUM" FOR INPUT AS 1:INPUT#1,JN:CLOSE 1:JN=JN+1:IF TODAY$="Sunday" OR TODAY$="Saturday" THEN 2060
2043 OPEN "JOKES.DAT" FOR INPUT AS 1:X=0
2044 IF EOF(1) THEN JN=1:RESET:GOTO 2046
2045 INPUT#1,Q$:X=X+1:IF X<>JN THEN 2044
2046 RESET:OPEN "NOTES-4.ED" FOR OUTPUT LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1
2047 OPEN "JOKE.NUM" FOR OUTPUT LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,JN:CLOSE 1
2060 IF JOKE<>1 THEN SHELL "UNPAID"
2065 JOKE=0:GOTO 490
2070 REM ##################################################################              ###############  Change Main Menu Message  ######################              ##################################################################
2080 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"<<< Change Main Menu Message >>>":PRINT:PRINT:PRINT"Current Message is:":PRINT:PRINT A1$:PRINT:PRINT:PRINT:PRINT"Enter New Message. [ENTER = Keep Current Message]  [N = No Message]":PRINT:PRINT"  ";
2090 FOR X=1 TO 129:PRINT"_";:NEXT:PRINT CHR$(30);CHR$(30):INPUT Q$
2100 IF LEN(Q$)>129 THEN 2080
2110 IF Q$="" THEN 20
2120 IF Q$="N" OR Q$="n" THEN Q$=""
2125 OPEN "MSGCHNG.DAT" FOR APPEND LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1
2130 OPEN "NOTES-4.ED" FOR OUTPUT LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1:GOTO 20
2140 REM ##################################################################              ###############  TYPE WRITTER MODE         ######################              ##################################################################
2150 LPRINT CHR$(27);"i";CHR$(1)
2160 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"<<< TYPE WRITTER MODE >>>      [TAB = Move to Right 5 Spaces]  [ESC = Exit]":PRINT:PRINT
2170 PRINT"....A....B....C....D....E....F....G....H....I....J....K....L....M....N....O....P"
2180 LN$="":COLOR 0,7:PRINT" ";:COLOR 7,0
2190 Q$=INKEY$:IF Q$="" THEN 2190
2200 IF Q$=CHR$(27) THEN LPRINT CHR$(27);"@":GOTO 490
2210 IF Q$=CHR$(9) THEN Q$="     ":REM *** TAB KEY ***
2220 IF Q$=CHR$(13) THEN LPRINT LN$:PRINT:GOTO 2160
2230 IF ASC(Q$)=8 AND LEN(LN$)=0 THEN 2190
2240 IF ASC(Q$)=8 THEN LN$=LEFT$(LN$,LEN(LN$)-1):PRINT:PRINT CHR$(30);TAB(80);:PRINT:PRINT CHR$(30);LN$;:COLOR 0,7:PRINT" ";:COLOR 7,0:GOTO 2190
2250 LN$=LN$+Q$
2260 IF LEN(LN$)>79 THEN LN$=(LEFT$(LN$,79))
2270 PRINT:PRINT CHR$(30);LN$;:COLOR 0,7:PRINT" ";:COLOR 7,0
2280 GOTO 2190
2290 REM ##################################################################              ####################   subliminal messages  ######################              ##################################################################
2300 INPUT "  Enter Password ";Q$:IF Q$="dean" OR Q$="DEAN" THEN 2320
2310 GOTO 490
2320 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"<<< SUBLIMINAL MESSAGE GENERATOR >>>":PRINT:PRINT:PRINT"Current message: ";
2330 OPEN "SUBLIMAL.MES" FOR INPUT AS 1:INPUT#1,MESS$:CLOSE 1:PRINT MESS$:PRINT:PRINT
2340 INPUT "Enter new message [Enter = Current Message] ";Q$:IF Q$="" THEN 490
2350 IF LEN(Q$)>35 THEN PRINT"Message too long.":GOTO 2340
2360 OPEN "SUBLIMAL.MES" FOR OUTPUT LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$:CLOSE 1
2370 OPEN "SUBLIMAL.LOG" FOR APPEND LOCK READ WRITE AS 1:WRITE#1,Q$;DATE$:CLOSE 1
2380 GOTO 490
2399 REM ##################################################################              ####################   BACKUP FILES TO D: DRIVE   ################              ##################################################################
2400 OPEN "D:\DDRIVE.CHK" FOR INPUT AS 1:CLOSE 1
2410 CLS:PRINT:PRINT"The computer is preparing to backup all the important data.":PRINT:PRINT"This process takes about 30 minutes.":PRINT
2420 SHELL"DT D: /D /M"
2430 SHELL"XCOPY PRC\*.PRC D:\PRC\*.PRC"
2440 SHELL"XCOPY *.DAT D:\"
2450 SHELL"XCOPY *.CUR D:\"
2460 SHELL"XCOPY *.TOT D:\"
2470 SHELL"XCOPY *.INV D:\"
2480 SHELL"XCOPY *.DEP D:\"
2490 SHELL"XCOPY *.CHK D:\"
2500 SHELL"XCOPY *.BAS D:\"
2501 SHELL"XCOPY *.PMS D:\ /M"
2510 SHELL"XCOPY BILLS.* D:\ /M"
2512 SHELL"XCOPY F:\WORD\*.DOC D:\WORD /M"
2520 Q$="XCOPY *."+RIGHT$(DATE$,2)+" D:\":SHELL Q$
2522 SHELL"XCOPY F:\PHONE\*.BAS D:\PHONE"
2523 SHELL"XCOPY F:\PHONE\*.LST D:\PHONE"
2524 SHELL"XCOPY F:\PHONE\*.EXE D:\PHONE"
2525 SHELL"XCOPY F:\PHONE\*.OVL D:\PHONE"
2530 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\0\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2531 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\1\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2532 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\2\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2533 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\3\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2534 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\4\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2535 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\5\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2536 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\6\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2537 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\7\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2538 SHELL"XCOPY F:\SHOPCARD\8\*.CRD D:\SHOPCARD\0 /A"
2539 SHELL"AUTOBOOT /NIL":REM ******* REBOOT SYSTEM AND GO TO TIMECARD ****
2540 OPEN "crdnumbr.dat" FOR INPUT AS 2
2550 INPUT#2,CDN$:CLOSE 2
2560 CDN=VAL(CDN$)
2570 X=CDN-480
2580 IF X<0 THEN X=3000+X
2590 IF X=CDN+1 THEN 490
2595 X$=STR$(X):X$=RIGHT$(X$,LEN(X$)-1)
2600 Q$="COPY F:\SHOPCARD\"+X$+".CRD D:\SHOPCARD":PRINT Q$
2605 SHELL Q$
2610 X=X+1:IF X>3000 THEN X=1
2620 GOTO 2590
2699 REM ##################################################################              ####################   CALC THE DAY OF THE WEEK   ################              ##################################################################
2710 DAYS31$(0)="Saturday":DAYS31$(1)="Sunday":DAYS31$(2)="Monday":DAYS31$(3)="Tuesday":DAYS31$(4)="Wednesday":DAYS31$(5)="Thursday":DAYS31$(6)="Friday"
2720 S31$=DATE$
2730 PS31=INSTR(S31$,"-"):MONTH31= VAL(LEFT$(S31$,PS31-1)):  S31$= MID$(S31$,PS31+1)
2740 PS31=INSTR(S31$,"-"):  DAY =  VAL(LEFT$(S31$,PS31-1)):  S31$= MID$(S31$,PS31+1)
2750 YEAR31=VAL(S31$)
2760 IF MONTH31>2 THEN 2780
2770 MONTH31=MONTH31+12 : YEAR31=YEAR31-1
2780 N31=DAY+2*MONTH31+INT(.6*(MONTH31+1))+YEAR31+INT(YEAR31/4)-INT(YEAR31/100)+INT(YEAR31/400)+  2
2790 N31=INT((N31/7-INT(N31/7))*7+.5)
2800 TODAY$=DAYS31$(N31)
2810 RETURN


## Copilot

Yes — the **Personal Calendar** button (`Z`) in `MAINMENU.BAS` runs **PMS**:

- Line **612** shows the menu label: `(Z) Personal Calendar`
- Line **955** is the action:

```basic
IF Q$="Z" THEN SHELL"PMS /D /B":GOTO 490
```

So it does **not** `CHAIN` to another `.BAS` file; it shells out to an external program named **`PMS`** with arguments **`/D /B`** (likely `PMS.EXE` / `PMS.COM` somewhere on PATH or in the current directory).

Related: the screen-blanker also launches it if a key is pressed:
- Line **1560**: `OPEN "C:\PMS.ED" ... : SHELL"PMS /D /B"`

### What we need next for the VB.NET “Personal Calendar” window
To populate it with the same data PMS used, we need to know where PMS stores its data files.

From `MAINMENU.BAS`, the only direct clue is **`C:\PMS.ED`** (a file that’s opened just to check existence). That suggests PMS uses `C:\PMS.*` files and/or `PMS.ED` as a config/data marker.

If you can, please provide one of these and I’ll map the data format:
1) A directory listing of `C:\` on the old machine for files starting with `PMS` (e.g., `PMS.*`), or
2) Contents of `C:\PMS.ED`, or
3) The PMS executable location and any accompanying `.DAT`/`.TXT` files (often in the same folder as the EXE).

Personal Calendar (Z) is the **PMS** program, and the data it likely uses is exactly those files in `C:\`:

- `C:\PMS.EXE` (the program)
- `C:\PMS.DAT` (main data)
- `C:\DATES.PMS` (date index / date list)
- `C:\PMS.NUM` (counter/record pointer)

Next step is figuring out the **record format** so we can load it into your VB.NET Personal Calendar window.

## Copilot

That `PMS.DAT` format is plain-text and it’s **GW-BASIC `WRITE#` style** (quoted strings + comma-separated fields). Each record appears to be:

1. **Date** as `MM-dd-yyyy` (string)
2. **Text/Description** (string; can be blank)
3. **Number** (numeric) — `28` in your samples

So the Personal Calendar button (PMS) is almost certainly a simple “date + note + attribute” calendar.

## What I need next to finish mapping the schema
Please paste:

1) **All of `DATES.PMS`** (or at least the first 30 lines)
2) **All of `PMS.NUM`**

Those two will tell us:
- whether `PMS.DAT` is the only data file, or if `DATES.PMS` is a sorted index
- what the `28` means (often “color”, “priority”, “days before”, “category id”, etc.)
- whether there are multiple entries per date and how they’re linked

## In the meantime: what we can already implement in VB.NET
Once you confirm where your VB.NET app should read from (likely `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\...` vs `C:\...`), we can implement:

- Load all lines from `PMS.DAT`

- Store rows in a table with columns:
  - `Date` (DateTime)

  - `Code` (Integer)  ‘the 28’
- Display in the Personal Calendar window with:
  - a MonthCalendar control
  - a list/grid showing entries for the selected date
  - highlight/bold dates that have an entry

## Copilot

Thanks—this tells us a lot.

## What `DATES.PMS` is
It’s also GW-BASIC `WRITE#` style text lines, but it’s **not the same schema as `PMS.DAT`**.

### Line 1: header / config
```
"1111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111111",1,31
```
That looks like:
- a 60-character string of `1`’s (very likely a **month day mask** / “days in month” template / UI ruler)
- followed by `1,31` (very likely **first day index** and **days in month**, or simply `minDay,maxDay`)

We won’t rely on that yet; we can treat it as metadata.

### Remaining lines: (Date, Text)
Every other line is:
```
"MM-dd-yyyy","some text..."
```
So `DATES.PMS` is essentially a **flat list of calendar entries**, where **multiple entries can share the same date** (as you can see with repeated `01-28-1993`, `02-28-1993`, etc.).

This is the file your Personal Calendar UI probably wants to display: *select a date → show all messages for that date*.

## Relationship between `PMS.DAT` and `DATES.PMS`
Right now, `PMS.DAT` looks more like a **summary list** or a different feature (maybe birthdays/anniversaries with a “remind X days before” code like `28`), while `DATES.PMS` is the **actual per-day event list**.

To be sure, we still need `PMS.NUM` (it may contain the record count / last index used for `DATES.PMS`).

## Next question (important)
Where should the Windows app read/write these files?

- **Keep legacy location**: `C:\DATES.PMS`, `C:\PMS.DAT`, `C:\PMS.NUM`
- **Move into the share** (recommended for consistency with the rest of your port): something like `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\DATES.PMS` (or a subfolder)

Which do you want?

## What I can implement next (once you answer location + give PMS.NUM)
In the VB.NET Personal Calendar window:
1) Load `DATES.PMS`
2) Parse into `(Date, Text)` rows
3) Bold/mark dates on a `MonthCalendar` that have entries
4) When a date is selected, list all entries for that date in a grid/list
5) (Optional) add Add/Edit/Delete and save back in the same `WRITE#`-compatible format

Please paste **`PMS.NUM`** contents next and tell me **which path** to use (C:\ vs `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\...`).

## @testsubject117

entire contents of pms.num: 0,"09-27-1993",171

## Copilot

Perfect. `PMS.NUM` is clearly a small state file in `WRITE#` format too:

```
0,"09-27-1993",171
```

That reads as 3 fields:
1) `0` (likely a mode flag, or “current index” / “current month offset”)
2) `"09-27-1993"` (likely “last date viewed/used”)
3) `171` (very likely “number of entries” or “last record number”)

Given your `DATES.PMS` has a header line plus many event lines, `171` being a count is plausible.

## What we should do next
I can now wire up the VB.NET **Personal Calendar** window to:

- Load **`DATES.PMS`** into a list of entries `(Date, Text)`
- Use the **MonthCalendar** to select a date
- Show all entries for that date
- Bold dates that have entries
- Optionally: default the calendar to the date stored in `PMS.NUM` (`09-27-1993`)

### One decision before I generate code
Where do you want the Windows app to read/write these PMS files?

**A)** Keep legacy paths (matches PMS.EXE):
- `C:\DATES.PMS`
- `C:\PMS.DAT`
- `C:\PMS.NUM`

**B)** Move into the share (matches your other modernized modules):
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\DATES.PMS`
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\PMS.DAT`
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\PMS.NUM`
(or possibly a `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\pms\` folder)

Which one? If you choose **B**, tell me whether you want a `pms` subfolder.

### Proposed paths (consistent with the other modules)
Unless you want a subfolder, I’ll use:

- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\DATES.PMS`
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\PMS.DAT`
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\PMS.NUM`

And we’ll follow the same rules as Mileage:
- create the files if missing (empty / minimal header as needed)
- keep them in GW-BASIC `WRITE#` compatible format (quoted strings, commas)

### Next thing I need
What is the VB.NET form/class/file name for the Personal Calendar window in your project?

Examples:
- `FormPersonalCalendar.vb`
- `FormCalendar.vb`
- `PersonalCalendar.vb`

If you don’t know, paste either:
1) the code where the **Personal Calendar** button/menu click opens the window, **or**
2) the files list / Solution Explorer snippet around “Calendar”.

Once I have the form file, I’ll regenerate it (or provide a full replacement) to:
- load `DATES.PMS`
- bold dates with entries
- show entries for the selected date
- default to the last date from `PMS.NUM` if present

## @testsubject117

```
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormPersonalCalendar

    Public Sub New()
        InitializeComponent()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.Text = "Personal Calendar"
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.MinimumSize = New Size(640, 420)

        Dim pnl As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .Padding = New Padding(16),
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 4
        }
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' header
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' body
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100)) ' filler
        pnl.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' buttons

        Dim lblHeader As New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 16.0F, FontStyle.Bold),
            .Text = "Personal Calendar"
        }

        Dim lblBody As New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 11.0F, FontStyle.Regular),
            .Text = "This screen is ready to be built." & Environment.NewLine &
                    "Press ESC or click Close to return to the Main Menu."
        }

        Dim btnClose As New Button() With {
            .Text = "Close",
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Anchor = AnchorStyles.Right
        }
        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Me.Close()

        Dim buttonPanel As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.RightToLeft,
            .AutoSize = True,
            .WrapContents = False
        }
        buttonPanel.Controls.Add(btnClose)

        pnl.Controls.Add(lblHeader, 0, 0)
        pnl.Controls.Add(lblBody, 0, 1)
        pnl.Controls.Add(New Panel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill}, 0, 2)
        pnl.Controls.Add(buttonPanel, 0, 3)

        Me.Controls.Clear()
        Me.Controls.Add(pnl)
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnKeyDown(e As KeyEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnKeyDown(e)
        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Escape Then
            Me.Close()
            e.Handled = True
        End If
    End Sub

End Class
```
The file we currently have is FormPersonalCalendar.vb, here's it's content: 

## Copilot

```vbnet name=FormPersonalCalendar.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Globalization
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Text
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormPersonalCalendar

    ' Keep data under the share (per your instruction)
    Private Const DataDir As String = "\\invoice\MainMenu\Data"

    Private ReadOnly DatesPmsPath As String = Path.Combine(DataDir, "DATES.PMS")
    Private ReadOnly PmsDatPath As String = Path.Combine(DataDir, "PMS.DAT")
    Private ReadOnly PmsNumPath As String = Path.Combine(DataDir, "PMS.NUM")

    Private _cal As MonthCalendar = Nothing
    Private _grid As DataGridView = Nothing
    Private _lblStatus As Label = Nothing

    Private _entries As List(Of CalendarEntry) = New List(Of CalendarEntry)()

    Public Sub New()
        InitializeComponent()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.Text = "Personal Calendar"
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.MinimumSize = New Size(900, 560)

        If Not EnsureDataDirExistsFriendly() Then
            Me.Close()
            Return
        End If

        EnsurePmsFilesExist()

        BuildUi()
        LoadAllData()
        ApplyBoldedDates()
        JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()
        RefreshSelectedDateGrid()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnKeyDown(e As KeyEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnKeyDown(e)

        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Escape Then
            Me.Close()
            e.Handled = True
            Return
        End If

        If e.Control AndAlso e.KeyCode = Keys.R Then
            LoadAllData()
            ApplyBoldedDates()
            RefreshSelectedDateGrid()
            e.Handled = True
            Return
        End If
    End Sub

    ' === UI ===
    Private Sub BuildUi()
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .Padding = New Padding(12),
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 3
        }
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

        Dim lblHeader As New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 16.0F, FontStyle.Bold),
            .Text = "Personal Calendar"
        }

        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1
        }
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
        body.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        _cal = New MonthCalendar() With {
            .MaxSelectionCount = 1
        }
        AddHandler _cal.DateSelected, Sub() RefreshSelectedDateGrid()
        AddHandler _cal.DateChanged, Sub() RefreshSelectedDateGrid()

        _grid = New DataGridView() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ReadOnly = True,
            .AllowUserToAddRows = False,
            .AllowUserToDeleteRows = False,
            .SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect,
            .MultiSelect = False,
            .AutoSizeColumnsMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnsMode.Fill
        }

        _grid.Columns.Clear()
        _grid.Columns.Add(New DataGridViewTextBoxColumn() With {.Name = "Date", .HeaderText = "Date"})
        _grid.Columns.Add(New DataGridViewTextBoxColumn() With {.Name = "Text", .HeaderText = "Text"})

        _grid.Columns("Date").Width = 110
        _grid.Columns("Date").AutoSizeMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnMode.None

        body.Controls.Add(_cal, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(_grid, 1, 0)

        Dim footer As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 3,
            .RowCount = 1
        }
        footer.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
        footer.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        footer.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

        _lblStatus = New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Text = "Ctrl+R = Reload   Esc = Close"
        }

        Dim btnReload As New Button() With {.Text = "Reload (Ctrl+R)", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnReload.Click,
            Sub()
                LoadAllData()
                ApplyBoldedDates()
                RefreshSelectedDateGrid()
            End Sub

        Dim btnClose As New Button() With {.Text = "Close (Esc)", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Me.Close()

        footer.Controls.Add(_lblStatus, 0, 0)
        footer.Controls.Add(btnReload, 1, 0)
        footer.Controls.Add(btnClose, 2, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(lblHeader, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 1)
        root.Controls.Add(footer, 0, 2)

        Me.Controls.Clear()
        Me.Controls.Add(root)
    End Sub

    ' === Data model ===
    Private NotInheritable Class CalendarEntry
        Public Property [Date] As DateTime
        Public Property Text As String
    End Class

    ' === File/folder checks ===
    Private Function EnsureDataDirExistsFriendly() As Boolean
        Try
            If Directory.Exists(DataDir) Then Return True

            MessageBox.Show("Personal Calendar cannot open because the data folder is not available:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            DataDir & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                            "Please run on the VM or connect to the share.",
                            "Personal Calendar",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                            MessageBoxIcon.Error)
            Return False
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show("Personal Calendar cannot access the data folder:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            DataDir & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                            ex.Message,
                            "Personal Calendar",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                            MessageBoxIcon.Error)
            Return False
        End Try
    End Function

    Private Sub EnsurePmsFilesExist()
        Try
            If Not File.Exists(DatesPmsPath) Then
                ' Minimal header line similar to what you showed (mask + 1,31).
                ' PMS likely expects it, so we keep it.
                File.WriteAllText(DatesPmsPath,
                                  ToBasicWriteLine(New String() {New String("1"c, 60), "1", "31"}) & Environment.NewLine,
                                  Encoding.ASCII)
            End If

            If Not File.Exists(PmsDatPath) Then
                File.WriteAllText(PmsDatPath, "", Encoding.ASCII)
            End If

            If Not File.Exists(PmsNumPath) Then
                ' Default: flag 0, lastDate = today, count 0
                File.WriteAllText(PmsNumPath,
                                  ToBasicWriteLine(New String() {"0", DateTime.Today.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture), "0"}) & Environment.NewLine,
                                  Encoding.ASCII)
            End If
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show("Could not create/open PMS files in:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            DataDir & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                            ex.Message,
                            "Personal Calendar",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                            MessageBoxIcon.Error)
            Me.Close()
        End Try
    End Sub

    ' === Load/parse ===
    Private Sub LoadAllData()
        _entries.Clear()

        ' Primary events file is DATES.PMS (based on your sample: "MM-dd-yyyy","text")
        LoadDatesPmsEntries()

        _lblStatus.Text = $"Loaded entries: {_entries.Count}    Ctrl+R = Reload   Esc = Close"
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDatesPmsEntries()
        If Not File.Exists(DatesPmsPath) Then Return

        Dim lines = File.ReadAllLines(DatesPmsPath, Encoding.ASCII).
            Select(Function(l) StripDosEofAndControls(l).Trim()).
            Where(Function(l) l.Length > 0).
            ToList()

        If lines.Count = 0 Then Return

        Dim startIndex As Integer = 0

        ' If first line looks like the header: 3 fields (string, number, number) and the string is all 1s
        Dim headerParts = ParseBasicWriteLine(lines(0))
        If headerParts.Count >= 3 Then
            Dim mask = headerParts(0)
            If Not String.IsNullOrEmpty(mask) AndAlso mask.All(Function(ch) ch = "1"c) Then
                startIndex = 1
            End If
        End If

        For i As Integer = startIndex To lines.Count - 1
            Dim parts = ParseBasicWriteLine(lines(i))
            If parts.Count < 2 Then Continue For

            Dim dtText = parts(0)
            Dim txt = parts(1)

            Dim dt As DateTime
            If DateTime.TryParseExact(dtText, "MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, DateTimeStyles.None, dt) Then
                _entries.Add(New CalendarEntry With {
                    .Date = dt.Date,
                    .Text = If(txt, "").Trim()
                })
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub ApplyBoldedDates()
        If _cal Is Nothing Then Return

        ' MonthCalendar only supports "bolded dates" by giving it an array.
        ' We'll bold every date that has at least one entry.
        Dim boldDates = _entries.
            Select(Function(e) e.Date).
            Distinct().
            OrderBy(Function(d) d).
            ToArray()

        _cal.BoldedDates = boldDates
        _cal.UpdateBoldedDates()
    End Sub

    Private Sub JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()
        Try
            If Not File.Exists(PmsNumPath) Then Return

            Dim raw = StripDosEofAndControls(File.ReadAllText(PmsNumPath, Encoding.ASCII)).Trim()
            If raw.Length = 0 Then Return

            ' Only care about second field: "MM-dd-yyyy"
            Dim parts = ParseBasicWriteLine(raw)
            If parts.Count < 2 Then Return

            Dim dtText = parts(1)
            Dim dt As DateTime
            If DateTime.TryParseExact(dtText, "MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, DateTimeStyles.None, dt) Then
                _cal.SetDate(dt.Date)
            End If
        Catch
            ' Non-fatal: ignore and just show today
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub RefreshSelectedDateGrid()
        If _grid Is Nothing OrElse _cal Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim selected = _cal.SelectionStart.Date

        Dim todays = _entries.
            Where(Function(e) e.Date = selected).
            OrderBy(Function(e) e.Text).
            ToList()

        _grid.Rows.Clear()
        For Each e In todays
            _grid.Rows.Add(e.Date.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture), e.Text)
        Next
    End Sub

    ' === BASIC WRITE# compatibility helpers (same idea as Mileage module) ===
    Private Shared Function StripDosEofAndControls(s As String) As String
        If s Is Nothing Then Return ""
        Dim sb As New StringBuilder(s.Length)
        For Each ch As Char In s
            If Char.IsControl(ch) Then Continue For
            sb.Append(ch)
        Next
        Return sb.ToString()
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ToBasicWriteLine(values As IEnumerable(Of String)) As String
        Dim parts As New List(Of String)()
        For Each v In values
            If v Is Nothing Then
                parts.Add("""""")
            Else
                Dim n As Integer
                If Integer.TryParse(v, NumberStyles.Integer, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, n) AndAlso
                   v.All(Function(ch) Char.IsDigit(ch) OrElse ch = "-"c) Then
                    parts.Add(n.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture))
                Else
                    Dim escaped = v.Replace("""", """""")
                    parts.Add("""" & escaped & """")
                End If
            End If
        Next
        Return String.Join(",", parts)
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseBasicWriteLine(line As String) As List(Of String)
        Dim result As New List(Of String)()
        Dim sb As New StringBuilder()
        Dim inQuotes As Boolean = False
        Dim i As Integer = 0

        While i < line.Length
            Dim ch = line(i)

            If inQuotes Then
                If ch = """"c Then
                    If i + 1 < line.Length AndAlso line(i + 1) = """"c Then
                        sb.Append(""""c)
                        i += 2
                    Else
                        inQuotes = False
                        i += 1
                    End If
                Else
                    sb.Append(ch)
                    i += 1
                End If
            Else
                If ch = ","c Then
                    result.Add(sb.ToString().Trim())
                    sb.Clear()
                    i += 1
                ElseIf ch = """"c Then
                    inQuotes = True
                    i += 1
                Else
                    sb.Append(ch)
                    i += 1
                End If
            End If
        End While

        result.Add(sb.ToString().Trim())
        Return result
    End Function

End Class
```

## @testsubject117

what files do I need to copy to \\invoice\mainmenu\data for the personal calendar

## Copilot

Copy these **three** files (from `C:\` on the old system) into `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\`:

1) `PMS.DAT`  
2) `DATES.PMS`  
3) `PMS.NUM`

`PMS.EXE` is **not required** for the VB.NET Personal Calendar window (we’re reading the data files directly), but you can archive it elsewhere if you want.

## @testsubject117

ok, how can I test the new personal calendar and make sure it works like the old one?

## Copilot

To test the new Personal Calendar against the old one, do this in two phases: **data loading/parsing** and **behavior matching**.

## 1) Prep: put the same data in the new location
1. Copy these from the old machine `C:\` → the share `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\`:
   - `DATES.PMS`
   - `PMS.DAT`
   - `PMS.NUM`
2. Confirm the files on the share are readable (open `DATES.PMS` in Notepad from the VM).

## 2) Test the new VB.NET Personal Calendar
1. Launch your Windows app.
2. Open **Personal Calendar**.
3. Expected results:
   - The calendar opens without errors.
   - The calendar jumps to the **last date** stored in `PMS.NUM` (example you gave: `09-27-1993`).
   - Dates that have entries in `DATES.PMS` should appear **bold** on the calendar.
   - Click a bold date (like `01-28-1993`, `03-03-1993`, `12-28-1993`) and confirm the right-side list shows **all entries** for that date (including duplicates if they exist in the file).

### Quick sanity checks using your sample data
- `01-28-1993` should show many “event ------------------- 1” rows
- `03-03-1993` should show 5 different short strings
- `12-28-1993` should show multiple entries including `"*********************************************************!"`

If any of those don’t appear, it’s almost always a parsing/date-format issue.

## 3) Compare to the old PMS behavior
On a machine that can still run it:
1. Run the legacy command the menu used:
   - `PMS /D /B`
2. Compare these behaviors:
   - Does it start on the same “current/last” date? (from `PMS.NUM`)
   - For a chosen date, does it show the same list of text entries (count + content)?
   - Does it show multiple entries per day? (it should)

### What will *not* match yet (by design)
The VB.NET window currently:
- is **read-only** (no add/edit/delete)
- doesn’t use `PMS.DAT` for display (we’re displaying `DATES.PMS` entries, which matches what you showed as the real per-date text list)
- doesn’t replicate the exact old UI/keyboard commands beyond basic navigation

## 4) If you want an exact match, I need one screenshot/detail
When you open PMS, what is its “main screen” like?
- month view with highlighted days?
- list view?
- does it show a single entry or multiple?
- any categories/colors tied to that `28` field in `PMS.DAT`?

If you can send a screenshot (or describe the screen), I can align the VB.NET behavior much closer (including editing and saving in the same file formats).

## @testsubject117

01-28-1993 shows nothing, 03-03-1193 shows nothing, 12-28-1993 shows nothing. Some of the Holidays are showing in each month, but that's all.

## Copilot

Those symptoms point to one thing: the control is showing **Windows’ built-in holidays (BoldedDates/AnnuallyBoldedDates)**, but **our entries from `DATES.PMS` aren’t being bolded/loaded**, so selecting those dates shows nothing.

Most likely causes (in order):

1) Your app is still reading an **empty/new `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\DATES.PMS`** (because the real file didn’t get copied, or got copied to a different folder/case), so it created a default header and has no events.
2) The share path is different than the one the app is using (`\\invoice\MainMenu\Data` vs `\\invoice\mainmenu\data` usually still works, but if it’s actually a different share/root, it won’t).
3) The file has **non-ASCII / weird characters** and our current `StripDosEofAndControls` is accidentally stripping newlines when reading (less likely, but possible).
4) Date typo: you wrote `03-03-1193` in the test (should be `1993`), but that wouldn’t explain the other dates.

### Fastest way to pinpoint it (2-minute check)
On the machine where you run the VB.NET app:

1) Open `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\DATES.PMS` in Notepad.
   - Do you see all those `"01-28-1993","event ..."` lines?
   - Or do you only see a single header line of `"111111....",1,31`?

If you **only see the header**, then the app created a new file and you’re not looking at the copied data.

2) Confirm the three files exist in *exactly* this folder:
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\DATES.PMS`
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\PMS.NUM`
- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\PMS.DAT`

And confirm file sizes are non-trivial (DATES.PMS should not be ~70 bytes).

### One code issue I do want to fix (likely the real culprit)
My helper `StripDosEofAndControls` removes *all* control characters — that includes **CR/LF**, and we use it in `LoadDatesPmsEntries()` *before* parsing. In your mileage module we stripped per-line after `ReadAllLines`, but here we do it too; it shouldn’t break `ReadAllLines`, but it can break `PMS.NUM` parsing and any line endings embedded strangely.

More importantly: the holidays showing means the calendar is fine; it’s just not bolding our data. To make this bulletproof, we should:

- Stop stripping CR/LF entirely (only strip DOS EOF `Chr(26)` and maybe tabs)
- Add a status line that shows **which file path is being loaded** and **how many entries** we parsed
- Add a temporary debug MessageBox if parsed count is 0

### Quick patch you can apply now (no repo tools needed)
In `FormPersonalCalendar.vb`, replace `StripDosEofAndControls` with a safer version:

```vbnet
Private Shared Function StripDosEofOnly(s As String) As String
    If s Is Nothing Then Return ""
    ' Remove DOS EOF (Ctrl+Z) only; keep normal whitespace and punctuation intact.
    Return s.Replace(ChrW(26), "")
End Function
```

Then update the two call sites:

- In `LoadDatesPmsEntries`:
  - change: `StripDosEofAndControls(l)` → `StripDosEofOnly(l)`
- In `JumpToLastDateIfAvailable`:
  - change: `StripDosEofAndControls(File.ReadAllText(...))` → `StripDosEofOnly(...)`

Also update the status label after load to show the file path:

```vbnet
_lblStatus.Text = $"Loaded entries: {_entries.Count} from {DatesPmsPath}"
```

### Before you change code, answer these 2 questions
1) When you open `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data\DATES.PMS` in Notepad, do you see your events or only the header line?
2) What is the exact folder you copied the files into (copy/paste the Explorer address bar text)?

Once you confirm that, I can tell you whether it’s a **data location/copy** issue or a **parser** issue, and give you the exact final fix.

## @testsubject117

when I open dates.pms I see a list of dates. the exact folder is \\invoice\mainmenu\data. Also, there are errors now.

## Copilot

The reason you’re seeing **only Holidays** (and none of your `DATES.PMS` entries) is almost certainly that the app is **creating/reading a different folder than the one you copied to**.

In the code I gave you, the path is hard-coded as:

- `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data`

…but you copied to:

- `\\invoice\mainmenu\data`

On Windows SMB shares this is *often* case-insensitive, but **it is not guaranteed to be the same share/root in all environments** (especially if there are multiple shares/aliases). The giveaway is: you *do* see a list of dates in the file you opened, yet the app sees zero entries and falls back to just holidays.

And you mentioned “there are errors now” — that’s consistent with the app failing to find/parse the files it expects and/or failing to create them due to permissions in the other location.

## Do this first (no code changes yet)
1) In File Explorer, go to **exactly**:
   - `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data`
2) Do you see `DATES.PMS`, `PMS.NUM`, `PMS.DAT` there?

If you **do not**, copy the three files into that exact folder and test again.

If you **do**, then the issue is parsing or runtime error, and I need the error details.

## I need the error text to fix the crash
Please paste:
- the **exact error message**, and
- the **stack trace**, or at least the line that mentions `FormPersonalCalendar.vb` and the method name.

If it’s a MessageBox error instead of a crash, paste the full text.

## Recommended fix (to prevent this mismatch permanently)
Pick ONE canonical path and use it everywhere. Since you’re consistently using lower-case in your ops steps, the simplest is to change the constant to match what you’re actually using:

```vbnet
Private Const DataDir As String = "\\invoice\mainmenu\data"
```

(Or even better: read it from a config setting later.)

But before you change anything, confirm whether `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data` and `\\invoice\mainmenu\data` are actually the same place on your system by checking whether creating a small test file in one shows up in the other.

### Quick check
Create `\\invoice\mainmenu\data\_case_test.txt` with “test”.
Then look in `\\invoice\MainMenu\Data` for `_case_test.txt`.

- If it **doesn’t** appear → they are different paths/shares, and we must standardize.
- If it **does** appear → path isn’t the issue, and we focus on the error/parse.

Send me the error text + result of the `_case_test.txt` check and I’ll give you the exact fix.

```
Option Strict On

Imports System

Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Globalization
Imports System.IO

Public Class FormPersonalCalendar

    ' Keep data under the share (per your instruction)

    Private ReadOnly PmsDatPath As String = Path.Combine(DataDir, "PMS.DAT")
    Private ReadOnly PmsNumPath As String = Path.Combine(DataDir, "PMS.NUM")

    Private _cal As MonthCalendar = Nothing
    Private _grid As DataGridView = Nothing
    Private _lblStatus As Label = Nothing

    Private _entries As List(Of CalendarEntry) = New List(Of CalendarEntry)()

    Public Sub New()
        InitializeComponent()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.Text = "Personal Calendar"
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.MinimumSize = New Size(900, 560)

        If Not EnsureDataDirExistsFriendly() Then
            Me.Close()
            Return
        End If

        EnsurePmsFilesExist()

        BuildUi()
        LoadAllData()
        ApplyBoldedDates()
        JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()
        RefreshSelectedDateGrid()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnKeyDown(e As KeyEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnKeyDown(e)

        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Escape Then
            Me.Close()
            e.Handled = True
            Return
        End If

        If e.Control AndAlso e.KeyCode = Keys.R Then
            LoadAllData()
            ApplyBoldedDates()
            RefreshSelectedDateGrid()
            e.Handled = True
            Return
        End If
    End Sub

    ' === UI ===
    Private Sub BuildUi()
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .Padding = New Padding(12),
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 3
        }

        Dim lblHeader As New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 16.0F, FontStyle.Bold),
            .Text = "Personal Calendar"
        }

        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1
        }
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
        body.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        _cal = New MonthCalendar() With {
            .MaxSelectionCount = 1
        }
        AddHandler _cal.DateSelected, Sub() RefreshSelectedDateGrid()
        AddHandler _cal.DateChanged, Sub() RefreshSelectedDateGrid()

        _grid = New DataGridView() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ReadOnly = True,
            .AllowUserToAddRows = False,
            .AllowUserToDeleteRows = False,
            .SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect,
            .MultiSelect = False,
            .AutoSizeColumnsMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnsMode.Fill
        }

        _grid.Columns.Clear()
        _grid.Columns.Add(New DataGridViewTextBoxColumn() With {.Name = "Date", .HeaderText = "Date"})
        _grid.Columns.Add(New DataGridViewTextBoxColumn() With {.Name = "Text", .HeaderText = "Text"})

        _grid.Columns("Date").Width = 110
        _grid.Columns("Date").AutoSizeMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnMode.None

        body.Controls.Add(_cal, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(_grid, 1, 0)

        Dim footer As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 3,
            .RowCount = 1
        }
        footer.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
        footer.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        footer.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

        _lblStatus = New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Text = "Ctrl+R = Reload   Esc = Close"
        }

        Dim btnReload As New Button() With {.Text = "Reload (Ctrl+R)", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnReload.Click,
            Sub()
                LoadAllData()
                ApplyBoldedDates()
                RefreshSelectedDateGrid()
            End Sub

        Dim btnClose As New Button() With {.Text = "Close (Esc)", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Me.Close()

        footer.Controls.Add(_lblStatus, 0, 0)
        footer.Controls.Add(btnReload, 1, 0)
        footer.Controls.Add(btnClose, 2, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(lblHeader, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 1)
        root.Controls.Add(footer, 0, 2)

        Me.Controls.Clear()
        Me.Controls.Add(root)
    End Sub

    ' === Data model ===
    Private NotInheritable Class CalendarEntry
        Public Property [Date] As DateTime
        Public Property Text As String
    End Class

    ' === File/folder checks ===
    Private Function EnsureDataDirExistsFriendly() As Boolean
        Try
            If Directory.Exists(DataDir) Then Return True

            MessageBox.Show("Personal Calendar cannot open because the data folder is not available:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            DataDir & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                            "Please run on the VM or connect to the share.",
                            "Personal Calendar",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                            MessageBoxIcon.Error)
            Return False
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show("Personal Calendar cannot access the data folder:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            DataDir & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                            ex.Message,
                            "Personal Calendar",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                            MessageBoxIcon.Error)
            Return False
        End Try
    End Function

    Private Sub EnsurePmsFilesExist()
        Try
            If Not File.Exists(DatesPmsPath) Then
                ' Minimal header line similar to what you showed (mask + 1,31).
                ' PMS likely expects it, so we keep it.
                File.WriteAllText(DatesPmsPath,
                                  ToBasicWriteLine(New String() {New String("1"c, 60), "1", "31"}) & Environment.NewLine,
                                  Encoding.ASCII)
            End If

            If Not File.Exists(PmsDatPath) Then
                File.WriteAllText(PmsDatPath, "", Encoding.ASCII)
            End If

            If Not File.Exists(PmsNumPath) Then
                ' Default: flag 0, lastDate = today, count 0
                File.WriteAllText(PmsNumPath,
                                  ToBasicWriteLine(New String() {"0", DateTime.Today.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture), "0"}) & Environment.NewLine,
                                  Encoding.ASCII)
            End If
        Catch ex As Exception
            MessageBox.Show("Could not create/open PMS files in:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            DataDir & Environment.NewLine & Environment.NewLine &
                            ex.Message,
                            "Personal Calendar",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
                            MessageBoxIcon.Error)
            Me.Close()
        End Try
    End Sub

    ' === Load/parse ===
    Private Sub LoadAllData()
        _entries.Clear()

        ' Primary events file is DATES.PMS (based on your sample: "MM-dd-yyyy","text")
        LoadDatesPmsEntries()

        _lblStatus.Text = $"Loaded entries: {_entries.Count}    Ctrl+R = Reload   Esc = Close"
    End Sub

    Private Sub LoadDatesPmsEntries()
        If Not File.Exists(DatesPmsPath) Then Return

        Dim lines = File.ReadAllLines(DatesPmsPath, Encoding.ASCII).
            Select(Function(l) StripDosEofOnly(l).Trim()).
            Where(Function(l) l.Length > 0).
            ToList()

        If lines.Count = 0 Then Return

        Dim startIndex As Integer = 0

        ' If first line looks like the header: 3 fields (string, number, number) and the string is all 1s
        Dim headerParts = ParseBasicWriteLine(lines(0))
        If headerParts.Count >= 3 Then
            Dim mask = headerParts(0)
            If Not String.IsNullOrEmpty(mask) AndAlso mask.All(Function(ch) ch = "1"c) Then
                startIndex = 1
            End If
        End If

        For i As Integer = startIndex To lines.Count - 1
            Dim parts = ParseBasicWriteLine(lines(i))
            If parts.Count < 2 Then Continue For

            Dim dtText = parts(0)
            Dim txt = parts(1)

            Dim dt As DateTime
            If DateTime.TryParseExact(dtText, "MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, DateTimeStyles.None, dt) Then
                _entries.Add(New CalendarEntry With {
                    .Date = dt.Date,
                    .Text = If(txt, "").Trim()
                })
            End If
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub ApplyBoldedDates()
        If _cal Is Nothing Then Return

        ' MonthCalendar only supports "bolded dates" by giving it an array.
        ' We'll bold every date that has at least one entry.
        Dim boldDates = _entries.
            Select(Function(e) e.Date).
            Distinct().
            OrderBy(Function(d) d).
            ToArray()

        _cal.BoldedDates = boldDates
        _cal.UpdateBoldedDates()
    End Sub

    Private Sub JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()
        Try
            If Not File.Exists(PmsNumPath) Then Return

            Dim raw = StripDosEofOnly(PmsNumPath, Encoding.ASCII)).Trim()
            If raw.Length = 0 Then Return

            ' Only care about second field: "MM-dd-yyyy"
            Dim parts = ParseBasicWriteLine(raw)
            If parts.Count < 2 Then Return

            Dim dtText = parts(1)
            Dim dt As DateTime
            If DateTime.TryParseExact(dtText, "MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, DateTimeStyles.None, dt) Then
                _cal.SetDate(dt.Date)
            End If
        Catch
            ' Non-fatal: ignore and just show today
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub RefreshSelectedDateGrid()
        If _grid Is Nothing OrElse _cal Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim selected = _cal.SelectionStart.Date

        Dim todays = _entries.
            Where(Function(e) e.Date = selected).
            OrderBy(Function(e) e.Text).
            ToList()

        _grid.Rows.Clear()
        For Each e In todays
            _grid.Rows.Add(e.Date.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture), e.Text)
        Next
    End Sub

    ' === BASIC WRITE# compatibility helpers (same idea as Mileage module) ===
    Private Shared Function StripDosEofOnly(s As String) As String
        If s Is Nothing Then Return ""
        ' Remove DOS EOF (Ctrl+Z) only; keep normal whitespace and punctuation intact.
        Return s.Replace(ChrW(26), "")
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ToBasicWriteLine(values As IEnumerable(Of String)) As String
        Dim parts As New List(Of String)()
        For Each v In values
            If v Is Nothing Then
                parts.Add("""""")
            Else
                Dim n As Integer
                If Integer.TryParse(v, NumberStyles.Integer, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, n) AndAlso
                   v.All(Function(ch) Char.IsDigit(ch) OrElse ch = "-"c) Then
                    parts.Add(n.ToString(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture))
                Else
                    Dim escaped = v.Replace("""", """""")
                    parts.Add("""" & escaped & """")
                End If
            End If
        Next
        Return String.Join(",", parts)
    End Function

    Private Shared Function ParseBasicWriteLine(line As String) As List(Of String)
        Dim result As New List(Of String)()
        Dim sb As New StringBuilder()
        Dim inQuotes As Boolean = False
        Dim i As Integer = 0

        While i < line.Length
            Dim ch = line(i)

            If inQuotes Then
                If ch = """"c Then
                    If i + 1 < line.Length AndAlso line(i + 1) = """"c Then
                        sb.Append(""""c)
                        i += 2
                    Else
                        inQuotes = False
                        i += 1
                    End If
                Else
                    sb.Append(ch)
                    i += 1
                End If
            Else
                If ch = ","c Then
                    result.Add(sb.ToString().Trim())
                    sb.Clear()
                    i += 1
                ElseIf ch = """"c Then
                    inQuotes = True
                    i += 1
                Else
                    sb.Append(ch)
                    i += 1
                End If
            End If
        End While

        result.Add(sb.ToString().Trim())
        Return result
    End Function

End Class
```
here's what I have for FormPersonalCalendar.vb: 

## Copilot

You’ve got a syntax bug that will cause errors and prevent the form from loading data.

### The actual bug
In `JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()` you have:

```vbnet
Dim raw = StripDosEofOnly(PmsNumPath, Encoding.ASCII)).Trim()
```

That line is wrong in 3 ways:
- you’re passing a **file path** into `StripDosEofOnly` (it expects file contents)
- you have an extra `)` which makes it a compile error
- you’re not reading the file at all

### Fix `JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()` (drop-in replacement)
Replace your entire `JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()` with this:

```vbnet
Private Sub JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()
    Try
        If Not File.Exists(PmsNumPath) Then Return

        Dim raw = StripDosEofOnly(File.ReadAllText(PmsNumPath, Encoding.ASCII)).Trim()
        If raw.Length = 0 Then Return

        ' Only care about second field: "MM-dd-yyyy"
        Dim parts = ParseBasicWriteLine(raw)
        If parts.Count < 2 Then Return

        Dim dtText = parts(1)
        Dim dt As DateTime
        If DateTime.TryParseExact(dtText, "MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, DateTimeStyles.None, dt) Then
            _cal.SetDate(dt.Date)
        End If
    Catch ex As Exception
        ' Non-fatal: ignore and just show today
    End Try
End Sub
```

### Next likely issue: you’re reading the wrong folder
Your `DataDir` is still:
```vbnet
Private Const DataDir As String = "\\invoice\MainMenu\Data"
```

But you said you copied the files into:
- `\\invoice\mainmenu\data`

If those are not the same location on your network, the app will load an empty/auto-created `DATES.PMS` and you’ll see nothing.

To eliminate that ambiguity, change `DataDir` to exactly what you’re using:

```vbnet
Private Const DataDir As String = "\\invoice\mainmenu\data"
```

### Quick test after the fix
1) Rebuild
2) Open Personal Calendar
3) The status line should say something like `Loaded entries: 170 ...`
4) `01-28-1993` should show the repeated “event ------------------- 1” rows.

If it still says `Loaded entries: 0`, tell me what the status line shows (and confirm the three files exist at the exact path in that line).

## @testsubject117

Severity	Code	Description	Project	File	Line	Suppression State	Details
Error (active)	BC30057	Too many arguments to 'Private Shared Function StripDosEofOnly(s As String) As String'.	AMiOffice	C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\FormPersonalCalendar.vb	289		
Error (active)	BC30205	End of statement expected.	AMiOffice	C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\FormPersonalCalendar.vb	289		


## Copilot

Those two errors are coming from the bad line you already spotted in `JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()`:

```vbnet
Dim raw = StripDosEofOnly(PmsNumPath, Encoding.ASCII)).Trim()
```

VB is complaining because:
- `StripDosEofOnly` only takes **one** argument (`s As String`)
- you’re passing **two** arguments (`PmsNumPath, Encoding.ASCII`)
- and there’s an extra `)` which triggers “End of statement expected”.

## Fix (exact edit)
Replace **that single line** with:

```vbnet
Dim raw = StripDosEofOnly(File.ReadAllText(PmsNumPath, Encoding.ASCII)).Trim()
```

That’s it.

## Correct full method (recommended)
To avoid any other small mistakes, here is the complete correct `JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()`—paste it over yours:

```vbnet
Private Sub JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()
    Try
        If Not File.Exists(PmsNumPath) Then Return

        Dim raw = StripDosEofOnly(File.ReadAllText(PmsNumPath, Encoding.ASCII)).Trim()
        If raw.Length = 0 Then Return

        Dim parts = ParseBasicWriteLine(raw)
        If parts.Count < 2 Then Return

        Dim dtText = parts(1)
        Dim dt As DateTime
        If DateTime.TryParseExact(dtText, "MM-dd-yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, DateTimeStyles.None, dt) Then
            _cal.SetDate(dt.Date)
        End If
    Catch
        ' Non-fatal
    End Try
End Sub
```

Change this constant to match the folder you actually copied into:

```vbnet
Private Const DataDir As String = "\\invoice\mainmenu\data"
```

Then run again and check the status label: it should say `Loaded entries: <non-zero>`.

If it still says 0, paste the exact status text and we’ll chase down which path it’s reading.

## Copilot

Understood on the casing/SMB point.

Given that, the remaining reason you’re seeing “holidays only” is: **the app is loading your file but parsing 0 entries** (so no bolded dates, and the grid stays empty). The earlier compile error prevented the form from finishing loading; after fixing that, we need to verify parsing.

Here’s what to do next, in order.

## 1) Fix the compile error (required)
Make sure `JumpToLastDateIfAvailable()` contains:

```vbnet
Dim raw = StripDosEofOnly(File.ReadAllText(PmsNumPath, Encoding.ASCII)).Trim()
```

That resolves the “too many arguments” and “end of statement expected”.

## 2) Add one-line “proof” that we loaded what we think we loaded
In `LoadDatesPmsEntries()`, right after you build `lines` (after the `ToList()`), add this temporary status line:

```vbnet
_lblStatus.Text = $"DATES.PMS lines={lines.Count}  first='{If(lines.Count > 0, lines(0), "")}'"
```

Run again and tell me what it shows.

### What this tells us
- If `lines.Count` is large (like 150+), we’re reading the file fine.
- If the first line is the `"111111...`, header detection should skip it.
- If `lines.Count` is 1, you’re only seeing the header (wrong file or overwritten file).
- If the first line looks mangled, we have an encoding issue.

## 3) Most likely parsing issue: your file is not actually “WRITE# CSV”
Your `DATES.PMS` sample lines look like:

```
"01-28-1993","event ------------------- 1"
```

That *should* parse with `ParseBasicWriteLine`.

But if the real file has anything like:
- extra spaces before the first quote
- non-printing characters (not Ctrl+Z)
- or `PRINT#`-style (no quotes, different delimiters)

…then `ParseBasicWriteLine` may be returning 1 field and we skip the line (`parts.Count < 2`).

### Quick check without changing code much
Also temporarily add this inside the loop in `LoadDatesPmsEntries()`:

```vbnet
If parts.Count < 2 Then
    ' TEMP: show the first unparseable line and stop
    MessageBox.Show("Unparseable line: " & lines(i), "PMS Debug")
    Exit For
End If
```

Run once—if you get that popup, paste the exact line it shows.

## 4) Remove holidays so you can visually confirm our bolding
`MonthCalendar` shows holidays by default; that’s masking whether our bolding is working.

After you create `_cal`, add:

```vbnet
_cal.AnnuallyBoldedDates = Array.Empty(Of DateTime)()
_cal.MonthlyBoldedDates = Array.Empty(Of DateTime)()
```

Now the *only* bold dates should be your PMS entries. That makes testing obvious.

## What I need from you
After you fix the compile error and add the temporary `_lblStatus` line, tell me:

- What does the status label show (lines count + first line)?
- Does the debug popup show an “Unparseable line”? If yes, paste it.

With those two pieces, I can give you the exact final parsing fix (it’ll be a small change—either trimming BOM/odd chars or handling a slightly different legacy format).

## Copilot

The old PMS screen (image1) is **not a month-calendar picker**—it’s a **scrollable list** of events with columns:

- **Date** (MM-dd-yy)
- **Day** (Mon/Tue/…)
- **Event** (text)

And it has actions like **next month / previous month / add / delete / other**.

So for your VB.NET version to “work like the old one”, we should change the UI to a **grid-first month view** (not MonthCalendar) and we should make sure we’re loading the *same file* PMS used for that list.

### Why you saw “holidays only”
That “holidays” behavior is coming from the `MonthCalendar` control—those are Windows’ built-in bolded dates, not your data. The DOS PMS didn’t have that UI at all, so it’s also a sign we chose the wrong control for matching behavior.

## What file drives the list in the DOS PMS?
Based on the screen, it’s almost certainly **`DATES.PMS`** (date + event text). `PMS.NUM` looks like last position/date + count. `PMS.DAT` may be a secondary list or settings.

Your `DATES.PMS` sample also matches “date + event”.

## What we should build now (to match the DOS PMS)
Replace the MonthCalendar UI with:

1) A top header row showing “Personal Calendar”
2) A `DataGridView` with 3 columns:
   - Date (display as `MM-dd-yy`)
   - Day (computed from the date)
   - Event (text)
3) A “current month” filter (like the DOS program’s view):
   - show only events for the selected month/year
4) Buttons matching the bottom bar:
   - Next month
   - Previous month
   - Add event
   - Delete selected
   - Other (optional stub for later)

This will also make it obvious whether parsing works, because you’ll see the rows immediately—no “bolding” required.

## One important thing to confirm
Your `DATES.PMS` file you pasted earlier contains a lot of repeated “event ------------------- N” test data, but the screenshot shows real items like “TAXES ARE DUE!” etc.

To make sure we’re targeting the correct dataset, answer this:

- Does your real `\\invoice\mainmenu\data\DATES.PMS` currently contain lines like `"04-15-94","TAXES ARE DUE!"`?

If yes, we can proceed confidently.

## Copilot

Got it: PMS is a **yearly recurring reminder list** (birthdays/anniversaries/deadlines). That means we should **treat entries as “month/day + text” that recur every year**, even though the file stores a full date with a specific year.

Practical implication for matching the DOS behavior (image1): when viewing “this month”, PMS likely shows events whose **MM-dd matches the current month** and then displays the stored year as part of the date (or uses it for sorting), but it’s conceptually recurring.

### What to implement to match the old PMS
1) **Grid/list view** (not MonthCalendar):
   - Date (MM-dd-yy)
   - Day (computed for *the next occurrence* in the selected year/month)
   - Event

2) **Month navigation**:
   - Previous month / Next month changes a “view month” (like the DOS bottom bar)

3) **Recurring logic**:
   - For the selected month (e.g., April), show all entries where `entryDate.Month = viewMonth.Month` regardless of year.
   - Sort by day-of-month.
   - For the “Day” column, compute using `DateSerial(viewYear, entryDate.Month, entryDate.Day)` so it’s correct for the year being viewed.
   - For display “Date”, we can mimic DOS by showing `MM-dd-yy` using the view year (or keep the original year; but DOS looks like it’s showing the original year sometimes—hard to be sure). To behave like a reminder system, it’s more useful to display the *occurrence date* in the viewed year.

4) **Add/Delete**:
   - Add creates a new line in `DATES.PMS` in the same `WRITE#` format: `"MM-dd-yyyy","Text"`
   - Delete removes selected lines
   - Update `PMS.NUM` count (3rd field) to match number of event lines (excluding header)

### Two quick clarifications before I hand you the replacement file
1) When PMS shows April (in your screenshot top left shows `04-27-26`), does the list include **all April events** regardless of year (sounds like yes)?  
2) Should the “Date” column show:
   - **Option A (recurring display):** `MM-dd-yy` using the *view year* (so April 15 always shows as `04-15-26` when viewing 2026), or
   - **Option B (stored display):** show the year that’s stored in the file (e.g., `04-15-94`)?

From the screenshot, it’s *possible* they kept the original year (employment start year), but for reminders (Taxes due) that original year isn’t meaningful. Many shops kept the original year as “reference” though.

If you tell me A or B, I’ll generate a full `FormPersonalCalendar.vb` replacement that matches the DOS layout and behavior.

Also: confirm we’re still using `\\invoice\mainmenu\data\DATES.PMS` as the source of truth (and not `PMS.DAT`).

## Copilot

Then we should implement it as a **month/day recurring “anniversary” list**, where the stored year is not used to decide whether it shows—only **month/day** matters. That matches “open and listed regardless of the start year”.

### Recommended display (to match DOS + be useful)
- Filter by **view month** (Prev/Next month)
- Show all entries whose `Month/Day` is in that month, regardless of year stored
- Sort by **day-of-month**
- Show:
  - **Date** as `MM-dd-yy` using the *view year* (so it looks like “upcoming occurrences” like the DOS screen)
  - **Day** computed from the occurrence date in the view year
  - **Event** text

We can still keep the original stored year internally for round-tripping when saving, but the *view* should be month/day recurring.

## Copilot

Here’s a drop-in replacement `FormPersonalCalendar.vb` that matches the DOS PMS behavior much more closely: **month-based list**, **recurring by month/day**, **Prev/Next month**, and **Add/Delete** that round-trips to `DATES.PMS` in GW-BASIC `WRITE#`-compatible format. It also maintains `PMS.NUM` (last viewed month + count).

Copy/paste this entire file over your current `FormPersonalCalendar.vb`.

```vbnet name=FormPersonalCalendar.vb
Option Strict On
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Globalization
Imports System.IO
Imports System.Linq
Imports System.Text
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormPersonalCalendar

    ' Per your instruction: under the share
    Private Const DataDir As String = "\\invoice\mainmenu\data"

    Private ReadOnly DatesPmsPath As String = Path.Combine(DataDir, "DATES.PMS")
    Private ReadOnly PmsNumPath As String = Path.Combine(DataDir, "PMS.NUM")

    Private _viewMonth As DateTime = New DateTime(DateTime.Today.Year, DateTime.Today.Month, 1)

    Private _allEntries As New List(Of PmsEntry)()

    ' UI
    Private _lblHeaderRight As Label = Nothing
    Private _lblMonth As Label = Nothing
    Private _grid As DataGridView = Nothing
    Private _lblStatus As Label = Nothing

    Public Sub New()
        InitializeComponent()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.Text = "Personal Calendar"
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.MinimumSize = New Size(900, 560)

        If Not EnsureDataDirExistsFriendly() Then
            Me.Close()
            Return
        End If

        EnsureFilesExist()

        BuildUi()

        LoadAllData()
        LoadViewMonthFromPmsNum()
        RefreshMonthLabel()
        BindGridForCurrentMonth()
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnKeyDown(e As KeyEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnKeyDown(e)

        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Escape Then
            Me.Close()
            e.Handled = True
            Return
        End If

        If e.KeyCode = Keys.PageDown Then
            NextMonth()
            e.Handled = True
            Return
        End If

        If e.KeyCode = Keys.PageUp Then
            PrevMonth()
            e.Handled = True
            Return
        End If

        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Insert Then
            AddEvent()
            e.Handled = True
            Return
        End If

        If e.KeyCode = Keys.Delete Then
            DeleteSelected()
            e.Handled = True
            Return
        End If
    End Sub

    ' =========================
    ' Model
    ' =========================
    Private NotInheritable Class PmsEntry
        Public Property StoredDate As DateTime         ' What is in file
        Public Property Text As String                 ' Event text
        Public Property SourceLineIndex As Integer     ' Line index in DATES.PMS (excluding header). Used for delete.
    End Class

    Private NotInheritable Class GridRow
        Public Property OccurrenceDate As DateTime     ' Date in view year
        Public Property DayName As String
        Public Property Text As String
        Public Property StoredDate As DateTime
        Public Property SourceLineIndex As Integer
    End Class

    ' =========================
    ' UI
    ' =========================
    Private Sub BuildUi()
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .Padding = New Padding(10),
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 3
        }
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

        ' Header bar (DOS-like: date/time + title)
        Dim header As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1
        }
        header.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
        header.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

        Dim lblTitle As New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 14.0F, FontStyle.Bold),
            .Text = "Personal Calendar"
        }

        _lblHeaderRight = New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 10.0F, FontStyle.Regular),
            .Text = DateTime.Now.ToString("MM-dd-yy  ddd  h:mm tt", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
        }

        header.Controls.Add(lblTitle, 0, 0)
        header.Controls.Add(_lblHeaderRight, 1, 0)

        ' Body: month nav + grid
        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 2
        }
        body.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        body.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        Dim nav As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .AutoSize = True,
            .WrapContents = False
        }

        Dim btnPrev As New Button() With {.Text = "< Previous Month", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnPrev.Click, Sub() PrevMonth()

        Dim btnNext As New Button() With {.Text = "Next Month >", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnNext.Click, Sub() NextMonth()

        _lblMonth = New Label() With {
            .AutoSize = True,
            .Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 11.0F, FontStyle.Bold),
            .Padding = New Padding(12, 7, 12, 0)
        }

        Dim btnAdd As New Button() With {.Text = "Add an Event (Ins)", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnAdd.Click, Sub() AddEvent()

        Dim btnDelete As New Button() With {.Text = "Delete (Del)", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnDelete.Click, Sub() DeleteSelected()

        nav.Controls.Add(btnPrev)
        nav.Controls.Add(btnNext)
        nav.Controls.Add(_lblMonth)
        nav.Controls.Add(New Label() With {.AutoSize = True, .Text = "   "})
        nav.Controls.Add(btnAdd)
        nav.Controls.Add(btnDelete)

        _grid = New DataGridView() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ReadOnly = True,
            .AllowUserToAddRows = False,
            .AllowUserToDeleteRows = False,
            .SelectionMode = DataGridViewSelectionMode.FullRowSelect,
            .MultiSelect = True,
            .AutoGenerateColumns = False,
            .AutoSizeColumnsMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnsMode.Fill
        }

        _grid.Columns.Clear()
        _grid.Columns.Add(New DataGridViewTextBoxColumn() With {
            .Name = "Date",
            .HeaderText = "Date",
            .DataPropertyName = "DateDisplay",
            .AutoSizeMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnMode.None,
            .Width = 95
        })
        _grid.Columns.Add(New DataGridViewTextBoxColumn() With {
            .Name = "Day",
            .HeaderText = "Day",
            .DataPropertyName = "DayName",
            .AutoSizeMode = DataGridViewAutoSizeColumnMode.None,
            .Width = 55
        })
        _grid.Columns.Add(New DataGridViewTextBoxColumn() With {
            .Name = "Event",
            .HeaderText = "Event",
            .DataPropertyName = "Text"
        })

        body.Controls.Add(nav, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(_grid, 0, 1)

        ' Footer
        Dim footer As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1
        }
        footer.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
        footer.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

        _lblStatus = New Label() With {.AutoSize = True}

        Dim btnClose As New Button() With {.Text = "Close (Esc)", .AutoSize = True}
        AddHandler btnClose.Click, Sub() Me.Close()

        footer.Controls.Add(_lblStatus, 0, 0)
        footer.Controls.Add(btnClose, 1, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(header, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 1)
        root.Controls.Add(footer, 0, 2)

        Me.Controls.Clear()
        Me.Controls.Add(root)

        ' Update clock display occasionally (not critical)
        Dim t As New Timer() With {.Interval = 1000}
        AddHandler t.Tick, Sub() _lblHeaderRight.Text = DateTime.Now.ToString("MM-dd-yy  ddd  h:mm tt", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
        t.Start()
    End Sub

    Private Sub RefreshMonthLabel()
        _lblMonth.Text = _viewMonth.ToString("MMMM yyyy", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture)
    End Sub

    ' =========================
    ' Month navigation
    ' =========================
    Private Sub PrevMonth()
        _viewMonth = _viewMonth.AddMonths(-1)
        RefreshMonthLabel()
        BindGridForCurrentMonth()
        SaveViewMonthToPmsNum()
    End Sub

    Private Sub NextMonth()
        _viewMonth = _viewMonth.AddMonths(1)
        RefreshMonthLabel()
        BindGridForCurrentMonth()
        SaveViewMonthToPmsNum()
    End Sub

    ' =========================
    ' Data I/O
    ' =========================
    Private Function EnsureDataDirExistsFriendly() As Boolean
        Try
            If Directory.Exists(DataDir) Then Return True

            MessageBox.Show("Personal Calendar cannot open because the data folder is not available:" & Environment.NewLine &
                            DataDir,
                            "Personal Calendar",
                            MessageBoxButtons.OK,
