please generate the current formledgermenu.vb code

## Copilot

```vbnet name=FormLedgerMenu.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits DosMenuFormBase

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        ' Matches the DOS/menu title style: big header says CHECKS, and the window caption is CHECKS.
        SetMenuTitle("CHECKS")

        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        ClearMenu()

        ' Left column (matches DOS numbering/layout reasonably)
        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "1", "Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts",
                      Sub() NotYet("Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts"))

        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "2", "Delete Checks",
                      Sub() NotYet("Delete Checks"))

        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "3", "View Checks",
                      Sub() OpenViewChecksPromptFlow())

        AddMenuButton(flpLeft, "5", "View Companys Totals",
                      Sub()
                          Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
                              f.ShowDialog(Me)
                          End Using
                      End Sub)

        ' Right column
        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "6", "Add OTHER Checks",
                      Sub() NotYet("Add OTHER Checks"))

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "7", "Delete OTHER Checks",
                      Sub() NotYet("Delete OTHER Checks"))

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "8", "View OTHER Checks",
                      Sub()
                          Using f As New FormOtherChecksView()
                              f.ShowDialog(Me)
                          End Using
                      End Sub)

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "9", "Find a Check #",
                      Sub()
                          Using f As New FormFindByCheckNumber()
                              f.ShowDialog(Me)
                          End Using
                      End Sub)

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "0", "Find an Invoice #",
                      Sub()
                          Using f As New FormFindByInvoiceNumber()
                              f.ShowDialog(Me)
                          End Using
                      End Sub)

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "A", "Find Checks that don't balance.",
                      Sub() NotYet("Find Checks that don't balance"))

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "S", "Sales Employee's checks",
                      Sub() NotYet("Sales Employee's checks"))

        AddMenuButton(flpRight, "Q", "Quit to Main Menu",
                      Sub() Me.Close())
    End Sub

    ' DOS flow (Customer -> Check # -> Year) implemented via small dialogs.
    ' This keeps the *look* consistent with your Main Menu (header + buttons),
    ' but still follows the same input sequence.
    Private Sub OpenViewChecksPromptFlow()
        Dim cust As String = Prompt("Enter customer name [Enter = all customers] ?", "")
        If cust Is Nothing Then Return ' Cancel

        Dim chk As String = Prompt("Enter Check Number [Enter = All Checks] ?", "")
        If chk Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim yr As String = Prompt("Enter Year [Enter = All Years] ?", "")
        If yr Is Nothing Then Return

        ' TODO: Wire these filters into FormLedgerView once the view supports it.
        ' For now, we open the view unfiltered (same as current behavior).
        Using f As New FormLedgerView()
            f.ShowDialog(Me)
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function Prompt(promptText As String, defaultValue As String) As String
        Using f As New FormPrompt(promptText, defaultValue)
            Dim dr = f.ShowDialog(Me)
            If dr <> DialogResult.OK Then Return Nothing
            Return f.Value
        End Using
    End Function

    ' Small reusable prompt dialog used by option (3) View Checks.
    Private NotInheritable Class FormPrompt
        Inherits Form

        Private ReadOnly _txt As New TextBox()

        Public ReadOnly Property Value As String
            Get
                Return _txt.Text
            End Get
        End Property

        Public Sub New(promptText As String, defaultValue As String)
            Me.Text = "CHECKS"
            Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
            Me.FormBorderStyle = FormBorderStyle.FixedDialog
            Me.MinimizeBox = False
            Me.MaximizeBox = False
            Me.ShowInTaskbar = False
            Me.ClientSize = New Drawing.Size(620, 140)

            Dim layout As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
                .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
                .Padding = New Padding(10),
                .ColumnCount = 1,
                .RowCount = 3
            }
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

            Dim lbl As New Label() With {
                .AutoSize = True,
                .Text = promptText
            }

            _txt.Dock = DockStyle.Top
            _txt.Text = defaultValue

            Dim buttons As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {
                .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
                .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.RightToLeft,
                .AutoSize = True
            }

            Dim ok As New Button() With {.Text = "OK", .DialogResult = DialogResult.OK, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim cancel As New Button() With {.Text = "Cancel", .DialogResult = DialogResult.Cancel, .AutoSize = True}
            buttons.Controls.Add(ok)
            buttons.Controls.Add(cancel)

            layout.Controls.Add(lbl, 0, 0)
            layout.Controls.Add(_txt, 0, 1)
            layout.Controls.Add(buttons, 0, 2)

            Me.Controls.Add(layout)
            Me.AcceptButton = ok
            Me.CancelButton = cancel
        End Sub
    End Class

End Class
```

## @testsubject117

ok, I'm now getting an error, not at the bottom, it says 0 errors, but when I click the play button I get a message that states there are build errors

## Copilot

That “0 errors” + “there are build errors” mismatch usually means the **Error List filter is hiding build errors**, or the errors are in a different configuration/project than the one you’re viewing.

Do this first (quick checks), then if it still fails paste the Output text.

## 1) Make sure you can see the build errors
1. Open **View → Output**
2. In the Output window dropdown, select **Build**
3. Click **Build → Rebuild Solution**
4. Copy/paste the full build output (at least the first error and the last 20 lines)

Also open **View → Error List** and set:
- Filter dropdown: **Build + IntelliSense**
- “Only show” / funnel icon: set to **All**
- Make sure “Scope” isn’t set to “Current Document”

## 2) Most likely causes from the changes we just made
These are the common compile-time issues after adding `DosMenuFormBase` + updating `FormLedgerMenu`:

### A) `DosMenuFormBase` not found / wrong namespace
Error will look like: *Type 'DosMenuFormBase' is not defined.*

Fix:
- Ensure the file is added as a **Class** (not a Module).
- Ensure the class is declared `Public Class DosMenuFormBase`.
- If your project uses a root namespace (Project Properties → Application → Root Namespace), `DosMenuFormBase` might actually be `YourRootNamespace.DosMenuFormBase`.

Quick workaround: in `FormLedgerMenu.vb` change:
```vbnet
Inherits DosMenuFormBase
```
to
```vbnet
Inherits Global.<YourRootNamespace>.DosMenuFormBase
```
(or add `Imports <YourRootNamespace>`).

### B) `BuildInfo` not found in the base class
If `DosMenuFormBase` calls `BuildInfo.DisplayVersion` but `BuildInfo` is in another namespace/module, you’ll get: *BuildInfo is not declared.*

Fix:
- Add `Imports` for the namespace containing `BuildInfo`, or qualify it.
- Or temporarily comment out the BuildInfo call and hardcode version blank.

### C) Duplicate class name / file conflict
If you accidentally created `DosMenuFormBase` as a WinForm item, Visual Studio may have generated a **partial form class with a Designer**, causing conflicts.

Fix:
- `DosMenuFormBase.vb` should be a single file without `Partial` and without `.Designer.vb`.
- If you see `DosMenuFormBase.Designer.vb`, delete it and recreate as a plain Class.

### D) References to forms that don’t exist
If any of these forms aren’t present/renamed, you’ll get type not defined:
- `FormLedgerCompanyTotals`
- `FormOtherChecksView`
- `FormFindByCheckNumber`
- `FormFindByInvoiceNumber`
- `FormLedgerView`

Fix: adjust the class names or comment out the handlers temporarily.

## 3) What I need from you
Paste **the build output** from **View → Output → Build** (the error lines). The first error line is usually enough to identify the exact fix.

## @testsubject117

```
1>C:\Users\CapnKirk\source\repos\AMiOffice\AMiOffice\FormLedgerMenu.vb(8,14): error BC30928: Base class 'DosMenuFormBase' specified for class 'FormLedgerMenu' cannot be different from the base class 'Form' of one of its other partial types.
========== Build: 0 succeeded, 1 failed, 0 up-to-date, 0 skipped ==========
========== Build completed at 11:07 AM and took 00.215 seconds ==========
```

## Copilot

That error means `FormLedgerMenu` still has **another partial definition** (almost certainly `FormLedgerMenu.Designer.vb`) that says:

```vbnet
Partial Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits System.Windows.Forms.Form
```

…and your code-behind now says:

```vbnet
Public Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits DosMenuFormBase
```

VB.NET requires all partial parts to inherit the **same** base class.

## Fix options (pick one)

### Option A (recommended): Convert the Designer partial to inherit `DosMenuFormBase`
1) In Solution Explorer, expand **FormLedgerMenu** and open **FormLedgerMenu.Designer.vb**.
2) Change:

```vbnet
Partial Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits System.Windows.Forms.Form
```

to:

```vbnet
Partial Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits DosMenuFormBase
```

3) Save, rebuild.

This is best if you want to keep the designer file around (even if it’s mostly empty).

### Option B: Delete the designer partial completely (if you’re building UI in code)
If `FormLedgerMenu` is 100% code-built and you don’t need the designer:
1) Delete **FormLedgerMenu.Designer.vb** (and the `.resx` if it exists).
2) Ensure `FormLedgerMenu.vb` is the only definition of the class.
3) Rebuild.

### Option C: Make `FormLedgerMenu` a plain code file (not a WinForm item)
If you created `FormLedgerMenu` originally as a Windows Form, Visual Studio will keep the designer partial. Option A is usually easiest.

## One more thing to watch
If you choose Option A, make sure `DosMenuFormBase` is in scope:
- If your project has a root namespace, `DosMenuFormBase` may be `AMiOffice.DosMenuFormBase`. If the designer can’t see it, you might need:
  - add `Imports` at top of the designer file, **or**
  - fully qualify: `Inherits Global.AMiOffice.DosMenuFormBase`

## Tell me which route you want
- If you paste the first ~20 lines of `FormLedgerMenu.Designer.vb`, I’ll tell you the exact edit (including the correct namespace qualification).

## @testsubject117

ok, that seems to work, however some things need to be changed. below the totle CHECKS (which also needs to be changed to match the font of the main menu) there is AMiOffice v1.0.0.0 (Build 28). We do not want to see the version or build number on any menu except the main menu and the about menu, please remove it from this menu. Also, this menu has 4 buttons on the left and 8 buttons on the right. Can you change it so there 12 buttons in 1 row top to bottom, in a window that expands to display the full window and does not cut off or require using the scroll bar at right.

## Copilot

Do this by changing **only `DosMenuFormBase`** (so the behavior is consistent for future menus) and then adjusting `FormLedgerMenu` to use a **single column panel**.

## 1) Remove version/build text from non-main menus
Right now `DosMenuFormBase.UpdateHeaderClock()` prepends the version:

```vbnet
Dim ver As String = "   " & _displayVersion
lblDateTime.Text = ver & "          " & ...
```

Make the base support “show version” as an option, default **False**, and only the main menu/about enable it.

### Update `DosMenuFormBase.vb`
- Add a protected property `ShowVersionInHeader As Boolean = False`
- Use it in `UpdateHeaderClock()`
- Also change the title label font to match your main menu (it already uses Segoe UI 22 bold + gold, same style as MAIN MENU)

```vbnet name=DosMenuFormBase.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class DosMenuFormBase
    Inherits Form

    Protected ReadOnly lblMainMenu As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly lblDateTime As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly tmrClock As New Timer()

    ' Keep these available; derived forms can choose to use one or both.
    Protected ReadOnly flpLeft As New FlowLayoutPanel()
    Protected ReadOnly flpRight As New FlowLayoutPanel()

    ' New: controls whether the version prefix is shown (default: OFF for sub-menus)
    Protected Property ShowVersionInHeader As Boolean = False

    Private _displayVersion As String = ""
    Private _keyHandlers As New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.BackColor = Color.Black
        Me.ForeColor = Color.White
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        _displayVersion = ""
        Try
            _displayVersion = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Catch
            _displayVersion = ""
        End Try

        BuildLayout()
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        AddHandler tmrClock.Tick, Sub() UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Start()

        AddHandler Me.KeyPress, AddressOf OnBaseKeyPress
        AddHandler flpLeft.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        AddHandler flpRight.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)

        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnFormClosed(e As FormClosedEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnFormClosed(e)
        Try
            tmrClock.Stop()
        Catch
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildLayout()
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 3,
            .BackColor = Color.Black,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' title
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' date/time
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F)) ' body

        ' Match main menu title style
        lblMainMenu.AutoSize = False
        lblMainMenu.Height = 44
        lblMainMenu.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblMainMenu.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 22.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point)
        lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Gold
        lblMainMenu.BackColor = Color.Black

        lblDateTime.AutoSize = False
        lblDateTime.Height = 34
        lblDateTime.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblDateTime.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Regular, GraphicsUnit.Point)
        lblDateTime.ForeColor = Color.White
        lblDateTime.BackColor = Color.Black
        lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)
        lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)

        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .BackColor = Color.Black
        }
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        body.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(lblMainMenu, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(lblDateTime, 0, 1)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 2)

        Controls.Clear()
        Controls.Add(root)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.BackColor = Color.Black
        panel.Padding = New Padding(0)
        panel.Margin = New Padding(0, 6, 0, 0)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub SetMenuTitle(title As String)
        lblMainMenu.Text = title
        Me.Text = title
    End Sub

    Protected Sub ClearMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        _keyHandlers = New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String, handler As Action)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)
        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub() handler()

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)

        If Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) Then
            _keyHandlers(key) = handler
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
            panel.ClientSize.Width -
            panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
            SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = targetWidth
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub OnBaseKeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs)
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then up = up.ToUpperInvariant()

        Dim act As Action = Nothing
        If _keyHandlers IsNot Nothing AndAlso _keyHandlers.TryGetValue(up, act) Then
            act()
        End If
    End Sub

    Protected Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

        Dim ver As String = ""
        If ShowVersionInHeader AndAlso Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_displayVersion) Then
            ver = "   " & _displayVersion & "          "
        End If

        lblDateTime.Text = ver &
                           now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                           now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                           now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub

    Protected Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

Now: only `FormMainMenu` and `FormAbout` should show version/build. For menus like `FormLedgerMenu`, don’t set `ShowVersionInHeader` (it remains False).

If you later refactor `FormMainMenu` to inherit this base, you’d set `ShowVersionInHeader = True` there.

---

## 2) Make `FormLedgerMenu` a single vertical list of 12 buttons (no scroll bar)
We’ll:
- ignore the right column
- put all 12 buttons into `flpLeft`
- **turn off AutoScroll**
- make the window tall enough so 12 buttons fit without scrolling
- also disable maximize/minimize if you prefer a fixed “menu screen”

Update `FormLedgerMenu.vb` like this:

```vbnet name=FormLedgerMenu.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits DosMenuFormBase

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        SetMenuTitle("CHECKS")

        ' No version/build on sub-menus
        ShowVersionInHeader = False
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        ' Single column behavior:
        flpRight.Visible = False
        flpRight.Enabled = False

        flpLeft.AutoScroll = False
        flpLeft.WrapContents = False
        flpLeft.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown

        ' Give enough room so it doesn't need a scrollbar.
        ' 12 buttons * (36 height + ~6 margin) + header area ~= ~620-700
        Me.Width = 900
        Me.Height = 720

        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        ClearMenu()

        Dim p = flpLeft

        AddMenuButton(p, "1", "Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts", Sub() NotYet("Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "2", "Delete Checks", Sub() NotYet("Delete Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "3", "View Checks", Sub() OpenViewChecksPromptFlow())
        AddMenuButton(p, "5", "View Companys Totals", Sub()
                                                          Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
                                                              f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                          End Using
                                                      End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "6", "Add OTHER Checks", Sub() NotYet("Add OTHER Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "7", "Delete OTHER Checks", Sub() NotYet("Delete OTHER Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "8", "View OTHER Checks", Sub()
                                                       Using f As New FormOtherChecksView()
                                                           f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                       End Using
                                                   End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "9", "Find a Check #", Sub()
                                                   Using f As New FormFindByCheckNumber()
                                                       f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                   End Using
                                               End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "0", "Find an Invoice #", Sub()
                                                       Using f As New FormFindByInvoiceNumber()
                                                           f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                       End Using
                                                   End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "A", "Find Checks that don't balance.", Sub() NotYet("Find Checks that don't balance"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "S", "Sales Employee's checks", Sub() NotYet("Sales Employee's checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "Q", "Quit to Main Menu", Sub() Me.Close())
    End Sub

    Private Sub OpenViewChecksPromptFlow()
        Dim cust As String = Prompt("Enter customer name [Enter = all customers] ?", "")
        If cust Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim chk As String = Prompt("Enter Check Number [Enter = All Checks] ?", "")
        If chk Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim yr As String = Prompt("Enter Year [Enter = All Years] ?", "")
        If yr Is Nothing Then Return

        ' TODO: apply filters when FormLedgerView supports it
        Using f As New FormLedgerView()
            f.ShowDialog(Me)
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function Prompt(promptText As String, defaultValue As String) As String
        Using f As New FormPrompt(promptText, defaultValue)
            Dim dr = f.ShowDialog(Me)
            If dr <> DialogResult.OK Then Return Nothing
            Return f.Value
        End Using
    End Function

    Private NotInheritable Class FormPrompt
        Inherits Form

        Private ReadOnly _txt As New TextBox()

        Public ReadOnly Property Value As String
            Get
                Return _txt.Text
            End Get
        End Property

        Public Sub New(promptText As String, defaultValue As String)
            Me.Text = "CHECKS"
            Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
            Me.FormBorderStyle = FormBorderStyle.FixedDialog
            Me.MinimizeBox = False
            Me.MaximizeBox = False
            Me.ShowInTaskbar = False
            Me.ClientSize = New Drawing.Size(620, 140)

            Dim layout As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
                .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
                .Padding = New Padding(10),
                .ColumnCount = 1,
                .RowCount = 3
            }
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

            Dim lbl As New Label() With {.AutoSize = True, .Text = promptText}

            _txt.Dock = DockStyle.Top
            _txt.Text = defaultValue

            Dim buttons As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill, .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.RightToLeft, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim ok As New Button() With {.Text = "OK", .DialogResult = DialogResult.OK, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim cancel As New Button() With {.Text = "Cancel", .DialogResult = DialogResult.Cancel, .AutoSize = True}
            buttons.Controls.Add(ok)
            buttons.Controls.Add(cancel)

            layout.Controls.Add(lbl, 0, 0)
            layout.Controls.Add(_txt, 0, 1)
            layout.Controls.Add(buttons, 0, 2)

            Me.Controls.Add(layout)
            Me.AcceptButton = ok
            Me.CancelButton = cancel
        End Sub
    End Class

End Class
```

### Important note about the “no scroll bar” requirement
If the user’s screen resolution is small, 12 buttons + header may still not fit. The above sets a taller form to avoid scrolling on typical displays. If you want it to *always* fit, we can also:
- reduce button height (e.g., 32)
- reduce margins
- slightly reduce font size on submenus

---

If you want, I can also update `DosMenuFormBase` to support a built-in **single-column mode** (so `FormLedgerMenu` just calls something like `UseSingleColumnLayout()`), but the above changes will fix your immediate issues.

## @testsubject117

the font for Main Menu is not segoe it is something else

## Copilot

You’re right to call that out: **`FormMainMenu` explicitly sets the button font to Segoe UI**, but the *header/title* font might be coming from the form’s default font (set in the Designer or application-wide), so it can look like “something else” even though parts of the code use Segoe.

### How to match the Main Menu header font exactly (no guessing)
Instead of hardcoding `"Segoe UI"` in `DosMenuFormBase`, we should base all header fonts on **`Me.Font`** (the form’s effective font), just like your `MakeHeaderLabel` does:

```vbnet
lbl.Font = New Font(Me.Font, FontStyle.Bold)
```

So we’ll change `DosMenuFormBase` to:
- set `lblMainMenu.Font` to a **larger size of `Me.Font`**
- set `lblDateTime.Font` to the **default `Me.Font`** (or slightly smaller)
- avoid specifying `"Segoe UI"` entirely

### Make this change in `DosMenuFormBase.vb`
Replace the hardcoded font lines with these:

```vbnet
' Title: use the form's default font family, just bigger + bold
Dim titleFamily = Me.Font.FontFamily
lblMainMenu.Font = New Font(titleFamily, 22.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point)

' Date/time: use the form's default font family at a normal size
lblDateTime.Font = New Font(titleFamily, 12.0F, FontStyle.Regular, GraphicsUnit.Point)
```

If you want it to be *exactly* the same sizing as your Main Menu title label, we can also do this instead:

- In `FormMainMenu`, what is the font/size of `lblMainMenu` in the designer?
  - If you paste the `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb` snippet for `lblMainMenu.Font`, I can match it precisely.

### Quick diagnostic you can run (no code changes)
Put this temporarily in `FormMainMenu.OnLoad`:

```vbnet
MessageBox.Show("Form font: " & Me.Font.Name & " " & Me.Font.SizeInPoints.ToString(),
                "Font Debug")
MessageBox.Show("Title font: " & lblMainMenu.Font.Name & " " & lblMainMenu.Font.SizeInPoints.ToString(),
                "Font Debug")
```

Then tell me what it reports. That will let me set `DosMenuFormBase` to the exact same font.

### What I recommend
Send either:
1) the `lblMainMenu.Font = ...` lines from **`FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`**, **or**
2) the output from the two MessageBoxes above.

Then I’ll give you the exact `DosMenuFormBase` font settings so every menu matches Main Menu perfectly.

## @testsubject117

but what is the font being used in Main Menu title?

## Copilot

From the code you pasted, the **Main Menu title font is not specified anywhere in `FormMainMenu.vb`**—only the **button** font is explicitly set to **Segoe UI 12 Bold**:

```vbnet
btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, ...)
```

The title label `lblMainMenu` is only assigned text:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Text = "MAIN MENU"
```

So the font used for the **Main Menu title** (`lblMainMenu`) comes from one of these (in order of likelihood):

1) **`FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`**: `lblMainMenu.Font = New Font(...)` (most common)
2) The form’s default font set in the designer: `Me.Font = New Font(...)` (then the label inherits it)
3) Application-wide default font (less common)

### How to know exactly (fastest)
Open **FormMainMenu.Designer.vb** and search for:

- `lblMainMenu.Font`
- or `Me.Font`

You’ll see something like:

```vbnet
Me.lblMainMenu.Font = New System.Drawing.Font("Microsoft Sans Serif", 24.0!, ...
```

That is the exact font.

### If you paste it here
Paste the specific line(s) from `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb` that set:
- `lblMainMenu.Font = ...` (and if not present, `Me.Font = ...`)

…and I’ll tell you precisely what the font is and how to make `DosMenuFormBase` match it.

## @testsubject117

        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))

## Copilot

Main Menu title font is **Castellar, 36pt, Bold**.

To make `FormLedgerMenu` (and other sub-menus) match that title style, update `DosMenuFormBase` so `lblMainMenu.Font` uses the same font.

### Change in `DosMenuFormBase.vb`
Find where you set `lblMainMenu.Font = ...` and set it to Castellar 36 Bold:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
lblMainMenu.Height = 60 ' increase to fit 36pt (tweak if clipped)
```

If the title gets clipped at the top/bottom, bump `Height` to ~70.

### Also: remove version/build from sub-menus
Keep `ShowVersionInHeader = False` by default in `DosMenuFormBase` (as described earlier), and only enable it on Main Menu / About.

If you paste your current `DosMenuFormBase.vb` (the header section), I can point to the exact lines to edit so it compiles cleanly with your current version.

## @testsubject117

```
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class DosMenuFormBase
    Inherits Form

    Protected ReadOnly lblMainMenu As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly lblDateTime As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly tmrClock As New Timer()

    Protected ReadOnly flpLeft As New FlowLayoutPanel()
    Protected ReadOnly flpRight As New FlowLayoutPanel()

    Private _displayVersion As String = ""
    Private _keyHandlers As New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.BackColor = Color.Black
        Me.ForeColor = Color.White
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        _displayVersion = ""
        Try
            _displayVersion = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Catch
            _displayVersion = ""
        End Try

        BuildLayout()
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        AddHandler tmrClock.Tick, Sub() UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Start()

        AddHandler Me.KeyPress, AddressOf OnBaseKeyPress
        AddHandler flpLeft.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        AddHandler flpRight.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)

        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnFormClosed(e As FormClosedEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnFormClosed(e)
        Try
            tmrClock.Stop()
        Catch
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildLayout()
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 3,
            .BackColor = Color.Black,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' title
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' date/time
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F)) ' body

        lblMainMenu.AutoSize = False
        lblMainMenu.Height = 44
        lblMainMenu.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblMainMenu.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        lblMainMenu.Height = 60 ' increase to fit 36pt (tweak if clipped)
        lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Gold
        lblMainMenu.BackColor = Color.Black

        lblDateTime.AutoSize = False
        lblDateTime.Height = 34
        lblDateTime.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblDateTime.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Regular, GraphicsUnit.Point)
        lblDateTime.ForeColor = Color.White
        lblDateTime.BackColor = Color.Black
        lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)
        lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)

        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .BackColor = Color.Black
        }
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        body.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(lblMainMenu, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(lblDateTime, 0, 1)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 2)

        Controls.Clear()
        Controls.Add(root)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.BackColor = Color.Black
        panel.Padding = New Padding(0)
        panel.Margin = New Padding(0, 6, 0, 0)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub SetMenuTitle(title As String)
        lblMainMenu.Text = title
        Me.Text = title
    End Sub

    Protected Sub ClearMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        _keyHandlers = New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String, handler As Action)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)
        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub() handler()

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)

        If Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) Then
            _keyHandlers(key) = handler
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
            panel.ClientSize.Width -
            panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
            SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = targetWidth
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub OnBaseKeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs)
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then up = up.ToUpperInvariant()

        Dim act As Action = Nothing
        If _keyHandlers IsNot Nothing AndAlso _keyHandlers.TryGetValue(up, act) Then
            act()
        End If
    End Sub

    Protected Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now
        Dim ver As String = If(String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_displayVersion), "", "   " & _displayVersion)

        lblDateTime.Text = ver & "          " &
                           now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                           now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                           now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub

    Protected Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

## Copilot

Update `DosMenuFormBase` in two places:

1) **Add a flag** to control whether the version prefix is shown (default OFF).
2) **Change `UpdateHeaderClock()`** to only prepend version when that flag is ON.

Here’s your `DosMenuFormBase.vb` rewritten with only the necessary edits (I kept your Castellar title font exactly as you already set it, and left the button/date-time fonts alone).

```vbnet name=DosMenuFormBase.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class DosMenuFormBase
    Inherits Form

    Protected ReadOnly lblMainMenu As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly lblDateTime As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly tmrClock As New Timer()

    Protected ReadOnly flpLeft As New FlowLayoutPanel()
    Protected ReadOnly flpRight As New FlowLayoutPanel()

    ' NEW: Only Main Menu / About should set this to True.
    Protected Property ShowVersionInHeader As Boolean = False

    Private _displayVersion As String = ""
    Private _keyHandlers As New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.BackColor = Color.Black
        Me.ForeColor = Color.White
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        _displayVersion = ""
        Try
            _displayVersion = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Catch
            _displayVersion = ""
        End Try

        BuildLayout()
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        AddHandler tmrClock.Tick, Sub() UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Start()

        AddHandler Me.KeyPress, AddressOf OnBaseKeyPress
        AddHandler flpLeft.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        AddHandler flpRight.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)

        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnFormClosed(e As FormClosedEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnFormClosed(e)
        Try
            tmrClock.Stop()
        Catch
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildLayout()
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 3,
            .BackColor = Color.Black,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' title
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' date/time
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F)) ' body

        lblMainMenu.AutoSize = False
        lblMainMenu.Height = 44
        lblMainMenu.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblMainMenu.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        lblMainMenu.Height = 60 ' increase to fit 36pt (tweak if clipped)
        lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Gold
        lblMainMenu.BackColor = Color.Black

        lblDateTime.AutoSize = False
        lblDateTime.Height = 34
        lblDateTime.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblDateTime.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Regular, GraphicsUnit.Point)
        lblDateTime.ForeColor = Color.White
        lblDateTime.BackColor = Color.Black
        lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)
        lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)

        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .BackColor = Color.Black
        }
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        body.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(lblMainMenu, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(lblDateTime, 0, 1)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 2)

        Controls.Clear()
        Controls.Add(root)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.BackColor = Color.Black
        panel.Padding = New Padding(0)
        panel.Margin = New Padding(0, 6, 0, 0)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub SetMenuTitle(title As String)
        lblMainMenu.Text = title
        Me.Text = title
    End Sub

    Protected Sub ClearMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        _keyHandlers = New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String, handler As Action)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)
        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub() handler()

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)

        If Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) Then
            _keyHandlers(key) = handler
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
            panel.ClientSize.Width -
            panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
            SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = targetWidth
        Next
    End Sub

    Private Sub OnBaseKeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs)
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then up = up.ToUpperInvariant()

        Dim act As Action = Nothing
        If _keyHandlers IsNot Nothing AndAlso _keyHandlers.TryGetValue(up, act) Then
            act()
        End If
    End Sub

    Protected Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

        Dim verPrefix As String = ""
        If ShowVersionInHeader AndAlso Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_displayVersion) Then
            verPrefix = "   " & _displayVersion & "          "
        End If

        lblDateTime.Text = verPrefix &
                           now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                           now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                           now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub

    Protected Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

### How to use it
- In **FormLedgerMenu**, do nothing (default is `ShowVersionInHeader = False`) and the version/build prefix disappears.
- In **Main Menu** and **About** (if/when they inherit this base), set:
  ```vbnet
  ShowVersionInHeader = True
  ```

If you paste your current `FormLedgerMenu.vb`, I can point out the one spot (if any) where you accidentally turned the version on.

## @testsubject117

it looks like the color of the title CHECKS is more orange than Main Menu.

## Copilot

Main Menu’s `lblMainMenu` is using whatever **ForeColor** is set in the designer; in `DosMenuFormBase` we hardcoded:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Gold
```

`Color.Gold` can look more orange on some displays. To match Main Menu exactly, set the base title color to the **same color value** used by Main Menu’s `lblMainMenu.ForeColor`.

### Easiest exact match
Check `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb` for the title color line, e.g. one of these:

- `lblMainMenu.ForeColor = System.Drawing.Color.Yellow`
- `lblMainMenu.ForeColor = System.Drawing.Color.Gold`
- `lblMainMenu.ForeColor = System.Drawing.Color.FromArgb(...)`

Then copy that exact line into `DosMenuFormBase`.

### If you just want it “more yellow” right now
Change this line in `DosMenuFormBase`:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Gold
```

to:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
```

That will make CHECKS match the brighter yellow look most DOS menus use and usually matches what people pick for MAIN MENU.

### If you want a perfect, consistent custom yellow (recommended)
Use a fixed RGB:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.FromArgb(255, 255, 0) ' pure yellow
```

or, if your main menu is slightly softer:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.FromArgb(255, 240, 0)
```

If you paste the `lblMainMenu.ForeColor = ...` line from `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb`, I’ll tell you exactly which one it is and what to set in the base so all menus match perfectly.

## @testsubject117

        lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Yellow

## Copilot

Change the title color in `DosMenuFormBase` to **Color.Yellow** (same as Main Menu).

In `DosMenuFormBase.BuildLayout()`, replace:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Gold
```

with:

```vbnet
lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
```

That will make the CHECKS title match the MAIN MENU title color exactly.

## @testsubject117

ok, that's better. now can we move the buttons so there are 1 row of buttons that are justified but indented left

## Copilot

Yes. For a single vertical column that’s **left-indented** and **not stretched full width**, you want two changes:

1) Don’t force button width to the panel width (your base currently does that in `ResizeButtonsToPanel`).
2) Add left padding / margin so the whole column is indented.

### Option A (quickest): Turn off the auto-resize-to-full-width behavior for this menu
Add a flag to `DosMenuFormBase` to control whether buttons are stretched. Then in `FormLedgerMenu` set it to False.

#### 1) Update `DosMenuFormBase.vb`
Add this property near `ShowVersionInHeader`:

```vbnet
Protected Property StretchButtonsToPanelWidth As Boolean = True
Protected Property ButtonColumnIndentPx As Integer = 0
Protected Property ButtonFixedWidthPx As Integer = 520
```

Then modify `ResizeButtonsToPanel` like this:

```vbnet
Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
    If panel Is Nothing Then Return

    If StretchButtonsToPanelWidth Then
        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
            panel.ClientSize.Width -
            panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
            SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = targetWidth
        Next
    Else
        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = ButtonFixedWidthPx
        Next
    End If
End Sub
```

And in `ConfigureMenuPanel`, apply indent padding:

```vbnet
panel.Padding = New Padding(ButtonColumnIndentPx, 0, 0, 0)
```

But since `ButtonColumnIndentPx` is per-form, we can’t use it in a `Shared` method. So simplest is: **remove `Shared`** and make it instance-based, or just set padding from the derived form.

#### Minimal change (no refactor): set indent from derived form
In the derived form (Ledger menu), do:

```vbnet
flpLeft.Padding = New Padding(24, 0, 0, 0)
```

and set `StretchButtonsToPanelWidth = False`.

#### So: easiest base edits are:
- add `StretchButtonsToPanelWidth` and `ButtonFixedWidthPx`
- keep padding changes in the derived form

---

### 2) Update `FormLedgerMenu.vb` to make it one indented column
In `OnLoad` add:

```vbnet
StretchButtonsToPanelWidth = False
ButtonFixedWidthPx = 620 ' tweak to taste

flpRight.Visible = False
flpRight.Enabled = False

flpLeft.AutoScroll = False
flpLeft.Padding = New Padding(24, 0, 0, 0) ' indent left
```

This produces:
- one vertical list
- left indented
- buttons all same width, not full-width stretched
- still “justified” as a clean single column

---

## Quick question so I set the right width
When you say “justified but indented left”, do you want:
1) Buttons all the **same fixed width** (e.g., 620px), left aligned (most common), or
2) Buttons **shrink to their text** width (ragged right), still left aligned?

Tell me (1) or (2), and what approximate width you want (or your window width), and I’ll give you the exact code blocks to paste for both files.

## @testsubject117

buttons all same width

## Copilot

Make these small edits:

## 1) `DosMenuFormBase.vb`: add a “fixed-width buttons” mode
Add these properties near the top of the class (right after `_keyHandlers` or near where you’d like them):

```vbnet
' Button sizing behavior (default matches Main Menu: stretch to panel width)
Protected Property StretchButtonsToPanelWidth As Boolean = True
Protected Property ButtonFixedWidthPx As Integer = 620
```

Then replace your existing `ResizeButtonsToPanel` with this version:

```vbnet
Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
    If panel Is Nothing Then Return

    If StretchButtonsToPanelWidth Then
        Dim targetWidth As Integer =
            panel.ClientSize.Width -
            panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
            SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

        If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = targetWidth
        Next
    Else
        For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
            Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
            If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = ButtonFixedWidthPx
        Next
    End If
End Sub
```

## 2) `FormLedgerMenu.vb`: force a single indented column of fixed-width buttons
In `OnLoad`, after `MyBase.OnLoad(e)` and `SetMenuTitle("CHECKS")`, add:

```vbnet
' One vertical column, indented left, fixed button width
StretchButtonsToPanelWidth = False
ButtonFixedWidthPx = 620 ' adjust if you want wider/narrower
flpLeft.Padding = New Padding(24, 0, 0, 0)

' Disable the second column entirely
flpRight.Visible = False
flpRight.Enabled = False

' Avoid scroll bar for this menu (increase form height if needed)
flpLeft.AutoScroll = False

' Make the window large enough to show all 12 buttons without scrolling
Me.Width = 1000
Me.Height = 720
```

And in `BuildMenu()`, make sure you add **all buttons to `flpLeft`** (single column), e.g.:

```vbnet
Dim p = flpLeft
AddMenuButton(p, "1", "...", ...)
' etc...
```

That will give you: **one column top-to-bottom**, **all buttons same width**, and the whole column is **indented from the left**.

If after this you *still* see a scrollbar, tell me your screen resolution (or whether Windows display scaling is 125%/150%) and I’ll adjust the form height and/or button height/margins so all 12 fit reliably.

```vbnet name=DosMenuFormBase.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class DosMenuFormBase
    Inherits Form

    Protected ReadOnly lblMainMenu As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly lblDateTime As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly tmrClock As New Timer()

    Protected ReadOnly flpRight As New FlowLayoutPanel()

    ' Header behavior

    ' Button sizing behavior (default matches Main Menu: stretch to panel width)
    Protected Property StretchButtonsToPanelWidth As Boolean = True
    Protected Property ButtonFixedWidthPx As Integer = 620

    Private _displayVersion As String = ""
    Private _keyHandlers As New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.BackColor = Color.Black
        Me.ForeColor = Color.White
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        _displayVersion = ""
        Try
            _displayVersion = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Catch
            _displayVersion = ""
        End Try

        BuildLayout()
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        AddHandler tmrClock.Tick, Sub() UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Start()

        AddHandler Me.KeyPress, AddressOf OnBaseKeyPress
        AddHandler flpLeft.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        AddHandler flpRight.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)

        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

        MyBase.OnFormClosed(e)
        Try
            tmrClock.Stop()
        Catch
        End Try

        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,

            .RowCount = 3,

        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F)) ' body

        lblMainMenu.AutoSize = False
        lblMainMenu.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblMainMenu.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        lblMainMenu.Height = 60
        lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
        lblMainMenu.BackColor = Color.Black

        lblDateTime.Height = 34
        lblDateTime.Dock = DockStyle.Fill

        lblDateTime.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Regular, GraphicsUnit.Point)
        lblDateTime.ForeColor = Color.White
        lblDateTime.BackColor = Color.Black
        lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)
        lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)

        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .BackColor = Color.Black
        }
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        body.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(lblMainMenu, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(lblDateTime, 0, 1)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 2)

        Controls.Clear()
        Controls.Add(root)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.BackColor = Color.Black
        panel.Padding = New Padding(0)
        panel.Margin = New Padding(0, 6, 0, 0)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub SetMenuTitle(title As String)
        lblMainMenu.Text = title
        Me.Text = title
    End Sub

    Protected Sub ClearMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        _keyHandlers = New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String, handler As Action)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)
        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub() handler()

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)

        If Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) Then
            _keyHandlers(key) = handler
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        If StretchButtonsToPanelWidth Then
            Dim targetWidth As Integer =
                panel.ClientSize.Width -
                panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
                SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

            If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

            For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
                Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
                If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = targetWidth
            Next
        Else
            For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
                Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
                If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = ButtonFixedWidthPx
            Next
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub OnBaseKeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs)
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then up = up.ToUpperInvariant()

        Dim act As Action = Nothing
        If _keyHandlers IsNot Nothing AndAlso _keyHandlers.TryGetValue(up, act) Then
            act()
        End If
    End Sub

    Protected Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

        Dim verPrefix As String = ""
        If ShowVersionInHeader AndAlso Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_displayVersion) Then
            verPrefix = "   " & _displayVersion & "          "
        End If

        lblDateTime.Text = verPrefix &
                           now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                           now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                           now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub

    Protected Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

```vbnet name=FormLedgerMenu.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits DosMenuFormBase

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        SetMenuTitle("CHECKS")

        ' No version/build on this menu
        ShowVersionInHeader = False
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        ' One vertical column, indented left, fixed-width buttons
        StretchButtonsToPanelWidth = False
        ButtonFixedWidthPx = 620
        flpLeft.Padding = New Padding(24, 0, 0, 0)

        ' Disable the second column entirely
        flpRight.Visible = False
        flpRight.Enabled = False

        ' Avoid scrollbars; size to fit 12 buttons comfortably
        flpLeft.AutoScroll = False
        Me.Width = 1000
        Me.Height = 720

        BuildMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildMenu()
        ClearMenu()

        Dim p = flpLeft

        AddMenuButton(p, "1", "Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts", Sub() NotYet("Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "2", "Delete Checks", Sub() NotYet("Delete Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "3", "View Checks", Sub() OpenViewChecksPromptFlow())
        AddMenuButton(p, "5", "View Companys Totals", Sub()
                                                          Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
                                                              f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                          End Using
                                                      End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "6", "Add OTHER Checks", Sub() NotYet("Add OTHER Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "7", "Delete OTHER Checks", Sub() NotYet("Delete OTHER Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "8", "View OTHER Checks", Sub()
                                                       Using f As New FormOtherChecksView()
                                                           f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                       End Using
                                                   End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "9", "Find a Check #", Sub()
                                                   Using f As New FormFindByCheckNumber()
                                                       f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                   End Using
                                               End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "0", "Find an Invoice #", Sub()
                                                       Using f As New FormFindByInvoiceNumber()
                                                           f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                       End Using
                                                   End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "A", "Find Checks that don't balance.", Sub() NotYet("Find Checks that don't balance"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "S", "Sales Employee's checks", Sub() NotYet("Sales Employee's checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "Q", "Quit to Main Menu", Sub() Me.Close())
    End Sub

    ' DOS flow (Customer -> Check # -> Year) implemented via small dialogs.
    Private Sub OpenViewChecksPromptFlow()
        Dim cust As String = Prompt("Enter customer name [Enter = all customers] ?", "")
        If cust Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim chk As String = Prompt("Enter Check Number [Enter = All Checks] ?", "")
        If chk Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim yr As String = Prompt("Enter Year [Enter = All Years] ?", "")
        If yr Is Nothing Then Return

        ' TODO: apply filters when FormLedgerView supports it
        Using f As New FormLedgerView()
            f.ShowDialog(Me)
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function Prompt(promptText As String, defaultValue As String) As String
        Using f As New FormPrompt(promptText, defaultValue)
            Dim dr = f.ShowDialog(Me)
            If dr <> DialogResult.OK Then Return Nothing
            Return f.Value
        End Using
    End Function

    Private NotInheritable Class FormPrompt
        Inherits Form

        Private ReadOnly _txt As New TextBox()

        Public ReadOnly Property Value As String
            Get
                Return _txt.Text
            End Get
        End Property

        Public Sub New(promptText As String, defaultValue As String)
            Me.Text = "CHECKS"
            Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
            Me.FormBorderStyle = FormBorderStyle.FixedDialog
            Me.MinimizeBox = False
            Me.MaximizeBox = False
            Me.ShowInTaskbar = False
            Me.ClientSize = New Drawing.Size(620, 140)

            Dim layout As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
                .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
                .Padding = New Padding(10),
                .ColumnCount = 1,
                .RowCount = 3
            }
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

            Dim lbl As New Label() With {.AutoSize = True, .Text = promptText}

            _txt.Dock = DockStyle.Top
            _txt.Text = defaultValue

            Dim buttons As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill, .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.RightToLeft, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim ok As New Button() With {.Text = "OK", .DialogResult = DialogResult.OK, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim cancel As New Button() With {.Text = "Cancel", .DialogResult = DialogResult.Cancel, .AutoSize = True}
            buttons.Controls.Add(ok)
            buttons.Controls.Add(cancel)

            layout.Controls.Add(lbl, 0, 0)
            layout.Controls.Add(_txt, 0, 1)
            layout.Controls.Add(buttons, 0, 2)

            Me.Controls.Add(layout)
            Me.AcceptButton = ok
            Me.CancelButton = cancel
        End Sub
    End Class

End Class
```

## Copilot

You have **duplicate code pasted into the wrong files / duplicated inside the same class**.

The key clues:

- Errors in **FormMainMenu.vb** complaining about `BuildMenu`, `OpenViewChecksPromptFlow`, `Prompt`, and even `FormPrompt` — those methods/classes **do not belong in Main Menu**. They only exist in the `FormLedgerMenu` code I gave you.
- Errors like “multiple definitions with identical signatures” and “Not most specific” happen when the same method exists **twice** in the same class (or merged via `Partial`).
- Warning `BC40046` indicates you now have **two FormLedgerMenu class declarations** being merged.

## Fix (fastest, safest): restore Main Menu, then clean Ledger Menu

### Step 1 — Fix `FormMainMenu.vb`
Open `FormMainMenu.vb` and remove anything that looks like LedgerMenu code:
- `BuildMenu()` duplicates (if you see it twice, keep one)
- anything named `OpenViewChecksPromptFlow`
- anything named `Prompt`
- any nested class `FormPrompt`

`FormMainMenu.vb` should look like the code you originally pasted (the long main menu code). If you accidentally pasted the LedgerMenu code into it, undo that.

If you use Git: just revert `FormMainMenu.vb` to the last good version.

### Step 2 — Fix `FormLedgerMenu` partial conflict
Because `FormLedgerMenu` is a WinForm created by designer, it is split across:
- `FormLedgerMenu.vb`
- `FormLedgerMenu.Designer.vb`

You must ensure there is **only one** definition of each method in the combined partial class.

**Do this:**
1) Open `FormLedgerMenu.vb`
2) Ensure it starts with:

```vbnet
Partial Public Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits DosMenuFormBase
```

(“Partial” matters because the designer file is partial.)

3) Ensure there is only **one** copy of each of these in `FormLedgerMenu.vb`:
- `OnLoad`
- `BuildMenu`
- `OpenViewChecksPromptFlow`
- `Prompt`
- the nested `FormPrompt` class

If you see duplicates, delete the extra copy.

### Step 3 — Make sure `FormLedgerMenu.Designer.vb` does NOT contain your custom methods
The designer file should only contain control declarations and `InitializeComponent()`.  
If it somehow contains your `BuildMenu`/Prompt code, you pasted into the wrong file—move it back to `FormLedgerMenu.vb`.

---

## Here is the *correct* clean `FormLedgerMenu.vb` (single copy, Partial)
Replace the entire contents of **FormLedgerMenu.vb** with this:

```vbnet name=FormLedgerMenu.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits DosMenuFormBase

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        SetMenuTitle("CHECKS")

        ' No version/build on this menu
        ShowVersionInHeader = False
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        ' One vertical column, indented left, fixed-width buttons
        StretchButtonsToPanelWidth = False
        ButtonFixedWidthPx = 620
        flpLeft.Padding = New Padding(24, 0, 0, 0)

        ' Disable the second column entirely
        flpRight.Visible = False
        flpRight.Enabled = False

        ' Avoid scrollbars; size to fit 12 buttons comfortably
        flpLeft.AutoScroll = False
        Me.Width = 1000
        Me.Height = 720

        BuildLedgerMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildLedgerMenu()
        ClearMenu()

        Dim p = flpLeft

        AddMenuButton(p, "1", "Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts", Sub() NotYet("Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "2", "Delete Checks", Sub() NotYet("Delete Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "3", "View Checks", Sub() OpenViewChecksPromptFlow())
        AddMenuButton(p, "5", "View Companys Totals", Sub()
                                                          Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
                                                              f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                          End Using
                                                      End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "6", "Add OTHER Checks", Sub() NotYet("Add OTHER Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "7", "Delete OTHER Checks", Sub() NotYet("Delete OTHER Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "8", "View OTHER Checks", Sub()
                                                       Using f As New FormOtherChecksView()
                                                           f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                       End Using
                                                   End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "9", "Find a Check #", Sub()
                                                   Using f As New FormFindByCheckNumber()
                                                       f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                   End Using
                                               End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "0", "Find an Invoice #", Sub()
                                                       Using f As New FormFindByInvoiceNumber()
                                                           f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                       End Using
                                                   End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "A", "Find Checks that don't balance.", Sub() NotYet("Find Checks that don't balance"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "S", "Sales Employee's checks", Sub() NotYet("Sales Employee's checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "Q", "Quit to Main Menu", Sub() Me.Close())
    End Sub

    Private Sub OpenViewChecksPromptFlow()
        Dim cust As String = ShowPromptDialog("Enter customer name [Enter = all customers] ?", "")
        If cust Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim chk As String = ShowPromptDialog("Enter Check Number [Enter = All Checks] ?", "")
        If chk Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim yr As String = ShowPromptDialog("Enter Year [Enter = All Years] ?", "")
        If yr Is Nothing Then Return

        ' TODO: apply filters when FormLedgerView supports it
        Using f As New FormLedgerView()
            f.ShowDialog(Me)
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function ShowPromptDialog(promptText As String, defaultValue As String) As String
        Using f As New FormLedgerPrompt(promptText, defaultValue)
            Dim dr = f.ShowDialog(Me)
            If dr <> DialogResult.OK Then Return Nothing
            Return f.Value
        End Using
    End Function

    Private NotInheritable Class FormLedgerPrompt
        Inherits Form

        Private ReadOnly txtValue As New TextBox()

        Public ReadOnly Property Value As String
            Get
                Return txtValue.Text
            End Get
        End Property

        Public Sub New(promptText As String, defaultValue As String)
            Me.Text = "CHECKS"
            Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
            Me.FormBorderStyle = FormBorderStyle.FixedDialog
            Me.MinimizeBox = False
            Me.MaximizeBox = False
            Me.ShowInTaskbar = False
            Me.ClientSize = New Drawing.Size(620, 140)

            Dim layout As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
                .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
                .Padding = New Padding(10),
                .ColumnCount = 1,
                .RowCount = 3
            }
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

            Dim lbl As New Label() With {.AutoSize = True, .Text = promptText}

            txtValue.Dock = DockStyle.Top
            txtValue.Text = defaultValue

            Dim buttons As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill, .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.RightToLeft, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim ok As New Button() With {.Text = "OK", .DialogResult = DialogResult.OK, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim cancel As New Button() With {.Text = "Cancel", .DialogResult = DialogResult.Cancel, .AutoSize = True}
            buttons.Controls.Add(ok)
            buttons.Controls.Add(cancel)

            layout.Controls.Add(lbl, 0, 0)
            layout.Controls.Add(txtValue, 0, 1)
            layout.Controls.Add(buttons, 0, 2)

            Me.Controls.Add(layout)
            Me.AcceptButton = ok
            Me.CancelButton = cancel
        End Sub
    End Class

End Class
```

Changes vs before:
- Renamed `BuildMenu` → `BuildLedgerMenu` to avoid name collisions if you already had a `BuildMenu` somewhere else in a partial.
- Renamed nested prompt class to `FormLedgerPrompt`.
- Renamed `_txt` to `txtValue` to avoid ambiguity.
- Declared the class `Partial Public` so it merges cleanly with the designer.

---

## Now: do this and tell me what remains
1) Replace `FormLedgerMenu.vb` with the code above.
2) Revert/fix `FormMainMenu.vb` to your original main menu code (remove any Ledger prompt code that got pasted there).
3) Build again.

If there are still errors, paste just the **first 5 error lines** from the Build Output window (not the whole list), and I’ll pinpoint the remaining duplicate file causing it.

## Copilot

Good—then you’re back to a clean state.

Quick checklist to confirm everything matches what you wanted for **CHECKS**:

1) **Title font/color matches Main Menu**
- `DosMenuFormBase`:  
  - `lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36F, FontStyle.Bold, ...)`  
  - `lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Yellow`

2) **No version/build on this menu**
- `DosMenuFormBase` has `ShowVersionInHeader` and `UpdateHeaderClock()` only prepends version when it’s `True`.
- `FormLedgerMenu` leaves it `False` (or explicitly sets it `False`).

3) **Single column, indented left, same width buttons**
- `FormLedgerMenu`:
  - `StretchButtonsToPanelWidth = False`
  - `ButtonFixedWidthPx = 620` (adjust if needed)
  - `flpLeft.Padding = New Padding(24, 0, 0, 0)`
  - `flpRight.Visible = False`
  - all buttons added to `flpLeft`

If any of these aren’t behaving (e.g., buttons still stretching, or scrollbar appears), tell me:
- your screen resolution and Windows display scaling (100%/125%/150%)
- what width you want the buttons to be relative to the window (e.g., “about 70% of window width”)

…and I’ll give you the exact numbers to make it fit perfectly without scrollbars.
