## Copilot

Update `DosMenuFormBase.BuildLayout()` so the header is a **single row** with:
- **Title (left)**
- **Day/Date/Time (right, yellow)**

Do it by replacing the two separate rows (`lblMainMenu` then `lblDateTime`) with a single header `TableLayoutPanel` that contains both labels on the same line.

Here is the updated **complete** `DosMenuFormBase.vb` (drop-in replace). Your `FormLedgerMenu.vb` does not need changes for this.

```vbnet name=DosMenuFormBase.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class DosMenuFormBase
    Inherits Form

    Protected ReadOnly lblMainMenu As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly lblDateTime As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly tmrClock As New Timer()

    Protected ReadOnly flpLeft As New FlowLayoutPanel()
    Protected ReadOnly flpRight As New FlowLayoutPanel()

    ' Header behavior
    Protected Property ShowVersionInHeader As Boolean = False

    ' Button sizing behavior (default matches Main Menu: stretch to panel width)
    Protected Property StretchButtonsToPanelWidth As Boolean = True
    Protected Property ButtonFixedWidthPx As Integer = 620

    Private _displayVersion As String = ""
    Private _keyHandlers As New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.BackColor = Color.Black
        Me.ForeColor = Color.White
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        _displayVersion = ""
        Try
            _displayVersion = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Catch
            _displayVersion = ""
        End Try

        BuildLayout()
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        AddHandler tmrClock.Tick, Sub() UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Start()

        AddHandler Me.KeyPress, AddressOf OnBaseKeyPress
        AddHandler flpLeft.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        AddHandler flpRight.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)

        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnFormClosed(e As FormClosedEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnFormClosed(e)
        Try
            tmrClock.Stop()
        Catch
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildLayout()
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 2,
            .BackColor = Color.Black,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' header row (title + clock)
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F)) ' body

        ' Header row: title left, clock right (same line)
        Dim header As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .BackColor = Color.Black,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .Margin = New Padding(0)
        }
        header.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        header.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        lblMainMenu.AutoSize = True
        lblMainMenu.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
        lblMainMenu.BackColor = Color.Black
        lblMainMenu.Margin = New Padding(0)

        lblDateTime.AutoSize = False
        lblDateTime.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleRight
        lblDateTime.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Regular, GraphicsUnit.Point)
        lblDateTime.ForeColor = Color.Yellow   ' CHANGED: yellow like main menu
        lblDateTime.BackColor = Color.Black
        lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)
        lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0) ' keep slight vertical alignment

        header.Controls.Add(lblMainMenu, 0, 0)
        header.Controls.Add(lblDateTime, 1, 0)

        ' Body: two columns available; derived forms can hide flpRight for single-column menus
        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .BackColor = Color.Black
        }
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight)

        body.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(header, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 1)

        Controls.Clear()
        Controls.Add(root)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.BackColor = Color.Black
        panel.Padding = New Padding(0)
        panel.Margin = New Padding(0, 6, 0, 0)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub SetMenuTitle(title As String)
        lblMainMenu.Text = title
        Me.Text = title
    End Sub

    Protected Sub ClearMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        _keyHandlers = New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String, handler As Action)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)
        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub() handler()

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)

        If Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) Then
            _keyHandlers(key) = handler
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        If StretchButtonsToPanelWidth Then
            Dim targetWidth As Integer =
                panel.ClientSize.Width -
                panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
                SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

            If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

            For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
                Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
                If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = targetWidth
            Next
        Else
            For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
                Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
                If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = ButtonFixedWidthPx
            Next
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub OnBaseKeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs)
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then up = up.ToUpperInvariant()

        Dim act As Action = Nothing
        If _keyHandlers IsNot Nothing AndAlso _keyHandlers.TryGetValue(up, act) Then
            act()
        End If
    End Sub

    Protected Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

        Dim verPrefix As String = ""
        If ShowVersionInHeader AndAlso Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_displayVersion) Then
            verPrefix = "   " & _displayVersion & "          "
        End If

        ' NOTE: lblDateTime is now right-justified and on the same line as title
        lblDateTime.Text = verPrefix &
                           now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                           now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                           now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub

    Protected Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

If you want the clock to align vertically to the “middle line” of the Castellar text more precisely, tell me if it’s sitting too high/low; we can adjust `lblDateTime.Padding` (top padding) or set `header` row height explicitly.

## @testsubject117

also, what I did on the main menu was set the background color to dark grey and the main menu title as well as the buttons to black so there is a slight contrast. can you do that as well?

## Copilot

Yes—update the base to use a **dark gray form background** while keeping the **title background black** and **buttons black**, so you get the same subtle contrast everywhere.

Below is the updated `DosMenuFormBase.vb` reflecting:
- Form + containers background: **Dark Gray** (`Color.FromArgb(32,32,32)`—adjustable)
- Header/title strip background: **Black**
- Menu panels background: **Dark Gray** (so black buttons “pop”)
- Title + clock text: **Yellow**
- Buttons remain **Black** with **DimGray** border (as before)

```vbnet name=DosMenuFormBase.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class DosMenuFormBase
    Inherits Form

    Protected ReadOnly lblMainMenu As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly lblDateTime As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly tmrClock As New Timer()

    Protected ReadOnly flpLeft As New FlowLayoutPanel()
    Protected ReadOnly flpRight As New FlowLayoutPanel()

    ' Header behavior
    Protected Property ShowVersionInHeader As Boolean = False

    ' Button sizing behavior (default matches Main Menu: stretch to panel width)
    Protected Property StretchButtonsToPanelWidth As Boolean = True
    Protected Property ButtonFixedWidthPx As Integer = 620

    ' Theme colors (tweak here if you want it lighter/darker)
    Private ReadOnly _bgDarkGray As Color = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
    Private ReadOnly _bgBlack As Color = Color.Black

    Private _displayVersion As String = ""
    Private _keyHandlers As New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.BackColor = _bgDarkGray
        Me.ForeColor = Color.White
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        _displayVersion = ""
        Try
            _displayVersion = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Catch
            _displayVersion = ""
        End Try

        BuildLayout()
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        AddHandler tmrClock.Tick, Sub() UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Start()

        AddHandler Me.KeyPress, AddressOf OnBaseKeyPress
        AddHandler flpLeft.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        AddHandler flpRight.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)

        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnFormClosed(e As FormClosedEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnFormClosed(e)
        Try
            tmrClock.Stop()
        Catch
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildLayout()
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 2,
            .BackColor = _bgDarkGray,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' header row (title + clock)
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F)) ' body

        ' Header row: title left, clock right (same line)
        Dim header As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .BackColor = _bgBlack,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .Margin = New Padding(0),
            .Padding = New Padding(8, 6, 8, 6)
        }
        header.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        header.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        lblMainMenu.AutoSize = True
        lblMainMenu.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
        lblMainMenu.BackColor = _bgBlack
        lblMainMenu.Margin = New Padding(0)

        lblDateTime.AutoSize = False
        lblDateTime.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleRight
        lblDateTime.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Regular, GraphicsUnit.Point)
        lblDateTime.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
        lblDateTime.BackColor = _bgBlack
        lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)
        lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)

        header.Controls.Add(lblMainMenu, 0, 0)
        header.Controls.Add(lblDateTime, 1, 0)

        ' Body: dark gray background so black buttons contrast
        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .BackColor = _bgDarkGray
        }
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft, _bgDarkGray)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight, _bgDarkGray)

        body.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(header, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 1)

        Controls.Clear()
        Controls.Add(root)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, bg As Color)
        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.BackColor = bg
        panel.Padding = New Padding(0)
        panel.Margin = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub SetMenuTitle(title As String)
        lblMainMenu.Text = title
        Me.Text = title
    End Sub

    Protected Sub ClearMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        _keyHandlers = New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String, handler As Action)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        ' Black buttons on dark gray background
        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)
        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub() handler()

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)

        If Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) Then
            _keyHandlers(key) = handler
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        If StretchButtonsToPanelWidth Then
            Dim targetWidth As Integer =
                panel.ClientSize.Width -
                panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
                SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

            If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

            For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
                Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
                If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = targetWidth
            Next
        Else
            For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
                Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
                If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = ButtonFixedWidthPx
            Next
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub OnBaseKeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs)
        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then up = up.ToUpperInvariant()

        Dim act As Action = Nothing
        If _keyHandlers IsNot Nothing AndAlso _keyHandlers.TryGetValue(up, act) Then
            act()
        End If
    End Sub

    Protected Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

        Dim verPrefix As String = ""
        If ShowVersionInHeader AndAlso Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_displayVersion) Then
            verPrefix = "   " & _displayVersion & "          "
        End If

        lblDateTime.Text = verPrefix &
                           now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                           now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                           now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub

    Protected Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

If you want the *exact* dark gray you used on Main Menu, paste the line from `FormMainMenu.Designer.vb` (or wherever you set it), e.g. `Me.BackColor = Color.FromArgb(...)`, and I’ll match the RGB exactly.

## @testsubject117

very nice. ok, only 2 changes. 1) the Day, Date and Time needs to move up 2 lines and be bolded, and 2) can we change the bottom button (Q) Quit to Main Menu to a (ESC) Close button that matches all the other close buttons and is at far right?

## Copilot

```vbnet name=DosMenuFormBase.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class DosMenuFormBase
    Inherits Form

    Protected ReadOnly lblMainMenu As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly lblDateTime As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly tmrClock As New Timer()

    Protected ReadOnly flpLeft As New FlowLayoutPanel()
    Protected ReadOnly flpRight As New FlowLayoutPanel()

    ' Header behavior
    Protected Property ShowVersionInHeader As Boolean = False

    ' Button sizing behavior (default matches Main Menu: stretch to panel width)
    Protected Property StretchButtonsToPanelWidth As Boolean = True
    Protected Property ButtonFixedWidthPx As Integer = 620

    ' Theme colors (tweak here if you want it lighter/darker)
    Private ReadOnly _bgDarkGray As Color = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
    Private ReadOnly _bgBlack As Color = Color.Black

    Private _displayVersion As String = ""
    Private _keyHandlers As New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.BackColor = _bgDarkGray
        Me.ForeColor = Color.White
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        _displayVersion = ""
        Try
            _displayVersion = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Catch
            _displayVersion = ""
        End Try

        BuildLayout()
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        AddHandler tmrClock.Tick, Sub() UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Start()

        AddHandler Me.KeyPress, AddressOf OnBaseKeyPress
        AddHandler flpLeft.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        AddHandler flpRight.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)

        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnFormClosed(e As FormClosedEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnFormClosed(e)
        Try
            tmrClock.Stop()
        Catch
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildLayout()
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 2,
            .BackColor = _bgDarkGray,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' header row (title + clock)
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F)) ' body

        ' Header row: title left, clock right (same line)
        Dim header As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .BackColor = _bgBlack,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .Margin = New Padding(0),
            .Padding = New Padding(8, 6, 8, 6)
        }
        header.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        header.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        lblMainMenu.AutoSize = True
        lblMainMenu.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
        lblMainMenu.BackColor = _bgBlack
        lblMainMenu.Margin = New Padding(0)

        lblDateTime.AutoSize = False
        lblDateTime.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleRight
        lblDateTime.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point) ' CHANGED: bold
        lblDateTime.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
        lblDateTime.BackColor = _bgBlack
        lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)
        lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0, 0, 0, 0) ' CHANGED: move up (was top padding 10)

        header.Controls.Add(lblMainMenu, 0, 0)
        header.Controls.Add(lblDateTime, 1, 0)

        ' Body: dark gray background so black buttons contrast
        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .BackColor = _bgDarkGray
        }
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft, _bgDarkGray)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight, _bgDarkGray)

        body.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(header, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 1)

        Controls.Clear()
        Controls.Add(root)
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, bg As Color)
        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.BackColor = bg
        panel.Padding = New Padding(0)
        panel.Margin = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub SetMenuTitle(title As String)
        lblMainMenu.Text = title
        Me.Text = title
    End Sub

    Protected Sub ClearMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()
        _keyHandlers = New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String, handler As Action)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)
        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub() handler()

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)

        If Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) Then
            _keyHandlers(key) = handler
        End If
    End Sub

    ' NEW: right-aligned "Close" button for ESC
    Protected Sub AddCloseButtonRight(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        Dim row As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .AutoSize = False,
            .Height = 42,
            .BackColor = panel.BackColor,
            .Margin = New Padding(0, 12, 0, 0)
        }
        row.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))
        row.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

        Dim btn As New Button()
        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Width = 220
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(ESC) Close"
        btn.Tag = "ESC"
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub() Me.Close()

        ' add to right cell
        row.Controls.Add(New Panel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill, .BackColor = panel.BackColor}, 0, 0)
        row.Controls.Add(btn, 1, 0)

        panel.Controls.Add(row)

        ' register ESC key handler
        _keyHandlers("ESC") = Sub() Me.Close()
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        If StretchButtonsToPanelWidth Then
            Dim targetWidth As Integer =
                panel.ClientSize.Width -
                panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
                SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

            If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

            For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
                Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
                If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = targetWidth
            Next
        Else
            For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
                Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
                If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = ButtonFixedWidthPx
            Next
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub OnBaseKeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs)
        ' Handle ESC specifically
        If e.KeyChar = ChrW(27) Then
            Dim escAct As Action = Nothing
            If _keyHandlers IsNot Nothing AndAlso _keyHandlers.TryGetValue("ESC", escAct) Then
                escAct()
            Else
                Me.Close()
            End If
            Return
        End If

        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then up = up.ToUpperInvariant()

        Dim act As Action = Nothing
        If _keyHandlers IsNot Nothing AndAlso _keyHandlers.TryGetValue(up, act) Then
            act()
        End If
    End Sub

    Protected Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

        Dim verPrefix As String = ""
        If ShowVersionInHeader AndAlso Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_displayVersion) Then
            verPrefix = "   " & _displayVersion & "          "
        End If

        lblDateTime.Text = verPrefix &
                           now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                           now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                           now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub

    Protected Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

```vbnet name=FormLedgerMenu.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits DosMenuFormBase

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        SetMenuTitle("CHECKS")

        ' No version/build on this menu
        ShowVersionInHeader = False
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        ' One vertical column, indented left, fixed-width buttons
        StretchButtonsToPanelWidth = False
        ButtonFixedWidthPx = 620
        flpLeft.Padding = New Padding(24, 0, 0, 0)

        ' Disable the second column entirely
        flpRight.Visible = False
        flpRight.Enabled = False

        ' Avoid scrollbars; size to fit 12 buttons comfortably
        flpLeft.AutoScroll = False
        Me.Width = 1000
        Me.Height = 720

        BuildLedgerMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildLedgerMenu()
        ClearMenu()

        Dim p = flpLeft

        AddMenuButton(p, "1", "Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts", Sub() NotYet("Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "2", "Delete Checks", Sub() NotYet("Delete Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "3", "View Checks", Sub() OpenViewChecksPromptFlow())
        AddMenuButton(p, "5", "View Companys Totals", Sub()
                                                          Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
                                                              f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                          End Using
                                                      End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "6", "Add OTHER Checks", Sub() NotYet("Add OTHER Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "7", "Delete OTHER Checks", Sub() NotYet("Delete OTHER Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "8", "View OTHER Checks", Sub()
                                                       Using f As New FormOtherChecksView()
                                                           f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                       End Using
                                                   End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "9", "Find a Check #", Sub()
                                                   Using f As New FormFindByCheckNumber()
                                                       f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                   End Using
                                               End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "0", "Find an Invoice #", Sub()
                                                       Using f As New FormFindByInvoiceNumber()
                                                           f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                       End Using
                                                   End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "A", "Find Checks that don't balance.", Sub() NotYet("Find Checks that don't balance"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "S", "Sales Employee's checks", Sub() NotYet("Sales Employee's checks"))

        ' Replace Q Quit with ESC Close, right-aligned
        AddCloseButtonRight(p)
    End Sub

    Private Sub OpenViewChecksPromptFlow()
        Dim cust As String = ShowPromptDialog("Enter customer name [Enter = all customers] ?", "")
        If cust Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim chk As String = ShowPromptDialog("Enter Check Number [Enter = All Checks] ?", "")
        If chk Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim yr As String = ShowPromptDialog("Enter Year [Enter = All Years] ?", "")
        If yr Is Nothing Then Return

        Using f As New FormLedgerView()
            f.ShowDialog(Me)
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function ShowPromptDialog(promptText As String, defaultValue As String) As String
        Using f As New FormLedgerPrompt(promptText, defaultValue)
            Dim dr = f.ShowDialog(Me)
            If dr <> DialogResult.OK Then Return Nothing
            Return f.Value
        End Using
    End Function

    Private NotInheritable Class FormLedgerPrompt
        Inherits Form

        Private ReadOnly txtValue As New TextBox()

        Public ReadOnly Property Value As String
            Get
                Return txtValue.Text
            End Get
        End Property

        Public Sub New(promptText As String, defaultValue As String)
            Me.Text = "CHECKS"
            Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
            Me.FormBorderStyle = FormBorderStyle.FixedDialog
            Me.MinimizeBox = False
            Me.MaximizeBox = False
            Me.ShowInTaskbar = False
            Me.ClientSize = New Drawing.Size(620, 140)

            Dim layout As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
                .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
                .Padding = New Padding(10),
                .ColumnCount = 1,
                .RowCount = 3
            }
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

            Dim lbl As New Label() With {.AutoSize = True, .Text = promptText}

            txtValue.Dock = DockStyle.Top
            txtValue.Text = defaultValue

            Dim buttons As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill, .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.RightToLeft, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim ok As New Button() With {.Text = "OK", .DialogResult = DialogResult.OK, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim cancel As New Button() With {.Text = "Cancel", .DialogResult = DialogResult.Cancel, .AutoSize = True}
            buttons.Controls.Add(ok)
            buttons.Controls.Add(cancel)

            layout.Controls.Add(lbl, 0, 0)
            layout.Controls.Add(txtValue, 0, 1)
            layout.Controls.Add(buttons, 0, 2)

            Me.Controls.Add(layout)
            Me.AcceptButton = ok
            Me.CancelButton = cancel
        End Sub
    End Class

End Class
```

## @testsubject117

ok, that changed the bottom button but see screenshot I've included. It needs to be a smaller, grey colored button and at the bottom right of the window: 

## Copilot

Make the Close button a **separate bottom bar** (not part of the FlowLayout list). That’s why it’s currently lining up like another menu button.

### What to change
1) In **`DosMenuFormBase`**, add a bottom `Panel` (docked Bottom) that contains a small gray `(ESC) Close` button anchored bottom-right.
2) Remove the old `AddCloseButtonRight(...)` approach (or just stop calling it).
3) In **`FormLedgerMenu`**, don’t add Close as a menu button; just enable the base close button.

Below are the updated files.

---

```vbnet name=DosMenuFormBase.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Collections.Generic
Imports System.Drawing
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Public Class DosMenuFormBase
    Inherits Form

    Protected ReadOnly lblMainMenu As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly lblDateTime As New Label()
    Protected ReadOnly tmrClock As New Timer()

    Protected ReadOnly flpLeft As New FlowLayoutPanel()
    Protected ReadOnly flpRight As New FlowLayoutPanel()

    ' NEW: bottom bar + close button
    Private ReadOnly pnlBottom As New Panel()
    Private ReadOnly btnEscClose As New Button()

    ' Header behavior
    Protected Property ShowVersionInHeader As Boolean = False

    ' Button sizing behavior (default matches Main Menu: stretch to panel width)
    Protected Property StretchButtonsToPanelWidth As Boolean = True
    Protected Property ButtonFixedWidthPx As Integer = 620

    ' Theme colors
    Private ReadOnly _bgDarkGray As Color = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
    Private ReadOnly _bgBlack As Color = Color.Black

    Private _displayVersion As String = ""
    Private _keyHandlers As New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        Me.KeyPreview = True
        Me.BackColor = _bgDarkGray
        Me.ForeColor = Color.White
        Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent

        _displayVersion = ""
        Try
            _displayVersion = BuildInfo.DisplayVersion
        Catch
            _displayVersion = ""
        End Try

        BuildLayout()
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        tmrClock.Interval = 1000
        AddHandler tmrClock.Tick, Sub() UpdateHeaderClock()
        tmrClock.Start()

        AddHandler Me.KeyPress, AddressOf OnBaseKeyPress
        AddHandler flpLeft.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
        AddHandler flpRight.SizeChanged, Sub() ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)

        Me.BeginInvoke(New Action(Sub()
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpLeft)
                                      ResizeButtonsToPanel(flpRight)
                                  End Sub))
    End Sub

    Protected Overrides Sub OnFormClosed(e As FormClosedEventArgs)
        MyBase.OnFormClosed(e)
        Try
            tmrClock.Stop()
        Catch
        End Try
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildLayout()
        ' Root host panel so we can Dock bottom bar and fill content above it
        Dim host As New Panel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .BackColor = _bgDarkGray,
            .Padding = New Padding(12)
        }

        ' Bottom bar with small gray ESC Close at bottom-right
        pnlBottom.Dock = DockStyle.Bottom
        pnlBottom.Height = 52
        pnlBottom.BackColor = _bgDarkGray
        pnlBottom.Padding = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)

        btnEscClose.Text = "(ESC) Close"
        btnEscClose.AutoSize = False
        btnEscClose.Width = 160
        btnEscClose.Height = 30
        btnEscClose.Anchor = AnchorStyles.Right Or AnchorStyles.Bottom
        btnEscClose.Left = pnlBottom.ClientSize.Width - btnEscClose.Width
        btnEscClose.Top = pnlBottom.Height - btnEscClose.Height
        btnEscClose.Margin = New Padding(0)

        ' Grey button styling (smaller than menu buttons)
        btnEscClose.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btnEscClose.BackColor = Color.DimGray
        btnEscClose.ForeColor = Color.White
        btnEscClose.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btnEscClose.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.Gray
        btnEscClose.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
        btnEscClose.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 10.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point)

        AddHandler btnEscClose.Click, Sub() Me.Close()
        pnlBottom.Controls.Clear()
        pnlBottom.Controls.Add(btnEscClose)
        AddHandler pnlBottom.Resize, Sub()
                                         btnEscClose.Left = pnlBottom.ClientSize.Width - btnEscClose.Width
                                         btnEscClose.Top = pnlBottom.ClientSize.Height - btnEscClose.Height
                                     End Sub

        ' Main content (header + body) fills above bottom bar
        Dim root As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 1,
            .RowCount = 2,
            .BackColor = _bgDarkGray,
            .Margin = New Padding(0)
        }
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize)) ' header row
        root.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F)) ' body

        ' Header row: title left, clock right (same line)
        Dim header As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .BackColor = _bgBlack,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .Margin = New Padding(0),
            .Padding = New Padding(8, 6, 8, 6)
        }
        header.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
        header.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 100.0F))

        lblMainMenu.AutoSize = True
        lblMainMenu.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        lblMainMenu.Font = New Font("Castellar", 36.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        lblMainMenu.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
        lblMainMenu.BackColor = _bgBlack
        lblMainMenu.Margin = New Padding(0)

        lblDateTime.AutoSize = False
        lblDateTime.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        lblDateTime.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleRight
        lblDateTime.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point) ' bold
        lblDateTime.ForeColor = Color.Yellow
        lblDateTime.BackColor = _bgBlack
        lblDateTime.Margin = New Padding(0)
        lblDateTime.Padding = New Padding(0) ' moved up (no top padding)

        header.Controls.Add(lblMainMenu, 0, 0)
        header.Controls.Add(lblDateTime, 1, 0)

        ' Body: dark gray background so black buttons contrast
        Dim body As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
            .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
            .ColumnCount = 2,
            .RowCount = 1,
            .BackColor = _bgDarkGray
        }
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))
        body.ColumnStyles.Add(New ColumnStyle(SizeType.Percent, 50.0F))

        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpLeft, _bgDarkGray)
        ConfigureMenuPanel(flpRight, _bgDarkGray)

        body.Controls.Add(flpLeft, 0, 0)
        body.Controls.Add(flpRight, 1, 0)

        root.Controls.Add(header, 0, 0)
        root.Controls.Add(body, 0, 1)

        host.Controls.Clear()
        host.Controls.Add(root)
        host.Controls.Add(pnlBottom)

        Controls.Clear()
        Controls.Add(host)

        ' Register ESC handler globally
        _keyHandlers("ESC") = Sub() Me.Close()
    End Sub

    Private Shared Sub ConfigureMenuPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, bg As Color)
        panel.Dock = DockStyle.Fill
        panel.FlowDirection = FlowDirection.TopDown
        panel.WrapContents = False
        panel.AutoScroll = True
        panel.BackColor = bg
        panel.Padding = New Padding(0)
        panel.Margin = New Padding(0, 10, 0, 0)
    End Sub

    Protected Sub SetMenuTitle(title As String)
        lblMainMenu.Text = title
        Me.Text = title
    End Sub

    Protected Sub ClearMenu()
        flpLeft.Controls.Clear()
        flpRight.Controls.Clear()

        ' Keep ESC close handler
        Dim esc = _keyHandlers("ESC")
        _keyHandlers = New Dictionary(Of String, Action)(StringComparer.OrdinalIgnoreCase)
        _keyHandlers("ESC") = esc
    End Sub

    Protected Sub AddMenuButton(panel As FlowLayoutPanel, key As String, text As String, handler As Action)
        Dim btn As New Button()

        btn.AutoSize = False
        btn.Height = 36
        btn.Font = New Font("Segoe UI", 12.0F, FontStyle.Bold, GraphicsUnit.Point, CByte(0))
        btn.TextAlign = ContentAlignment.MiddleLeft
        btn.Text = "(" & key & ") " & text
        btn.Tag = key
        btn.Margin = New Padding(3, 3, 3, 6)

        btn.UseVisualStyleBackColor = False
        btn.BackColor = Color.Black
        btn.ForeColor = Color.White
        btn.FlatStyle = FlatStyle.Flat
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.DimGray
        btn.FlatAppearance.BorderSize = 1
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.FromArgb(32, 32, 32)
        btn.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.FromArgb(64, 64, 64)
        btn.AutoEllipsis = True

        AddHandler btn.Click, Sub() handler()

        panel.Controls.Add(btn)

        If Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(key) Then
            _keyHandlers(key) = handler
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub ResizeButtonsToPanel(panel As FlowLayoutPanel)
        If panel Is Nothing Then Return

        If StretchButtonsToPanelWidth Then
            Dim targetWidth As Integer =
                panel.ClientSize.Width -
                panel.Padding.Left - panel.Padding.Right -
                SystemInformation.VerticalScrollBarWidth - 6

            If targetWidth < 150 Then targetWidth = 150

            For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
                Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
                If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = targetWidth
            Next
        Else
            For Each c As Control In panel.Controls
                Dim btn = TryCast(c, Button)
                If btn IsNot Nothing Then btn.Width = ButtonFixedWidthPx
            Next
        End If
    End Sub

    Private Sub OnBaseKeyPress(sender As Object, e As KeyPressEventArgs)
        ' ESC
        If e.KeyChar = ChrW(27) Then
            Dim escAct As Action = Nothing
            If _keyHandlers IsNot Nothing AndAlso _keyHandlers.TryGetValue("ESC", escAct) Then
                escAct()
            Else
                Me.Close()
            End If
            Return
        End If

        Dim ch As String = e.KeyChar.ToString()
        If ch = vbCr OrElse ch = vbLf Then Return

        Dim up As String = ch
        If up.Length = 1 AndAlso Char.IsLetter(up(0)) Then up = up.ToUpperInvariant()

        Dim act As Action = Nothing
        If _keyHandlers IsNot Nothing AndAlso _keyHandlers.TryGetValue(up, act) Then
            act()
        End If
    End Sub

    Protected Sub UpdateHeaderClock()
        Dim now As DateTime = DateTime.Now

        Dim verPrefix As String = ""
        If ShowVersionInHeader AndAlso Not String.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(_displayVersion) Then
            verPrefix = "   " & _displayVersion & "          "
        End If

        lblDateTime.Text = verPrefix &
                           now.ToString("dddd") & "  " &
                           now.ToString("MM-dd-yyyy") & "          " &
                           now.ToString("hh:mm:ss tt")
    End Sub

    Protected Sub NotYet(feature As String)
        MessageBox.Show("Not implemented yet: " & feature, "Port status", MessageBoxButtons.OK, MessageBoxIcon.Information)
    End Sub

End Class
```

```vbnet name=FormLedgerMenu.vb
Option Strict Off
Option Explicit On

Imports System
Imports System.Windows.Forms

Partial Public Class FormLedgerMenu
    Inherits DosMenuFormBase

    Protected Overrides Sub OnLoad(e As EventArgs)
        MyBase.OnLoad(e)

        SetMenuTitle("CHECKS")

        ' No version/build on this menu
        ShowVersionInHeader = False
        UpdateHeaderClock()

        ' One vertical column, indented left, fixed-width buttons
        StretchButtonsToPanelWidth = False
        ButtonFixedWidthPx = 620
        flpLeft.Padding = New Padding(24, 0, 0, 0)

        ' Disable the second column entirely
        flpRight.Visible = False
        flpRight.Enabled = False

        ' The bottom ESC Close button is now in the base bottom bar,
        ' so do NOT add it as a menu button.
        flpLeft.AutoScroll = False

        Me.Width = 1000
        Me.Height = 720

        BuildLedgerMenu()
    End Sub

    Private Sub BuildLedgerMenu()
        ClearMenu()

        Dim p = flpLeft

        AddMenuButton(p, "1", "Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts", Sub() NotYet("Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "2", "Delete Checks", Sub() NotYet("Delete Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "3", "View Checks", Sub() OpenViewChecksPromptFlow())
        AddMenuButton(p, "5", "View Companys Totals", Sub()
                                                          Using f As New FormLedgerCompanyTotals()
                                                              f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                          End Using
                                                      End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "6", "Add OTHER Checks", Sub() NotYet("Add OTHER Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "7", "Delete OTHER Checks", Sub() NotYet("Delete OTHER Checks"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "8", "View OTHER Checks", Sub()
                                                       Using f As New FormOtherChecksView()
                                                           f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                       End Using
                                                   End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "9", "Find a Check #", Sub()
                                                   Using f As New FormFindByCheckNumber()
                                                       f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                   End Using
                                               End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "0", "Find an Invoice #", Sub()
                                                       Using f As New FormFindByInvoiceNumber()
                                                           f.ShowDialog(Me)
                                                       End Using
                                                   End Sub)
        AddMenuButton(p, "A", "Find Checks that don't balance.", Sub() NotYet("Find Checks that don't balance"))
        AddMenuButton(p, "S", "Sales Employee's checks", Sub() NotYet("Sales Employee's checks"))
    End Sub

    Private Sub OpenViewChecksPromptFlow()
        Dim cust As String = ShowPromptDialog("Enter customer name [Enter = all customers] ?", "")
        If cust Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim chk As String = ShowPromptDialog("Enter Check Number [Enter = All Checks] ?", "")
        If chk Is Nothing Then Return

        Dim yr As String = ShowPromptDialog("Enter Year [Enter = All Years] ?", "")
        If yr Is Nothing Then Return

        Using f As New FormLedgerView()
            f.ShowDialog(Me)
        End Using
    End Sub

    Private Function ShowPromptDialog(promptText As String, defaultValue As String) As String
        Using f As New FormLedgerPrompt(promptText, defaultValue)
            Dim dr = f.ShowDialog(Me)
            If dr <> DialogResult.OK Then Return Nothing
            Return f.Value
        End Using
    End Function

    Private NotInheritable Class FormLedgerPrompt
        Inherits Form

        Private ReadOnly txtValue As New TextBox()

        Public ReadOnly Property Value As String
            Get
                Return txtValue.Text
            End Get
        End Property

        Public Sub New(promptText As String, defaultValue As String)
            Me.Text = "CHECKS"
            Me.StartPosition = FormStartPosition.CenterParent
            Me.FormBorderStyle = FormBorderStyle.FixedDialog
            Me.MinimizeBox = False
            Me.MaximizeBox = False
            Me.ShowInTaskbar = False
            Me.ClientSize = New Drawing.Size(620, 140)

            Dim layout As New TableLayoutPanel() With {
                .Dock = DockStyle.Fill,
                .Padding = New Padding(10),
                .ColumnCount = 1,
                .RowCount = 3
            }
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))
            layout.RowStyles.Add(New RowStyle(SizeType.AutoSize))

            Dim lbl As New Label() With {.AutoSize = True, .Text = promptText}

            txtValue.Dock = DockStyle.Top
            txtValue.Text = defaultValue

            Dim buttons As New FlowLayoutPanel() With {.Dock = DockStyle.Fill, .FlowDirection = FlowDirection.RightToLeft, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim ok As New Button() With {.Text = "OK", .DialogResult = DialogResult.OK, .AutoSize = True}
            Dim cancel As New Button() With {.Text = "Cancel", .DialogResult = DialogResult.Cancel, .AutoSize = True}
            buttons.Controls.Add(ok)
            buttons.Controls.Add(cancel)

            layout.Controls.Add(lbl, 0, 0)
            layout.Controls.Add(txtValue, 0, 1)
            layout.Controls.Add(buttons, 0, 2)

            Me.Controls.Add(layout)
            Me.AcceptButton = ok
            Me.CancelButton = cancel
        End Sub
    End Class

End Class
```

## @testsubject117

can the close button be lighter gray

## Copilot

Yes—change the Close button’s `BackColor` (and optionally its hover/down colors) in `DosMenuFormBase`.

In `DosMenuFormBase.BuildLayout()` where you configure `btnEscClose`, replace:

```vbnet
btnEscClose.BackColor = Color.DimGray
btnEscClose.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.Gray
```

with:

```vbnet
btnEscClose.BackColor = Color.Silver
btnEscClose.ForeColor = Color.Black
btnEscClose.FlatAppearance.BorderColor = Color.Gainsboro
btnEscClose.FlatAppearance.MouseOverBackColor = Color.Gainsboro
btnEscClose.FlatAppearance.MouseDownBackColor = Color.DarkGray
```

That will make it a lighter gray button (more like a standard “secondary” button) while still fitting your dark theme.

## @testsubject117

ok, I like it. now let's get all the menu choices working for formledgermenu

## Copilot

To get all the menu choices working, I need to wire each button to the *actual existing forms / workflows* in your project (right now several are still calling `NotYet(...)`).

I can do this two ways:

1) **You tell me what each option should do** (which form to open, any parameters/filters), and I’ll generate the final `FormLedgerMenu.vb`.
2) If you can share the repo (or paste the list of existing form/class names), I can map options to what already exists.

### What I need from you (minimal)
For each menu option below, tell me the target (form name or method) that should run:

- **(1)** Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts → ?
- **(2)** Delete Checks → ?
- **(3)** View Checks → (you already have prompt flow; should it open `FormLedgerView` with filters? yes/no)
- **(5)** View Companys Totals → (currently `FormLedgerCompanyTotals`) confirm OK?
- **(6)** Add OTHER Checks → ?
- **(7)** Delete OTHER Checks → ?
- **(8)** View OTHER Checks → (currently `FormOtherChecksView`) confirm OK?
- **(9)** Find a Check # → (currently `FormFindByCheckNumber`) confirm OK?
- **(0)** Find an Invoice # → (currently `FormFindByInvoiceNumber`) confirm OK?
- **(A)** Find Checks that don't balance → ?
- **(S)** Sales Employee's checks → ?

### If you prefer, paste this instead
Paste the contents of your Solution Explorer list under something like “Forms” (or run a quick search in VS):
- Search for: `Public Class Form`  
and paste the results (the form class names). Then I’ll propose a complete wiring plan and generate the updated code.

Once I have those mappings, I’ll output the final working `FormLedgerMenu.vb` (and any small helper changes needed) with all options implemented.

## @testsubject117

let's start with 1) add checks to ledger & cash Receipts: When i choose that in the dos version, it goes to a screen that says ***** Add A Check ***** then Company Name [Q = quit]? ______, then if I type a company name it gives me "Possible Companies:" and lists company names found such as if I type TEST is returns Possible Companies: Tebinc.prc, tecfarma.prc, technifa.prc, technolu.prc, tecproce.prc, tedeahun.prc, rempcoen.prc, and tessecma.prc., once I've typed a correct company name, it changes to ask Check amount? If I enter a check amount, it moves to a screen that states: I need the 1st and last invoice number so I can automatically scan for invoices, enter the lowest invoice number to be paid [-1 = No Auto Scan]? 

## Copilot

Got it. For option **(1) Add Checks to ledger & cash receipts**, you’re describing a multi-step “wizard” flow:

1. Title: `***** Add A Check *****`
2. Prompt: `Company Name [Q = quit]?`
3. As you type a partial name, it shows `Possible Companies:` and a list of matches (looks like it’s matching `*.prc` company files/records).
4. Once a valid company is selected, prompt: `Check amount?`
5. Then prompt: `Enter the lowest invoice number to be paid [-1 = No Auto Scan]?`
   (and later it will ask for highest/last invoice number and proceed to scanning/applying invoices)

### Before I generate code: I need to connect this to your existing data/code
To implement this correctly (and not re-invent your data access), I need to know where company names and invoices come from in your current VB.NET project.

Please answer **one** of these (A is easiest):

#### A) Do you already have a Windows form for this flow?
Search your project for likely names and tell me what exists:
- `FormAddCheck`
- `FormAddACheck`
- `FormLedgerAddCheck`
- `FormCashReceipts`
- `FormCheckEntry`
- anything with “Add Check” in the name

If it exists, we’ll just wire menu item (1) to open it.

In DOS you get results like `tebinc.prc`, etc. That strongly suggests **company master files** with extension `.prc`.

Where are those stored in your Windows version?

## @testsubject117

what do you mean language or toolchain, these files are .bas files from gwbasic

## Copilot

By “language/toolchain” I mean which BASIC dialect/interpreter/compiler the `.bas` source was written for, because `.bas` can be QuickBASIC, QBasic, GW-BASIC, PowerBASIC, VB-DOS, etc., and they differ in syntax and I/O behavior.

In your case: **GW-BASIC** is exactly the answer I needed.

That helps because GW-BASIC commonly uses patterns like:
- `LINE INPUT`, `INPUT#`, `PRINT#`
- `OPEN "I"/"O"/"R"` (including fixed-length/random records)
- `FIELD`, `LSET/RSET`, `GET/PUT` for random files
- `DEF SEG`, `POKE/PEEK` sometimes
- `ON ERROR GOTO`, `ON KEY`, `INKEY$` for key handling

When you upload the files, I’ll interpret them specifically as GW-BASIC and document:
- file formats implied by `FIELD` layouts
- record sizes and offsets
- how menus/prompt flows are driven (`INKEY$`, loops, screen writes)
- where company matching and invoice scanning live

Whenever you’re ready, upload the converted ASCII `.bas` files (zip is best).

## @testsubject117

my visual basic and windows crashed with a blue screen, after rebooting, visual studio would not launch or uninstall. I was finally able to uninstall and reinstall visual studio 2026. Now when I open the solution to this project everything seems fine, however if I try to click the play button while in release mode, I get an error that states "you are debugging a release build of amioffice.dll. using just my code with release build using compiler optimizations results in a degraded debugging experience

## Copilot

That message is a **warning**, not a crash: Visual Studio is telling you you’re starting the debugger (F5 / Play) while the project is built as **Release** with **optimizations**, so stepping/breakpoints can be weird.

### Fix (recommended): Debug in Debug
1. In the top toolbar, change **Solution Configurations** from **Release** → **Debug**.
2. Build + run with **F5**.

If you don’t see the dropdown: **Tools → Customize… → Toolbars** (enable *Standard*), or use **Build → Configuration Manager…**.

### If you *must* debug Release
You have a few options:

#### Option A — Turn off “Just My Code” (fast)
- **Tools → Options → Debugging → General**
  - Uncheck **Enable Just My Code (Managed only)**
  - (Optional) Check **Suppress JIT optimization on module load (Managed only)**

#### Option B — Generate full symbols for Release (best for stepping)
1. Right-click project **AMiOffice** → **Properties**
2. **Compile** tab:
   - Set **Advanced Compile Options…**
     - **Enable optimizations**: *unchecked* (for “Release-with-debug”)
     - **Generate debug info**: *Full* (or *Pdb-only* depending on your VS/VB options)
3. Rebuild.

#### Option C — Start without debugging
If you just want to run it:
- **Ctrl+F5** (Start Without Debugging)

### Why this happened after reinstall
After reinstall, VS often resets:
- your active config to **Release**
- debugging options like Just My Code / optimization suppression
- symbol settings

### Quick question so I tell you the exact clicks
Is your project **VB.NET Framework WinForms** or **VB.NET SDK-style (.NET 6/7/8/9) WinForms**?

Also: does it still run, and you’re only seeing this warning—or does it fail to start? If it fails, paste the exact dialog text and any Output window lines.

## @testsubject117

it will only run if I change it to debug, if I have it set to release I get the dialogue about turn off just my code. How can I tell if it's vb.net framework winforms vs. vb.net sdk-style?

## Copilot

You can tell by looking at the **project file** and the **Project Properties** UI.

## 1) Easiest: check the project file extension + contents
In Solution Explorer:

- If you see **AMiOffice.vbproj** (you will), right‑click it → **Edit Project File** (or “Unload Project” → “Edit …”).

### A) SDK-style (.NET / modern)
You’ll see something like:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0-windows</TargetFramework>
    <UseWindowsForms>true</UseWindowsForms>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Key indicators:
- `Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"`
- `TargetFramework` is `netX.Y-windows`
- `UseWindowsForms>true`

### B) .NET Framework (classic)
You’ll see:

```xml
<Project ToolsVersion="15.0" DefaultTargets="Build" xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFrameworkVersion>v4.8</TargetFrameworkVersion>
    ...
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Key indicators:
- `xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/developer/msbuild/2003"`
- `TargetFrameworkVersion` is `v4.x`

## 2) Also visible in Project Properties
Right-click project → **Properties**:

- **.NET Framework** projects usually have tabs like: **Application / Compile / Debug / References**
- **SDK-style** projects often show a simpler UI and may show **Target framework** as `net8.0-windows` etc.

---

## Why Release won’t run (and what to do)
That dialog about “turn off Just My Code” should not prevent running; it’s normally a warning shown when you start debugging Release. If it “only runs in Debug”, one of these is happening:

### Fix 1 (most likely): you’re pressing F5 (debugging) in Release
In Release, use **Ctrl+F5** to run without debugging.

### Fix 2: Release isn’t building the output / startup is wrong
Check:
- Right-click solution → **Set Startup Projects…**
  - choose **Single startup project** = your WinForms EXE project (not a DLL)
- Build → **Rebuild Solution**
- Then run Release with **Ctrl+F5**

### Fix 3: Release build fails but Debug succeeds
If Release has compile/link errors, you’d see them in Error List. If it “does nothing”, check Output window:
- View → Output → “Build”

---

## One diagnostic question
When you try to “run in Release”:
- Are you hitting **F5** (Start Debugging) or **Ctrl+F5** (Start Without Debugging)?
- And is AMiOffice supposed to be an **.exe**? You mentioned `AMiOffice.dll`—if your startup project is a class library, it won’t run.

If you paste the first ~20 lines of your `AMiOffice.vbproj` (or just tell me whether it contains `Project Sdk=`), I’ll tell you exactly which type you have and the exact Release settings to make it run.

## Copilot

Yes—paste the contents of `AMiOffice.vbproj` (or at least the top ~40–80 lines). From that I can tell immediately whether it’s:

- **SDK-style** (`<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">` … `TargetFramework` … `UseWindowsForms>true`)
or
- **classic .NET Framework** (`ToolsVersion` + MSBuild 2003 XML namespace + `TargetFrameworkVersion`).

If you prefer not to paste the whole file, just paste the very first line and the lines containing either `TargetFramework`/`TargetFrameworks` or `TargetFrameworkVersion`.

Also: it can include local paths/usernames—feel free to redact those if you want.

## @testsubject117

```
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>WinExe</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>net10.0-windows</TargetFramework>
    <StartupObject>AMiOffice.My.MyApplication</StartupObject>
    <EnableNETAnalyzers>false</EnableNETAnalyzers>

    <UseWindowsForms>true</UseWindowsForms>
    <MyType>WindowsForms</MyType>

    <AssemblyTitle>AMiOffice</AssemblyTitle>
    <Product>AMiOffice</Product>
    <Company>Kirk Saffell</Company>
    <Description>AMiOffice Application</Description>

    <!-- Versioning policy:
         - AssemblyVersion: changes rarely (typically major releases only)
         - FileVersion: changes every Publish (uses BuildNumber)
         - InformationalVersion: semver + build metadata -->
    <Version>1.0.0</Version>
    <AssemblyVersion>1.0.0.0</AssemblyVersion>

    <!-- Ensure BuildNumber is never blank for normal builds -->
    <BuildNumber Condition="'$(BuildNumber)'==''">0</BuildNumber>

    <!-- Update every Publish -->
    <FileVersion>$(Version).$(BuildNumber)</FileVersion>

    <!-- What Windows shows as "Product version" -->
    <ProductVersion>$(Version)</ProductVersion>

    <!-- Human-facing / marketing version -->
    <InformationalVersion>$(Version) (Build $(BuildNumber))</InformationalVersion>

    <GenerateAssemblyInfo>true</GenerateAssemblyInfo>

    <BuildInfoFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\Generated\BuildInfo.g.vb</BuildInfoFile>

    <Copyright>Copyright Kirk Saffell 2026</Copyright>
    <Title>AMiOfficeApplication</Title>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- VB global imports to make designer code compile reliably -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Import Include="System" />
    <Import Include="System.Windows.Forms" />
    <Import Include="System.Drawing" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Update="FormAbout.Designer.vb">
      <DependentUpon>FormAbout.vb</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>
    <EmbeddedResource Update="FormAbout.resx">
      <DependentUpon>FormAbout.vb</DependentUpon>
    </EmbeddedResource>
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- Hard-stop: never compile stray repo-root folders -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Remove="..\My Project\**\*.vb" />
    <Compile Remove="..\obj\**\*.vb" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <None Remove="ESC Logo.jpg" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <EmbeddedResource Include="ESC Logo.jpg">
      <CopyToOutputDirectory>Never</CopyToOutputDirectory>
    </EmbeddedResource>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Update="My Project\Application.Designer.vb">
      <DesignTime>True</DesignTime>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Application.myapp</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>

    <Compile Update="My Project\Resources.Designer.vb">
      <DesignTime>True</DesignTime>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Resources.resx</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>

    <Compile Update="My Project\Settings.Designer.vb">
      <DesignTimeSharedInput>True</DesignTimeSharedInput>
      <AutoGen>True</AutoGen>
      <DependentUpon>Settings.settings</DependentUpon>
    </Compile>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <EmbeddedResource Update="My Project\Resources.resx">
      <CustomToolNamespace>My.Resources</CustomToolNamespace>
      <Generator>VbMyResourcesResXFileCodeGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Resources.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </EmbeddedResource>
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <None Update="My Project\Application.myapp">
      <Generator>MyApplicationCodeGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Application.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </None>

    <None Update="My Project\Settings.settings">
      <CustomToolNamespace>My</CustomToolNamespace>
      <Generator>SettingsSingleFileGenerator</Generator>
      <LastGenOutput>Settings.Designer.vb</LastGenOutput>
    </None>
  </ItemGroup>

  <!-- Prevent compiling any stray BuildInfo file from the project root -->
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Remove="BuildInfo.g.vb" />
    <Compile Remove="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <Target Name="IncrementBuildCounter" BeforeTargets="Publish">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildCounterFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\build-counter.txt</BuildCounterFile>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      Condition="!Exists('$(BuildCounterFile)')"
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="0" />

    <ReadLinesFromFile File="$(BuildCounterFile)">
      <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildCounterLines" />
    </ReadLinesFromFile>

    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.Int32]::Parse('%(BuildCounterLines.Identity)'))</BuildNumber>
      <BuildNumber>$([MSBuild]::Add($(BuildNumber), 1))</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="$(BuildNumber)" />
  </Target>

  <Target Name="GenerateBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildCounterFile>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\build-counter.txt</BuildCounterFile>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <WriteLinesToFile
      Condition="!Exists('$(BuildCounterFile)')"
      File="$(BuildCounterFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="0" />

    <ReadLinesFromFile File="$(BuildCounterFile)">
      <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildCounterLines2" />
    </ReadLinesFromFile>

    <!-- Convert first line item into a real property -->
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.String]::Copy('%(BuildCounterLines2.Identity)'))</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>
    <Message Text="GenerateBuildInfo: BuildCounterFile=$(BuildCounterFile) BuildNumber=$(BuildNumber)" Importance="high" />

    <!-- Trim + fallback -->
    <PropertyGroup>
      <BuildNumber>$([System.String]::Copy('$(BuildNumber)').Trim())</BuildNumber>
      <BuildNumber Condition="'$(BuildNumber)'==''">0</BuildNumber>
    </PropertyGroup>

    <MakeDir Directories="$([System.IO.Path]::GetDirectoryName('$(BuildInfoFile)'))" />

    <WriteLinesToFile
      File="$(BuildInfoFile)"
      Overwrite="true"
      Lines="Option Strict On&#xD;&#xA;Option Explicit On&#xD;&#xA;&#xD;&#xA;Friend Module BuildInfo&#xD;&#xA;    Friend Const ProductVersion As String = &quot;$(ProductVersion)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;    Friend Const BuildNumber As String = &quot;$(BuildNumber)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;&#xD;&#xA;    Friend ReadOnly Property DisplayVersion As String&#xD;&#xA;        Get&#xD;&#xA;            Return &quot;AMiOffice v&quot; &amp; ProductVersion &amp; &quot; (Build &quot; &amp; BuildNumber &amp; &quot;)&quot;&#xD;&#xA;        End Get&#xD;&#xA;    End Property&#xD;&#xA;End Module&#xD;&#xA;" />

    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Remove="$(BuildInfoFile)" />
      <Compile Include="$(BuildInfoFile)" Visible="false" />
    </ItemGroup>
  </Target>

</Project>
```

## Copilot

Yes — this is **SDK-style VB.NET WinForms**, targeting **.NET 10**:

- `\<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk"\>` ⇒ SDK-style
- `<TargetFramework>net10.0-windows</TargetFramework>` + `<UseWindowsForms>true</UseWindowsForms>` ⇒ modern .NET WinForms

## Why Release “won’t run”
That dialog (“you are debugging a release build… turn off Just My Code…”) typically appears when you press **F5** in **Release**. It should not prevent the app from running, but with some VS setups it can feel like it’s blocking.

### Do this first (most likely fix)
Run Release **without debugging**:
- Build configuration: **Release**
- Start: **Ctrl + F5** (Start Without Debugging)

If Ctrl+F5 works, nothing is actually wrong with Release—only debugging it.

## If you want F5 to work in Release (and still be debuggable)
### Option 1: Disable “Just My Code”
**Tools → Options → Debugging → General**
- Uncheck **Enable Just My Code**
- (Optional) Check **Suppress JIT optimization on module load**

Restart VS and try F5 again in Release.

### Option 2: Make Release generate symbols and be “debuggable”
In SDK-style projects, you can force PDBs for Release by adding this to your `.vbproj`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(Configuration)'=='Release'">
  <DebugType>portable</DebugType>
  <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Then **Rebuild** and press F5 in Release.

### Option 3 (best practice): create a “ReleaseDebug” config
Keep Release optimized for publishing, but have a config that runs like Release with better debugging.

- Build → Configuration Manager → New…
  - Copy settings from Release
  - Name: `ReleaseDebug`
Then in `.vbproj`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(Configuration)'=='ReleaseDebug'">
  <Optimize>true</Optimize>        <!-- or false if you want perfect stepping -->
  <DebugType>portable</DebugType>
  <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
</PropertyGroup>
```

## One more important check: are you launching the EXE project?
You mentioned `AMiOffice.dll` in the warning, but your project outputs `WinExe`. In SDK-style WinForms the build produces both:
- `AMiOffice.exe`
- `AMiOffice.dll` (the managed assembly)

That’s normal. But make sure VS startup project is correct:
- Right-click **AMiOffice** project → **Set as Startup Project**

## Tell me which case you’re seeing
When you click Play in Release:
1) Does it show that dialog and then *stop*, or can you continue and it runs?
2) If you choose the option to disable Just My Code (or click OK), what happens?

If you paste the exact buttons/options shown in that dialog, I’ll tell you the exact choice to make so Release runs normally with F5.

## @testsubject117

when in release I get the dialogue about disable just my code. If I click disable just my code, it does run the amioffice menu

## Copilot

That’s expected behavior: you’re starting **debugging (F5)** a **Release/optimized** build, so Visual Studio warns that “Just My Code” + optimizations can make debugging unreliable. Clicking **Disable Just My Code** lets it proceed and the app runs.

If you want to stop seeing that prompt, you have three good options:

## Option 1 (recommended): Run Release without debugging
Use **Ctrl+F5** in Release. No debugger, no prompt.

## Option 2: Permanently disable “Just My Code”
- **Tools → Options → Debugging → General**
  - Uncheck **Enable Just My Code (Managed only)**

Then F5 in Release won’t prompt about it (and you’ll still be able to debug, just with more framework/optimized stepping weirdness).

## Option 3: Make Release “debug-friendly” (keeps Just My Code)
Add this to your `.vbproj` so Release always has symbols:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="'$(Configuration)'=='Release'">
  <DebugType>portable</DebugType>
  <DebugSymbols>true</DebugSymbols>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Rebuild. You may still get some optimization-related quirks, but it usually reduces friction.

### Practical workflow suggestion
- Use **Debug** config for normal development/debugging.
- Use **Release** for publishing/perf checks (run via Ctrl+F5).

If you tell me which you prefer (hide the warning entirely vs keep “Just My Code”), I’ll recommend the exact setting and minimal project-file change for your setup.

## @testsubject117

I'm trying to convert the file cardscan.bas, but whenever I try and load it in gwbasic I get an error: "LINE BUFFER OVERFLOW"

## Copilot

In GW-BASIC, **“LINE BUFFER OVERFLOW”** almost always means the interpreter hit a source line that’s **longer than GW-BASIC’s maximum line length** (commonly ~255 characters, sometimes effectively less depending on tokens). This often happens in legacy code that has:
- huge `PRINT` statements
- long `DATA` lines
- long quoted strings
- long `IF ... THEN ... ELSE ...` on one line
- long `DEF FN...` lines

### Best way to convert it to ASCII (without loading in GW-BASIC)
You don’t actually need to `LOAD` it into GW-BASIC to convert it.

#### If `cardscan.bas` is already a plain text `.BAS`
Try opening it directly in:
- Notepad++
- VS Code
- even plain Notepad

If it opens and looks readable, it’s already ASCII.

#### If it’s tokenized GW-BASIC source (saved from GW-BASIC)
A tokenized BAS will look like binary/garbage in a text editor. In that case you need a detokenizer—but you can still do it **without GW-BASIC successfully loading it**.

### Option A (most reliable): detokenize with a modern tool
Use a detokenizer that can handle long lines better than GW-BASIC. Common choices:
- **BAS2TXT** / **GW-BASIC detokenizer** utilities
- DOSBox + a different BASIC (sometimes QBasic can load some, sometimes not)
- Python detokenizer scripts for GW-BASIC

If you tell me:
1) does `cardscan.bas` look like readable text in Notepad?
2) file size (KB)
3) first ~50 characters if opened in Notepad (or say “it’s garbage/binary”)

…I’ll tell you exactly which path to use.

### Option B: fix the long line(s) so GW-BASIC can load it
If you *must* use GW-BASIC to `LOAD` it, you need to split the too-long line(s).

#### How to identify the offending line
GW-BASIC often can’t tell you the line number on overflow. So do this in a text editor:

1) Search for extremely long lines:
   - lines with very long quoted strings `"...."`
   - `DATA` lines with lots of comma-separated values
   - `PRINT` with lots of `;` concatenations

2) Once found, split them using GW-BASIC-friendly patterns:
- For long strings in PRINT:
  ```basic
  PRINT "first part";
  PRINT "second part"
  ```
- For long string constants:
  ```basic
  A$ = "first part" + "second part"
  ```
  (or `A$="first part": A$=A$+"second part"`)

- For long DATA:
  Split into multiple `DATA` lines (same line number not required; just additional `DATA` statements).

### Option C: If it’s tokenized and you can’t load it because of overflow
That usually indicates the tokenized file contains a line that expands past the buffer when detokenized. A detokenizer tool (Option A) is the right solution.

---

## Quick questions (so I can give the exact next step)
1) When you open `cardscan.bas` in Notepad/VS Code, is it **readable BASIC text** or **binary/garbage**?
2) Roughly how big is the file?
3) If it’s readable text, can you paste the **longest-looking line** (or the section around it)? (You can redact data values if needed.)

With those answers, I’ll tell you the quickest exact fix and, if needed, the best detokenizer approach for GW-BASIC tokenized files.
